title: Bash redirect/mutation target with $VAR or ~ is auto-allowed outside the project
description: CRITICAL permission bypass. src/agent/tools/bash/check.rs resolve_target (line 104) and src/permission/engine/build.rs classify_path (line 82) never expand shell $VAR / ~ before classifying a redirect or mutation target. Command: echo pwned > $HOME/.bashrc under default config. The echo segment matches the builtin echo ** allow; the target $HOME/.bashrc is not absolute so resolve_target lexically joins it as <cwd>/$HOME/.bashrc; resolve_absolute (permission/path.rs:145) finds it nonexistent, reattaches the tail under the canonical cwd, so classify_path marks in_cwd=true; BuiltinAllow allows the Edit claim -> meet(Allow,Allow)=Allow, NO prompt. But bash expands $HOME and writes the real ~/.bashrc outside the project. Same for cp secret $HOME/x, ~/x, $PWD/../x. Affects both semantic-bash and coarse paths. Fix: expand ~/$VAR before classifying, or route any redirect/mutation target containing ~ $ or an unresolved variable to a complex/whole-command claim so it prompts.
close_reason: Gate bash redirect/mutation targets the shell expands (/~/backtick) so they route through the external-dir prompt instead of the in-cwd fast path. Fix in bash layer only (check.rs target_expands_outside_cwd), both semantic + coarse paths.

title: Compaction summaries silently dropped from outgoing provider requests
description: value_to_rig_message_for_provider matches only user/assistant/tool/toolResult; system-role messages hit _ => None. After any auto-fold or /compress on the main rig path, the fold summary (role:system) is dropped from every request -> model sees head+tail with the folded middle AND its summary gone. Also drops mid-session 'Updated memory' reinject. Masked by session restore merging system history into preamble. Fix: add a system arm mapping to rig system message (or append to preamble) + a factory test with SUMMARY_PREFIX. File: src/agent/agent_loop/rig_stream_factory.rs:374-441. Round R2.
close_reason: Fixed: value_to_rig_message_for_provider now maps role:system to a user message so compaction fold summaries reach the model on the live path. Regression test added; restore path unaffected (system still merged to preamble at spawn).

title: Permission bypass: command substitution rides in on allow-listed head
description: `echo $(rm -rf ~)` matches the builtin `echo **` allow rule and executes with NO prompt. has_complex_constructs marks it complex, then check_bash_segments pushes the whole raw string as ONE Execute claim with head=first token, which matches safe-bash allow rules -> Effect::Allow. Same for backticks, <(...), >(...), arithmetic. Mutation extractor doesn't descend into the substitution either. Fix: tag complex whole-command Resource::Command so ConfiguredRule/BuiltinAllow refuse to allow it (force Ask/Deny). Files: src/agent/tools/bash/check.rs:185, src/semantic/adapters/bash.rs:468. Round R1.
close_reason: Fixed: complex commands (substitution/subshell) no longer silently allowed by configured-rule or session-allowlist deciders; deny still honored. 3 regression tests added.

title: Plugin confirm-gate fails open: gated tool executes while dialog on screen
description: on-tool-start hooks get HOOK_TIMEOUT=5s, but harness/confirm blocks up to DIALOG_TIMEOUT=600s waiting for a human. Any answer slower than 5s times the hook out; timeout arm logs and continues; ToolHookResult returns block:None; plugin_hooks treats hook error as don't-fail-the-call -> the rm executes while confirm dialog is still displayed, later 'deny' silently discarded. Documented flagship safety pattern (plugins.md:333). Fix: on tool-hook dispatch timeout, fail CLOSED for on-tool-start. Files: src/plugin/mod.rs:650,706, src/agent/agent_loop/plugin_hooks.rs:126. Round R4.
close_reason: Fixed: eval_with_timeout now waits while a confirm/select dialog is in flight (shared dialog_pending counter), so the on-tool-start confirm gate no longer fails open — the tool waits for the human answer and honors deny. Stuck non-dialog hooks still time out fast. 2 regression tests added.

title: Post-audit remediation (v0.16.0 multi-axis review)
description: Umbrella for findings from the 12-axis code review tracked in dirge-p0sv. Children grouped by round R1-R8: R1 security, R2 data/context loss, R3 file-mutation, R4 plugin safety, R5 gates/provider, R6 skills, R7 integrations, R8 doc-drift+refactor.

title: Plugin-registered Janet tools bypass the permission engine
description: JanetLoopTool::execute (plugin/extension.rs:377-463; builder/loop_tools.rs:522-538) runs the Janet handler with NO enforce()/permission check, unlike every built-in tool. Janet has full file/network/shell stdlib (only os/exit, proc-kill, sigaction neutered). An LLM calling a plugin-registered tool performs arbitrary side effects without hitting allow/deny/ask. Tool-name shadowing is blocked and before/after hooks fire, but neither enforces user permission. Fix: thread a PermCheck into JanetLoopTool::from_meta and gate execute like built-ins.
close_reason: Plugin tools now route through the permission chokepoint. Added Operation::Plugin (high-risk: not builtin-allowed, not Accept-coerced, like MCP/bash). JanetLoopTool gates execute() via a deny_tools probe (concrete name + plugin_tool umbrella) + enforce(plugin_tool, Scope::Raw(name)) before any Janet dispatch. Default Ask in standard mode, Allow under yolo, Deny via deny_tools/rules. Tests: 3 in extension.rs (deny/ask-noninteractive/yolo-runs), 2 in checker_tests (accept-not-coerced, yolo-allows/standard-asks), 1 mapping assertion.

title: HIGH: tool output not scrubbed for secrets before LLM/session storage
description: Secret redaction (sandbox.rs is_sensitive_env_name/value) is applied only to subprocess env (input). Tool RESULTS flow verbatim to the LLM (via cap_oversized_tool_results) and to session storage. cat .env / echo $API_KEY / env leak. Fix: a single redact_secrets() boundary on tool output reusing the existing detector. sandbox.rs:121,181; ui/events.rs:317 (ANSI-only).

title: HIGH: /allow remove <n> doesn't revoke engine grant
description: remove_session_allowlist_at only mutates the display list; the engine allowlist (runtime source of truth read by SessionAllowlistPolicy) keeps the grant. Revoked perms stay active. Must remove engine entry by matched (op, original), not index.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #204 (TDD, 4 new tests); 2104 tests pass at -D warnings

title: Perm review F1: bash arg-side path checks for file-mutating commands
description:
SECURITY GAP found in opencode-vs-dirge review. Today (post-M3, fbcc09b) dirge's bash permission flow extracts ONLY redirect targets (>, >>, &>, etc.) via extract_redirect_targets and routes them through write rules. Arguments of file-mutating commands (rm, cp, mv, chmod, chown, ln, mkdir, rmdir, touch, tee, dd) are NOT extracted — they go only through the bash command-pattern rules. 

Concrete bypass: a user who configures bash rules permissively (e.g., 'rm *: allow' for convenience) silently allows 'rm /etc/passwd' even though write rules deny /etc/**. Opencode (shell.ts:374-410) walks the 'command' AST nodes, identifies file-mutating heads, and routes each positional path arg through the external_directory / write permission.

Port: extend src/semantic/adapters/bash.rs with an extract_mutation_paths(command) function that walks the tree-sitter 'command' nodes; for each command whose head matches the list above, extract positional args that look like paths (skip -flags / --long-flags) and emit them. In src/agent/tools/bash.rs check_bash_segments, after the existing redirect-target loop, walk extracted mutation paths and route through enforce(tool='write', Scope::PathResolve(path)).

title: Perm M3: port maki's tree-sitter bash analyzer (close git&&rm bypass)
description:
SECURITY: 'git diff && rm -rf /' currently allowed because dirge's bash redirect-target check (src/agent/tools/bash.rs:350) routes through bash rules with the file path as input, which has no path-style match → falls to default Allow. Pre-existing pre-fix-for-7403792.

Solution: port maki's tree-sitter bash analyzer verbatim from /Users/yogthos/src/maki/maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:33-43 (parser thread_local), 394-439 (collect_commands walker), 441-475 (analyze_bash + complexity gates). The walker splits compounds via 'pipeline'/'list' AST traversal and extracts every 'command' / 'redirected_statement' / 'subshell' / etc node; each segment then goes through the permission chokepoint independently.

Behavior at completion:
- 'git diff && rm -rf /' → enforce('bash', 'git diff') + enforce('bash', 'rm -rf /') — second check fires
- Subshells / command substitution mark whole command 'complex' → forces prompt (conservative)
- Pipes split into separate segments
- Quoted operators correctly NOT split (AST respects quoting)

Depends on: dirge-{M1}

Maki license is GPL-compatible — verify before copying. Add 'Ported from maki-agent/src/permissions.rs' attribution comment.

title: Merge finalization critic + code-reviewer into one judge; make advisory re-enter (kill display-only path)
close_reason: Merged the finalization critic and diff-aware code-reviewer into ONE judge call (critic::run_unified_review): judges completeness AND reviews the run's diff, returning one consolidated follow-up. Advisory (default) now re-enters the loop once with any finding (high/critical must-fix, medium/low optional) instead of a display-only background notice the model never saw; blocking still persists to MAX_REVIEW_REACT. build.rs appends the reviewer role to the (custom-able) critic preamble when code_review!=off. Deleted the detached-advisory path: background_review_notice, REVIEW_NOTICE_SINK + setter/getter, main.rs channel setup, mod.rs review_notice_rx consumer, decide_review_reaction/ReviewReaction, advisory_notice/blocking_followup, run_critic/build_prompt/CRITIC_FORMAT. code_review_fn stays armed for /review only. Net -176 lines. New tests: parse_unified, build_unified_followup (reenter for high-when-complete / low-only / combined), run_unified_review, finalization_unified_judge_reenters_on_finding. Full suite 4334 pass.

title: /model free-form ids silently stay on active client; add family-aware cross-provider routing
close_reason: Family-aware /model routing: model_family + resolve_model_switch in provider/resolve.rs; free-form ids (glm-4.6) route to the configured provider of their family, exact pins still win, unconfigured families refuse with a warning. Banner reads session state (events.rs). apply_next_model no longer clobbers session.provider and refuses cross-provider swaps (done.rs). 16 new tests; e2e-verified via mock GLM endpoint.

title: Writer sandbox binds parent .git read-write — writer can rewrite parent refs/hooks (PR670 follow-up)
Subagent worktree writers ignore profile allow/deny (PR670 follow-up)
Split issues (passive backlog) from todos (active work queue); beads-style hash ids + epics
description: GH #668: issue create currently scopes new issues to the session, so filing 'an issue for later' lands it on the session board -> the finish-your-work nudge nags and the model does a close/reopen dance. Fix: issue create files UNASSIGNED (session_id NULL) = passive backlog; issue start claims it to the session = active. Nudge/panel already read board_for_session so passive never nags. Add beads-style hash ids (drg-xxxx, id TEXT PK) replacing integer autoincrement, with a one-time migration of the legacy integer-id table. Add nullable epic_id column to group issues under an epic (epic = any parent issue). Split the turn-start context injection into 'active work queue' vs 'backlog' so the model has a clear current task each turn. Implemented via dirge delegation; reviewed here.
close_reason: Landed in PR #669 (merge b9ffd31a); released in 0.19.8

title: LSP servers (rust-analyzer) orphaned on signal exit — setsid detaches them, only Drop reaps
description: rust-analyzer is spawned via child_guard::detach_session (setsid: new session, no controlling terminal, own process group). The ONLY thing that kills it is ProcessGroupGuard::drop -> kill(-pgid, SIGKILL), which requires Rust destructors to run. Because setsid detaches the server from dirge's terminal, terminal-delivered signals (SIGHUP on terminal close, SIGINT/SIGQUIT to the foreground group) never reach it. So when dirge dies WITHOUT running Drop — SIGTERM (kill), SIGHUP (terminal/tab close), a real SIGINT — rust-analyzer is orphaned and keeps indexing (1GB+ RAM). dirge has NO signal handler anywhere (verified: no tokio::signal/ctrlc). Normal interactive /quit and clean --print/--loop returns DO drop the manager and reap the server; this is strictly an abnormal-exit leak. Same gap affects MCP servers, DAP adapters, and bg-shells (all setsid + Drop-only kill; DAP shutdown_active_session and bg-shell kill_all run only on the normal return path). Fix: global child-pgid registry + a tokio::signal reaper (SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP) that kill(-pgid) all groups, restores the terminal, and exits. Relates to dirge-8m69 (graceful LSP shutdown handshake).
close_reason: Global child-pgid registry in child_guard + tokio signal reaper (SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP) that kill(-pgid) all detached groups, restore the terminal, and exit 128+signum. Covers LSP/DAP/MCP-client (shared ProcessGroupGuard auto-registers) plus bash + mcp_server guards. src/signal.rs::install_reaper wired early in main. Was: setsid detaches servers from the terminal, so signal-death (no Drop) orphaned rust-analyzer.

title: agent_loop: checkpoint-reuse fold returns old-list index — snapshots spliced at wrong position
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:957 with compression.rs:794-809 and run.rs:1180-1182 (logic). The checkpoint-reuse fold returns SummaryOutcome::Succeeded(cut) where cut is an OLD-list index, but Succeeded(idx) is documented (run.rs:727-731) and consumed by restore_working_files (run.rs:1180: (summary_idx+1).min(len)) as the NEW-list index of the inserted summary — which on this path is always 0 (apply_checkpoint_summary produces [summary] + messages[cut..]). The inline path correctly returns Succeeded(start). Incremental checkpointing is default-on, so this is the common fold path: with cut > n/2 the post-compaction file snapshots insert at the END of the list (after newest messages); with cut <= n/2 they splice mid-tail, potentially between an assistant tool_use and its toolResult — breaking the orphaned-pair invariant and risking a provider 400. Secondary: ContextCompacted.first_kept_index carries two different meanings per path.
Fix: return the new-list summary index (0) on the reuse path; document first_kept_index semantics. Audit item 3.

title: renderer: remove_chat never loads next snapshot — surviving chat history overwritten
description:
src/ui/renderer.rs:1187-1198 (logic/structural). remove_chat never calls load_active() when removing the ACTIVE chat, and its only call site (ui/mod.rs:2228, Ctrl+X CloseChat) always passes the active index. After chats.remove(idx), active_chat indexes the next chat's snapshot but the hot fields (buffer/source/scroll/selection) still hold the closed chat's content — screen keeps showing the closed chat. Next switch_chat save_active() writes the dead content into the surviving chat's snapshot slot, permanently replacing its history.
Fix: load_active() after removal (and see companion issue on subagent chat-index maps). Audit item 2.

title: ui: shell-exit drain clobbers is_running — double agent run
description:
src/ui/mod.rs:3113 (logic). Shell Exited else-branch (!! invisible or interrupted ! run) runs drain_interjections!() then unconditionally sets is_running = false, clobbering the true that install_into just set when the queue was non-empty. Status shows idle during the drained run; a second prompt takes the not-running submit path and install_into overwrites agent_rx/agent_abort/agent_interject/agent_cancel — the first runner's JoinHandle is dropped, giving two concurrent agent runs with interleaved side effects. Ctrl+C can't cancel the drained run (abort branch at 1768 requires is_running), and Ctrl+C on empty input exits the app mid-run. The other three drain sites (3933, 4033, 4114) correctly do not force false.
Fix: only set is_running=false when the drain didn't spawn (match the other sites). Audit item 1.

title: Wavescope entropy audit remediation (2026-07-11)
description: Multi-agent audit driven by wavelet entropy analysis (get_entropy_bands + get_complexity_heatmap over all 403 src files; top ~16 by structural entropy audited region by region). 49 verified findings: 3 P1, 14 P2, 32 P3. Categories: logic errors, single-source-of-truth drift, unreachable code, structural gaps. All verified against code before filing; FTS finding verified empirically against sqlite3. No overlap with previously open issues.

title: Alert-overlay scroll clamp uses pre-Layout height; long permission command unreachable
description: src/ui/renderer.rs:973-989 vs src/ui/tui/layout.rs:119: tui_redraw sizes the overlay rows=wrapped.clamp(1, rows_q-9) (bypassing the 8-row cap) and computes alert_max_scroll with inner_h=rows, but render_frame -> Layout::with_panels clamps input_rows to MAX_INPUT_ROWS=8 so the painted inner height is 8. On a 30-row terminal an 18-row alert head gives rows=19, no overflow, alert_max_scroll=0; the painter shows 6 head rows plus a 'more lines, scroll' hint but alert_scroll_down clamps to 0 -- the keys do nothing and the command tail is unreadable before approval. Re-opens dirge-coy3/#587. Fix: compute alert_max_scroll from the post-Layout-clamp inner height (or drop the clamp for overlay bodies).
close_reason: Fixed on p1-review-fixes: extracted overlay_scroll_geom() pure helper in bottom.rs; both the painter and the renderer's alert branch now compute scroll geometry from it using the post-Layout-clamp inner height (rows.min(MAX_INPUT_ROWS)), so alert_max_scroll matches the painter's window and a long permission command is scrollable. Test overlay_scroll_geom_uses_painted_height. Integration RED skipped (tui_redraw probes real tty, no size-injection seam).

title: Anthropic token refresh overwrites Claude Code credentials file, dropping its other fields
description: src/provider/anthropic_oauth.rs persist_credentials_to_path (167-174) writes ~/.claude/.credentials.json as exactly {claudeAiOauth:{accessToken,refreshToken,expiresAt}} via save_json_0600 -> full overwrite. That is Claude Code's own file; its real claudeAiOauth object carries extra fields (scopes, subscriptionType, ...) and possible sibling top-level keys. A mid-session dirge refresh (wired at client.rs:322) drops them all, and since the refresh token is single-use and now rotated, Claude Code is left with a stripped credential it may reject. Contrast auth/store.rs save_openai (148-180) which preserves unknown fields and sibling providers. Fix: read-modify-write, merge the three fields into the existing object.
close_reason: Fixed on p1-review-fixes: persist_credentials_to_path is now a read-modify-write merge that updates only dirge's three fields (via serde_json::to_value, no hardcoded keys) and preserves other claudeAiOauth fields + sibling top-level keys. No lock re-acquire (caller holds it). Test persist_preserves_foreign_credential_fields.

title: Inline critic/goal/code-review judge calls have no timeout and can freeze finalization
description: src/agent/agent_loop/critic.rs run_judge! (55-66), code_review.rs (852-934), run.rs (389-395,474-490): side-LLM judge calls on the finalization path have no timeout at any layer; run_oneshot (provider/summarize.rs:193-243) drains a stream with no chunk or overall deadline. Contrast the compaction summarizer and background checkpoint which are bounded by explicit 120s timeouts (run.rs:738-743,975) precisely because a provider that stalls without erroring freezes the session. With code_review_mode=Blocking (or critic/goal gate armed), a judge provider that opens a stream and stops emitting freezes finalization forever; two-pass review gives two chances. Fix: one shared bounded-judge helper (timeout + fail-open) mirroring COMPACTION_SUMMARY_TIMEOUT.
close_reason: Fixed on p1-review-fixes: run_judge! macro now wraps the judge future in tokio::time::timeout(JUDGE_TIMEOUT=120s) and fails open on elapse (new Err(_) arm), mirroring COMPACTION_SUMMARY_TIMEOUT. Bounds all four judge sites (critic, goal, both code-review passes) at one point. Test judge_timeout_fails_open_to_empty via paused clock.

title: Resume-after-failure gate re-issues permission-denied and storm-suppressed tool calls
description: src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs poll_finalization_follow_up (304-320): last_action_failed_and_stopped (237-262) checks only tr.is_error and does not exclude permission denials or storm-breaker backfill results. (A) user denies a bash call -> error 'Permission denied by user' -> model stops and asks -> gate injects a [resume] nudge -> model re-issues the denied call, re-prompting/auto-denying up to MAX_RESUME_NUDGE(3) times; contradicts Outcome::Denied (activity.rs:47) and critic [DENIED] handling (run.rs:2465). (B) storm give-up backfills is_error:true results (tools.rs:1011) then the give-up narrative (run.rs:1934) matches the gate; guards.reset_turn (1450) cleared the storm window so the re-issued call executes again. Fix: exclude permission-denial and backfill/guard-stub results, or skip the gate after a storm give-up.
close_reason: Fixed on p1-review-fixes: last_action_failed_and_stopped now arms only on is_retryable_failure(tr) — excludes Outcome::Denied (permission refusals) and storm-breaker backfill stubs (hoisted to tools::SUPPRESSED_CALL_NOTE). Resume nudge no longer re-issues denied/suppressed calls; a mixed tail with a genuine error still arms. Tests: false_on_permission_denial, false_on_suppressed_backfill_stub, true_on_genuine_error, true_when_mixed_denial_and_genuine.

title: JSON-RPC write timeout leaves a partial frame and silently desyncs the stream
description: src/jsonrpc_client.rs:156-174 (and notify 206-215): on WRITE_TIMEOUT or write error the pending entry is rolled back but inner.closed is never set and the transport is not torn down. The encode_frame future is dropped mid-write (timeout wraps it), leaving a truncated Content-Length frame in the pipe; the next request writes a fresh frame at the wrong byte offset. Peer stalls >10s (indexing/GC/swap), recovers, and from then on every frame boundary is desynced while closed stays false and callers keep retrying on the corrupt stream (LSP and DAP). Fix: on write timeout or any partial-write error, mark closed, drain pending, tear the transport down.
close_reason: Fixed on p1-review-fixes: request/notify now call fail_transport() on any write failure or timeout — sets inner.closed and drains pending — so a partial frame can't leave the stream open for a desyncing next write. Test write_failure_closes_transport with self-contained mock Protocol/writer/reader.

title: Compaction summary serializes block-array tool results as empty strings
description: src/agent/compression.rs serialize_turns_for_summary (677-695) reads content only via .as_str(); production tool results are block arrays ([{type:text,text:...}]) so they serialize as empty. Same class as dirge-u5ka, already fixed in content_chars (196) and content_text (375) but missed here. run_compaction_pass_with_focus (agent_loop/run.rs:938) feeds live messages straight in, so every tool result (errors, command output, file paths) reaches the summarizer as '[i] toolResult: '. Recall eval passes only because its fixtures are scalar strings. Fix: use content_text() here.
close_reason: Fixed on branch p1-review-fixes: serialize_turns_for_summary now routes content through content_text() so block-array tool results are flattened instead of serialized empty. Test serialize_turns_includes_block_array_tool_results.

title: Read-cache hit returns pre-injection-guard content, defeating the scanner
description: src/agent/tools/read.rs:463 caches the pre-guard text (info) and the cache-hit path at line 259 returns it verbatim without guard_untrusted_result. Scenario: model reads a poisoned file (first result fenced, or body withheld under InjectionScanMode::Block per content_guard.rs:441); reads the same unchanged file again same cache generation -> raw un-fenced content enters context, defeating Advisory fencing AND Block-mode withholding. The default_note prepend is also lost on hits. Fix: cache the guarded output, or apply guard_untrusted_result on the hit path too.
close_reason: Fixed on p1-review-fixes: read.rs now guards the body BEFORE caching (cache.set stores the guarded value) and applies the per-request note via with_note() on both the hit and miss paths, so a cache hit is fenced/withheld identically to a miss. Test cache_hit_returns_guarded_body_not_raw.

title: Background review: idle avatar while is_running=true strands queued messages (#621)
description: Two busy-indicators desync. is_running:bool governs send-vs-queue; the avatar face (^_^)=AvatarState::Done is set unconditionally at handle_done top (done.rs:170). After finalization the plugin done_phase early-return and the /plan drive_plan_review path keep is_running=true while painting Done, and set agent_interject=None. User sees idle face, types a message, it queues (is_running=true) but the interject signal is dead and finalize (which drains) was skipped. On completion several paths drop the queue without dispatch: handle_error clear (error.rs:111), done_phase None arm (mod.rs:3716 flips is_running=false without draining), non-draining Retry relaunch. Fix: (1) don't paint Done at done.rs:170 unconditionally — only when going idle; show a busy face on paths that keep is_running=true. (2) done_phase None arm should route through finalize_idle_turn to drain. GH #621.

title: cargo install dirge-agent fails: rig caret pin drifts onto breaking patch
description: Published dirge-agent 0.19.1 depends on rig with a caret constraint. rig ships breaking API changes in patch releases (0.37.0 -> 0.37.1 added required Text.additional_params), so cargo install (no --locked) resolves the newer rig and fails to compile with E0063. #615 upgraded main to rig 0.39 but is unpublished and still uses a caret (^0.39), so it will recur on the next rig patch. Fix: pin rig and rig-core to exact versions, bump dirge version, publish. GH #616.

title: Ghostty: unthrottled focus reassert causes ~1min MCP-log/render stall
description: Ghostty echoes focus events (ESC[I/ESC[O) whenever ?1004h (focus reporting) is enabled. dirge's sole FocusGained handler calls renderer.reassert_modes_light(), which re-emits TERMINAL_MODE_REASSERT (includes ?1004h) UNTHROTTLED. On Ghostty this is a tight feedback loop: ?1004h -> FocusGained -> reassert -> ?1004h -> ... saturating the UI select loop and starving MCP-log notification paints for ~1min. iTerm2 doesn't echo focus-on-enable so it's instant there. Confirmed empirically: MCP servers themselves start in <0.35s regardless of TERM; the delay is 100% display-side. force_terminal_reassert already throttles (500ms) to break exactly this loop (renderer.rs:248-252 documents it); the light path lacks the guard. Fix: throttle reassert_modes_light on last_mode_reassert.

title: F1: skip full scrollback rebuild on no-op/duplicate terminal resize
description: Every crossterm Event::Resize (input_reader.rs:122, dims discarded -> UserEvent::Resize unit variant event.rs:256) unconditionally calls renderer.rebuild() (mod.rs:1536), which re-parses markdown + re-wraps EVERY source block in the scrollback (renderer.rs rebuild(), up to 20k rows). No dimension-change guard, so duplicate/spurious resizes (crossterm emits dupes; some terminals fire Resize on focus) do a full rebuild for nothing. Fix: forward the new (cols,rows) on Resize and skip rebuild()+repaint when unchanged from the last handled size.
close_reason: Merged in PR #608: UserEvent::Resize carries dims; handler skips rebuild+repaint on unchanged size.

title: Read-injection scanner: scan untrusted tool results (read/MCP/websearch) for prompt-injection at ingestion
description: dirge fences bash(!)/webfetch output but read-tool, MCP, and websearch results enter context unscanned (chokepoint RigToolAdapter::execute rig_tool.rs:194; read.rs:432, mcp/tool.rs:294, websearch.rs). Port GSD read-injection-scanner's 4 detector families (standard injection, summarisation-survival, markdown-link exfiltration, invisible-Unicode incl. U+E0000 Tag block). Shared pure scanner module + tri-state config (off/advisory/block, default advisory); advisory fences+annotates, block opt-in on >=N high-severity. Reuse/extend content_guard (memory_db.rs:272).
close_reason: Merged in PR #600: read/MCP/websearch results scanned for injection at ingestion; advisory-default fences, opt-in block; read-scanning uses only the prompt-injection subset to avoid false positives.

title: DeepSeek: disable-thinking one-shots use wrong wire shape (chat_template_kwargs ignored)
description: reasoning_disable_for_kind in provider/summarize.rs sends {chat_template_kwargs:{thinking:false}} for the deepseek kind to turn off reasoning on summarizer/critic/approval one-shots. Verified against api.deepseek.com: this is IGNORED (deepseek-v4 still emits reasoning_content, ~130 reasoning tokens). The hosted DeepSeek API disables thinking only via {thinking:{type:disabled}} (verified: 0 reasoning tokens). Fix: for the deepseek kind emit {thinking:{type:disabled}} (keep chat_template_kwargs for self-hosted custom/openrouter/vLLM). DeepSeek-specific.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #598 (merged): deepseek/glm disable-thinking uses thinking:{type:disabled}; deepseek effort switched to top-level reasoning_effort with max tier.

title: Question modal: multi-select custom answer can't be submitted; Space does nothing on custom row
description: In the interactive question modal (multi_select + custom), after typing a custom answer the cursor stays on the (custom) row. Pressing Enter re-opens the text editor (custom && cursor==num_options branch) instead of confirming, so the questionnaire can never be submitted. Space on the custom row is a no-op. Users also can't discover that Space toggles checkboxes. Fix: on the custom row in multi-select, Enter confirms once custom text exists; Space (re)opens the editor seeded with the existing text; footer shows 'Space edit' on the custom row.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #595 (merged): multi-select custom answer now confirms via Enter, Space edits on custom row.

title: Permission prompt truncates long/multi-line commands with no way to see the full text
description: GitHub #587 (Steffen Dienst): the bash permission alert shows 'command: <cmd>' but truncates multi-line/long commands with a bare '…', and the modal swallows all non-decision keys, so the user must approve a command they can't fully read. Ctrl+O doesn't expand it. The in-scrollback ALERT copy was deliberately removed (overlay is single source of truth), so the overlay itself must show the whole command. Fix: make the permission overlay scrollable — window the head, pin the action-keys row, add a scroll-status line with key hints (Up/Down, PgUp/PgDn, Ctrl+O). The full command is then always readable before deciding.
close_reason: Made the permission alert overlay scrollable: window the command head by an offset, pin the [y]/[a]/[n]/[ESC] action row, add a scroll-status line, and route Up/Down/PgUp/PgDn/Ctrl+O in the Permission modal to scroll instead of decide. Full command is always readable before approving. Tests + clippy pass.

title: Auto-recover terminal modes on FocusGained (alt screen + mouse)
description: Mouse/selection breaks after switching away and back to the terminal window: an external FocusLost/refocus cycle drops dirge out of the alternate screen, so the wheel scrolls native scrollback and mouse events stop reaching the app. Periodic mouse-only reassert can't fix it (problem is the main screen, not mouse mode); only Ctrl+L (full re-assert incl. ?1049h) recovers. Fix: enable focus reporting (?1004h), forward FocusGained, and on it run the full re-assert wrapped in ?2026 synchronized output so recovery is automatic and flicker-free. Replaces the manual Ctrl+L hatch as the primary recovery.
close_reason: Enabled focus reporting (?1004h) and re-assert full terminal modes (alt screen + mouse + paste) on FocusGained, wrapped in synchronized output. Switching away/back to the window auto-recovers the alt screen with no keypress; Ctrl+L kept as manual backstop.

title: Plan-mode reminder tells model to write PLAN.md while plan mode denies write
description: append_mode_reminder('plan') appends 'Save it to PLAN.md', landing after the plan prompt body (closer to the action boundary), but prompts/plan.md denies write/edit/apply_patch and says deliver-in-chat. Every /prompt plan session gets contradictory instructions; compliant model attempts a denied write. Also prompts/plan.md:24 'exception of the plan file described below' is dangling (no plan file described). Fix: rewrite the plan arm to the deliver-in-chat contract; delete the dangling clause. Files: src/agent/builder/preamble.rs:92, prompts/plan.md. Round R8.

title: DAP Janet bridge hardcodes thread_id=0 for step/continue/stack-trace
description: (dap/step)/(dap/continue)/(dap/stack-trace) call step_over(0)/continue_(0)/stack_trace(0,None); session.rs sends thread_id verbatim and never records the last stopped thread. Adapters that validate threadId (debugpy) return 'thread not found' -> documented /dap-repl and profiler workflows silently fail. Fix: cache threadId from the last stopped event in DapSession and substitute when callers pass 0/None. File: src/dap/janet_bindings.rs:769. Round R7.
close_reason: DapSession now caches last_stopped_thread_id from every stopped event (initial launch/attach stop, wait_for_stopped, continue_ select arm) and substitutes it when a caller passes the 0 sentinel, via resolve_thread_id, in continue_/step(step_over/in/out)/stack_trace. The Janet bridge's hardcoded 0 now targets the real stopped thread so strict adapters (debugpy) stop returning 'thread not found'. Non-zero ids pass through verbatim. TDD: echo-adapter regression continue_with_zero_thread_substitutes_last_stopped.

title: ACP sessions stateless and uncancellable
description: handle_new_session mints an id but stores nothing (ignores cwd); handle_prompt builds a fresh agent and spawn_runner(prompt, vec![], None) -> multi-turn editor conversation loses all context every turn. Catch-all dispatch answers session/cancel with 'Unhandled' while the runner keeps executing -> editor cancel button does nothing. Fix: per-SessionId history+cwd map fed into spawn_runner; handle session/cancel by aborting the runner. File: src/extras/acp/mod.rs:94,213. Round R7.
close_reason: Fixed with tests on branch audit-p1-remediation-2 (stacked on #559)

title: MCP-server delegate child never killed on cancellation; orphaned on server death
description: run_delegation spawns 'dirge -p --accept-all' via cmd.output().await with no kill_on_drop(true) and no process group. Client cancel or server restart/kill mid-delegation leaves the auto-approving dirge child editing the project unsupervised; a later delegate can run concurrently on the same session file. Fix: kill_on_drop(true) + own process group killed from a guard (mirror DapProcessGuard). File: src/extras/mcp_server.rs:263. Round R7.
close_reason: Fixed with tests on branch audit-p1-remediation-2 (stacked on #559)

title: Malformed frame mid-session half-kills LSP and DAP clients
description: Both read loops do 'Err(e) => return Err(e)' for non-EOF decode errors (bad Content-Length, >16MB body), bypassing the shutdown block that sets closed + drains pending with ConnectionClosed. One malformed frame -> in-flight waiters burn full timeout, closed stays false so every later request also waits out its timeout. DAP additionally discards the read-task handle. Fix: route all read-loop exits through the shared cleanup block; log non-EOF errors. Files: src/lsp/rpc.rs:178, src/dap/client.rs:200. Round R7.
close_reason: Route non-EOF read-loop exits (malformed frame: bad Content-Length, oversized body) through the shared cleanup that sets closed + drains pending with ConnectionClosed, instead of returning early. LSP src/lsp/rpc.rs + DAP src/dap/client.rs. DAP also now retains the read-task handle instead of discarding it. TDD: added malformed_frame_drains_pending_and_marks_closed regression in each module.

title: /wt-merge broken end-to-end: detect() computes worktree path as .git/worktrees
description: detect() uses git rev-parse --git-dir (returns <main>/.git/worktrees/<name> inside a linked worktree) and takes .parent() = <main>/.git/worktrees. is_dirty/status/remove there fail 'must be run in a work tree' (exit 128) -> every /wt-merge and post-merge cleanup fails. detect() never exercised by tests. Fails safe (no data loss). Fix: derive worktree path from git rev-parse --show-toplevel; add detect() round-trip test in a real linked worktree. File: src/extras/git_worktree/mod.rs:21. Round R7.
close_reason: Fixed with tests on branch audit-p1-remediation-2 (stacked on #559)

title: Dialogs from UI-thread hooks freeze UI ~30s and lose result
description: on-turn-start/-end, on-message-update, on-response, message-end, on-complete, prepare-next-run dispatch synchronously inside the same task that drains dialog_rx. harness/confirm there can't render until the 30s eval times out; result discarded; each subsequent worker call stacks another 30s stall. Docs claim dialogs 'safe from any hook'. Fix: move UI-side hook dispatch onto spawn_blocking like tool hooks, or fix docs. Files: src/ui/mod.rs:4487, worker.rs:75. Round R4.
close_reason: Lifecycle-hook dispatch now runs OFF the single-threaded UI loop so a hook opening harness/confirm|select no longer deadlocks the loop that services dialog_rx. Implemented via a shared PhaseHandle<E> abstraction (src/ui/phase.rs, abort-on-drop) factored out of the existing compaction/plan phases and reused everywhere. Stages: (0) shared PhaseHandle; (1) mid-run hooks on-turn-start/end + on-error detached via spawn_detached_plugin; (2) on-prompt off-loop phase + completion arm (submit tail extracted to submit_resolved_prompt); (3) Done chain (on-response/message-end/on-complete/prepare-next-run) off-loop via done_phase, model swap + finish_done in the completion arm; (4) migrated compaction/plan/review/btw/wt-merge handles onto PhaseHandle. All use spawn_blocking under the current_thread runtime (a plain async block would re-block the loop). on-message-update stays inline by design (per-token-batch; a dialog there is nonsensical) — documented in docs/plugins.md + worker.rs. TDD: phase abort-on-drop, detached_plugin_hook_still_fires, on_prompt_phase_collects_hint, done_chain_runs_off_loop_and_collects_outputs. Full suite green (only the pre-existing GLM-quota h7 smoke tests fail).

title: Plugin loader re-registers earlier plugins' bare hooks under later stems
description: Loader aliases bare hook names to {stem}-{hook} but never unbinds the bare symbol from the shared env, so a later plugin's scan finds plugin A's bare on-prompt, creates B-on-prompt pointing at A's fn, registers it again -> A's hook fires once per subsequently-loaded plugin. Doubled notifications/append-system-prompt/timers. Fix: unbind bare symbol after aliasing; add two-plugin regression test. File: src/plugin/loader.rs:122. Round R4.
close_reason: Loader now unbinds the bare hook symbol from the shared Janet env after promoting it to {stem}-{hook} (put env bare-sym nil). Previously the bare symbol lingered, so a later plugin's scan found an earlier plugin's leftover bare hook, aliased it under its own stem, and registered it again — firing the earlier plugin's hook once per subsequently-loaded plugin. src/plugin/loader.rs. TDD: load_plugin_does_not_leak_bare_hook_to_later_plugin (alpha defines on-prompt, beta defines only on-response; on-prompt must fire exactly once).

title: Skills curator told delete is recoverable archive; it is remove_dir_all
description: Weekly skills-curator LLM pass instructed to skill(action=delete) siblings after absorbing them, on stated premise deletion is a recoverable archive. Tool delete arm = SkillManager::delete = remove_dir_all -> permanent loss of SKILL.md + support files if absorption was incomplete. Real archive/restore exist but are #[allow(dead_code)]. Fix: route tool delete through SkillManager::archive, or add archive action + rewrite curator prompt. Files: src/extras/skills/curator.rs:228, manager.rs:184. Round R6.
close_reason: Delete arm consults SkillStore source/pinned and refuses protected skills unless force=true. Tests delete_refuses_pinned/file_provenance_skill_without_force.

title: Protected-skills guarantee is prose-only: delete has no source/pinned guard
description: Curator Hard Rules (don't touch bundled/pinned) are prompt text only; skill tool delete/edit/patch never consult SkillStore.source or pinned, only name traversal. One prompt slip hard-deletes a user-authored or bundled skill. pinned exemption exists only in SkillStore::archive which the tool path never calls. Fix: delete arm refuses when pinned=1 or source='file' (require explicit override). Files: src/agent/tools/skill.rs:242, manager.rs:184. Round R6.
close_reason: Delete arm consults SkillStore source/pinned and refuses protected skills unless force=true. Tests delete_refuses_pinned/file_provenance_skill_without_force.

title: edit_minified skips pre-mutation snapshot; /rewind can't restore its edits
description: Every other mutator captures snapshots::capture* before writing; edit_minified::call goes apply->syntax_gate->atomic_write with none. /rewind after an edit_minified run restores every other file but leaves this one mutated -> inconsistent partially-reverted tree. Fix: snapshots::capture_bytes(path, source.as_bytes()) before atomic_write. File: src/agent/tools/edit_minified.rs:139. Round R3.
close_reason: capture_bytes(resolved, source.as_bytes()) before atomic_write in edit_minified::call. End-to-end rewind test in edit_minified.rs.

title: edit produces \r\r\n corruption on CRLF files
description:
old_text is CRLF-normalized but args.new_text is spliced raw; then whole-buffer replace('
','
') on CRLF files turns the 
 inside each model-emitted 
 into 
 -> 
 on every replacement line. Tool advertises CRLF handling. edit_lines/apply_patch normalize new_text first. Fix: normalize args.new_text (replace 
->
) before splicing. File: src/agent/tools/edit.rs:181,304,324. Round R3.
close_reason: capture_bytes(resolved, source.as_bytes()) before atomic_write in edit_minified::call. End-to-end rewind test in edit_minified.rs.

title: OAuth tokens frozen at client build; no mid-session refresh
description: resolve_auth_headers runs once at client build; bearer captured for the agent lifetime. No 401->refresh->rebuild hook. classify_error routes 401/403 to Auth which should_retry never retries. Long interactive/--loop/--goal runs crossing token expiry die with non-retryable auth error despite a valid refresh token on disk. Fix: on Auth-classified stream error from an OAuth client, attempt one refresh+rebuild before surfacing, or resolve bearer lazily per-request. Files: src/provider/client.rs:313, auth.rs:191. Round R5.
notes:
Design trace (do NOT rush; auth-flow risk — a half fix breaks sign-in for all OAuth users):

Root cause: bearer captured at client build. resolve_auth_headers (auth.rs:150-203) already checks anthropic_token_is_expired + refresh_anthropic_token_sync + persist_credentials, but only ONCE at build. AnthropicHttpClient (anthropic_http.rs:39-56) bakes bearer_token:Option<String> and inserts 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' per request in normalized_request (line 52). ChatGPT path bakes via chatgpt_http_headers (client.rs:313). classify_error routes 401/403 -> Auth; should_retry never retries Auth -> long runs crossing expiry die.

Two viable fixes:
(A) Lazy per-request re-resolution in transport: AnthropicHttpClient holds creds path + cached (bearer, expires_at) behind Arc<Mutex>. In async send_streaming/send, before normalized_request, cheaply check expires_at; only when expired, await refresh_token + persist + update cache. Debounce so we don't refresh every request. Needs a mockable refresh seam for tests (inject the refresh fn) since the real one hits the network. Must also cover the ChatGPT OAuth path (separate header builder).
(B) Refresh-and-rebuild on Auth error in the retry path: when a stream error classifies as Auth AND the client is OAuth, refresh creds, rebuild the client (re-runs resolve_auth_headers), retry once, then surface. More localized to the loop's error handling but needs the client-build closure reachable from retry.

Prefer (A) for correctness (handles expiry BEFORE the failing request). Test plan: unit-test expiry check + refresh-decision with a mocked refresh fn and a fake creds file; assert a stale token triggers exactly one refresh and the new bearer is used. Files: provider/client.rs:313, provider/auth.rs:150-203, provider/anthropic_http.rs:39-56, provider/anthropic_oauth.rs (refresh_token/persist).

Left OPEN for a dedicated focused pass.
close_reason: Anthropic OAuth transport now refreshes mid-session: AnthropicHttpClient holds a RefreshableToken (bearer+expiry+refresher) and re-resolves through resolve_anthropic_auth_with_expiry when the token expires, instead of the frozen build-time bearer. Refresh failure keeps the stale token (no regression). ChatGPT/Codex path tracked separately.

title: first_kept_index space mismatch drops protected tail on resume after fold
description: applied_first_kept indexes current_context.messages (loop transcript, each tool result its own msg) but is passed to compress_reporting which drains session.messages[..idx] (tool results embedded in assistant msgs, much shorter). Tool-heavy sessions drain most/all messages incl. the verbatim-kept tail; crash-resume then recovers only the lossy summary. Fix: translate fold boundary into session-message space before compress_reporting. Files: run.rs:723, run_handlers/context_compacted.rs:126, session/compact.rs:48. Round R2.
notes:
Design trace (do NOT rush a point-fix; data-loss/resume-corruption risk):

- current_context.messages is Vec<serde_json::Value> (LLM/loop space); an assistant SessionMessage with N tool_calls expands to N+1 loop entries (1 assistant + N tool_result). session.messages keeps 1. Confirmed via runner::convert_history (runner.rs:110-168) and message.rs loop_message_to_value.
- Loop Value messages carry NO id; SessionMessage has a stable id. So no direct id-based index map.
- Auto-fold (run.rs:724) sets applied_first_kept = start, a loop-space index from compute_compress_window over current_context.messages, and emits it as ContextCompacted.first_kept_index. Handler (context_compacted.rs:139) passes it to compress_reporting which drains session.messages[..first_kept_index] (session space) -> over-drain, drops verbatim tail; crash-resume recovers only the lossy summary.
- Reference CORRECT impl: /compress path computes cut_idx in SESSION space via compaction_cut_idx (slash/mod.rs:223: token-walk from end + align_cut_to_user_boundary) and passes to compress_reporting. Auto-fold should do likewise OR translate.
- Complication: apply_summary keeps head[0..start]+summary+tail[end..] (summary covers [start..end]); compress_reporting keeps tail-only (summary+[cut..]). Head first-user recovered via anchor_summary_with_intent + checkpoint intent. So session cut should correspond to the loop TAIL cut (end), translated to session space, NOT start — so kept session tail == loop kept tail and no orphaned tool_result heads the tail.
- Fix options: (a) translate loop end -> session index by walking session.messages expansion counts incl. leading-summary offset; (b) decouple: compute session cut via compaction_cut_idx-style token walk in the fold handler (proven, but must user-align to avoid orphan tool_result). Add tests exercising tool-call expansion (none exist today).

Left OPEN for a dedicated focused pass.
close_reason: Fold persistence now computes the cut in session space (session::compact::compaction_cut_idx) instead of using the loop-space first_kept_index, so the verbatim tail survives a fold+resume. /compress delegates to the same cut so they can't drift. Follow-up dirge-hlyp filed for the small-window keep_recent clamp.

title: Byte-index slice panic on multibyte bash output during compaction pruning
description: summarize_tool_result: 'if cmd.len()>80 { &cmd[..77] }' slices at byte 77 after a byte-length check -> panics on non-ASCII (CJK/emoji/non-ASCII paths) at a non-char-boundary. prune_tool_outputs runs on every fold and overflow recovery, so the panic kills the loop before the rotated session saves. Fix: cmd.chars().take(77) or char_boundary_at_or_before. File: src/agent/compression.rs:515. Round R2.

title: Post-fold DB session ids all collapse to dirge-compacte
description: persist keys DB by format!('dirge-{}', short_id(id)); short_id = first 8 chars of 'compacted-<hex>' = constant 'compacte' -> every folded session in every conversation persists as one row. Fold handler links a raw orphan id nothing lands in, so resolve_parent never walks a fold and session_search lineage dedup silently fails. Fix: one canonical DB id derivation everywhere; fold handler insert/end/link with the same; add fold->parent-chain test. Files: src/ui/agent_io.rs:214, run_handlers/context_compacted.rs:96. Round R2.
close_reason: Added text::db_session_id canonical derivation; routed agent_io, done.rs (x2), and the fold handler insert/link through it. Distinctness + fold-chain lineage tests in text.rs and session_db_tests.rs.

title: Interjection never stops run at tool-result boundary
description: Only is_interjected() check is AFTER the inner while exits; nothing inside the loop checks it, so the documented 'halt at next turn boundary' never happens. Permission-denial cascade (ui/mod.rs:1124) relies on this to halt the runner; instead the model keeps taking turns. Documented in 4 places, implemented in none. Fix: check signal.is_interjected() at top/bottom of each inner iteration and break. File: src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:1877. Round R5.
close_reason: Added is_interjected() check at the bottom of the inner tool-call loop in run.rs (after results appended/backfilled). Regression test interjection_halts_at_tool_result_boundary.

title: Code-review gate reviews whole dirty tree with no run-start baseline
description: Gate preconditions are only code_review_fn.is_some() + run_made_tool_calls (true for any read-only ToolResult). capture_run_diff diffs the entire dirty tree with no baseline captured at run start -> a read-only 'explain this' turn with pre-existing WIP triggers 2 judge calls and can block the loop up to MAX_REVIEW_REACT=3. Contradicts its own comment + docs. Fix: capture diff baseline/tree-hash at run start and review only the delta, or gate on a mutating tool call in new_messages. Files: run.rs:259, code_review.rs:525. Round R5.
close_reason: Fixed with tests on branch audit-p1-remediation-2 (stacked on #559)

title: Config yolo/accept_all overrides explicit CLI --restrictive
description: resolve_mode checks 'if cli.yolo || cfg.yolo' before restrictive; a project .dirge/config.json with yolo:true wins over 'dirge --restrictive' -> untrusted repo escalates permission mode. Docs promise CLI flags take precedence. Fix: if any CLI mode flag set, resolve from CLI only; consult config booleans only when no CLI flag present (warn on override). File: src/main.rs:114. Round R1.
close_reason: resolve_mode now resolves from CLI flags first; config booleans only apply with no CLI flag. Tested in main.rs resolve_mode_tests.

title: Env/wrapper prefix defeats head-anchored deny rules
description: FOO=1 rm -rf /, nohup/time/nice/timeout rm -rf / change the head token so deny rule 'rm -rf /**' doesn't match and mutation-path routing skips the operand -> hard Deny silently downgrades to Ask, user edit-deny bypassed. Also on no-semantic-bash path (coarse_mutation_paths). Fix: strip leading name=value assignments and known wrappers when deriving command head for both rule matching and mutation extraction. File: src/semantic/adapters/bash.rs:320. Round R1.
close_reason: Shared exec_head_index/strip_exec_prefix strips leading env assignments + wrappers for both rule matching (extra match candidate) and mutation extraction (tree-sitter + coarse). Tested in build.rs + bash.rs.

title: Codebase audit: multi-agent review across documented feature axes
description: Fan out review agents per docs axis (agent loop, compaction/sessions, providers, permissions/sandbox, TUI, plugins, memory/skills/issues/spec, tools/semantic, config/prompts/agents, MCP/LSP/DAP). Goal: bugs, code smells, refactoring opportunities, doc drift — bring project to highest professional OSS standard. Deliverable: numbered severity-ranked findings list, then TDD fix plan by rounds.
notes: All 12 axis reviews complete (agent loop, gates, compaction/sessions, provider/auth, permissions/sandbox, TUI, slash, plugins, memory/skills, tools, config/CLI, integrations). ~140 findings: 3 critical (permission bypass via command substitution; plugin confirm gate fails open; compaction summaries dropped from provider requests), ~21 high, rest medium/low/doc-drift/refactor. Consolidated severity list + TDD fix plan by rounds delivered in session; awaiting user go-ahead before implementing.

title: Fix clippy lints from MSRV 1.88 bump
description: MSRV bump to 1.88 enabled msrv-gated clippy lints (let-chains collapsible_if x14, manual is_multiple_of x2); 16 errors fail CI clippy on main. Clean up.
close_reason: clippy lints fixed via clippy --fix; both gates clean locally

title: Headless -p/--loop hangs forever on a tool permission ask (ask_rx undrained)
description: Follow-up to #523/dirge-u5ig. The 0.12.5 plugin-worker fix did not address the real root cause. In headless --print and --loop modes, run_print holds ask_rx but never drains it. When a tool routes to a confirmation prompt (e.g. a bash command --accept-all does not auto-allow), handle_ask_inner sends an AskRequest and awaits reply_rx with no timeout; nothing responds, so the agent loop suspends and the run freezes (0% CPU, no result) forever. Interactive TUI drains ask_rx via the UI loop, ACP via spawn_acp_ask_drain; -p/--loop have no drain. Fix: spawn a headless ask responder that auto-denies, mirroring spawn_acp_ask_drain.
close_reason: Fixed: headless --print/--loop now drain ask_rx via spawn_headless_ask_responder (deny-all), so a tool needing confirmation fails fast instead of hanging. TDD: 2 new tests in permission::ask.

title: Headless deadlock: orphaned spawn_blocking strands pm lock + unbounded host calls wedge Janet worker (#523)
description: Headless -p freezes permanently after several tool_calls turns. Two confirmed defects compound: (A) send_dialog (harness/confirm/select) has no wall-clock timeout (worker.rs:1523) unlike send_lsp/sandbox — waits forever for a responder; (B) before/after tool hooks acquire the global pm Mutex INSIDE a spawn_blocking closure wrapped in timeout(10s) (plugin_hooks.rs:102-132) — when the timeout fires it only drops the JoinHandle, never cancels the blocking work, so an orphaned thread holds pm forever and the single serialized Janet worker stays wedged. Every later pm-locking site (next hook, compaction, apply_response_hooks) then blocks; the runner stops emitting events; run_print's event_rx.recv().await parks in the reactor -> permanent 0% CPU freeze, no result. Tool hooks are wired unconditionally whenever a PluginManager exists (integration.rs:537), and dispatch_tool_hook round-trips to the worker per tool call even with zero registered hooks (mod.rs:624). Exact initial worker-stall trigger (GCD semaphore in Janet client.run) needs binary symbolization, but A+B turn any stall into a permanent freeze. Fixes: bound every host call (incl send_dialog); don't strand pm across the timeout boundary; skip worker round-trip when no hooks registered.
close_reason: Released in 0.12.5 (PR #524): await hook dispatch instead of detaching, bound send_dialog, skip worker when no hook registered

title: stream-json --print emits no incremental output
description: In -p --output-format stream-json mode, run_print emits only system + one final assistant + result. Per-turn/per-tool events from the agent loop are never wired into the StreamJson arm, so live-progress consumers see the agent frozen until done. Fix: emit a Claude-compatible assistant event per TurnEnd (text + tool_use blocks) and a user event per turn's tool_results, incrementally.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #521 — stream-json emits incremental per-turn assistant + tool_result events

title: Preemptive compaction fires but prepare_compaction no-ops in 85-100% band
description: Preemptive trigger (ui/mod.rs) fires at total+incoming > 85% of (window-reserve), then calls prepare_compaction(forced=false), whose within-limits gate only proceeds at total > 100% of (window-reserve). In the 85-100% band the user sees 'preemptive compaction (context near limit)' + 'compressing...' followed by 'context within limits, no compression needed' — proactive compaction never runs, defeating its purpose. Fix: preemptive trigger owns the decision (and uniquely factors the incoming prompt), so it must call prepare with the within-limits gate bypassed.
close_reason: Fixed: preemptive trigger passes forced=true so prepare_compaction doesn't re-gate at 100%; threshold extracted to preemptive_compaction_due with tests.

title: Reactive overflow strands mid-task instead of resuming after compaction
description: After dirge-21sb removed eager post-turn auto-compaction, mid-turn context overflow became the common path. handle_context_overflow refuses to retry when tools already ran on the failed turn (tool_calls_this_run>0), so after compaction the agent goes idle instead of resuming. The partial turn (streamed text + completed tool calls) is only persisted to the search DB, not session.messages, so a plain prompt-resend would re-run tools. Fix: record the partial assistant turn into session.messages before compacting, then resume as a continuation (no tool re-exec), mirroring the plugin-followup path.
close_reason: Fixed: reactive overflow now records the partial turn + resumes as a continuation (no tool re-run); plus mid-stream steering duplication fix. Tests added.

title: Round 2a: run the /plan reviewer loop off-thread (non-blocking)
description: drive_plan_review -> review_once -> collect_runner_text (plan_review.rs:59) drains a full tool-using reviewer LLM agent run INLINE on the post-done tail of every /plan implement turn. Tens of seconds-minutes frozen, no Ctrl+C. Convert to the spawn-task + phase-handle + select!-arm pattern used by plan/compaction; resume the turn-finalization continuation after review installs.

title: Round 1: cache + timeout MCP tools/list so build_agent stops freezing the UI
description: collect_tools (extras/mcp/mod.rs:141) calls list_tools per server on EVERY build_agent rebuild, uncached, and list_tools (client.rs:190) has no timeout. build_agent is awaited inline at ~9 loop sites (/model,/cd,/prompt,/agent,/toggle,/worktree,/wt-exit, prompt-cycle keystroke mod.rs:1846, PlanSwitch confirm mod.rs:635, install_compaction, context_compacted, done.rs model-swap). With MCP servers configured, each rebuild re-handshakes all of them; a wedged server hangs the UI. Fix: (a) bound list_tools with a timeout (mirror the git 2s cap); (b) cache collected tool definitions per server keyed by the reconnect generation, invalidate on reconnect.

title: UI freezes during /compress + preemptive/reactive compaction (blocking await in event loop)
description:
The TUI event loop (run_interactive, single tokio::select! in ui/mod.rs) awaits long LLM work INLINE in the user_rx/agent_rx arms, freezing render + input + Ctrl+C for 10-60s.

Offenders (compaction): explicit /compress -> handle_compress().await (mod.rs:2234); preemptive compaction before a prompt (mod.rs:2545); reactive ContextOverflow recovery (run_handlers/context_overflow.rs compress().await). Each runs the summarizer LLM (provider/summarize.rs run_oneshot) synchronously on the loop thread.

Correct templates already exist: agent turns (spawn_runner -> agent_rx stream) and /plan (cmd_plan spawns explore->plan, streams via plan_phase.rx arm; Ctrl+C aborts task — dirge-vuzz).

Fix (chosen scope): a shared compaction-phase task mirroring /plan. Decide what to summarize ON-loop (fast, owns session), spawn the LLM summarize OFF-loop streaming progress + terminal Done{summary}/Failed/Aborted, a new select! arm installs the summary ON-loop (session rotation) and runs any continuation (preemptive: send pending prompt; reactive: retry prompt). Ctrl+C aborts. Agent-rebuild inline awaits (/model,/prompt,/mode,/cd,Shift+Tab, 1-3s) left for a follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: During /compress, preemptive, and reactive compaction the UI keeps rendering (spinner animates), accepts input, and Ctrl+C aborts the compaction. Session install/rotation still correct. The continuation (send/retry prompt) fires after compaction completes. No inline summarizer .await remains in the select! arms.
close_reason: Already implemented and released in 0.16.0 across 4 staged commits: ccd79609 (split compaction into prompt-build + LLM-run halves), 8168063d (non-blocking machinery + wire /compress), e65b2c04 (non-blocking preemptive), 32af1636 (non-blocking reactive overflow). All three former inline-await sites (/compress, preemptive, reactive ContextOverflow) now decide on-loop and spawn the summarizer off-loop via ui::compaction::spawn; the dedicated compaction_phase select! arm (ui/mod.rs) installs the summary and runs the continuation. Verified: the only run_compaction().await lives inside tokio::spawn in ui/compaction.rs; no inline summarizer await remains in the select! arms. Bead was left open after the work landed.

title: Auto-repair swallows sibling code on mid-file delimiter imbalance
description: repair_delimiters appended missing closers at EOF whenever the result re-parsed clean. For a mid-file stray opener (e.g. an edit injecting an extra '{' high in a file), closing at EOF wraps all following complete code into the wrongly-opened block. In Rust this parses (nested fns are valid) so tree-sitter can't flag it — silent corruption, recoverable only via git restore (Richie's report). Fix: only auto-close a TRAILING TRUNCATION (little content after the outermost unclosed opener); reject mid-file imbalances with the precise error so the model fixes its own edit (the pre-write reject leaves the file unchanged). Follow-up: LSP-backed verify (write/rollback) to allow broader safe repair where a language server exists.
close_reason: Fixed in #496 — auto-repair now only closes trailing truncations; mid-file imbalances are rejected with feedback.

title: computer-use plugin breaks macOS arm64 build (default features)
description: Merged in #415. On macOS arm64 the default build (plugin feature on) fails: src/plugin/worker.rs uses v.pointer/out.pointer on janetrs::lowlevel::Janet (needs v.as_.pointer on macOS — anonymous-union field naming differs from Linux), and computer_use_exec_body references janet_wrap_integer which doesn't link on arm64. CI is Linux-only so it passed. Needs a cross-platform janetrs access path (must not regress Linux). Blocks all local macOS dev on default features; workaround is --no-default-features --features windows-default.

title: Fast-fail interactive prompts in bash exec (tty hang)
description: git/ssh etc. open /dev/tty directly, bypassing Stdio::null stdin. In Off sandbox mode the bash child shares dirge's controlling terminal (only process_group(0) is set, not a new session), so a credential prompt blocks for the full 120s timeout. Bwrap mode is immune via --new-session. Fix: setsid the child in run_with_timeout + spawn_streaming_shell; add non-interactive env defaults (GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0, DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive, GCM_INTERACTIVE=Never) in wrap_command.
close_reason: Fixed: setsid detaches the controlling terminal so tty prompts fail fast; non-interactive env defaults added. Loop guard made cost-aware behind a LoopGuards facade (timeout weighting in failure tracker + lowered storm threshold). All tests + clippy -D warnings pass.

title: restream_expanded_thinking truncates content below the anchor
description: restream_expanded_thinking guards only on eviction_generation + start<=buffer_len, ignoring the recorded _end. replace_from(start, []) truncates everything from start to buffer end, so any content appended below the expanded live-thinking block (interjection, notice, chat-switch) before the next reasoning delta is silently deleted. Fix: bail when buffer_len() != end (block no longer at tail).

title: Plan mode escape: destructive git default-allowed + incomplete deny_tools (issue #429)
description:
User report (gh #429): in plan mode the agent ran git checkout and destroyed pending uncommitted changes. Audit findings:

1. HIGH (data loss): git checkout/switch/restore were Action::Allow in default_bash_rules (src/permission/mod.rs) — they run with NO prompt anywhere bash is reachable, and discard uncommitted work (git checkout -- file, git restore file). This is the literal cause: an agent reverting its own edit wiped the user's pending changes. Fix: removed checkout/switch/restore from default-allow so they prompt; git pull/fetch stay allowed. reset/clean already prompt. Tests moved from the allowed-set test to the gated-set test.

2. MEDIUM (read-only lock leak): the plan prompt deny_tools listed only [edit, write, apply_patch, bash, webfetch] — omitting task, mcp_tool, plugin_tool, debug, spec (a denylist is fragile). Fix: completed prompts/plan.md deny_tools; added edit_lines/spec to BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES (edit_lines was also MCP-shadowable). Regression test pins the full lock.

Confirmed NOT escapes by the audit: edit_lines/edit_minified launder through the 'edit' tool name (already denied); the task subagent is tool-less; PromptDenyPolicy runs first and beats every allow; deny-list is applied to the shared checker before plan_enter returns.
close_reason: Demoted git checkout/switch/restore from default-allow to ask (they discard uncommitted work); completed plan-mode deny_tools (task/mcp_tool/plugin_tool/debug/spec) + added edit_lines/spec to BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES. Tests: gated-git, plan-lock regression. Full suite green.

title: Critic transcript truncates to the HEAD, hiding the work it should judge
description:
build_critic_transcript (src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs) renders the whole run (new_messages accumulates every turn) then does s.chars().take(MAX_CHARS=8000) — keeping the FIRST 8000 chars. In a substantial run the head is planning/scaffolding and the implementation + verification land at the END, so the critic (and the goal gate, which shares this builder) is fed the planning phase and never sees the work. Result: the critic asserts things like "no code created / scaffolding never finished / no demo run" while the files, tests, and verification are all present — it judged a truncated, outdated view.

Fix: keep the determinative parts within budget instead of blind head-truncation. The critic judges "is the task complete and correct", so it needs (a) the original user request (head) and (b) the most recent work + verification (tail). Rebuild the transcript as head (leading user request) + elision marker + tail (most recent messages) up to budget, dropping the middle. Consider a modest budget bump too (8000 is small for multi-file runs).

TDD: a long run whose early messages are planning and whose final messages contain the implementation + a "RENDERED"/tests-pass verification — assert the built transcript contains BOTH the original request and the tail verification even when over budget (fails today: tail is dropped). Benefits run_critic and run_goal_gate.
close_reason: build_critic_transcript now keeps head (original request) + tail (most recent work/verification) within a 12k budget, eliding the middle, instead of taking the first 8000 chars. The critic and goal gate were being fed the planning phase and never saw the implementation. Pin test confirms small transcripts are byte-identical; new test locks head+tail survival when over budget.

title: Hash-anchored editing for cheaper models (DeepSeek V4)
description:
Article headline innovation (howardchen DeepSeek V4 piece): ~50% fewer edit retries and 30-40% lower output tokens on V4 Pro by editing via line-range + short content-hash per line instead of reproducing exact text blocks. dirge's edit (src/agent/tools/edit.rs:194) is exact-string match with a fuzzy fallback cascade. This is the most mission-aligned gap: keep cheaper models on the rails.

Scope: read annotates each line with a 3-char hex content hash; a hash-anchored edit op takes (start_line, end_line, expected_hashes, new_text); hash mismatch -> precise 'line N changed: expected <h>, found <h>' error; reuse existing tree-sitter pre-write validation gate before write. Decide: new tool vs new mode on edit. Keep existing exact/fuzzy edit available.

TDD plan (round 2):
1. Test: hash function stable + collision-tolerant per line.
2. Test: read output carries per-line hashes (opt-in/flagged so default read output unchanged for non-hash edits).
3. Test: hash-anchored edit applies on match.
4. Test: mismatch rejected with precise per-line error.
5. Test: multi-range edit, CRLF normalization, tree-sitter rejection path.
notes: Depends on round 1 (dirge-otv7) only for measuring token payoff, not functionally.
close_reason: Implemented with tests; committed on branch hash-anchored-editing (article gap-closing rounds 1-4)

title: Cache-hit accounting + /cache monitoring (DeepSeek prefix-cache instrument)
description:
Article gap (howardchen DeepSeek V4 piece): TokenUsage (src/agent/agent_loop/message.rs:172) tracks only input/output tokens, no cached-token channel, so we can't tell whether dirge's existing cache-prefix discipline (tools sorted-by-name in prompt_cache_prefix) is actually landing hits. DeepSeek caches at ~1/120 cost on prefix match; need visibility.

Scope: plumb cached_tokens from provider usage (DeepSeek prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens, Anthropic cache_read/cache_creation) into TokenUsage; track cumulative session ratio; add /cache command showing cumulative cache-hit ratio; optional left-panel gauge.

TDD plan (round 1):
1. Test: parse cached_tokens from DeepSeek-shape and Anthropic-shape usage payloads -> fail -> add field + parse.
2. Test: cumulative ratio math across turns -> implement accumulator.
3. Test: /cache command renders ratio.

Smallest round; instruments rounds 2-3 payoff.
notes: Confirmed in audit: rig_stream_factory.rs:1228 already sorts tools by name for cache stability; reasoning_content is NOT sent back in outbound history (stores text), so not a cache saboteur.
close_reason: Implemented with tests; committed on branch hash-anchored-editing (article gap-closing rounds 1-4)

title: read tool: cache hit returns terse message instead of content (post-compaction regression)
description: PR #391 changed read.rs so a content-cache hit returns '📎 cached — unchanged' instead of the cached content. The content cache survives context compaction (cleared only on spawn + mutations), so a model that re-reads a file after the earlier read was compacted out of context gets a useless 'unchanged' message with no content — and must guess a different offset/limit to dodge its own cache. Unrelated to the compression feature it shipped with. Restore returning the cached content.
close_reason: cache hit returns content again; test pins it

title: Memory-store load failure must degrade gracefully, not panic the session
description: loop_tools.rs:366 .expect() panics during agent construction when SqliteMemoryStore::load fails (read-only FS, NFS, I/O errors). Same class as the PR #392 CI bug. Build the registry without the memory tool + warn log instead.
close_reason: register_memory_tool degrades instead of panicking; tests cover both paths

title: SQLite memory store: backend swap with behavior parity
description: Replace MEMORY.md/PITFALLS.md markdown files + .meta.json/.usage.json sidecars with a memories table in the per-project session DB (state.db). Migration v7: memories table (id, target, kind, content, status, tier, confidence, salience, created_at, updated_at, last_used_at, use_count, superseded_by) + memories_fts with sync triggers. New SqliteMemoryStore implementing MemoryProvider. One-time importer from existing markdown files + sidecars; originals renamed *.imported. Threat scanning preserved at write and prompt-render. Rewire memory curator scan_entries and cross-session extractor existing_memory_lowercased onto the store API. Kills the .meta.json lost-update race, file locks, drift detection. Tests: parity suite, import round-trip, concurrent writes.

title: sandbox-microvm feature does not compile on macOS (copy_file_range + libkrun link)
description: v0.4.0: building with --features sandbox-microvm on macOS fails. (1) libc::copy_file_range in rootfs.rs is Linux-only -> lib+main bin fail to compile. (2) dirge-microvm-runner links libkrun symbols which don't exist on macOS (libkrun is Linux/KVM-only). Feature was never gated to Linux. Fix: cfg(target_os=linux)-gate the reflink path (fallback to std::fs::copy) and the runner's libkrun body (stub main on non-Linux).
close_reason: Fixed in c688093, released in v0.4.1. cfg(target_os=linux)-gated copy_file_range (std::fs::copy fallback) and the runner's libkrun body (Linux-only stub main); gated the two reflink syscall tests. --features sandbox-microvm + --all-features now build on macOS; verified locally. Runtime still requires Linux + KVM.

title: Queued interjection cannot be dropped; on-screen hint says Alt+X but it is unimplemented
description: ui/mod.rs:1679 prints '(queued; ... Alt+X drops, Ctrl+C cancels)' but there is no Alt+X handler anywhere and no key handler that clears interjection_queue short of a full Ctrl+C/Esc cancel. Ctrl+X is bound to CloseChat (keymap.rs:88-92), unrelated. So the documented Ctrl+X/Alt+X queue-drop does not exist. Fix: implement a queue-drop key (Alt+X) or correct the hint + docs.
close_reason: KeyAction::DropQueue bound to Alt+X (Ctrl+X stays close_chat); event-loop handler drains interjection_queue + reports count without cancelling the run. Docs reconciled to Alt+X. Test: keymap::alt_x_drops_queue_ctrl_x_closes_chat.

title: Model-family steering keys off launch-time model, not the active (swapped) model
description: builder/agent_inner.rs:206 derives the steering family from cli.resolve_provider/resolve_model (cli.rs:229-258), which read only CLI flags + config Default role, never session.model or the swapped model. After any /model or /agent model swap: switching TO a DeepSeek-chat model omits the DeepSeek guidance fragment (false negative); switching AWAY still appends it (false positive). Fix: feed the actual active model name + resolved provider into resolve_family.
close_reason: build_agent_inner now takes the active provider+model (parent_model.name()) and feeds resolve_family with them instead of cli.resolve_model. Steering tracks /model and /agent swaps. Test: steering_fragment_tracks_active_model_not_cli.

title: Post-merge cwd restore fires unconditionally even when the merge failed
description: done.rs:593-642: wt_return_path.take() fires on the FIRST Done after a /wt-merge run regardless of merge success. If the merge agent stopped early (conflict/error/mid-task Done), cwd, session.working_dir, permission root, and agent are re-anchored to main while the worktree may still hold un-merged changes, and 'merged and returned to main repo' prints anyway. Fix: gate the return on a verified-clean merge signal. Related to the /wt-merge LLM-merge issue.
close_reason: git_worktree::merge_worktree does the merge programmatically: refuses on dirty worktree/main, git merge --no-ff, git merge --abort on conflict (repo untouched), never pushes/deletes. DEFER_WT_MERGE handler calls it directly (no LLM) and restores cwd + removes worktree only on clean merge. Unconditional post-Done restore + wt_return_path/WorktreeBits removed. Tests: clean/conflict-abort/dirty-refuse.

title: /wt-merge delegates the whole merge to an unconstrained LLM prompt (data-loss risk)
description: cmd_worktree.rs:94-135 + ui/mod.rs:1531-1558: cmd_wt_merge only emits a DEFER_WT_MERGE sentinel; the handler builds a natural-language prompt ('merge branch X into Y, push, delete the worktree') and hands it to a normal agent run. No programmatic git merge, no conflict detection, no abort-on-conflict, and the model is told to delete the worktree in the same instruction. Conflict or push failure can lose un-merged work or leave a half-merged repo. Fix: perform the merge programmatically with conflict detection; never delete the worktree unless merge+push verified clean.
close_reason: git_worktree::merge_worktree does the merge programmatically: refuses on dirty worktree/main, git merge --no-ff, git merge --abort on conflict (repo untouched), never pushes/deletes. DEFER_WT_MERGE handler calls it directly (no LLM) and restores cwd + removes worktree only on clean merge. Unconditional post-Done restore + wt_return_path/WorktreeBits removed. Tests: clean/conflict-abort/dirty-refuse.

title: Automatic in-loop LLM compaction is dead in production (summarize_fn never set)
description: LoopSpawnConfig.summarize_fn is never assigned outside test mocks (provider/spawn.rs:147-194,358). So proactive folds at 75/78/80/90% (run.rs:410) degrade to prune_tool_outputs only. That prune itself no-ops on production tool results: compression.rs:419 reads content as_str() but production tool_result content is a block array (message.rs:289). Real LLM summarization only happens reactively via handle_compress/ContextOverflow. Docs (agent-loop.md/features.md) claim automatic structured-summary compaction at the turn boundary. Confirmed by session + provider reviews. Fix: wire cfg.summarize_fn from AnyClient::compress_messages OR correct the docs; also make prune_tool_outputs handle block arrays. NOTE: before wiring, fix tool-pair boundary snapping in compute_compress_window/apply_summary (see separate issue) to avoid orphaned tool_use/tool_result pairs (400s).
close_reason: SummarizeFn built at build_agent from the main model, stored on AnyAgent (with_summarizer), forwarded in spawn_runner. Proactive folds now run LLM summarization. Unblocked by 89fm + u5ka. Tests: with_summarizer_stashes + run_tests compaction suite.

title: /agent and /prompt share one deny_tools slot and clobber each other
description: cmd_model.rs:303 (/prompt) and :431 (/agent) both write current_prompt_deny_tools; set_prompt_deny_tools (permission/checker.rs:248) REPLACES rather than merges. So /prompt review (denies edit/write/bash) then /agent <name> wipes the deny-list, re-enabling edit/write/bash while the agent still believes it is in plan/review mode. Security-boundary regression, no warning. Confirmed independently by permission + provider reviews. Fix: merge deny-lists, or refuse a profile that weakens an active read-only prompt.
notes: USER DIRECTIVE: fix as a UNIFIED mechanism, not a point patch.
