The conflict surface (deny_tools single-slot clobber dirge-x7c8, model-not-reverted dirge-anhw, reviewer-fork bash deniable) all stem from profile/prompt/permission composition writing to shared single-slot state and overwriting instead of merging/layering. Design one composition layer (e.g. a layered stack: base prompt -> active /prompt -> active /agent profile, each contributing prompt+model+deny/allow, with explicit revert) covering all three. Treat dirge-x7c8 + dirge-anhw together.
close_reason: Unified composition layer (ContextFiles::recompute_composition): /prompt and /agent are independent layers folded into the effective fields. Denies UNION (no clobber); /agent off restores prompt layer + pre-agent model (model_before_agent). All set-sites routed through layer setters. 6 composition unit tests.

title: UI loop hangs / blocks on many actions
description: The TUI frequently becomes blocked/unresponsive during various actions. Review how the UI event loop works (ui/mod.rs run loop, blocking slash handlers, render/draw calls, channel waits, sync handler work on the UI thread) and identify what blocks the loop. Likely culprits: synchronous/blocking work performed inline on the UI thread (slash handlers, compaction, network, subprocess), awaits that park the event loop, or lock contention. Diagnose the blocking points and fix so the UI stays responsive.
notes: Primary fix (status-line git branch caching) merged in #374. REMAINING follow-up: /plan runs its explore/plan forks inline on the UI loop (cmd_plan.rs run_phases) — parks the event loop for the LLM-call duration. Fix is to spawn the phase runners + monitor via channel. Larger change; left open.
close_reason: Merged in #379 — /plan forks now run off the event loop; loop stays responsive

title: Finish rig→loop tool phase-out: delete duplicated tool construction
description: loop_tools.rs:91 comment admits tool construction is 'temporarily duplicated' between build_agent_inner (legacy rig path, retained only for the rig Agent preamble) and build_loop_tools (live dispatch). Comment states the rig Agent's tools are 'no longer invoked after phase 4.5h-6'. Adding any tool currently requires editing both. Confirm the rig dispatch path is truly dead, then remove the duplicate construction so tools are defined once. MED-HIGH value.

title: Consolidate message→JSON serialization (3 copies)
description: serialize_assistant (agent_loop/stream.rs:354) and loop_message_to_value (agent_loop/run.rs:1485, agent_loop/integration.rs:698 + nested assistant_to_value :700) all emit the identical {role,content,stopReason,errorMessage} JSON shape. Three private copies that must stay in lockstep. Extract one pub fn serialize_message(&LoopMessage)->Value into agent_loop/message.rs and route all sites through it. ~80 LOC removed, single source of truth. Verified via grep. No behavior change — pure de-dup.
close_reason: Merged in #362 — single serialize_message source of truth in message.rs

title: Round B: merge sanitize_output into strip_escapes + require_and_resolve tool preamble
description: #1 sanitize_output and strip_escapes are byte-identical DoS-capped ANSI state machines (45 call sites); ansi.rs:94-98 already flags the merge as intended-but-unfinished. Make sanitize_output = strip_escapes(strip_orphan_mouse_reports(s), KEEP_BOTH); delete the ~80-LOC duplicate. Security-relevant (gates untrusted LLM/bash output). #3 tools::require_and_resolve centralizing require_absolute_path + check_perm_path_resolve (Audit-H12 symlink-swap invariant) across 8 tools.

title: Critic reads stale compaction summary as live requirements
description: The in-loop critic (critic_provider tier 3) is fed the agent's merged system prompt as its 'rules to judge against'. That merged prompt is preamble + history, and history concatenates all System messages — including the context-compaction summary ([CONTEXT COMPACTION — REFERENCE ONLY]). The summary's '## Active Task' describes work that was current at compaction time but has since been completed, so the critic blocks finalization on already-done/superseded work (observed: it demanded a 'Phase 3 / Janet loader' that didn't exist in the session). Fix: strip the compaction summary from the critic's rules + harden the preamble to discount REFERENCE-ONLY blocks.
close_reason: stale compaction summary stripped from critic rules (#346)

title: P1: Exploration tool-frugality rules in plan/explore prompts
description: Add explicit frugality rules to dirge's plan/explore prompt path: minimize tool calls, ban orientation reads, never call same tool on same file twice, force a 2-3 sentence summary instead of dumping files. PORT vix prompts/plan_workflow/explore.md:16-35.

title: P1: Todo-completion nudge (block premature end_turn with pending todos)
description: On end_turn, if pending/in_progress todos remain, inject up to 3 nudges to finish or clear the list before stopping. PORT vix internal/daemon/session.go:1551-1558 + hasPendingTodos (session_todo.go).

title: P1: Mandatory reason/intent fields on read/grep/glob/find/lsp + bash anti-misuse fields
description: Add a required 'reason' string (what file, expected info, goal) to read/grep/glob/find_files/lsp tool schemas, and bash reason_to_use_instead_of_{read,edit,glob} fields. Surface in tool-call UI. PORT vix tool_schemas.go:117-122 (reason) + :210-221 (bash justification).

title: P1: Hard read-before-edit gate
description: Block edit/apply_patch unless the target file was read this session; mark a path read on successful read/edit/write so chained edits skip re-reads. Error tells the model to read first. PORT vix internal/daemon/session_read_gate.go:45-87 (readTrackingTools + gate). dirge today only has a prompt hint (exemplars.rs, tool_input_repair/hints.rs).

title: Port battle-tested vix agentic-loop features into dirge (phased)
description: Port proven mechanisms from the vix coding agent (~/src/vix) into dirge's agent loop to improve coding-model capability. Port vix's logic faithfully rather than reinventing. Phase 1 = quick wins; Phase 2 = efficiency; Phase 3 = meatier workflow/verification changes. See review in session.
close_reason: All phases landed: P1 quick wins, P2 efficiency (minify, read-gate, watchdog), P3 phased plan workflow (explore→plan→implement→reviewer-loop via /plan). 16/16 sub-tasks merged to main.

title: DAP hardening: fix review findings from #318 (UB, hangs, panics, tests)
description: Follow-up to #318. Fix: C1 mem::zeroed Sender UB (janet_bindings); H1 missing DAP write timeout (client); H2 truncate UTF-8 panic on adapter output (session); M1 process-guard drop order + pgid>1 guard (client); M2 Janet dap_send_and_wait ignores shutdown; M3 event handler under lock (client); M4 Runtime::new test compile break (debug.rs); M5 UI snapshot starvation; plus framing tests, perm-deny test, dap_context or→and, dap_profiler s/ms, remove LOOP_PLAN.md, graceful disconnect.

title: Session persistence: resumed sessions self-conflict after first save, stop persisting
description: save_session(&Session) compares on-disk mtime to session.loaded_mtime to detect concurrent writers, but never refreshes loaded_mtime after its own write (signature is immutable). For a RESUMED session (loaded_mtime=Some), the first save advances the file mtime beyond the recorded loaded_mtime; every subsequent save then sees disk_mtime > loaded_mtime, falsely detects a concurrent instance, diverts the write to <id>.conflict-<ts>.json, and returns Err. Net: the real session file stops updating after the first save and conflict files pile up — 'session doesn't get saved consistently'. Fresh sessions (loaded_mtime=None) are unaffected. Fix: save_session(&mut Session) refreshes loaded_mtime to the just-written file's mtime after the atomic write.

title: Plugin can crash the whole tool via Janet os/exit (bypasses hook try-wrapping)
description: Plugins run in init_with_default_env() — the full Janet stdlib, including os/exit which calls C exit() and terminates the entire dirge process. This bypasses the (try ...) that wraps every hook/command/tool dispatch (mod.rs:275,671) and the FFI catch_unwind guards, so a buggy or hostile plugin can take down all of dirge ('the plugin asked dirge to quit'). Fix: neuter os/exit (and process-control escape hatches) in the shared plugin env so they raise a CATCHABLE Janet error instead of terminating the host; the existing dispatch try-wrapping then surfaces it as a '[plugin] ... errored' notification and dirge survives.
close_reason: Fixed in #284: HARNESS_SANDBOX neuters os/exit / os/proc-kill / os/sigaction in the shared plugin env to raise catchable errors, so a plugin can no longer terminate the host. Confirmed sole plugin-reachable quit path. Tests + full plugin suite green.

title: edit replace_all panics/corrupts on overlapping fuzzy-match ranges
description: When exact match fails and a fuzzy fallback fires with replace_all:true, the matchers can return overlapping byte ranges; the reverse-splice loop then corrupts/panics. Fix: sort ranges by start and drop overlaps before splicing (ideally in the matchers).
close_reason: Fixed in round 3 stability batch (TDD): LSP SpawnSlotGuard, edit keep_disjoint_ranges, memory superset-drift, per-attempt lock staleness. Full suite green.

title: LSP cancelled spawn leaks the spawning slot, disabling the server for the session
description: LspManager::get_or_spawn inserts an Arc<Notify> into state.spawning then awaits do_spawn; cleanup runs only after the await. If the future is dropped (Ctrl-C abort during a cold rust-analyzer/jdtls spawn) the slot is orphaned and that server is permanently unspawnable for the session. Fix: Drop guard that removes the slot + notify_waiters on drop.
close_reason: Fixed in round 3 stability batch (TDD): LSP SpawnSlotGuard, edit keep_disjoint_ranges, memory superset-drift, per-attempt lock staleness. Full suite green.

title: cd into another dir bypasses external-write gating for relative redirect/mutation targets
description: bash compound like `cd /etc && echo x > passwd` writes outside the project with no prompt: cd is auto-allowed, the relative redirect target is classified against the static project cwd (in_cwd:true, builtin-allowed), but bash cd-s first so the write lands at /etc/passwd. check_bash_segments has no cd awareness. Fix: track per-segment cwd from leading cd/pushd and anchor relative redirect/mutation targets there, or treat them as external/Ask when a cd-outside-project precedes them.
close_reason: relative redirect/mutation targets now resolved against the cd-folded effective cwd; out-of-project writes gate. Merged.

title: UTF-8 byte-slice truncation panics (7 sites)
description: Multiple &s[..N] byte slices on model/user/path content panic on multibyte UTF-8 at the cut. Sites: tool_input_repair.rs:1100 (format_structured_error, crashes the agent run), tui/panels.rs:387 (crashes TUI), session_search.rs:445 truncate_content, tools.rs:293/349 repair telemetry, memory_store.rs:576 truncate_for_error, skills/manager.rs:277 truncate, cmd_session.rs &id[..8] x4. Fix: a char-boundary-safe truncate helper applied to all sites + tests with CJK/emoji.
close_reason: char-safe crate::text::head/tail applied to all 7 byte-slice sites. Merged.

title: Loop: orphaned tool_call_id (partial storm suppression / interrupted batch) 400s the next request
description: When the storm breaker suppresses SOME tool calls in a multi-call assistant message (partial), or a batch is cancelled/interrupted mid-way, fewer tool-result messages are appended than there were tool_calls. The assistant message keeps all tool_calls, so the next provider request has an unanswered tool_call_id and 400s ('must be followed by tool messages responding to each tool_call_id'). Recoverable harness bug. Fix: backfill a synthetic error tool-result for every unanswered id so history stays well-formed and the model sees the gap.

title: Permission: allow-always never matches multi-line bash commands (regex not DOTALL)
description: Root cause of persistent re-prompts. Command-style permission patterns (Pattern::new_command, path_style=false) compile to a regex WITHOUT the DOTALL flag, so '.*' cannot span newlines. A session grant like 'npx *' -> '^npx(?: .*)?$' fails to match 'npx tsx -e "import x\nimport y"' (multi-line -e script), so the command re-prompts forever even after the user picks allow-always. Models very commonly emit multi-line -e/-c scripts and heredoc-ish args, so this breaks allow-always broadly. Fix: build command-style regexes with RegexBuilder::dot_matches_new_line(true); keep path patterns line-sensitive. Add a deterministic test corpus of realistic compound/multi-line commands verifying the suggest->allow->recheck invariant.
close_reason: 3 root causes fixed (DOTALL command regex, bare-command ** optional args, benign-builtin default-allow alignment) + npx/node/python/python3 removed from defaults (arbitrary code) + deterministic gating corpus. Merged in PR #271.

title: Background-injected MCP tools invisible under dynamic_tool_search
description: Regression from PR #266 (background MCP loading). When dynamic_tool_search is enabled, build_loop_tools snapshots the ToolSearchTool registry + an empty loaded-set at BUILD time, before MCP connects. extend_loop_tools appends MCP tools to loop_tools but updates neither, so filter_tool_defs (rig_stream_factory.rs) filters them out of every request AND tool_search can't discover them — the model can neither see nor call background-loaded MCP tools. Fix: extend_loop_tools must also mark injected tool names into the shared tool_def_filter loaded-set so they pass the request filter. dynamic_tool_search is opt-in so default users are unaffected.
close_reason: extend_loop_tools now marks injected MCP tool names into the tool_def_filter loaded-set so they pass the request filter under dynamic_tool_search. Merged in PR #267.

title: harness/lsp: startup deadlock if plugin calls harness/lsp at load time
description: LSP responder spawns after plugin load; a top-level (harness/lsp) call at load blocks the worker on reply_rx forever (no drainer yet), and main can't reach the spawn. Fix: spawn responder before plugin-load loop.

title: Questionnaire custom answer: text runs off to the side instead of soft-wrapping
description: When typing a custom answer in the question UI, text scrolls off horizontally and disappears. Need soft-wrap onto new lines and to keep the cursor/end-of-text always visible.
close_reason: Merged in PR #216: questionnaire custom answer soft-wraps via wrap_editor

title: Input textbox: doesn't scroll past max height; typed text vanishes; no up/down arrow nav
description: The TUI compose input box stops expanding at a max height and further typed lines aren't rendered (text disappears). Need: vertical scroll so the newest lines stay visible, and Up/Down arrow keys to move the cursor across the wrapped/entered lines.
notes: Scroll half fixed in PR (input-scroll-and-nav). Arrow up/down soft-wrap nav split to a follow-up issue (needs display/raw cursor mapping).

title: LLM connect/send errors ('error sending request') not retried
description: classify_error returns Other for reqwest connect/send failures like 'Http client error: error sending request for url (...)', so the retrying_stream_fn wrapper does not retry them. Add 'error sending request' (and related connect-failure shapes) to the Network branch so transient connection failures get the standard backoff retry.

title: MED-HIGH: retry loop scaffolding re-implemented 3x; btw/summarize ignore cancellation
description: recovery.rs classification is shared, but the attempt/loop/backoff-sleep driver is hand-copied 3x. Only agent_loop/retry.rs honors AbortSignal during backoff and emits StreamEvent::Retry; provider/mod.rs:593 (btw_query) and provider/summarize.rs:141 sleep uninterruptibly and retry silently. Fix: policy.run_with_retry(fn, signal) helper owning the cancellable loop + retry callback.
notes: Duplication half fixed + merged in PR #209 (run_with_retry helper). RESIDUAL: cancellation-during-backoff needs an AbortSignal threaded through btw_query/summarize call sites — still open.
close_reason: Duplication fixed in PR #209 (run_with_retry). Cancellation-during-backoff residual is narrow: only /btw is affected (the task tool already drop-cancels btw_query via select! racing), and threading an AbortSignal through the signal-less call sites isn't worth the API churn for that one path. Triage-closed.

title: MED-HIGH: lsp anchors relative paths to self.cwd before perm check
description: lsp.rs joins relative paths to self.cwd before check_perm_path_resolve, while read/write/edit/apply_patch reject relative and let the engine resolve against working_dir. self.cwd can drift from engine working_dir, so the path checked != path used. Fix: route through shared resolve_absolute / pass raw like other FS tools. lsp.rs:226 vs read.rs:184/write.rs:94/edit.rs:137.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #208: lsp rejects relative paths via shared guard; cwd anchor removed

title: HIGH: UI re-hardcodes divergent path-tool list vs engine is_path_tool_name
description: UI hardcodes a smaller path-tool set (read|write|edit|list_dir) in 3 files; the engine canonical is_path_tool_name has ~14 incl semantic tools. grep/glob/apply_patch/find_files/lsp/repo_overview/semantic mislabeled in UI. Fix: UI calls engine::is_path_tool_name. tool_display.rs:35, permission_ui.rs:29, ui/mod.rs:2555 vs engine/classify.rs:36. Part of chokepoint-A.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #206: storm classifiers derive from tool_operation; permission_ui path arms deduped

title: HIGH: storm-breaker uses hardcoded mutating/exempt tool lists vs metadata
description: Doom-loop storm-breaker partitions tools via its own default_mutating/default_exempt name lists (comment admits 'kept in sync with BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES'). A new mutating tool gets execution_mode=Sequential (dispatches right) but is absent from default_mutating, so the loop guard goes blind to it. Fix: consult tool.execution_mode()/metadata. storm.rs:216,230 vs builder.rs:743,1581,1625. Part of chokepoint-A (tool metadata registry).
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #206: storm classifiers derive from tool_operation; permission_ui path arms deduped

title: Hard cutover: op-based permission config schema
description: Replace legacy per-tool PermissionConfig fields (write/bash/edit + ToolPerm enum + tools map) with the op-based rules: Vec<RuleConfig> array. No legacy translation path. Migrate all test sites + docs (CONFIG.md, docs/permissions.md).
close_reason: Merged in PR #203 — op-based rules schema replaces legacy per-tool config; all 2100 tests pass at -D warnings

title: Pillar 2 / Phase 2.5b: scavenge from ContentBlock::Text, not just Thinking
description: Reasonix scavenges from BOTH reasoning AND content channels (loop.ts:910-913, repair/index.ts:71). Dirge only scans ContentBlock::Thinking (run.rs:558-566). A model emitting DSML invoke blocks in regular text content (the common R1-in-content case) is silently missed. Extend the filter_map to include Text blocks. End-to-end test: DSML orphan call in Text-only response gets recovered.

title: Pillar 2 / Phase 2.5a: hoist truncation pass above storm filter
description: Reasonix runs truncation BEFORE storm filter (repair/index.ts:88-109 then 113-121). Dirge runs truncation inside validate_and_repair at dispatch time — AFTER storm filter. Result: two calls whose truncated args repair to the same form survive storm and dispatch identically. Move repair_truncated_json invocation to run.rs between scavenge merge and storm.filter_calls; remove the pre-pass from validate_and_repair. End-to-end test: two truncated-to-same calls → storm suppresses one.

title: Pillar 2 / Phase 2: scavenge provider-coverage test matrix
description: Scavenge only reads ContentBlock::Thinking today. DeepSeek's reasoning_content field, OpenAI o1 reasoning, Anthropic extended-thinking may not all route through rig into Thinking. Add provider matrix tests asserting each surface produces a Thinking block that scavenge_tool_calls can extract. Fix any adapter gaps found.
close_reason: Completed + merged as PR #178 (scavenge provider-coverage matrix); status was stale in_progress.

title: Pillar 2 / Phase 1: truncation brace-closer in tool_input_repair
description: Port Reasonix's repair/truncation.ts brace/bracket/string-stack closer to dirge tool_input_repair.rs as RepairKind::TruncationFixed. Currently a model hitting max_tokens mid-tool-call has no recovery; the call fails as 'invalid' in RepairStats. Reasonix tracks an open-stack of {/[/" chars while parsing left-to-right and emits the matching closers when the stream ends unbalanced.

title: --loop mode termination skips on_session_end
description: HeadlessLoopExit::MaxIterations at main.rs:901 returns Ok(()) without firing the hook. Third top-level exit path (interactive + --print + --loop); only first two wired.

title: Session::reset_to_new + plugin_tree paths skip both lifecycle hooks
description: reset_to_new at src/session/mod.rs:766 rotates session.id + clears messages with no hook fires. Only caller is plugin_tree.rs:101 (TreeOp::NewSession). Plugin-driven session transitions are a complete blind spot.

title: /quit + Interrupted exit paths skip on_session_end
description: cmd_quit returns Err(Interrupted); ui/mod.rs:1471 matches and breaks the event loop. The dirge-bx4g hook at line 893 only catches idle Ctrl+C/D, not error-driven exits. /quit silently misses every on_session_end.

title: auto-compaction in agent_loop bypasses on_pre_compress
description: run_compaction_pass_with_focus in src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:170 calls compression::build_summary_prompt directly without touching memory_provider. The on_pre_compress hook only fires for the manual /compress slash command. Auto-fold is the common case; plugin providers' insights get dropped silently.

title: fix: session_search returns current session's own turns
description: builder.rs:338,749 pass current_session_id=None to SessionSearchTool::new. Dedup in extras/session_search.rs:169 requires the id to be set. Thread session id through. TDD.

title: fix: skill action='view' typo in builder preamble
description: builder.rs:207 tells the model to call skill with action='view'; real enum is load/create/edit/patch/delete/list. Calls fail. Change to 'load'. TDD.

title: fix: memory tool action names in system prompt
description: prompt.rs:87 advertises actions view/write/delete; real schema at memory.rs:76 is view/add/replace/remove. Model following the prompt errors out on writes/deletes. Fix prompt to match tool. TDD.

title: Phase 8 integration: memory injection into system prompt panics on file read errors — agent builder uses unwrap() for load_memory/load_pitfalls. Disk error or corrupt file crashes the entire agent startup.
close_reason: false alarm — code uses if let Ok(...) pattern, not unwrap(). Reviewer misread the code.

title: Phase 4 background_review: passes only 'response.to_string()' as transcript — tool calls and their results are missing from the review context. The review model cannot assess tool interaction quality.
close_reason: Fixed: review now receives full session transcript via build_transcript(session) which includes user messages, assistant text, tool call names+args, and tool results. 5 new tests verify the transcript builder handles all message types, tool states (completed/interrupted/failed), truncation of large results, and system messages.

title: ratatui migration: integration + delete old paint paths
description: Phase 6: switch the main loop to terminal.draw(|f| renderer.render(f)) on every state change. Delete the old draw_panel, draw_left_panel, draw_left_panel_idle, draw_avatar, draw_bottom, render_viewport, ensure_room direct-stdout paths. write_line / write just push to chat buffer.
acceptance_criteria: Main loop calls terminal.draw on each redraw; no direct stdout writes remain in renderer.rs; UI matches prior visual goals.
close_reason: ratatui integration done (P6a/P6b/P6c). Scene + render_frame is the single paint path; 1321 lines of legacy direct-stdout paint deleted; 941 tests pass.

title: ratatui migration: bottom strip widget (avatar + input + overlay)
description: Phase 5: avatar box, input box, overlay all rendered through one BottomStrip widget. Single overlay slot (set_alert_overlay/clear). When overlay active, input is replaced inside same frame.
acceptance_criteria: BottomStrip widget renders avatar centered vertically + input editor or overlay; verticals align with chat frame ║.

title: ratatui migration: side panel widgets
description: Phase 4: left panel (AGENT STATUS / SUBAGENTS) + right panel (SYSTEM with sub-panels) become ratatui widgets. Sub-panels (SYSTEM LOAD / MCP / LSP / TODOS / MODIFIED) are light-rounded Block widgets with left-aligned content.
acceptance_criteria: LeftPanel + RightPanel widgets render; sub-panels stack vertically; content left-aligned per user spec.
close_reason: SubPanel + LeftPanel + RightPanel + 6 TestBackend tests in cd7fbae

title: ratatui migration: chat region widget
description: Phase 3: port chat content rendering to a ratatui widget. Takes the buffer of LineEntries + scroll offset + selection state, paints into the chat Rect inside the heavy ║ borders. Markdown chambers rendered as part of LineEntry text.
acceptance_criteria: ChatPane widget renders LineEntries with color + selection; scroll offset honored; tests cover wrapping + scroll.
close_reason: ChatPane widget + 5 TestBackend tests in facd384

title: ratatui migration: top/bottom frame widget
description: Phase 2: implement the unified top frame as a ratatui widget that paints ═══[AGENT STATUS]═══╔═══[AGENT LOG STREAM]═══╗═══[SYSTEM]═══ into a Buffer. TDD with TestBackend asserting buffer cells.
acceptance_criteria: TopFrame widget renders the unified frame into a Buffer; tests verify exact cell content at row 0 + chat bottom frame row.
close_reason: TopFrame + ChatBotFrame widgets landed with 4 TestBackend tests in 882fdc7

title: ratatui migration: Layout + Rect model
description: Phase 1 of UI ratatui migration. Add ratatui dep, define a Layout struct that computes named Rects (chat, left_panel, right_panel, top_frame, chat_bot_frame, bottom_strip, avatar_box, input_box, right_margin, status) from (cols, rows, input_rows, overlay_active). TDD: test rect tiling (no overlap, full coverage), test edge cases (narrow terminal, very tall input).
acceptance_criteria: ratatui dep added; Layout::new(cols, rows, ...) returns named Rects; unit tests cover tiling correctness for several terminal sizes.
close_reason: Layout struct + 9 TDD tests landed in 9eb3bcf

title: Empty Read chambers re-manifesting on parallel reads
description:
User reports: agent does 7 parallel Read calls. First 6 chambers show TOP + BOTTOM only (no content). LAST chamber has full content. Same symptom shape as dirge-jzj (closed in commit 70e7290) but appears to be regressing or has a separate cause.

Reproduction: parent agent doing 'Let me look at the remaining files' while 4 background subagents are running (at the cap). Reads in parent fire in parallel (agent_loop default is Parallel). ToolResults arrive out-of-order; fresh-chamber preamble at ui/mod.rs:2597 should paint a new chamber + content for each non-current id but isn't.

Possible regressions:
- Phase D-E (subagent_chat_rx handler) added new mpsc::select arm. If event ordering interacts badly with parallel ToolResult handling, chambers could be racing.
- write_line_to_chat for inactive chats pushes to slot.buffer + bumps slot.lines. Could affect content_row() computation if called between a parent's chamber-open and content-paint? But the active chat is always 0; slot writes go to non-active. Shouldn't matter.
- Auto-resume gate (dirge-9xo) is gated on !is_running — shouldn't fire during parent tool execution.

Needs: live RUST_LOG trace of LoopEvent::ToolExecutionStart / End for one such sequence to see WHICH ids land in WHICH state at the preamble check. Static analysis maxed out.
notes: Diagnostic instrumentation landed in commit 311f208. Reproduce with RUST_LOG=dirge::ui::chamber=trace; the per-event trace stream will show whether the body is missing on arrival (tool-side bug) or being dropped by the chamber routing (UI-side bug).
close_reason: Cannot reproduce after extensive investigation. 15 isolated tests + 4 parallel layer audits (bridge, tokio::select!, LoopTool dispatch, renderer) all clear. The dirge-jzj fix logic is intact in the current code path (verified via git blame). Diagnostic tracing remains in place under 'dirge::ui::chamber=trace' if the symptom ever reappears in production. See ba35f6a + this commit for the full investigation trail.

title: Subagent: main agent doesn't auto-resume when background subagent completes
description:
Currently when a background subagent (task tool with background=true) finishes, BackgroundStore::notify queues a system-reminder. The parent agent only sees it on the NEXT user turn via prepend_pending_notifications. So the parent sits idle until the user prompts.

Opencode's approach (packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts:215-240, resumeWhenIdle + continueIfIdle): when a background subagent completes, opencode injects a synthetic user message and starts a new turn on the parent — auto-resuming the loop.

Fix for dirge: in the UI loop's subagent_chat_rx handler (Complete/Failed events), if the parent is currently idle, automatically spawn a new turn with the pending-notification text. The mechanism is already there (BackgroundStore has notifications, prepend_pending_notifications composes the prompt) — just trigger it instead of waiting for user input.

title: UI: allow safe slash commands while agent is running
description:
Today every keypress with an agent active hits 'agent is busy — wait, interrupt (Ctrl+C), or use /quit' (src/ui/mod.rs:2013). But read-only commands (/tasks, /help, /sessions list, /tree show) don't actually need the agent to be idle. They just inspect state. Maki and opencode let inspection commands run during agent activity.

Fix: maintain an allowlist of safe-during-agent commands. The dispatch path checks the allowlist BEFORE the busy gate — anything in the list runs immediately; everything else still gates on is_running.

Allowlist (read-only / inspection):
- /tasks (lists chat windows)
- /help
- /sessions (no-arg: list)
- /tree (no-arg: show)
- /memory list
- /skill list
- /prompt (no-arg: show active)
- /model (no-arg: show active)

Commands that MUTATE state (/clear, /compress, /cd, /prompt set, /model switch) stay gated.

title: UI: ! / !! bash escape from input prompt (port from maki)
description:
Typing '!<cmd>' at the input prompt should execute the shell command and feed the output back to the agent. '!!<cmd>' executes WITHOUT telling the agent (silent — purely for the user's local terminal use).

Port verbatim: maki-ui/src/app/shell.rs:27-50 parse_shell_prefix() — self-contained, no ratatui coupling. Returns ShellPrefix { prefix_len, command, visible }.

Integration: in dirge's submission path (src/ui/mod.rs, where Enter triggers send-to-agent), detect '!' / '!!' prefix BEFORE forwarding to agent. Spawn the shell via tokio::process; on completion: if visible=true, inject as a user-visible message that flows to the agent; if visible=false, print to scrollback and skip the agent.
close_reason: Already implemented in src/shell.rs:1+ (ShellPrefix::Visible/Invisible) and wired at src/ui/mod.rs:1706/1737. Initial audit missed it because the module lives at src/shell.rs not src/ui/shell.rs.

title: UI: fuzzy search Ctrl-F via nucleo_matcher (port from maki)
description:
Current Ctrl-F search (src/ui/mod.rs:1369-1410, update_search at :4937) uses substring match via line.to_lowercase().contains(&query_lower). Maki uses nucleo_matcher for fuzzy matching ranked by score.

Port: copy from maki-ui/src/components/search_modal.rs:
- lines 8-9 (nucleo imports)
- lines 24-29 (SearchMatch struct)
- lines 157-185 (update_matches: nucleo::Atom::indices, sort by score descending)
- lines 305-351 (match-building from buffer lines)

Keep dirge's existing search overlay rendering (draw_search_bar) and key handling. Just swap the matching algorithm. Add nucleo-matcher to Cargo.toml.

title: UI: raw-mode bare LF in renderer.write produces staircase on reasoning stream
description:
USER REPORT: reasoning text (DarkMagenta) renders with each word on a new line, progressively indented — classic raw-mode \n-without-\r staircase pattern. Pre-existing bug (predates today's work).

The renderer has two writeln!(stdout, ...) call sites that emit bare LF:
- src/ui/renderer.rs:741 — write_line: writeln!(stdout, "{}", chunk) — chunk + \n
- src/ui/renderer.rs:809 — write (streaming inline): bare writeln!(stdout) — \n only

In raw mode (enable_raw_mode disables OPOST/ONLCR), \n is JUST LF: cursor moves down one row but column stays. Each subsequent call DOES MoveTo before writing, which should reset the col — yet user reports staircase. Likely cause: a buffering / order-of-ops interaction where the MoveTo escape sequence lands AFTER the LF reaches the terminal, or somewhere the MoveTo is skipped on a corner case.

Defensive fix: replace both writeln! sites with explicit write!(stdout, "\r\n") (or chunk + \r\n). Belt-and-suspenders: even if MoveTo handles it correctly normally, the explicit CR resets col immediately so any later code that bypasses MoveTo also stays sane.

User context: iTerm2, no tmux. Reasoning text only — content tokens render fine (they use replace_from + render_viewport which paints per-row with explicit MoveTo).

title: Perm review F2: alias write/apply_patch to edit permission
description:
HIGH-correctness gap found in opencode-vs-dirge review. Opencode (permission/index.ts:291-301) defines EDIT_TOOLS = ['edit', 'write', 'apply_patch'] and aliases all three to the same permission name 'edit' during rule evaluation. A user writing 'edit: deny' blocks all three uniformly.

Dirge keeps them as separate permission tools (one rule namespace each). A user writing 'permission: { edit: { **: deny } }' expecting to lock down all edits still has write and apply_patch silently going through (post-M4 they Ask, but a follow-up 'write: allow' from the user would unintentionally re-open the gate).

Port: in enforce() at src/agent/tools/mod.rs, when the tool name is 'write' or 'apply_patch', ALSO consult the 'edit' rules. Take the more restrictive result of the two checks (any deny wins, any explicit ask beats allow). Document the aliasing in PermissionConfig docs.

title: Perm M4: flip unmatched-tool default from Allow to Ask
description:
Today src/permission/checker.rs:103 has default_action = Action::Allow. Anything not explicitly configured falls through to Allow without prompting — a security gap especially for write/edit/apply_patch which currently have NO default rules installed at all (mismatch with bash which has default_bash_rules, mcp_tool which defaults Ask since 27bd70a).

Flip default to Ask. Add builtin-allow list for safe read-only tools (port maki's BUILTIN_ALLOW_RULES at permissions.rs:16-24, adapted for dirge's tool set: read/glob/grep/list_dir/list_symbols/find_definition/find_callers/find_callees/get_symbol_body/repo_overview).

write/edit/apply_patch/bash/webfetch/websearch/task/skill/memory all become Ask by default unless explicitly allowlisted. Document the migration in README + CHANGELOG. Users with existing configs unaffected — only the no-config baseline changes.

Add --yolo CLI flag (currently only via config). Maps to set the global allow_all atomic (port maki's PermissionManager::toggle_yolo at permissions.rs:219-222).

Depends on: dirge-{M2}, dirge-{M3}

title: Perm M1: single chokepoint (port maki's enforce shape)
description:
Refactor dirge's 3 permission entry points (check_perm, check_perm_path, check_perm_path_resolve in src/agent/tools/mod.rs) into a single chokepoint patterned after maki's PermissionManager::enforce (maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:283). One function, takes (tool, scope), routes internally based on tool category. No behavior change for users — pure refactor with all existing callers updated. Behind-the-scenes the new fn calls the same PermissionChecker logic.

Ref: maki-agent/src/permissions.rs:283-350 — port the signature shape (async, returns Result<(), PermissionError>, takes &EventSender + user_response_rx + cancel for UI integration). Adapt to dirge's existing AskSender + PermCheck.

title: Perm M2: unified rule schema (port maki's TOML shape via JSON)
description:
Replace dirge's per-tool field PermissionConfig (separate Option<ToolPerm> per built-in tool) with maki's uniform shape: { tool_name: { allow: [patterns], deny: [patterns] } }. Single PermissionRule struct (maki-config/src/lib.rs:268-273). Keep existing JSON config (dirge uses JSON not TOML), but flatten the schema. Add a dual-read path so old config.json files (existing user configs in the wild) still parse — log a deprecation warning and auto-migrate on save. Migration test required.

Depends on: dirge-{M1}

title: UI: per-tool-call-id chamber state (parallel tool execution)
description: The UI tracks chamber state via a single bool tool_chamber_open + Option<String> last_tool_name. Under parallel tool execution (the default per agent_loop/types.rs:402), multiple ToolExecutionStart events fire before any ToolExecutionEnd. Subsequent ToolCalls close prior chambers prematurely (now via passive close after 7403792, previously with false 'denied' wording). ToolResults that arrive after a newer ToolCall's chamber opens land as out-of-place '↳ trailers' below the wrong chamber. Fix: chamber state keyed by tool_call_id (HashMap), each in-flight tool gets its own chamber frame, the abort/passive close functions take an id.

title: H-batch1-2: history up/down byte-offset panic risk on multi-byte input
description: ui/input.rs:988-1023 — col = self.cursor - line_start is byte distance; self.cursor = (pos + col).min(target_line_end) can land mid-codepoint when previous line has multi-byte chars. Subsequent replace_range/slice panics. Convert column to char index then back to byte offset on target line.

title: H-batch1-1: bash inherits all env vars including API keys
description: tools/bash.rs:208-320 no .env_clear(). Sensitive vars (OPENROUTER_API_KEY, EXA_API_KEY, PARALLEL_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.) flow to every bash child. Use .env_clear() + curated allowlist (PATH, HOME, USER, LANG, TERM, etc.). See pi bash-executor.ts.

title: C8: /cd, /worktree, /wt-exit, /prompt, /regen-prompts silently drop LSP
description: ui/slash.rs:1290, 1300, 1763 + analogues in mod.rs — every build_agent call after these commands passes None for lsp_manager. User silently loses LSP for the rest of the session after first /cd. Re-thread lsp_manager (and re-root for /cd/worktree). opencode cd handler re-roots LSP.

title: C7: serialize_conversation drops tool_calls entirely
description: provider.rs:418-429 emits only [role]: content; msg.tool_calls (args, results, errors) invisible to summarizer. Tool-heavy sessions lose bulk of state post-compact. Include structured tool I/O like pi's compaction prompt.

title: C6: compaction summary truncates input to 6000 chars
description: provider.rs:312-320 cuts the prefix to first 6KB then appends '... [truncated]'. A 300K-token session is summarized from ~1500 tokens of content. Feed the full prefix (opencode session/compaction.ts:136-294) or chunk-summarize (pi compaction/). Pair fix with C7.

title: C5: ! cmd injects shell output into LLM context unfenced
C4: bash redirection target not permission-checked
description: tools/bash.rs:322-339 and semantic-bash never inspect redirect targets. echo pwned > /etc/something matches the safe echo ** rule and writes to /etc/something. Extract redirect/path args per command and route through path gate. See opencode shell.ts:191-208 pathArgs.

title: C3: compound_statement bypass in semantic-bash splitter
description: semantic/adapters/bash.rs:162-175 treats compound_statement/if_statement/while_statement/for_statement/case_statement/function_definition as opaque single segments. { rm -rf /tmp/foo; } matches no per-command rule and falls through to Allow. is_complex (line 127) doesn't flag these either. Mirror opencode shell.ts:127 using descendantsOfType('command') — recurse into every command descendant.

title: C2: webfetch SSRF via HTTP redirects
description: tools/webfetch.rs:270-273 builds reqwest client without redirect::Policy::none() or custom policy. validate_url_host_safety runs once on initial URL; 30x to RFC1918/169.254 is silently followed. Install custom redirect::Policy::custom re-running validate_url_host_safety on each Attempt::url().

title: C1: apply_patch uses unresolved path after perm check (symlink-swap risk)
description: tools/apply_patch.rs:251 calls check_perm_path (not check_perm_path_resolve), then apply_create/update/delete/rename operate on raw user-supplied path. Other tools (read.rs:161, write.rs:88, edit.rs:133) route through resolved_path. H12 fix didn't propagate. Thread check_perm_path_resolve per op and pass resolved string into apply_*.

title: H1: propagate AbortSignal into JanetLoopTool::execute
description: JanetLoopTool::execute takes _signal and drops it. Plugin tool handlers run unboundedly inside spawn_blocking holding the PM mutex; the user's Ctrl+C/Esc has no effect until the handler returns. Phase-6 wired AbortSignal into native tools; plugin tools must too.\n\nFix: race the spawn_blocking join against wait_for_cancel(signal) (same pattern as the phase-6 wrapper in builder.rs). On cancel return Err("cancelled"). Document that Janet is single-threaded so handlers can't be preempted — only joined. Plugin authors should keep work bounded.

title: C1/C2: fix customType wrapper + add e2e bridge test for renderer dispatch
description: Phase 9d renderer lookup uses wrong JSON nesting level — payload.get('type') against the {role,content} wrapper always returns None, so registered renderers never fire end-to-end. Smoke test calls invoke_message_renderer directly with a hand-built payload so it passes a broken path.\n\nFix: extend harness/add-custom-message to (customType content &opt display); wrap as {role:'custom', customType, content, display:true} matching pi's CustomMessage shape (messages.ts:48). UI consumer reads customType at top level. Add bridge/UI-equivalent test that walks the full LoopMessage::Custom -> AgentEvent::CustomMessage -> renderer-resolve path so future drift is caught.

title: Path traversal via .. survives lexical-fallback when both canonicalize calls fail
description: permission/checker.rs:495-513 — when canonicalize(joined) AND canonicalize(parent) both fail (nonexistent subdir + ..), falls through to lexical joined.to_string_lossy(). Path::starts_with matches components, so /cwd/nonexistent/../../etc/passwd classifies as internal. Need lexical .. normalization in fallback.

title: Provider SSE stream has no per-chunk timeout
description: agent/runner.rs:259 stream.next().await with no tokio::time::timeout — stalled provider hangs agent forever. opencode chunk-timeout pattern: per-chunk read deadline.

title: ANSI injection in permission ALERT prompt
description: ask_req.tool / ask_req.input rendered un-sanitized at mod.rs:2584-2585. Reopen path already sanitizes — asymmetric. Sec impl: ANSI at the permission-decision moment.

title: Chat history ignores 120-col content_width cap
description: max_line_width and wrap_line use raw content_cols, so on wide terminals scrollback overflows the centered band into divider/panel margin.

title: R1: fix 3 critical plugin bugs (FFI panic, dialog deadlock, init hang)
description: From the plugin subsystem audit: (1) wrap JanetCFunctions in catch_unwind so Rust panics don't unwind across the C-FFI boundary into Janet; (2) cancel send_dialog's reply_rx.recv() on worker shutdown so the worker thread doesn't block forever when the UI exits mid-dialog; (3) add timeout to the init handshake so a worker panic before init_tx.send() doesn't hang the main thread. Also: (4) bounds-assert wrap_string's i32 cast for the unlikely >2GB case, (5) make take_string_slot atomic to close the race window, (6) don't eat unrelated user events in the dialog arm.

title: Config-bindable Shift+Enter newline + enable keyboard enhancement so it's delivered
Worktree writer permission checker inherits LLM auto-approver, weakening the human backstop (PR670 follow-up)
Coordinated dispatch hardening: unbounded retry chain, batch can exceed 4, unbounded dispatches map, no orphan-worktree GC, setup errors surfaced twice (PR670 follow-up)
TODOS panel: track the agent's active issue; fix empty-after-fold/resume (GH #663)
description: From GH issue #663 (blob42). The TODOS panel mirrors the session's live issue board but (1) went empty after a compaction fold/resume because it was scoped to the rotating session.id, and (2) didn't repopulate when the agent picked up an existing issue (issue start never re-stamped session_id). Reworked: board scoped by stable lineage origin (origin_of via the shared sessions table) so it survives folds/resume; issue start / update->in_progress claims the issue for the current conversation; panel pins the active in_progress item at top with a distinct glyph + focus color. Design decision (per yogthos): this-conversation board with claim-on-pickup, no completed-history tail.
close_reason: Landed on fix/audit-p2-bugs (532675c5). Origin-scoped board (origin_of) + claim-on-pickup (assign_to_session on start/update->in_progress) + active-row focus color/glyph in the panel. Tests: issue_db board_scopes_by_lineage_origin_across_fold, assign_to_session_claims_foreign_issue_onto_board; issue tool start_claims_a_foreign_issue_for_this_session; panel_render todo_row_tests. Note: blob42's separate '/toggle todos should hide the panel' point is NOT addressed here (out of this message's scope).

title: session_db: repair migration to purge pre-v6 phantom FTS postings from existing DBs
description:
Follow-up to dirge-vpma.4. The v6 rebuild now clears messages_fts with the FTS5 'delete-all' command, so DBs migrating v5->v6 from this version forward are clean. But a DB that already ran the OLD v6 migration (plain DELETE FROM messages_fts) still carries the phantom postings — tool_name/tool_calls tokens, including secrets in folded bash tool_calls, remain matchable via search_messages forever.

Fix: a new schema migration (say v-next) that runs 'delete-all' on messages_fts and re-backfills redacted content for every message row, the same backfill run_migration_v6 does. Must be idempotent and cheap on already-clean DBs.

Constraint: SCHEMA_VERSION is #[cfg]-split (15 with experimental-graph-search, 13 without), so the new version guard cannot hardcode a number relative to the feature-gated ceiling — mirror the pattern used for the skills tables (created ahead of the version early-return) or add the migration inside the existing ladder with a feature-independent guard. See the note at run_migration_v7 about the external-content delete problem.

title: ui: drain_interjections skips begin_snapshot_turn — rewind buckets misattributed
description:
src/ui/mod.rs:652-682 (structural). Every other path that records a user message and spawns a runner calls begin_snapshot_turn(session) to open a rewind bucket keyed to that message id (run_handlers/submit.rs:153, mod.rs:2829, 3087, 3866, 3908), but drain_interjections! does add_message + spawn_runner + install_into without it. A prompt queued during non-blocking /compress (or /btw, /wt-merge) drains into a fresh turn whose file edits are captured into the PREVIOUS turn's snapshot bucket (snapshots.rs:97) — rewinding to the prior message also reverts the drained turn's edits, and the drained turn's own message id has no bucket.
Fix: begin_snapshot_turn inside the macro before spawn. Fix together with the is_running clobber issue. Audit item 19.

title: paths: project root resolved five different ways — unify behind ProjectPaths
description:
Cross-cutting (ssot/structural). The .dirge/ tree is anchored differently per feature, and DIRGE_PROJECT_ROOT moves only one group:
1. ProjectPaths (src/extras/dirge_paths.rs:76): git-root walk-up + DIRGE_PROJECT_ROOT. Used by sessions DB, memory, /spec, /issues, /graph, agent_io.
2. Literal PathBuf::from(".dirge") — CWD-relative, no walk-up, no override: main.rs:1034 + ui/slash/cmd/plugins.rs:176 (plugins), config/mod.rs:1323 (project config.json), context/prompts.rs:243 (prompts).
3. skill.rs:115 find_project_ancestor_dirs: walks ALL git ancestors (inner overrides outer), no override env.
4. context/agent_defs.rs:30: cwd.join(".dirge/agents"), no walk-up.
5. extras/mcp_server.rs:556: std::env::current_dir() for the session pointer.
Launch dirge in <repo>/sub/: session DB + memory attach to <repo>/.dirge/ while project config/plugins/prompts/agents silently fail to load (they look in sub/.dirge/). The comment justifying CWD-relative ("matches .dirge/skills") is already false — skills walk up. Design decision needed (walk-up semantics are a behavior change); recommend ProjectPaths as the single resolver.
Audit item 17.
design:
DEFERRED from the vpma P2 sweep — this is a behavior change, not a mechanical unification, so it needs a design decision before implementing.

Recommended target: make ProjectPaths (src/extras/dirge_paths.rs) the single project-root resolver (git-root walk-up + DIRGE_PROJECT_ROOT override) and route the five CWD-relative / ad-hoc sites through it:
- main.rs:1034, ui/slash/cmd/plugins.rs:176 (plugins)
- config/mod.rs:1323 (project config.json)
- context/prompts.rs:243 (prompts)
- context/agent_defs.rs:30 (.dirge/agents)
- extras/mcp_server.rs:556 (session pointer via current_dir)

Open decisions for a human:
1. Behavior change: today plugins/config/prompts/agents load CWD-relative; unifying makes them walk up to the git root / honor DIRGE_PROJECT_ROOT. Launching in <repo>/sub/ would start loading <repo>/.dirge/ config where it silently loaded nothing before. Confirm this is the desired semantics (it fixes the split-brain where session DB + memory already anchor at the git root).
2. skill.rs:115 find_project_ancestor_dirs deliberately walks ALL git ancestors and merges inner-overrides-outer. That MUST be preserved — do not collapse it to a single-root lookup. Decide whether ProjectPaths grows an ancestor-chain accessor or skill.rs keeps its own walk.
3. Migration/compat: existing users launching from a subdir may see new config/plugins start loading. Needs a changelog note and possibly a one-release warning.

Suggest splitting into: (a) a mechanical 'add ProjectPaths accessors + route the trivially-safe sites' task, and (b) this design ticket for the behavior-change sites.
close_reason: Landed on fix/audit-p2-bugs (commit ed220749). Routed the four CWD-relative project-file sites — .dirge/plugins (main.rs + slash /plugins), .dirge/config.json (config::project_config_file_path), .dirge/prompts (context::local_prompts_dir), .dirge/agents (context::project_agents_dir) — through ProjectPaths (git-root walk-up + DIRGE_PROJECT_ROOT), matching where sessions+memory already anchor. Design decision #1 approved by user: subdirectory launches now load <repo>/.dirge/ config/plugins/prompts/agents. Decision #2 honored: skill.rs ancestor-chain walk left untouched. Decision #3: CHANGELOG Unreleased note added. Remaining audit item 5 (mcp_server session pointer) split out as dirge-gwn3 (P3).

title: input: Ctrl+K kills to end of buffer, not end of line
description:
src/ui/input.rs:1193-1199 (logic). KillToLineEnd kills buffer[cursor..] to end of BUFFER, not end of line — cursor_line_end exists and is unused here; contrast KillToLineStart (1201-1210) which correctly uses cursor_line_start; keymap.rs:459 documents "cursor to end of line". Multi-line draft (Shift+Enter/Ctrl+J/paste), cursor mid-line-1, Ctrl+K: all subsequent lines silently wiped.
Fix: kill to cursor_line_end. Audit item 16.

title: cli: --loop-max N runs N-1 iterations (N=1 runs zero)
description:
src/main.rs:1904-1913 with src/extras/loop/mod.rs:67-72 (logic). Off-by-one: state.iteration += 1 runs before should_stop() (iteration >= max), so the check fires before the Nth iteration executes. dirge --loop --loop-max 1 "task": iteration becomes 1, 1 >= 1 stops immediately, prints "max iterations (1) reached", zero agent iterations run; any N runs N-1.
Fix: check after the iteration (or compare > max). Audit item 15.

title: plugin_tree: SwitchSession adopts raw-parsed Session — bypasses migrate/version-guard/mtime
description:
src/ui/plugin_tree.rs:143-164, root src/session/storage.rs:394-406 (structural). Plugin SwitchSession adopts a Session parsed raw by find_sessions_by_prefix (*session = loaded), bypassing every invariant load_session enforces: no migrate_session (v<2 files skip recompute_all_estimates — context usage understated 5-10x, compaction may not fire before real overflow), no SESS-15 loaded_from_newer_version guard (a file written by newer dirge is silently truncated on next save — the exact permanent data loss storage.rs:121-135 exists to prevent), and loaded_mtime = None (concurrent-writer conflict detection at 169-182 disabled — second instance's saves clobbered). The slash switch path (switch.rs:22) routes through load_session_tip and gets all three.
Fix: route plugin switch through load_session_tip. Audit item 14.

title: storage: delete_session removes one file — fold-chain rotations stay resumable
description:
src/session/storage.rs:287-301 + src/ui/slash/cmd/sessions/delete.rs:11-31 (structural). delete_session removes one <id>.json, but a fold chain is many files sharing one effective_origin; find_sessions_by_prefix (408-410) dedups rotations to a single row, so /sessions delete <tip-prefix> reports deleted while every older rotation file (and asset dirs) stays on disk. After deleting the tip, find_recent_sessions dedup surfaces the older rotation of the same origin: the deleted conversation reappears in recents with pre-fold state, fully resumable via load_session_tip. Related (distinct): dirge-5he3 orphaned image assets after fold.
Fix: delete the whole origin chain (all rotations + assets). Audit item 13.

title: dap: set_breakpoints sends single bp — DAP replace semantics delete prior breakpoints
description:
src/agent/tools/debug.rs:376-391 with src/dap/session.rs:664-680 (logic). Each set_breakpoints tool call sends only the single new breakpoint (vec![bp]), and DAP setBreakpoints REPLACES the source's entire breakpoint set — setting a second breakpoint in the same file silently deletes the first. The session-side cache (session.breakpoints.insert) has the prior records available to merge, and the tool output ("Breakpoint set at file:line") never tells the model anything was cleared. Agent sets foo.py:10 then foo.py:50 — adapter holds only :50; continue sails past line 10 and the agent debugs from wrong premises.
Fix: merge cached breakpoints for the source into each setBreakpoints request. Audit item 12.
close_reason: set_breakpoints merges cached+incoming (dedup by line) via merge_source_breakpoints and resends full list; clear_breakpoints handles the empty-set replace. Landed on fix/audit-p2-bugs.

title: rig_stream: abort not raced against chunk wait — unobserved up to 300s
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/rig_stream.rs:149-206 (logic). The abort signal is checked only BEFORE each chunk wait; tokio::time::timeout(t, raw.next()) / raw.next().await is never raced against signal.cancelled(), and the comment at 145-148 claims a post-await check that does not exist. Cancellation is cooperative-only here (no JoinHandle::abort; consumer loop does a bare stream.next().await), so an abort during a silent provider gap (long reasoning pause, stalled connection) is not observed until the next chunk or the chunk timeout — up to DEFAULT_STREAM_CHUNK_SECS = 300 (60s with an open tool call). UI appears hung after abort; the rig request keeps running. retry.rs:218-236 already select!-races cancelled() during backoff — same pattern applies.
Fix: select! the abort into the chunk wait. Audit item 11.

title: agent_loop: transcript_from_value_slice reads .as_str() only — hook transcript near-empty
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:674-689 (ssot). transcript_from_value_slice extracts content only via .and_then(|v| v.as_str()), but assistant and toolResult messages serialize content as block ARRAYS (message.rs:413-433). This re-implements content extraction and diverged from compression::content_text (compression.rs:375-385), which was already fixed for exactly this class (dirge-u5ka). Auto-fold with a memory provider: fire_pre_compress receives a transcript of only text-only user messages and system markers — every assistant turn and tool result in the discarded middle silently omitted — while the /compress slash path (ui/slash/mod.rs:350) feeds a full transcript. Same hook, two paths, materially different inputs.
Fix: use compression::content_text. Audit item 10.

title: agent_loop: fast checkpoint-reuse fold skips on_pre_compress and on-compact hooks
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:944-966 (structural). The fast (checkpoint-reuse) fold branch discards messages[0..cut] without firing the on_pre_compress memory hook (build_augmented_focus, 994-995) or the on-compact plugin hook (1000-1006) — both fire only in the inline branch (if !reused && start < end). With incremental checkpointing default-on, most auto-folds take the fast path once a background checkpoint exists, so a plugin memory provider never sees the discarded slice on the high-frequency path — precisely the silent-insight-drop dirge-h5tv was added to prevent — and the on-compact first-refusal contract is bypassed. Only the observe-only on_before hook fires (895-897).
Fix: fire both hooks on the reuse path before discarding. Audit item 9.
close_reason: Fast reuse fold now fires on_pre_compress + on_compact over the discarded slice inside the committed reuse branch (exactly once; inline path is !reused-guarded). on_compact gets first refusal and overrides the checkpoint summary when valid. Landed on fix/audit-p2-bugs.

title: ui: subagent chat-index maps never reindexed after remove_chat
description:
src/ui/mod.rs:4530-4531 (ssot). subagent_chat_map (id->chat idx) and chat_idx_to_subagent (idx->id) store absolute chat indices and are never reindexed after remove_chat shifts every higher index down. Two subagents in chats 1 and 2; close chat 1: the survivor's entry still says idx 2 — out of range (Token/Complete lines silently dropped by chats.get_mut) or, after a new chat spawns, pointing at the WRONG chat. Ctrl+K (mod.rs:2284) can resolve the wrong subagent id for the focused tab.
Fix: reindex both maps in remove_chat (do together with the remove_chat load_active fix). Audit item 8.

title: renderer: inactive-chat writes skip source — resize wipes subagent transcript
description:
src/ui/renderer.rs:1286-1301 (ssot). write_line_to_chat for an INACTIVE chat pushes rows into slot.buffer only, never slot.source, violating the documented invariant (465-470, 525-529) that source is the width-independent source of truth from which rebuild() re-derives buffer. A subagent streams its transcript into an inactive chat (ui/mod.rs:4535-4643); user switches to it; any terminal resize rebuild() regenerates buffer from the empty source — the entire transcript vanishes. Same desync: enforce_cap (2326-2333) drains rows that don't correspond to source blocks (drops wrong rows), replace_from block math misaligns, and inactive slot.buffer is exempt from the 20k scrollback cap (unbounded growth for a chatty subagent).
Fix: append to source as well (or route through the same append path as active writes). Audit item 7.

title: config: update_config_file rewrites corrupt config.json with only sandbox keys
description:
src/config/mod.rs:1493-1516 (structural). update_config_file (sandbox-microvm feature) parses the existing config.json with serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default() — a present-but-corrupt file silently becomes an empty map, and the function writes back only the sandbox keys, destroying the user's providers/keybindings/permissions with no warning. load() (1331-1353) treats the exact same file state as a hard error and exits: two contradictory corrupt-file policies for one file.
Fix: hard-error on unparseable config, matching load(). Audit item 6.

title: input: Ctrl+G external editor destroys paste bodies and pending images
description:
src/ui/input.rs:1039 + 510-527 (logic, data loss). open_in_external_editor writes the raw buffer including \x01<idx>\x01 paste/image markers to the temp file — paste bodies never reach $EDITOR (user sees ^A0^A) — and on return set_text clears self.pastes plus kill-ring and undo, so surviving markers dangle and expanded() silently drops them. Paste a 50-line block, Ctrl+G, save with any change: the submitted prompt is missing the entire pasted body, unrecoverable. Pending images lost the same way.
Fix: expand markers into the temp file (and re-collapse or accept expansion on return). Audit item 5.
close_reason: External editor seeds expanded text and folds result back via apply_editor_result, preserving pending images. Landed on fix/audit-p2-bugs.

title: session_db: v6 redaction rebuild leaves phantom FTS postings — secrets stay searchable
description:
src/extras/session_db.rs:603-608, same pattern at :475-477 in v2 (logic). The v6 rebuild clears the external-content FTS index with plain DELETE FROM messages_fts, which unindexes each row using the content table's CURRENT messages.content — but the indexed text was the composite content || ' ' || tool_name || ' ' || tool_calls written by the v2 triggers. All tool_name/tool_calls tokens survive as phantom postings. Verified empirically with sqlite3: after DELETE FROM, MATCH 'AKIA_SECRETTOKEN99' still returns the rowid. A pre-v6 DB with a secret in tool_calls (env var in a bash command) keeps that secret permanently matchable via search_messages, which returns the raw row — defeating the SESS-14 scrub. Later 'delete' commands remove only the stored projection's tokens and can never clean the phantoms.
Fix: use INSERT INTO messages_fts(messages_fts) VALUES('delete-all'); add a repair migration for existing DBs. Audit item 4.
close_reason: v6 rebuild now clears messages_fts via FTS5 'delete-all' (external-content table left phantom postings under plain DELETE FROM). messages_fts_trigram (standalone) unchanged. Regression test pins the semantics. Repair migration for already-migrated DBs filed as dirge-vpma.50. Landed on fix/audit-p2-bugs.

title: Overlay body wrap parameters computed three inconsistent ways; box mis-sized
description: src/ui/tui/bottom.rs:391/408 vs 492/496 vs 513/519: paint_overlay_box wraps at inner_w-2 with a label-hanging indent, overlay_head_tail_wrapped matches it, but overlay_wrapped_row_count (the one the renderer uses to size the box, renderer.rs:970,1000) wraps at inner_w-4 with a fixed two-space indent. A long command line paints more continuation rows than counted -> box too short, scroll mode engaged despite room; short-label lines count extra -> phantom blank rows. Related to alert-scroll dirge-agbo. Fix: make overlay_wrapped_row_count share a helper with overlay_head_tail_wrapped so all three agree by construction.

title: Detached review/checkpoint tasks keep the loop event channel open past AgentEnd
description: src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:416-460,752-784 + integration.rs:768-783: detached tasks hold clones of the LoopEvent sender so the channel does not close at AgentEnd; the pump (while let Some=loop_rx.recv()) and LoopRunner.task stay alive until every background review/checkpoint finishes (unbounded per the judge-timeout bug dirge-ax46). (a) consumers that drain to channel-close (headless/test) block on a background judge after the run is logically done; (b) consumers that stop at Done never see the advisory SystemNotice, silently defeating default Advisory review mode. Fix: bound the detached review and/or emit advisory results on a channel decoupled from run lifetime with an explicit post-AgentEnd contract.

title: Semantic and coarse bash mutator lists have drifted (truncate/install/shred missing)
description: src/semantic/adapters/bash.rs:310 FILE_MUTATORS omits truncate, install, shred which src/agent/tools/bash/check.rs:448 COARSE_MUTATORS includes. In the default semantic-bash build, truncate -s0 /etc/x, shred /etc/x, install /dev/null /etc/x never produce an Edit claim for their path operands, so the write deny rules / external-dir gate that extract_mutation_paths exists to enforce are bypassed the moment a user adds any execute allow covering them (or relies on an edit:deny to protect a path). Related to the redirect-bypass dirge-k0oa. Fix: single shared mutator list consumed by both extractors.
close_reason: Single shared FILE_MUTATORS in permission::engine::types (14 entries incl truncate/install/shred) now consumed by both the semantic extract_mutation_paths and the coarse coarse_mutation_paths. Removes the drift where truncate/install/shred were missing from the semantic list, so their path operands bypassed the Edit write-gate in the default build.

title: Unify oversized-text truncation (reimplemented ~6 ways with divergent byte/char semantics)
description: Divergent sites: heal.rs:71-98 (70/30 head-tail, bytes-vs-chars mixed); compression.rs truncate_with_head_tail; critic.rs:41-48 truncate_rules (chars, head-only); run.rs:2413-2528 build_critic_transcript (chars, head+tail elision); code_review.rs:756-770 cap_diff (bytes, head-only); tools.rs:298-306 and 353-361 (bytes via text::head, two near-identical inline copies). Each re-decides unit, head/tail policy and note format; the bytes/chars divergence already produced real bugs (heal shrink dirge-unqq; critic comment says old byte caps needlessly shortened multibyte prompts). Fix: one chars-based truncation utility parameterized by budget/strategy/note.

title: Four skills-table writers enforce different invariants; DB drifts from on-disk SKILL.md
description: src/extras/skill_db.rs:211-270,408-467 and agent/tools/skill.rs:199,224,230-248. (a) SkillStore::create threat-scans; register_file_skill does not, so repo-supplied skill content enters DB/FTS unscanned with no render-time re-scan. (b) skill.rs edit calls register_file_skill(name,empty,content,true) with an empty description, whose UPDATE (427-431) clobbers the real description discovery registered (loop_tools.rs:288) so ranking/FTS degrade until next session. (c) the patch action (skill.rs:230-248) mutates SKILL.md on disk but never refreshes DB content/FTS, so search matches stale text. (d) create stores raw frontmatter+body; discovery stores parsed body only. Fix: route every mutation through one upsert that scans, parses the description from frontmatter, and reindexes.

title: /btw markdown result collapses line structure and re-wraps at char boundaries
description: src/ui/mod.rs:4008-4016 + renderer.rs:2548-2561: markdown_to_styled returns one LineEntry per rendered row (no newline), but the loop feeds each row to Renderer::write which appends to one shared partial and only commits on newline/overflow, so headings, bullets and paragraphs concatenate into single long lines re-broken at arbitrary columns. Compounding: pre-wrap width is line_width() (cols-2) while write chunks at max_line_width(), and write's column accounting is chars().count() so SGR bytes and wide glyphs each count as one column (the char-chunk wrap wrap_line was migrated off of). The session-reload path uses the correct write_line per entry (events.rs:259). Fix: use write_line per LineEntry wrapped at max_line_width() and make write's column math width-aware.

title: YankPop reuses stale byte offsets after a kill, panicking mid-codepoint
description: src/ui/input.rs:1243-1260: yank_state is cleared only by reset_kill_accumulation, but the kill actions (KillToLineEnd 1182, KillToLineStart 1190, KillWordBack 1201) go through push_kill (851) which does NOT clear it. After a Yank sets yank_state, an intervening Ctrl+U/Ctrl+W mutates the buffer, then Alt+Y (YankPop) passes its guard (kill_ring.len()>1 && range_end<=buffer.len()) -- a byte-length check, not a char boundary -- and replace_range(state.cursor..range_end,"") can land inside a multibyte char -> panic 'byte index is not a char boundary' from default keybindings; with ASCII it silently corrupts unrelated text. Fix: clear yank_state in push_kill, or verify buffer[state.cursor..range_end] still equals the last-yanked text before replacing.
close_reason: Two-part fix: push_kill now clears yank_state (the kill actions routed through it and never called reset_kill_accumulation, so a yank followed by Ctrl+U/W/K left stale offsets); and YankPop's guard now verifies buffer.get(range) is still on char boundaries AND still equals the yanked text before replace_range, so a stale state is a safe no-op instead of a panic/corruption.

title: heal restore-time shrink handles only string content and no-ops on real resume paths
description: src/agent/agent_loop/heal.rs:40-67: shrink_oversized_tool_results reads content via as_str() only, but the loop-transcript shape it claims to support serializes content as a block array (message.rs:424-433), so on every real resume (rig_history_to_loop_messages -> heal_loaded_messages, integration.rs:727) the shrink silently no-ops and healed_count/chars_saved under-report. compression::cap_oversized_tool_results (compression.rs:225-290) already handles both shapes -- duplicated mechanism, one copy broken. Also truncate_for_model (71-98) compares content.len() bytes against a char budget and reports byte counts, wrong for multibyte. Fix: delegate to the compression cap.

title: transient_recoveries is never reset, so blips hours apart accumulate into a false hard-fail
description: src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:1433,1672,1682: transient_recoveries is documented (207-210) as counting CONSECUTIVE recoveries so a dead network still terminates, but it is only incremented and compared, never reset. In a long autonomous run, three transient blips hours apart each followed by hundreds of healthy turns exhaust MAX_TRANSIENT_RECOVERIES and the fourth blip terminates the run as a hard error though the network is fine. Fix: reset to 0 after an assistant turn completes with a non-error stop reason.

title: Resume restores the saved model but not the saved provider
description: src/main.rs:734,754-758,1167-1193 (session/mod.rs:288): Session persists provider but startup never reads it -- provider comes from cli.resolve_provider, the client is built from it, then resolve_startup_model adopts session.model on a plain resume. dirge -c after changing the config default (or after a new *_API_KEY shifts autodetect) sends e.g. saved gpt-4o to a DeepSeek client -> 404, no warning. Fix: on resume without --provider, resolve the client from session.provider, or detect the mismatch and re-derive/warn.
close_reason: On a plain resume (resumed && no --provider/DIRGE_PROVIDER override) main.rs now adopts session.provider before building the client, via should_adopt_session_provider(), so the restored model no longer goes to a mismatched client (config-default change or new *_API_KEY autodetect shift). Warns which provider was adopted.

title: Provider base-URL resolution differs per provider; config ignored and http:// unvalidated
description:
src/provider/client.rs:245-262 + resolve.rs:126-138. DeepSeek and GLM use DEEPSEEK_BASE_URL/GLM_BASE_URL env > hard default and silently IGNORE config providers.

.base_url; Custom uses config > CUSTOM_BASE_URL (opposite precedence); OpenAI-ChatGPT uses config > hard-coded Codex URL; others config-only. The https/allow_insecure validation runs only on config-declared URLs (resolve.rs:126), so an http:// value in DEEPSEEK_BASE_URL/GLM_BASE_URL/CUSTOM_BASE_URL sends the API key and all traffic plaintext with no check. A user setting providers.deepseek.base_url to a proxy is silently ignored and still hits api.deepseek.com. Fix: one shared resolver (config > env > default) with scheme validation on whichever source wins.
close_reason: One shared resolve_provider_base_url(kind, config_url, env) with uniform precedence config > env > default for all providers (DeepSeek/GLM now honor config base_url instead of ignoring it; Custom keeps config>env). Env-sourced URLs (DEEPSEEK/GLM/CUSTOM_BASE_URL) now https-checked here since they bypass resolve.rs validation and carry no allow_insecure — http:// is rejected instead of leaking the key in plaintext. Routed through the injected env closure (was std::env::var direct) so it's unit-testable.

title: Anthropic OAuth login selects the provider but not OAuth auth, yielding an unusable client
description: src/provider/resolve.rs:437-460,482 + client.rs:188,350-356. (a) user ran only 'dirge auth anthropic' (creds file, no env keys/config): auth_detect_provider picks anthropic (444) but create_client_with_resolved_auth defaults to ProviderAuth::ApiKey (188) and resolve_api_key_from bails 'No API key found for Anthropic' -- the stored login is never used (defeats GH #617; OpenAI works because resolve_provider_credential consults the OAuth store at client.rs:475, no Anthropic analog). (b) user exports only ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN: the fallback (482) returns the sk-ant-oat bearer as an API key and the plain anthropic::Client sends it as x-api-key without the Bearer header/oauth beta/payload shaping AnthropicHttpClient adds -> rejected. Fix: make Anthropic-OAuth presence imply ProviderAuth::Anthropic; drop ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN from the API-key fallback.
close_reason: Two-part fix. (1) effective_auth() in client.rs: for the anthropic provider with no EXPLICIT auth, a present OAuth login (creds file ~/.claude/.credentials.json OR non-empty ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN) implies ProviderAuth::Anthropic, so resolve_anthropic_auth builds the AnthropicHttpClient (Bearer + oauth beta + payload shaping) instead of the ApiKey default bailing 'No API key found'. (2) Dropped ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN from provider_env_var_fallbacks(Anthropic) so the sk-ant-oat bearer is never mis-sent as an x-api-key. Verified by unit tests (effective_auth decision + fallback list); a live Anthropic-OAuth round-trip wasn't exercised (no Anthropic creds in this env).

title: --provider override sends the config-default provider's model to the overridden provider
description: src/cli.rs:368-373 (+ main.rs:735-742, cli.rs:418-423): with config {provider:glm, providers:{glm:{model:glm-5.2}}}, dirge --provider openai (no openai model entry) hits resolve_model's fallback resolve_role(Default) which tracks cfg.provider (glm) -> glm-5.2 sent to the OpenAI client -> 404. Worse, main.rs:742 computes model_explicit from the Default role's entry so this wrong model is flagged explicit. resolve_temperature (cli.rs:418) has the same fall-through. Fix: when --provider/DIRGE_PROVIDER overrides, resolve model/temperature/explicitness from the overriding provider's entry, never from the Default role.
close_reason: Added Cli::resolution_entry that returns the --provider/DIRGE_PROVIDER override's entry (None if absent) or the Default role's entry otherwise. resolve_model + resolve_temperature + main.rs config_model/model_explicit all read from it, so an override to a provider without a model entry no longer falls through to the config-default (glm) provider's model/temp -- model_explicit becomes false and default_model_for_alias picks the override provider's own default instead of sending glm-5.2 to OpenAI (404).

title: session_search existence probe is always true; cross-session scroll anchors wrong session
description: src/extras/session_search.rs:371-393 with session_db.rs:1590-1653: find_message_session's probe get_anchored_view(...).is_ok() is always true (the COUNT returns 0 and the SELECT just returns rows for a missing id), so the compression-split rebinding below it is dead and SCROLL anchors in the wrong session. Scrolling to a message in a child session after a split returns a window of parent messages mislabeled as anchored. get_bookends (328,345) has the same misuse: sentinel anchor id 1 returns 4-not-3 messages and window=100000 materializes the whole transcript per hit. Fix: probe with SELECT 1 FROM messages WHERE session_id=? AND id=?; add a cheap first/last-N query.

title: content_guard data-URI check only inspects the first match, masking a later payload
description: src/extras/content_guard.rs:292,385-398: the data-URI detector uses re.find (single match); pattern data:(image/|font/)? matches an allowlisted data:image/ first, continues, and never inspects a later data:text/html,<script> vector. Content 'data:image/png;base64,x ... data:text/html,...' bypasses scan_untrusted. Fix: iterate find_iter for the data: pattern and flag if any match is outside the image/font allowlist.
close_reason: scan_untrusted's LinkExfiltration loop now iterates find_iter for the data: URI pattern and flags if ANY match is outside the image/font allowlist, instead of re.find + continue which stopped at the first (allowlisted) data:image/ and never inspected a later data:text/html,<script>.

title: bump_salience demotes entries already above the cap, then the ledger blocks retry
description: src/extras/memory_db.rs:1346-1359: SET salience = MIN(?cap, salience + ?delta) LOWERS salience for entries above the cap. memory_curator.rs:283 calls it with cap 0.9; expand_entry reinforces up to 1.0 and the cluster representative is chosen by highest use_count (exactly the entries above 0.9). A heavily-consulted entry at 0.95 that graduates gets written down to 0.9 and the graduation ledger prevents retry. Fix: CASE WHEN salience>=?cap THEN salience ELSE MIN(?cap, salience+?delta) END (never reduce).

title: replace_entry and overview-refresh bypass memory char-budget and dup checks
description: src/extras/memory_db.rs:1004-1064 and 854-883: add_entry (899) and supersede_entry (1107) reject over-budget and duplicate entries; replace_entry does neither (replacing 'x' with a 50KB body stores it verbatim, no compaction). The overview branch in add_entry (854-883) returns before the size check at 897, so refreshing an existing overview (done each session by the review pass) accepts arbitrary content into an entry make_room_in_hot is forbidden to evict (956-960) -> permanent eviction-exempt system-prompt bloat. Fix: apply entry_cost>char_limit and dup checks in replace_entry and the overview in-place branch; run make_room_in_hot when a replace grows an entry.

title: ACP prompt loop swallows AgentEvent::Error and reports a normal EndTurn
description: src/extras/acp/mod.rs:533-535: AgentEvent::Error(_) is handled with a bare break, discarding the error text, then responds StopReason::EndTurn (613-618). Provider auth failure, network error, or tool crash mid-run -> the editor client sees a normally-ended turn with truncated/empty content and no diagnostic. Contrast ContextOverflow (536-553) which surfaces its message as a chunk before breaking. Fix: mirror the ContextOverflow arm, send the error as a chunk (or an ACP error) before breaking.

title: DAP pause skips the thread-id 0-sentinel resolution the sibling ops apply
description: src/dap/session.rs:970: pause builds PauseArgs{thread_id} verbatim, skipping resolve_thread_id which continue_ (747), step (899) and stack_trace (1002) all apply.
