The debug tool defaults pause to thread 0 (agent/tools/debug.rs:439) and the Janet bridge hardcodes 0; strict adapters (debugpy) reject threadId 0 with 'thread not found', so pausing a free-running debuggee fails whenever the caller omits the id. Fix: resolve_thread_id(thread_id) before building PauseArgs.

title: JSON-RPC frame decoder reads headers with unbounded read_line (OOM)
description: src/jsonrpc_framing.rs:53-84: decode_frame caps the body at 16MB (MAX_BODY_BYTES) but reads header lines with unbounded read_line and accepts unlimited header lines. A misconfigured adapter command (or a stream desynced per the partial-frame bug) emitting bytes with no newline grows line without bound -> OOM. The same class was already fixed for MCP stderr with an explicit OOM comment (extras/mcp/client.rs:205). Fix: take()-limited reads or a capped line reader; error past ~8KB of header.
close_reason: e5iw: decode_frame now reads headers with a bounded read_header_line (8KiB/line, 64KiB total) instead of unbounded read_line, erroring InvalidData on a never-terminating or flooded header block. dxpn: reverse-request ack in the read loop's dispatch is now wrapped in WRITE_TIMEOUT like every other write, so a full-stdin peer can't wedge the read loop permanently.

title: JSON-RPC reverse-request ack written inside read loop with no write timeout (deadlock)
description: src/jsonrpc_client.rs:292-297: the ReverseRequest ack is written from the shared read loop with no WRITE_TIMEOUT, defeating the module's own wedged-peer protection (header 32-41). rust-analyzer routinely sends server->client requests (workspace/configuration, workDoneProgress/create). If the server floods stdout while its stdin pipe is full, the read loop blocks in encode_frame holding the writer mutex; since reading stopped, the server can never drain stdin -> mutual pipe deadlock, connection wedges permanently without being marked closed, concurrent request() callers eat 10s writer-lock timeouts. Fix: wrap the ack in WRITE_TIMEOUT, or offload acks to a separate writer task.
close_reason: e5iw: decode_frame now reads headers with a bounded read_header_line (8KiB/line, 64KiB total) instead of unbounded read_line, erroring InvalidData on a never-terminating or flooded header block. dxpn: reverse-request ack in the read loop's dispatch is now wrapped in WRITE_TIMEOUT like every other write, so a full-stdin peer can't wedge the read loop permanently.

title: Child process spawn+kill is done four ways; LSP and MCP orphan their grandchildren
description: DAP does setsid + process-group SIGKILL guard + kill_on_drop (src/dap/client.rs:199-218,273-287, an admitted copy of bash PgKillGuard exec.rs:30-46). LSP spawns with only kill_on_drop (src/lsp/spawn.rs:202): no setsid, no group kill, no shutdown/exit handshake. MCP command servers rely on rmcp TokioChildProcess drop, direct-child only (extras/mcp/client.rs:152). typescript-language-server SIGKILLed on session end orphans its tsserver node child; same for jdtls->java, pyright->node worker; repeated sessions accumulate orphans. LSP servers also keep dirge's controlling terminal (no setsid), the TUI-corruption hazard the DAP comment documents. Fix: one shared spawn helper (setsid+PgKillGuard+kill_on_drop+stderr drain) for DAP/LSP/MCP; send LSP shutdown/exit before killing.
close_reason: Orphan-reaping core done via new src/child_guard.rs (shared detach_session setsid + ProcessGroupGuard killpg-on-drop). LSP (spawn.rs, guard bundled in Spawned.guard), MCP (client.rs, guard bundled in running_service Option so it drops with the RunningService on reconnect/shutdown), and DAP (migrated off its duplicate DapProcessGuard/inline setsid) all now isolate their child in its own session (no TTY-corruption) and SIGKILL the whole process group on teardown. Verified by child_guard::guard_group_kill_reaps_the_child_on_drop (real spawn+group-kill). LSP graceful shutdown/exit handshake split to dirge-8m69 (async-teardown plumbing, separable).

title: Snapshot dedup pool leaks every unique pre-edit content for process lifetime
description: src/agent/tools/snapshots.rs:84,104-125: intern stores an Arc clone in store.pool and nothing removes pool entries except clear() (/clear). The begin_turn eviction comment ('pool entries they alone referenced drop') is false: evicting a TurnBucket drops the bucket Arc but the pool map still holds one, refcount never hits zero. A long session editing many files accumulates every unique pre-edit content (up to 8MiB each) forever; MAX_TURNS caps buckets not the pool. Fix: prune pool entries with Arc::strong_count==1 on eviction, or key with Weak.

title: Bash tool silently drops all stream output after the first non-UTF-8 byte
description: src/agent/tools/bash/exec.rs:196,206 (and 384,388 spawn_streaming_shell): read_line errors are treated as EOF (Err(_) => so=None). read_line fails InvalidData on non-UTF-8. cat a binary, git log with a latin-1 commit message, or any non-UTF-8 output -> everything from the first invalid byte on is silently dropped, no marker, exit 0. The model reasons from plausible partial output. Fix: read raw bytes (read_until) + lossy-decode, or append an [invalid UTF-8] marker on Err.
close_reason: Both fixed in one shared byte-based capped reader (read_chunk_capped) replacing read_line in both bash drain loops. do5l: raw bytes + lossy decode, no more InvalidData-as-EOF dropping output after a non-UTF-8 byte. yw3o: per-line 16KiB ceiling bounds a single newline-free line so the 256KiB drain cap engages instead of OOMing.

title: Bash drain cap does not bound a single newline-free line (OOM)
description: src/agent/tools/bash/exec.rs:165-215: the TOOL-7 drain cap bounds merged but read_line buffers a whole line into so_buf before the cap check. base64 -w0 big.bin or tr -d '\n' < huge.log yields one multi-GB line -> the 256KiB cap never engages and the process OOMs, the exact outcome TOOL-7 exists to prevent. read.rs already caps per-line at MAX_LINE_BYTES. Fix: length-limited take() reader or chunked read_until with a per-line ceiling.
close_reason: Both fixed in one shared byte-based capped reader (read_chunk_capped) replacing read_line in both bash drain loops. do5l: raw bytes + lossy decode, no more InvalidData-as-EOF dropping output after a non-UTF-8 byte. yw3o: per-line 16KiB ceiling bounds a single newline-free line so the 256KiB drain cap engages instead of OOMing.

title: Input box unreadable on light terminal theme (#628)
description: On a light terminal or a theme with a light/Reset background, the composer's white input text has no dark surface behind it and becomes invisible. Give the input box its own dark 'input_bg' surface painted over the global background fill so it stays a dark field regardless of terminal theme.
notes: PR #629 opened (branch fix-input-box-light-terminal). Fix: input_bg theme field, painted over the background fill on the input box rect. Awaiting CI.
close_reason: Fixed via PR #629 (squash 819f4a06). Input box now paints its own dark input_bg surface.

title: Expand desktop notifications to Linux and Windows
description: PR #611 adds opt-in desktop notifications, macOS-only via notify-rust, with Linux/Windows no-op stubs. notify-rust is cross-platform (zbus on Linux, WinRT toast on Windows). Rebase #611 onto current main (0.19.2 + #623), implement the Linux and Windows backends via notify-rust, then cut a patch release. Fixes #594 fully.

title: dirge auth openai insufficient to launch: provider falls back to openrouter
description: Cli::resolve_provider only checks explicit --provider/config then env-var autodetect then hard-defaults to openrouter. It never consults stored dirge auth OAuth credentials, so a user who runs 'dirge auth openai' with no OPENAI_API_KEY env var and no provider in config is asked for an OpenRouter key at launch. Fix: consult stored OpenAI/Anthropic OAuth logins between env autodetect and the openrouter default. GH #617.

title: F3: query tty_size once per paint instead of ~8 /dev/tty opens
description: tty_size() (terminal.rs:300) does a fresh open('/dev/tty')+ioctl+close per call; tui_redraw invokes it ~8x per paint (visible_lines, left/right panel visibility, layout). Winsize only changes on resize, so 7/8 are redundant -> ~1k /dev/tty opens/sec while streaming. Fix: query tty_size once at the top of tui_redraw and thread (cols,rows) into visible_lines/panel-visibility/layout (layout probe already takes cols/rows).
close_reason: Merged in PR #609: terminal size cached on Renderer; refreshed at tui_redraw top + rebuild; ~8 /dev/tty opens per paint down to 1.

title: F2: throttle per-token ambient panel recompute in the render loop
description: The main run_interactive loop (mod.rs:1299) re-runs build_panel_data + set_left_panel_info at loop top on EVERY iteration; during streaming the loop wakes per token (50-200/s), so ambient panel/left-panel data (already commented 'fine for ambient status') is recomputed per token even when unchanged. The paint is coalesced but the recompute isn't. Fix: throttle the loop-top ambient panel recompute to a modest interval (~100ms), and if the input editor exposes a cheap revision/dirty signal, skip cache_bottom's editor re-wrap when unchanged. Must not stale panels noticeably or lag typing.
close_reason: Merged in PR #610: ambient panel recompute throttled to 100ms; per-token loop-top rebuild eliminated.

title: Auto-repair trailing over-close (extra closers) + steer model off bash paren-counting
description: Model loops hand-counting parens on a Clojure file (writes via bash heredoc + python count, bypassing write/edit). dirge already localizes imbalance precisely via tree-sitter+delimiter_balance (syntax_validator.rs) and auto-appends missing closers, but repair_delimiters is UNCLOSED-only — a trailing over-close (extra )) is rejected, not fixed. (1) Extend repair_delimiters to mechanically remove an unambiguous TRAILING run of extra closers (symmetric with unclosed-append; keep rejecting a mid-file stray). (2) Steering nudge (prompts/steering/*) telling the model to use write/edit for source (they validate+auto-repair+localize) not bash heredocs + hand paren counting.
close_reason: Merged in PR #604: trailing over-close auto-repair (repair_overclose) + steering nudge to use write/edit not bash paren-counting.

title: Audit-open finalization gate wired to the existing issue system
description: Add a finalization gate that blocks/warns on open issues via IssueStore::open_count() (issue_db.rs:398, COUNT status NOT IN done/cancelled). MUST reuse the existing issue system (issue.rs/issue_db.rs), NOT a parallel tracker. Tri-state off/advisory/blocking; plugs into gate-as-data (dirge-<gate>).
close_reason: Merged in PR #602: GateMode primitive + session-scoped opt-in open-issues finalization gate (default off, bounded).

title: Critic abstention: distinguish insufficient-spec/evidence from incomplete (parse_verdict 3rd state)
description: critic.rs parse_verdict (critic.rs:201) is 2-way (INCOMPLETE vs else). Add ABSTAIN verdict (richer return enum), route to a distinct nudge that asks for a held-out test / spec clarification instead of 'not done'. Update CRITIC_FORMAT/PREAMBLE token. GSD honest-verifier: exogenous tag → abstain drops confident-false-pass 100%->17%.
close_reason: Merged in PR #601: critic ABSTAIN verdict routes unverifiable-from-spec work to a test-request nudge instead of false-passing or nagging.

title: Gate-as-data: generalize finalization gates to a uniform mode+outcome (off/advisory/blocking)
description: poll_finalization_follow_up (run.rs:251) is a hardcoded priority chain; only code-review has tri-state (CodeReviewMode types.rs:159, ReviewReaction run.rs:2177). Generalize into a shared GateMode + GateOutcome so every gate's off/advisory/blocking is a config field, not bespoke code. Behavior-preserving for existing gates.
close_reason: Merged in PR #602: GateMode primitive + session-scoped opt-in open-issues finalization gate (default off, bounded).

title: Consolidate per-provider reasoning wire tuning into a provider adapter module
description: Reasoning tuning is split across build_provider_additional_params (rig_stream_factory.rs, enable shape) and reasoning_disable_for_kind (summarize.rs, disable shape) with two conventions — tuning a provider means editing both files. Introduce src/provider/adapter.rs with a ReasoningProfile { effort: EffortWire, disable: DisableWire } and reasoning_profile(Option<&str>) single source of truth; both call sites delegate. Behavior-preserving; move the thinking_level_to_*_effort + budget_for_level helpers into the adapter. First concrete piece of a general provider-adapter layer (endpoint/capability facts can fold in later).
close_reason: Merged in PR #599: src/provider/adapter.rs consolidates reasoning wire tuning; both call sites delegate; behavior-preserving.

title: DeepSeek: reasoning_effort uses nested shape + can't reach 'max' tier
description: build_provider_additional_params (rig_stream_factory.rs) sends nested {reasoning:{effort}} for deepseek and caps Xhigh->high. Verified: top-level reasoning_effort meaningfully controls deepseek depth (low mean 384 vs max mean 731 reasoning tokens, non-overlapping); deepseek accepts 'max' (OpenAI rejects it). Add deepseek-specific effort mapping reaching 'max' at Xhigh, and (pending nested-vs-top test) possibly switch deepseek to top-level reasoning_effort to match the official client. DeepSeek-specific.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #598 (merged): deepseek/glm disable-thinking uses thinking:{type:disabled}; deepseek effort switched to top-level reasoning_effort with max tier.

title: Review modes deny all bash, blocking read-only git inspection
description: Review mode (prompts/review.md deny_tools:[bash]) terminally denies the whole bash tool at PromptDenyPolicy precedence #1, so read-only 'git diff'/'git log' can't run and there's no way to inspect the diff. Also, read-only git prompts in every session because Operation::Execute is never builtin-allowed. Fix: (1) add a conservative read-only-git allow to BuiltinAllowPolicy (git diff/log/status/show/blame/... on a non-complex single segment, rejecting shell metachars, code-executing git global flags like -c, and write flags like --output) so observation is transparent like Operation::Read; (2) drop 'bash' from review.md deny_tools so effectful bash hits the Ask path (human/approval_provider) instead of a hard wall. Plan mode's hard deny is unchanged.
notes: Root cause: review.md / review-security.md deny_tools include 'bash', which PromptDenyPolicy (#1) enforces as a terminal whole-tool Deny — pre-empting the existing default_bash_rules (#5) that already auto-allow git status/log/diff/show and read-only inspection tools. Fix is minimal: drop 'bash' from both review prompts' deny_tools. No engine change needed (initial BuiltinAllow read-only-git idea was redundant with default_bash_rules and reverted). Effectful bash in review still governed by the Ask path (default rules + approval_provider); plan mode keeps its full bash deny. Caveat: default_bash_rules also allow git add/commit + cargo/make/test runners in review mode now — tightening that to strictly read-only needs sub-tool granularity (not built).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #596 (merged), released in 0.18.6: review/review-security no longer deny bash, so read-only git inspection works; effectful commands still prompt.

title: Editor follow-along: open files dirge reads/edits in an external editor (issue #572)
description: GH #572 (ericschmar): let the user's editor follow along with the files dirge accesses. Adaptation of the external-editor feature (#592): opt-in config editor_open_command (template with {path}/{line}); when set, dirge launches it DETACHED (non-blocking, opens in Zed/VS Code/etc. window) each time it reads/edits a file, passing the resolved abs path + line. Like Zed's AI panel. Terminal/editor-agnostic, no click/OSC8 dependency. Interactive TUI only. Dedup consecutive identical launches.
close_reason: Opt-in editor follow-along (GH #572): editor_open_command config template ({path}/{line}); on each read/edit tool call dirge resolves the abs path and launches the command detached (setsid, null stdio, reaped in a thread so no zombies), deduped against the last launch. Pure helpers build_editor_open_argv + file_target_for_tool (22 unit tests). Best-effort, opt-in, TUI-only. Implemented via dirge delegation; reviewed (added child-reaping), fmt/clippy/635 UI tests pass.

title: Agent halts mid-work after an unresolved tool failure; needs manual user nudge
description: dirge's loop re-prompts only while the model keeps emitting tool calls. When the model ends a turn text-only (stop_reason=Stop) right after a tool call was rejected (tool_input_invalid / repair-exhausted) or otherwise errored, the run breaks and waits for the user. Every continuation nudge in poll_finalization_follow_up is conditional/one-shot (hook/verifier/critic/code-review/goal/todo), so a plain session has nothing to push the model to finish. The tool-result feedback path itself is sound (error result IS fed back, terminate=None, escalation falls back cleanly) — the gap is the absence of a general 'you stopped with an unresolved tool failure, finish it' nudge. Fix (#1, targeted+bounded): add a finalization-poll source that fires ONCE when the finalizing turn made no tool call AND the immediately-preceding tool-result group has >=1 is_error, nudging the model to complete/retry or state why it can't.
notes: Investigation: critic (run_critic) ALREADY covers case 2 (ambiguous 'is task complete' -> VERDICT COMPLETE/INCOMPLETE -> [critic] nudge), but gated on critic_provider, fires once/run, fail-open calibrated. GAP is case 1: a definitive failure-stop (tool call rejected/errored, model then stops text-only) is not deterministically caught. FIX = new deterministic finalization-poll source ordered BEFORE the critic: fires when the tail of new_messages is [ToolResult group with >=1 is_error][final Assistant with no tool calls], injects a bounded [resume]-tagged nudge to complete/retry the failed step or explain why not. Anti-loop: only fires when the error group is immediately before the stopped turn (false once the model replies text-only) + a counter cap. Always-on + deterministic (no LLM). Case 2 unchanged.
close_reason: Added a deterministic resume-after-failure nudge (case 1). Pure last_action_failed_and_stopped() helper fires when a tool-error group immediately precedes a stopped (no-tool-call) Assistant turn; new bounded (MAX_RESUME_NUDGE=3) finalization source ordered after hook/before verifier injects a [resume] nudge to complete or explain. Anti-loop: stops matching once the model replies text-only. Case 2 (ambiguous completeness) unchanged — stays the LLM critic's job. Implemented via dirge delegation; reviewed, escalation integration test updated for the intended extra continuation, fmt/clippy/608 agent_loop tests pass.

title: Mouse/selection break: recover terminal dropped to main screen (Ctrl+L full re-assert)
Code-review controls: off/advisory/blocking mode, advisory runs in background
Code reviewer: add off/advisory/blocking config, default advisory, run advisory in background
description: The diff-aware code reviewer runs synchronously at finalization (git diff + LLM review, both awaited), and high/critical re-enter the loop — disruptive in tight debug loops. Add a code_review config (off/advisory/blocking, default advisory) + prompt-level override; advisory runs in the background (non-blocking notices, no re-entry); off skips the diff+review entirely; blocking keeps today's synchronous re-entry.
close_reason: Resolved by PR #577 (dirge-ovjk): resolve_codex_default maps the non-explicit openai default (gpt-4o) to the Codex default (gpt-5.5) on a codex client; explicit --model is preserved. Stored as session.model at startup.

title: Typing while scrolled up in chat yanks the view to the bottom
description: When the main chat log is scrolled up, typing a character in the input snapped the view back to the newest content (scroll_snap_for -> ScrollSnap::TypeThrough), so you couldn't read older messages while composing. Fix: typing no longer snaps; Down (when scrolled up) remains the deliberate jump-to-end key.

title: ChatGPT/Codex OAuth bearer frozen at client build; no mid-session refresh
description: Companion to dirge-956a (which fixed the Anthropic OAuth transport). The ChatGPT/Codex path (ProviderKind::OpenAI with is_chatgpt_auth) bakes the bearer into a static HeaderMap via chatgpt_http_headers at client build (client.rs ~295) and uses CodexHttpClient, so a long session crossing token expiry dies the same non-retryable way. Give CodexHttpClient the same RefreshableToken/refresh-seam treatment (load_fresh_openai_oauth already refreshes-if-expired, so wrap it as the refresher). Kept separate from 956a because it's a different transport (static HeaderMap vs per-request header build) and conflating them widens auth-flow blast radius.
close_reason: Fixed in R1 security cluster (audit-p2-r1-security branch): https-only guard extended to Anthropic OAuth; independent OAuth state vs PKCE verifier; advisory file lock around OAuth refresh; ChatGPT/Codex mid-session bearer refresh.

title: Test-gap: fold->persist->resume seam, code-review poll branch, config precedence, plugin timeout, skill lifecycle
description: Concentrated coverage holes where verified bugs live: (1) no fold->persist->resume integration test (dirge-g1ze/-4kgk/-f67u); (2) FollowUpSource::CodeReview poll branch untested - react counter, advisory emit, blocking path, diff-precondition skip (dirge-1g3v); (3) no resolve_mode CLI-vs-config precedence test + config tests gated on lsp feature (dirge-rb3f); (4) no plugin HOOK_TIMEOUT fail-open / two-plugin-load tests (dirge-hwzs/-awwr); (5) no skill file<->DB lifecycle or restore-under-pressure tests (dirge-rdif/-0cln/-d04r); (6) no malformed-frame-mid-session or child-death-while-pending tests (dirge-syom); (7) no CRLF new_text or wide-char wrap tests (dirge-do90/-p054). These land with their respective fix issues (TDD). Round: cross-cutting.
close_reason: seams 1 (fold/persist/resume) + 3 (resolve_mode precedence) landed; remaining 5 seams tracked in dirge-8gdv

title: MAX_REVIEW_REACT counts wrong thing: advisory reviews burn budget, clean reviews uncounted
description: code_review_reacts increments whenever findings are non-empty incl. advisory-only reviews that finalize -> 3 medium/low-only reviews exhaust the fix-loop budget without any blocking engagement, and the same advisories re-emit as duplicate SystemNotices each finalization. Conversely a zero-finding review costs 2 judge calls and doesn't count, so goal-gate re-entries can drive ~24 uncounted judge calls on a dirty tree. Fix: increment only when a blocking follow-up is returned; dedupe repeat advisory notices. File: src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:275. Round R5.
close_reason: Fixed on audit-p2-r5-gates: decide_review_reaction counts only blocking engagements against MAX_REVIEW_REACT and dedupes advisory notices per run. Unit tests in run_tests.rs.

title: TUI keystroke-path plugin dispatch takes a blocking lock the loop-top avoids
description: Loop-top drains use try_lock (H-R1: a long plugin tool holding the mutex must not freeze the UI), but the plugin-shortcut handler and on-prompt submission do lock_ignore_poison + a synchronous Janet eval on the UI event-loop task -> pressing a plugin-bound key or submitting a prompt while a plugin tool holds the manager mutex freezes the UI (no repaint, no Ctrl+C) until it returns. Fix: try_lock with a 'plugin busy' line, or move invoke_command/dispatch onto spawn_blocking with a select! arm. File: src/ui/mod.rs:2197,2707. Round R4.
close_reason: Plugin-shortcut handler uses new try_lock_ignore_poison + 'busy' line instead of a blocking lock, matching loop-top H-R1 try_lock. src/sync_util.rs + src/ui/mod.rs. (on-prompt path already off-loop via dirge-qhfk.)

title: TUI: no panic hook; UI-thread panic ends session silently into redirected log
description: panic=unwind so TerminalGuard::drop restores the terminal, but the default panic hook prints to stderr which is redirected to the log file during the session -> any UI-thread panic makes the TUI vanish with nothing shown; user has no idea it crashed or where. Fix: install a panic hook at startup that does the terminal reset then writes the panic message + log-file path to /dev/tty before delegating. File: src/ui/terminal.rs:238. Round R8.
close_reason: Panic hook installed in TerminalGuard::new: resets terminal + writes panic message and log path to /dev/tty, then chains the default hook. Fix in src/ui/terminal.rs.

title: TUI markdown chat wrapping counts chars not display width (CJK/emoji overflow)
description: word_wrap budgets by visible char count, so a CJK/emoji line paints 2 cols but counts 1 -> a 120-char CJK line is accepted as fitting 120 cols, clipped mid-content or colliding with the right border. Same file already fixed this for tables (uses UnicodeWidthStr) and wrap.rs is width-aware; paragraph wrapping is the one width-blind path. Fix: accumulate UnicodeWidthChar::width in word_wrap's budget loop, keep byte-offset mapping for ANSI passthrough. File: src/ui/markdown.rs:71-114. Round R8.
close_reason: word_wrap now accumulates UnicodeWidthChar cell width instead of char count; CJK/emoji lines wrap by display width. src/ui/markdown.rs.

title: Refactor: replace DEFER_* string-typed slash control flow with a typed enum
description: handle_slash signals deferred work via anyhow error-string prefixes parsed by the caller (DEFER_COMPRESS/WT_MERGE/WT_EXIT/etc, 5 protocols); fragile colon-splitn parsing, no exhaustiveness, real errors starting with a prefix misroute. Fix: return Result<SlashOutcome> where SlashOutcome is an enum (Handled, DeferCompress{...}, DeferWtMerge{branch,target,main,wt}, ...); the ui/mod match becomes typed and total. Closes dirge-oiyf too. Files: src/ui/mod.rs:2422, slash/cmd/*. Round R8.

title: Refactor: dedupe judge-gate rules-truncation and fail-open shapes
description: critic/goal/code_review each re-implement rules-block truncation (3 MAX_RULES_CHARS) with divergent predicates - critic/code_review compare rules.len() (bytes) then chars().take(N), so multibyte-heavy rules append '(truncated)' with nothing removed; goal uses chars().count() correctly. Each also re-implements the fail-open match judge(prompt).await + tag-prefixed LoopMessage::User shape. Fix: one truncate_rules(rules,max) helper + a shared run_judge fail-open wrapper. Files: critic.rs:80, goal.rs:60, code_review.rs:783. Round R8.

title: Refactor: extract shared JSON-RPC correlation layer for LSP+DAP
description: Framing is unified in jsonrpc_framing.rs but the correlation layer above it (pending map, AtomicU64 id counter, closed flag, writer mutex, read loop) is re-duplicated as two ~200-line stacks whose read loops carry the identical missed-cleanup bug (dirge-syom). Fix: extract a generic correlation client parameterized by the protocol's message-classification fn; LSP and DAP become thin adapters. Files: src/lsp/rpc.rs:50, src/dap/client.rs. Round R7.

title: Refactor: route all slash agent-rebuilds through the existing rebuild_agent helper
description: The ~20-line build_agent rebuild block is inlined at 6+ sites (cd, toggle, worktree, sessions, model, slash/mod, ui/mod wt-exit) despite rebuild_agent already existing and used by /prompt and /regen-prompts. This exact duplication caused the historical C8 bug (lsp_manager dropped at some sites). Fix: route all SlashCtx rebuilds through rebuild_agent; add a non-ctx variant for the ui/mod wt-exit site. File: src/ui/slash/cmd/agent/mod.rs:11. Round R8.

title: Refactor: consolidate the 5-way copy-pasted file-mutation pipeline
description: read-gate -> size cap -> CRLF normalize -> syntax_gate -> snapshot -> atomic_write -> mark_modified -> cache.clear+mark_read is hand-copied across edit/edit_lines/apply_patch/edit_minified/write, and the copies have drifted - every tools-layer bug found (CRLF doubling, missing snapshot, lossy decode, mixed endings) is a divergence between siblings. Fix: extract mutate_file(path, old_bytes, new_content, cache) owning normalize/gate/snapshot/write/cache so a new mutator can't skip a step. Files: src/agent/tools/{edit,edit_lines,apply_patch,edit_minified,write}.rs. Round R3.
close_reason: extracted src/agent/tools/text_io.rs (decode_for_edit + SourceText::reencode) owning the decode/normalize/BOM/line-ending seam; edit, edit_lines, apply_patch update all route through it

title: Doc-drift: harness/log documented as writing to log file but is a no-op
description: plugins.md:95 and :349 say harness/log 'Writes to dirge's log file (visible with --verbose)' and recommend it for debugging, but worker.rs:127 is (defn harness/log [msg] nil) - every call discarded, the documented debugging workflow silently does nothing. Fix: restore behavior (queue drained into tracing::info!) or fix both doc sections. Files: src/plugin/worker.rs:127, docs/plugins.md:95,349. Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: tool-input-repair.md documents wrong relational key names (silent no-op)
description: Doc example uses { fields:[...], default:{...} } but the code reads 'requires' and 'defaults' (semantic.rs:45,64) - a tool author following the doc gets a silently ignored hint. Also MdLinkUnwrapped additionally unwraps the 'text is a path suffix of the URL' case, undocumented. Fix: correct key names to requires/defaults and document the suffix-unwrap case. Files: docs/tool-input-repair.md:74,41. Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: mcp-server.md misdescribes session storage and promises unavailable resume
description: Doc says 'dirge's session lives in that project's .dirge/' - only the pointer file does; sessions live in the global user data dir. list_sessions calls find_recent_sessions(20) = all projects, not 'in this project'. Description says 'resume by passing its id' but neither delegate nor new_session accepts a session id. Fix: add a session_id param + filter list_sessions by project, or correct the doc and tool descriptions. Files: docs/mcp-server.md:30,83, src/extras/mcp_server.rs:227. Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: DAP config section documents a system that doesn't exist; count/table drift
description: docs/dap.md:377 documents per-adapter overrides via a 'dap' config.json key, but dap/config.rs only parses compile-time include_str!(defaults.json) and no dap config key exists -> silently ignored. Also '12 DAP functions' vs 14 registered/16 wrappers, dap/launch-module missing from the FFI table, dangling empty duplicate '## Full worked example (Python)' heading at :515. Fix: implement the dap config key (merge over defaults) or delete the section; fix the count/table. File: docs/dap.md:377,299,515. Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: -r/--resume claims picker but loads most-recent; stale-resume warnings skip -c/-r
description: (1) -r/--resume prints 10 recent sessions to stderr then unconditionally next() takes the first with no input read -> behaves like -c plus noise. (2) warn_on_stale_resume (cwd mismatch, >24h) is only called on the --session path, not -c/-r, so 'dirge -c' in a different dir or resuming a week-old session silently proceeds. Fix: implement the picker or fix -r help text; call warn_on_stale_resume after all three resume paths. File: src/main.rs:675,759. Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: config.md merge/keybindings/context_window/env-toggle/undocumented keys
description: docs/config.md drifts: (1) keybindings documented as key-by-key merge but it's a Vec replaced wholesale (a project entry wipes global); (2) context_window 'Default 128000' omits the per-model table that usually decides (e.g. sonnet=1M); (3) WEBSEARCH/WEBFETCH_ENABLED env vars OR-override an explicit config false; (4) undocumented parsed keys tools.{bash,webfetch,task}_output_inline_max_bytes and top-level auth. Fix: correct merge/default/precedence docs and add the missing key rows. Files: docs/config.md:12,90,113. Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: permissions.md precedence/default/restrictive semantics wrong
description: Three drifts in docs/permissions.md: (1) * fallback default documented as 'allow' but code default is Ask (engine/build.rs:193); (2) precedence table omits the terminal ConfiguredDenyPolicy that sits between yolo and session-allow (dirge-ct16); (3) Restrictive documented as gating every write but explicit configured allow rules survive it (ConfiguredRulePolicy is never demoted). Fix: correct the default, add configured-deny to the precedence table, reword Restrictive to 'unmatched/builtin-allowed writes Ask; explicit allow rules still allow'. Files: docs/permissions.md:49,139,388. Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: storm window documented 32, implementation is 6
description: features.md:72 says 'within a 32-call window' but StormBreaker::default and the custom-config path both use window_size=6. Fix: correct the doc to 6, or lift the value into a named constant shared by both construction sites. Files: docs/features.md:72, src/agent/agent_loop/storm.rs:306, run.rs:127. Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: retry policy documented 3 retries/4s, code is 5 retries/16s
description: features.md:100 says '1s->2s->4s, max 3 retries' but RecoveryPolicy::default max_retries=5 (schedule to 16s, tests pin 6 attempts). The recovery.rs:63 comment also still says 3. Flagged independently by 3 reviewers. Fix: update features.md and the recovery.rs comment to 5 retries / 1-2-4-8-16s. Files: docs/features.md:100, src/agent/recovery.rs:63. Round R8.

title: harness/mutate-input rewrites tool args after user permission approval (undocumented)
description: on-tool-start runs after permission checks and applies mutate_input as the final args the tool executes with -> user approves bash command X, plugin substitutes Y, Y runs without re-approval. Plugins run arbitrary code by design so not a sandbox escape, but the containment docs never state mutation bypasses the just-granted approval. Fix: re-run permission evaluation on mutated args, or document that mutation happens post-approval. Files: src/agent/agent_loop/plugin_hooks.rs:155, docs/plugins.md:72. Round R8.

title: Graceful AbortSignal::cancel not observed during retry backoff
description: On a retryable error the wrapper does tokio::time::sleep(backoff).await and only then checks is_cancelled(). backoff honors provider Retry-After capped at 5min, so a cooperative-only cancel (no task.abort()) stalls up to that long. Interactive UI pairs cancel with JoinHandle::abort so masked, but consumers relying on the signal alone (as the module doc suggests) wait out the sleep. Fix: race the sleep against a cancellation future via tokio::select!. File: src/agent/agent_loop/retry.rs:213. Round R5.

title: Blocking SQLite open+query inline on the async loop task (issue-board reminder)
description: The issue-board reminder runs IssueStore::open_at + store.board_reminder (synchronous rusqlite I/O) directly in run_agent_loop on the executor thread, unlike the code-review gate which wraps its git shell-out in spawn_blocking. A contended/locked state.db stalls the whole loop task. Fix: move open+query into tokio::task::spawn_blocking. File: src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:976. Round R5.
close_reason: issue-board reminder open+query moved into spawn_blocking

title: Reasoning content duplicated when provider emits both ReasoningDelta and complete Reasoning
description: The complete-Reasoning arm unconditionally pushes a new Thinking block, unlike the ToolCall arm which dedupes against delta-built blocks (H-7 fix). A stream of ReasoningDelta... Reasoning(complete) yields two Thinking blocks with the same text -> doubled transcript thinking + scavenge source. Fix: mirror H-7: if current_thinking_idx is Some, replace that block with the complete payload instead of pushing a second. File: src/agent/agent_loop/rig_stream.rs:272. Round R5.
close_reason: complete-Reasoning arm replaces the delta-built Thinking block in place, mirroring H-7

title: Discovery 'anchored window around match' is actually anchored at session's first message
description: search_messages never selects m.id, so find_message_id_near reconstructs the anchor via get_anchored_view(session,1,0) which returns exactly the session's first message regardless of timestamp -> every discovery hit's window/anchor_index describes the session opening, not the FTS match. Also get_anchored_view's anchor_row.saturating_sub(1) wraps -1 to usize::MAX for foreign anchor ids. Fix: return m.id from the FTS join and pass it as the anchor; clamp anchor_row-1 at zero before cast. Files: src/extras/session_search.rs:359, session_db.rs:1288,1582. Round R2.
close_reason: search_messages returns m.id; discovery anchors on the match; anchor_row-1 clamped at 0 before usize cast

title: get_session_meta only finds metadata for the 50 most-recent sessions
description: Discovery-hit metadata looked up by scanning list_sessions_rich(None) = LIMIT 50 ORDER BY last_active DESC. An FTS hit in an older session gets empty source/model/title/started_at silently (Ok with blanks). Also does a throwaway get_anchored_view probe just to 404-check. Fix: direct SELECT source,model,title,started_at FROM sessions WHERE id=?1. Files: src/extras/session_search.rs:344, session_db.rs:1243. Round R2.
close_reason: Direct SELECT source,model,title,started_at WHERE id=?1; discovery meta no longer blanks out for sessions past the recent 50

title: Multi-line harness/add-steering/add-followup splits into multiple user messages
description: Unlike add-custom-message and every other registry (which use harness/-escape), the steering/followup queues append content unescaped into a newline-delimited blob. (harness/add-steering 'step 1\nstep 2') arrives at the loop as two separate LoopMessage::User entries -> a formatted multi-line instruction is shredded. Fix: escape on append, unescape on drain, matching the custom-message wire format. File: src/plugin/worker.rs:287,296, mod.rs:547. Round R4.

title: Worker timed-out evals still execute later; late harness/block wiped by next pre-clear
description: eval_with_timeout enqueues Cmd::Eval and abandons only the reply on timeout; the command still runs later. After a fail-open timeout the next dispatch's pre-clear (set harness-block nil) is queued behind the blocked hook; when the user finally answers deny, the plugin's harness/block executes and is immediately erased by that queued pre-clear -> denial vanishes silently. No interrupt facility, so one (while true) hook permanently wedges all future hooks/tools/commands. Fix: tag each Eval with a generation, skip stale queued commands; longer-term wire janet interrupt/recreate worker after repeated timeouts. Files: src/plugin/worker.rs:1137, mod.rs:657. Round R4.
notes: Scope finding (deferred pass): confirmed two coupled problems on the single Janet worker thread (worker.rs worker_loop:1407-1418 runs client.run(&code) serially, no interrupt). (1) STALE SIDE-EFFECT: eval_with_timeout (worker.rs:1208) abandons only the reply on timeout; the Eval stays queued/running. A hook that blocks without a registered dialog (dialog_pending==0, e.g. while-true or a blocking syscall) times out fail-open at 5s, then its late harness/block lands and the NEXT dispatch's pre-clear (dispatch_tool_hook pre-clear, mod.rs) wipes it -> denial vanishes. (2) PERMANENT WEDGE: a (while true) hook never returns, so every subsequent Eval queues behind it forever -> all future hooks/tools/commands freeze. SECURITY-ADJACENT: this is the permission-gate (harness/block) path; a wrong fix can make the gate fail OPEN. Recommended design (needs supervision): (a) add a shared AtomicU64 generation; tag each Cmd::Eval with the generation at send; on host timeout bump it; worker skips/ignores side-effects from a stale-generation eval so a late block from an abandoned eval can't corrupt a newer generation. (b) For the wedge, Janet has no safe cross-thread fiber interrupt, so recreate the worker thread after N consecutive timeouts (kill+respawn+re-init, accepting plugin-state reset) rather than trying to interrupt. Do NOT fire-and-forget; pair with the user.

title: /graph search swallows --compress/--depth flags into the FTS5 query
description: Query built with take_while(s != --kind && s != --tier), so --compress (usage line) and --depth (parsed later) are included in the search string. '/graph search auth --compress' searches FTS5 for 'auth --compress' -> wrong results while compress mode still activates. Fix: extend the terminator set to all known flags or split flags from positionals in a shared parser. File: src/ui/slash/cmd/graph.rs:40. Round R8.
close_reason: positional_query helper terminates the /graph search query at the first --flag; --compress/--depth no longer leak into FTS5. Delegated to dirge, reviewed+verified (fmt fixed).

title: /compress always prints 'prompt cleared (back to default behavior)'
description: install_compaction unconditionally writes this line after rebuilding the agent, but nothing in the function clears the prompt layer -> users falsely told their active /prompt was reset on every compaction incl. automatic ones. Fix: delete the line or replace with an accurate 'agent rebuilt' note. File: src/ui/slash/mod.rs:453. Round R8.
close_reason: Removed misleading 'prompt cleared' line from install_compaction; compaction doesn't reset the prompt layer and the result is already reported below.

title: DAP attach stalls full timeout then records running debuggee as Stopped
description: attach waits timeout(30s) for a stopped event that usually never comes on a running process, racing the Janet bridge 30s DAP_CMD_TIMEOUT, then unconditionally sets status=Stopped -> debug panel and summaries misreport a running debuggee. Fix: set Running when no stopped event arrived; use a short grace wait (~1s) not the full request timeout. File: src/dap/session.rs:462. Round R7.
close_reason: attach waits a 1s grace (ATTACH_STOP_GRACE) not the full request timeout, and records Running when no stopped event arrives instead of a fabricated Stopped; split out attach_with_client for testing

title: DAP pause can never interrupt continue (active mutex held across stop-wait)
description: continue_ holds self.active.lock().await through its tokio::select! stop-wait; pause starts by acquiring self.active. Continue a free-running program then pause -> pause blocks on the mutex up to continue's 30s timeout, the one window where pause is meaningful. Fix: clone the client handle out of the lock before waiting for the stop event so other requests issue concurrently. File: src/dap/session.rs:571,707. Round R7.
close_reason: continue_ hands the event receivers off and releases the active lock before parking on the stop-wait, so a concurrent pause/snapshot no longer blocks for continue's full timeout

title: DAP stale queued stopped events satisfy later step/pause waits
description: stopped events accumulate in an unbounded channel drained only on demand; nothing clears pre-existing events before issuing next/pause. Multi-threaded debuggee where two threads stop -> the next step 'completes' instantly consuming the stale event; reported reason/thread belong to the previous stop, every subsequent op skewed by one. Fix: drain the stopped channel immediately before step/continue/pause (mirror drain_output/drain_termination). File: src/dap/session.rs:196. Round R7.
close_reason: drain_stopped() clears stale queued stopped events before each continue/step/pause, mirroring drain_output/drain_termination, so ops no longer complete on a previous stop

title: apply_patch failures return Ok('FAILED:...'), invisible to recovery machinery
description: Mid-batch failure pushes 'FAILED: {e}' into results and returns Ok(results.join). Not an error, so the consecutive-failure recovery checkpoint, repeat-loop guard, and critic transcript labeling (which key off errored tool results) never see apply_patch failures -> a model looping on a failing update op gets none of the documented recovery interventions. Fix: return Err when the batch ends in failure (keep per-op text as message), or when no op succeeded. File: src/agent/tools/apply_patch.rs:404. Round R3.
close_reason: apply_patch batch ending in failure now returns Err (keeps per-op text), so recovery checkpoint/repeat-guard/critic labeling see the failure

title: Relay summary points at a never-written file when the disk write fails
description: wrote_ok is tracked but format_summary is unconditionally handed &path, so on create_dir_all/fs::write failure the LLM-facing text still says 'Full output stored at ... Use the read tool', contradicting the doc comment ('summary with no path'). Model then burns turns reading a nonexistent file. Fix: when !wrote_ok omit the path sentence and say the full output could not be stored. File: src/agent/tools/output_relay.rs:286. Round R3.
close_reason: format_summary now takes Option<&Path>; on a transient-write failure relay_if_large passes None and the trailer says the output could not be stored instead of pointing the model at a nonexistent file

title: BOM files: edit_lines deterministically false-rejects any range including line 1
description: read strips U+FEFF from line 1 before hashing; edit_lines recomputes hashes from raw disk with only CRLF normalization, so line 1 still carries the BOM and its hash never matches what the model echoed -> any edit_lines touching line 1 of a BOM file rejected 'line(s) changed since you read them', unfixable model-side. Fix: strip a leading U+FEFF in edit_lines before hashing/splicing, re-prepend on write. Files: src/agent/tools/read.rs:347, edit_lines.rs:219. Round R3.
close_reason: decode_for_edit strips a leading BOM before hashing (matching read.rs) and re-prepends on write, so edit_lines on line 1 of a BOM file no longer false-rejects

title: Mixed-line-ending files wholesale rewritten to CRLF by edit/edit_lines/apply_patch
description: has_crlf = any CRLF anywhere; on write-back every \n becomes \r\n. A mixed-ending file (common after cross-platform edits) has all its LF-only lines flipped to CRLF incl. lines nowhere near the edit, despite comments claiming 'so we don't silently rewrite line endings'. Noisy unintended diffs. Fix: detect mixed endings and preserve per-line (splice bytes), or at least report the normalization. Files: edit.rs:179,324, edit_lines.rs:220, apply_patch.rs:130. Round R3.
close_reason: line-ending shape detected at decode; mixed-ending files normalize to the dominant ending and report it, instead of has_crlf=any flipping every LF to CRLF

title: edit/edit_lines write from_utf8_lossy output; pre-existing non-UTF-8 bytes -> U+FFFD
description: File decoded lossily, edit applied, whole lossy buffer written back -> invalid bytes far from the edit (latin-1 comment, or bytes appended externally after the session read) silently become replacement chars. Read gate mostly prevents but mark_read is session-lifetime, so read-then-externally-modified reaches it. Fix: detect from_utf8_lossy produced Cow::Owned and refuse with 'not valid UTF-8' instead of writing mangled bytes. Files: src/agent/tools/edit.rs:180,350, edit_lines.rs:221,252. Round R3.
close_reason: edit/edit_lines/apply_patch now decode via shared text_io::decode_for_edit which refuses non-UTF-8 instead of from_utf8_lossy mangling bytes to U+FFFD

title: /spec anchors store at process cwd while sibling commands use session.working_dir
description: spec.rs builds ProjectPaths from cwd; issues.rs/graph.rs/clear.rs use session.working_dir. swap_to_session replaces session.working_dir but never set_current_dir, so after /sessions <id> into a session saved elsewhere, /issues reads that project's state.db while /spec reads the cwd project's -> two commands over the 'same' session DB disagree. Fix: standardize all slash ProjectPaths on session.working_dir. File: src/ui/slash/cmd/spec.rs:11. Round R8.
close_reason: /spec now builds ProjectPaths from ctx.session.working_dir (via resolve_project_paths helper) like /issues, /graph, /clear — no longer anchors at process cwd, so commands over the same session agree

title: DEFER_* slash sentinels are colon-delimited and break on paths containing ':'
description: handle_slash signals deferred work via anyhow error-string prefixes; DEFER_WT_MERGE packs 5 fields (branch:target:main:wt:repo) parsed with splitn(5,':'), DEFER_WT_EXIT splitn(2,':'). A repo path with ':' (legal on macOS/Linux) shifts every field -> wrong branch/paths or truncated chdir; user-typed merge target unvalidated; on field-count mismatch both arms have no else -> silent no-op. Fix: replace the string protocol with a typed SlashOutcome enum stashed in UI state. Files: src/ui/slash/cmd/worktree.rs:131, ui/mod.rs:2529. Round R8.

title: /loop start <prompt> leaks the verb 'start' into the loop prompt
description: Dispatch requires parts[1]=='start' but the handler only strips '/loop' from raw text, so tokens include 'start' which isn't --max and lands in prompt_tokens. '/loop start fix the tests' -> LoopState prompt 'start fix the tests', corrupting every iteration. Fix: strip the start verb too (or build from parts[2..] with --max handling); add a unit test on the parsed prompt. File: src/ui/slash/cmd/loop_cmd/start.rs:15. Round R8.
close_reason: Strip leading 'start' dispatch verb before parsing /loop prompt; extracted testable parse_loop_start helper. Delegated to dirge mcp, reviewed+verified.

title: Invalid permission config block silently drops all rules instead of failing fast
description: RuleConfig is deny_unknown_fields, so one misspelled field makes the whole permission block fail to deserialize; build_channels prints a stderr warning (scrolls away under TUI) and continues with PermissionConfig::default(), discarding every rule incl. hard denies (rm ** deny, external_directory denies). Exactly the failure the config loader hard-exits for elsewhere. Fix: make an unparseable permission block a startup error, or refuse-to-run when the discarded config had deny rules. Files: src/main.rs:149, config/mod.rs:1028. Round R1.

title: Cross-process auth-store race clobbers rotated refresh token
description: save_openai/Anthropic save are unlocked load-modify-write. Two dirge processes with an expired credential both refresh with the same refresh token; OpenAI rotates on use, so the loser persists a now-invalid refresh token over the winner's fresh one -> next expiry fails refresh, forces re-login. Fix: advisory file lock (flock on auth.json) around load->refresh->save, re-check freshness after acquiring. Files: src/auth/store.rs:148, src/provider/client.rs:457, auth.rs:194. Round R5.
close_reason: Fixed in R1 security cluster (audit-p2-r1-security branch): https-only guard extended to Anthropic OAuth; independent OAuth state vs PKCE verifier; advisory file lock around OAuth refresh; ChatGPT/Codex mid-session bearer refresh.

title: Explicit --model gpt-4o silently rewritten to gpt-5.5 under Codex
description: codex_model_name replaces any name equal to default_model_for('openai') (='gpt-4o') with gpt-5.5, so it can't tell 'user never set a model' from 'user explicitly asked for gpt-4o' -> explicit choice overridden with no warning. Fix: resolve the Codex default earlier where explicit-vs-default is still known (CLI/config layer) instead of pattern-matching the default string at dispatch. Files: src/provider/dispatch.rs:134, resolve.rs:35. Round R5.
notes: Scope finding (deferred pass): far more invasive than framed. codex_model_name is load-bearing at ~40 runtime completion_model() sites — session.model is persisted as 'gpt-4o' for a default-codex session and every runtime rebuild (ui/mod.rs slash cmds, compaction, /model, run_handlers, resume, acp) relies on the central rewrite to remap gpt-4o->gpt-5.5. Removing/relocating the rewrite regresses EVERY codex user unless: (1) the resolved codex default is stored INTO session.model at startup (needs the built client for codex-detection, which is created after Session::new, plus context_window reconciliation), and (2) already-persisted old default-codex sessions are migrated on resume (they carry model='gpt-4o'). Recommend a dedicated scoped pass with the user, not a quick fix. Doing the other 4 deferred issues first.
close_reason: Resolved the Codex default once (resolve_model_name) with the explicit-vs-default signal and stored it as session.model; removed the in-completion_model remap so the ~40 sites read one correct name. Role clients + ACP resolve the same way. Fresh + resume + test harness covered.

title: PKCE code_verifier reused as OAuth state parameter
description: append_pair('state', &verifier): the PKCE secret is embedded in the authorize URL (terminal, browser history, URL-logging proxy) and echoed in the localhost redirect. An observer of either URL obtains code + verifier and can complete the exchange. state-mismatch check gives CSRF protection but not disclosure mitigation. Verify whether the endpoint requires state==verifier; if not use an independent random state kept only in memory + back-channel POST. File: src/provider/anthropic_oauth.rs:67. Round R5.
close_reason: Fixed in R1 security cluster (audit-p2-r1-security branch): https-only guard extended to Anthropic OAuth; independent OAuth state vs PKCE verifier; advisory file lock around OAuth refresh; ChatGPT/Codex mid-session bearer refresh.

title: Anthropic OAuth token may be sent over plaintext http
description: The https-only guard (token too sensitive for allow_insecure) is gated on is_chatgpt_auth||uses_openai_oauth only; the AnthropicOauth branch accepts any validated base_url. providers.anthropic={auth:anthropic, base_url:http://..., allow_insecure:true} sends sk-ant-oat bearer in Authorization over plaintext. Same argument the code makes for the Codex token. Fix: extend the https-only bail to also fire when auth==Anthropic. File: src/provider/client.rs:267. Round R5.
close_reason: Fixed in R1 security cluster (audit-p2-r1-security branch): https-only guard extended to Anthropic OAuth; independent OAuth state vs PKCE verifier; advisory file lock around OAuth refresh; ChatGPT/Codex mid-session bearer refresh.

title: Escalation stream route bypasses the retry wrapper
description: Default and review-fork routes wrap in retrying_stream_fn; the escalation StreamFn is threaded raw (build.rs:602 returns model.build_stream_fn, spawn.rs:202 clones unwrapped, stream.rs uses it directly). Escalation fires once after repair-exhaustion; a transient 503/rate-limit on that single call surfaces immediately, wasting the escalation the user paid a second provider for. Fix: wrap escalation StreamFn in retrying_stream_fn. Files: src/provider/build.rs:602, spawn.rs:202. Round R5.

title: File store and salience DB diverge on delete: ghost active rows re-enter curator
description: Tool delete removes the directory but leaves the skills row status='active' + FTS row. render_candidate_list builds from list_active filtered to source='learned' -> next curator pass handed a skill that no longer exists; its skill(load) fails mid-consolidation. Same when curator dir-move succeeds but the ignored s.archive() fails. Fix: tool delete arm also calls store.archive (best-effort, logged); log the curator's DB-archive error instead of discarding. Files: src/agent/tools/skill.rs:242, src/extras/skills/curator.rs:152. Round R6.

title: restore_entry can demote the eviction-exempt overview
description: restore_entry re-implements hot-tier compaction with bare least_salient_index, unlike insert_into_hot which never picks 'overview' and honors the working reserve. Small hot tier + restoring a large entry demotes the overview to breadcrumb (contradicts 'never an eviction victim'); a demoted overview then renders twice (project_overview block filters by kind, breadcrumb index by tier). Fix: restore_entry reuses insert_into_hot victim-selection; exclude kind='overview' from breadcrumb render. File: src/extras/memory_db.rs:1268. Round R2.
close_reason: restore_entry reuses make_room_in_hot (overview-exempt victim selection); breadcrumb render excludes kind=overview

title: Restored skills stay status=archived in DB forever
description: Recovery = moving the dir back out of .archive/. On next startup register_file_skill update branch refreshes description/content/updated_at but never status, so the row keeps status='archived' -> permanently excluded from list_active (preamble salience), invoke (AND status='active'), and search. Skill works only as an unranked file. Fix: register_file_skill update branch resets status='active' (directory presence is ground truth). File: src/extras/skill_db.rs:417. Round R6.
close_reason: register_file_skill UPDATE branch now resets status='active'; recovered skill dirs re-activate. Delegated to dirge, reviewed+verified.

title: Legacy MEMORY.md import can index FTS under wrong rowid; partial import strands rest
description: INSERT OR IGNORE + last_insert_rowid(): if an insert is ignored (dup uid), last_insert_rowid returns the previous rowid and the following INSERT INTO memories_fts attaches new text to the wrong row -> search returns mismatched content. Also no transaction: mid-loop error leaves earlier rows committed, COUNT>0 gate blocks re-import of the rest, files never parked to *.imported -> part of legacy memory stranded. Fix: one transaction + check execute row count (skip FTS when 0) instead of trusting last_insert_rowid after OR IGNORE. File: src/extras/memory_db.rs:2000. Round R2.

title: skill_db multi-step writes lack transactions (row + FTS)
description: create (INSERT skills then INSERT skills_fts) and register_file_skill (UPDATE/INSERT + DELETE fts + INSERT fts) run bare, unlike memory_db which wraps every row+FTS mutation in a txn. Failure between statements (crash, or SQLITE_BUSY past 5s while a post-session background writer holds state.db) leaves a skill row with no FTS row (unsearchable) or stale/duplicate FTS, no self-heal. Fix: conn.transaction() around create and register_file_skill. File: src/extras/skill_db.rs:238,417. Round R6.

title: insert_message runs 4 statements with no transaction; can corrupt external-content FTS
description: INSERT messages + messages_fts + messages_fts_trigram + UPDATE sessions run as separate autocommit statements. Crash after the first leaves a message row with no FTS projection (invisible, message_count drifts); later delete_session_messages issues the external-content FTS5 'delete' with a recomputed projection for a never-indexed rowid -> FTS5-documented index corruption. delete path already uses a txn; insert doesn't. Fix: wrap insert_message in unchecked_transaction(). File: src/extras/session_db.rs:1145. Round R2.

title: is_verification_command substring markers count git checkout/npm install as green
description: Markers matched with contains: 'check' matches 'git checkout main', 'npm' matches 'npm install', 'test' matches 'ls tests/'. After a code edit any of these succeeding sets ran_verification=true, verification_failed=false -> VerifiedGreen fabricated: verify nudge silenced and critic told a build/test passed when nothing was verified. Fix: tokenize (reuse tree-sitter bash segmenting) and match markers on command words, excluding checkout/install/add. File: src/agent/agent_loop/verifier.rs:181. Round R5.
close_reason: Fixed on audit-p2-r5-gates: anchored exit-code failure signal + tokenized verification-command match. Tests in verifier.rs.

title: Verifier failure signal 'Exit code:' is an unanchored substring scan
description: result_indicates_failure returns true if 'Exit code:' appears anywhere in tool text. A green 'make test' whose wrapper echoes 'Exit code: 0' marks the run VerifiedRed -> wrong FAILED_NUDGE and feeds the critic 'Don't pass a red build - INCOMPLETE', blocking a correct green run. Fix: anchor to the harness marker (parse N != 0 on the final line) or thread the real exit status. File: src/agent/agent_loop/verifier.rs:171. Round R5.
close_reason: Fixed on audit-p2-r5-gates: anchored exit-code failure signal + tokenized verification-command match. Tests in verifier.rs.

title: Verify pass can't drop a FALSE_POSITIVE-annotated (vs omitted) finding
description: Pass 2 asks for VERIFIED/FALSE_POSITIVE but re-parses with parse_findings which has no notion of those tokens. A common LLM shape (re-list every candidate with inline verdict, e.g. '- High - SQLi. FALSE_POSITIVE: not in diff') still carries a severity label -> parsed as a survivor and can wrongly BLOCK finalization. Only omission works; all tests use omit-style. Fix: discard blocks containing FALSE_POSITIVE before severity detection; add annotate-style test. File: src/agent/agent_loop/code_review.rs:832,896. Round R5.
close_reason: Fixed on audit-p2-r5-gates: verify_pass now discards FALSE_POSITIVE-annotated blocks before parse_findings; annotate-style tests added.

title: R7: Docs + site
description: Document the diff-aware code reviewer (docs/) and add a note to ../dirge-code.github.io. Cover: opt-in via critic_provider, severity gating, two-pass, /code-review.
acceptance_criteria: docs updated; site note added

title: R6: /code-review on-demand slash command
description: Slash command running the two-pass review on the current working-tree diff regardless of loop state; surfaces findings. Follow the DEFER_PROMPT_RUN or direct-judge pattern as appropriate. Register in slash/cmd + dispatch + slash_commands() list.
acceptance_criteria: /code-review runs two-pass on working-tree diff and surfaces findings; registered + listed

title: R5: Severity gate + bounded feedback
description: Critical/High verified findings -> blocking [code-review] follow-up injected into the loop (agent fixes or justifies), bounded by MAX_REVIEW_REACT (mirror MAX_GOAL_REACT). Medium/Low -> advisory render only (surfaced under a distinct channel, non-blocking). Wire counter through poll_finalization_follow_up.
acceptance_criteria: high/critical block + feed back with a bound; med/low advisory non-blocking; bound prevents loops; unit tests on the gate split

title: R4: Pass 2 verify/dedupe
description: Port roborev verify/dedupe preamble (review/synthesis.go:19-40). Second judge pass re-checks each finding against current code, marks VERIFIED/FALSE_POSITIVE, merges duplicates; drop false positives before surfacing/gating. Parsing pure + unit-tested.
acceptance_criteria: verify pass drops false positives and dedupes; parse tested; two-pass orchestration in run_code_review

title: R3: Pass 1 wired at finalization (CodeReviewGate)
description: CodeReviewGate reusing build_judge_fn + the critic_provider client (no new provider). Wired in provider/build.rs alongside critic; mode-scoped like critic (respect prompt critic:false / add code_review frontmatter later). At finalization, when code edited this run (verifier signal) and gate configured: capture diff, build review prompt (preamble+diff+transcript), call judge, parse findings. Slots into poll_finalization_follow_up near the critic as a SEPARATE gate.
acceptance_criteria: gate fires only when code edited + configured; produces parsed findings; off by default (no critic_provider); precedence documented in poll_finalization_follow_up

title: R2: Run-diff capture helper
description: Helper to compute the run's uncommitted diff (git diff HEAD + untracked files via --no-index), exclude-filtered (lockfiles/generated), size-capped with a truncation note (mirrors roborev max_prompt_size snapshot logic). Thin git shell-out (reuse git_worktree git() pattern); pure formatting/truncation/exclude logic unit-tested.
acceptance_criteria: diff formatting, truncation, and exclude filtering unit-tested; git invocation isolated behind a thin fn

title: R1: Finding model + ported verdict parser (pure)
description: New src/agent/agent_loop/code_review.rs. Port from ~/src/roborev: Severity{Critical,High,Medium,Low} with ordering; Finding{severity,location,harm,fix}; parse_findings() ported from internal/storage/verdict.go:51-305 (severity-label detection, --- splitting, legend/rubric skip); REVIEW_PREAMBLE + SECURITY_PREAMBLE ported verbatim from prompt/templates/default_review.md.gotmpl and default_security.md.gotmpl (evidence discipline, don't-report list, severity defs, toolchain-from-manifest). TDD, port roborev's verdict test cases. No loop wiring.
acceptance_criteria: parse_findings + severity ordering unit-tested with roborev's cases; preambles carry evidence discipline; module compiles, no wiring

title: Epic: diff-aware code reviewer (roborev-style) on the critic
description: Add a two-pass diff-aware code reviewer built on the critic's plumbing. Fires at finalization when a run edited code, reviews the actual git diff, verifies findings to drop false positives, then blocks on critical/high and surfaces medium/low as advisory. Also /review on demand. Ports prompt craft + finding/verdict model from ~/src/roborev (Go); none of its daemon/queue/sqlite infra. Decisions: finalization+/review trigger, severity-gated blocking, two-pass from the start.
notes: Design: REUSE the existing critic_provider judge client (no new provider config). The code reviewer is a sibling gate to the completeness-critic, built on the same CriticFn plumbing + build_judge_fn, but with its own preamble + a findings pipeline. Naming: use 'code_review' — the word 'review' is already taken by agent/review.rs (post-session memory/skill learning), review_provider/spawn_review_runner, the review/review-security prompt modes, and plan/workflow review. New module: src/agent/agent_loop/code_review.rs; tag [code-review]; distinct from [critic]/[verify-before-done].

title: R5: file-skill registration + curator archival
description: Replace curator.rs age-based active/stale/archived transitions with salience-driven archival (effective salience threshold; keep pinned + agent-created guards). Wire skill effectiveness into the review pass (record_outcome success/failure). Retire the .usage.json sidecar (one-time migration into the skills table, then stop writing it). TDD: salience archival threshold, review outcome recording.
close_reason: Curator now salience-driven; .usage.json retired; 50 curator + 24 learning-loop tests green

title: R4: /learn command (standards-guided prompt)
description: build_learn_prompt analog + authoring standards block. Live agent gathers sources with existing file/web tools, saves via skill create tool. Bare /learn distills current conversation. TDD: sources/requirements separation, empty fallback, save-via-tool.
close_reason: /learn command + build_learn_prompt; 5 prompt tests + 94 slash tests green

title: R2: skills table + migration + SkillStore
description: New skills table mirroring memories schema (uid,name,description,content,status,salience,confidence,timestamps,use_count,success/failure_count,last_success_at,superseded_*) + skills_fts. CRUD + salience wired via R1 module. TDD: insert/reinforce/decay/effectiveness/eviction mirror memory tests.
close_reason: skills table + SkillStore + salience reuse; 15 tests green, clippy clean

title: R3: skill tool + prompt injection
description: Turn SkillStore into a superset of the .usage.json UsageStore (add view/patch counts, created_by/agent-created, upsert-on-discovery) plus salience. Repoint SkillTool + preamble + loop_tools discovery to SkillStore: record view/use/create/patch, register file skills on discovery, order the preamble skill list by effective salience. Replace curator's UsageStore reads. TDD: store superset methods + ordering.
close_reason: SkillStore superset + hot-path cutover (tool+preamble), salience-ordered listing; 17 store tests + 139 skill tests green

title: R1: extract shared salience module
description: Extract salience fns (default_salience_for_kind, effective_salience, effectiveness_bonus, apply_disuse_decay, use-reinforcement) from memory_db.rs into src/extras/salience.rs. Memories keep passing. TDD: parity tests first.
close_reason: Extracted salience.rs; memory parity green (81 tests)

title: Epic: reusable skill learning with salience (DB-resident)
description: Adopt Hermes-style /learn: distill sources into reusable skills, stored as sqlite rows reusing the memory salience engine so the library self-prunes (decay + effectiveness + curator archival). Decisions: DB-resident content; include learn-time verification gate. See rounds R1-R6.
notes: REVISED after discovering dirge already ports the Hermes file-based skill system (SkillManager, SkillTool, .usage.json UsageStore, age-based curator, guard, preamble). Decision: salience SIDECAR, not DB-resident content. The R2 skills table replaces .usage.json — keyed by skill name, content mirrored for FTS/ranking — and adds the memory salience engine (reinforce/decay/effectiveness/confidence). Curator becomes salience-driven instead of age-based. Skill content stays file-based (git-shareable). /learn authors a file skill via SkillManager.

title: Mouse controls break intermittently: scroll scrolls whole terminal, select-to-copy stops capturing
description: Sometimes the UI gets into a state where scrolling up scrolls the whole app/terminal off instead of scrolling the main pane, and select-to-copy breaks (app no longer captures selection). Page up/down still scrolls within main pane. Suggests mouse capture mode is being toggled off or not restored. Investigate mouse capture enable/disable and any code paths (e.g. spawning subprocess, external editor, resize, redraw) that could leave mouse mode disabled.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #556 (74a9dc5): idle-timer reassert of mouse capture + bracketed paste

title: Release 0.14.2
description: Cut 0.14.2: update CHANGELOG from PRs #548/#549/#552/#553/#554 merged since 0.14.1, bump Cargo.toml, verify musl build compiles (the 0.14.0 break), tag v0.14.2.

title: Flaky test: last_init_error_tracks_open_failures races on global LAST_INIT_ERROR
description: extras::session_db_tests::last_init_error_tracks_open_failures asserts the process-global LAST_INIT_ERROR is Some after a failed SessionDb::open, but a concurrent successful open() in another parallel test clears it to None, so the assertion flakes. Passes in isolation. Fix: isolate the global for this test (serialize, or assert on the returned Err rather than the shared global).

title: Compaction OAuth fallback gaps: /compress hard-errors + fragile error matcher
description: Follow-up to PR #529. Two gaps for Anthropic-OAuth users without summarization_provider: (1) explicit /compress hard-errors with no prune-only fallback unlike reactive overflow; (2) is_anthropic_oauth_compaction_disabled_error matches err.to_string() which breaks if error is wrapped with context — would silently disable the prune-only routing.
notes: Fix in PR #530 (branch fix/compaction-oauth-prune-fallback). Awaiting review/merge.

title: No request-establish timeout on model client / wrap_rig_stream
description: Latent robustness gap noted in #523: wrap_rig_stream wraps only the established stream body's .next(); the request-establish phase and the provider clients (client.rs:355) set no timeout, so a stalled connection/handshake can hang a run with no bound. Not the cause of the #523 headless deadlock (that was ask_rx undrained, fixed in 0.13.1). Add a request/response timeout to the provider client build and/or bound the stream-establish phase.
notes: Scope finding (deferred pass): the establish phase IS unbounded — invoke_one_stream (src/agent/agent_loop/rig_stream_factory.rs:275) does model.stream(request).await with no timeout; chunk_timeout only guards reads AFTER establishment. StreamOptions.request_timeout (stream.rs:83) is threaded from LoopConfig but carries #[allow(dead_code)] — never read; defaults to None (types.rs:656) with NO config key, so nothing sets it (only a unit test does). retry.rs:60's comment that 'request_timeout already aborts a stuck/hung stream' is currently false. features.md documents request_timeout as a whole-run 'thinking-stall watchdog' backstop — a DIFFERENT semantic from a connection/handshake establish bound. So a correct fix needs a design decision: (a) bound establish at rig_stream_factory.rs:275 via tokio::time::timeout — mechanism is a ~10-line contained change; (b) decide whether establish-timeout is a new config concept or reuses request_timeout (semantic clash with the watchdog); (c) pick a default (establishment is fast, so a generous 120-180s bounds only genuine stalls without killing long legit streams). Recommend the user choose the policy; the mechanism is ready to wire once decided. NOT the cause of the #523 deadlock (that was ask_rx undrained, already fixed).
close_reason: Bounded the establish phase with a consistent named timeout (timeouts.request_establish, default 300s); removed the dead request_timeout field

title: TUI: tool chambers + chat don't span to right pane edge (dead band + artifacts)
description: chamber_widths uses content_width()=line_width().min(120)-1 which is gutter-blind and capped at 120, while ChatPane paints to Layout::chat.width-1 (reclaims hidden-panel gutters). At ~122-151 cols (panels hidden until 152) the box is ~119 wide in a ~145-col paint area -> ~26-col dead band on the right where stale glyphs from earlier/wider frames survive (resize, picker overlay). USER DECISION: full pane width for BOTH chambers and chat text (un-cap, no centering). Fix: derive chamber/content width from the actual chat paint area (Layout::chat.width-1, panel-aware) instead of content_width()'s 120 cap; chat wraps to chamber width so both follow. Add a test asserting chamber frame width + 1 == Layout chat.width across panel-hidden/shown widths. Root cause detail in PR #524 thread / agent analysis. Files: src/ui/tool_display.rs:347, src/ui/renderer.rs:1335, src/ui/tui/chat.rs:91-110, src/ui/tui/layout.rs:129-147.

title: slash-aliases: warn when alias key shadows a built-in command
description: build_alias_map warns only for unknown targets, never when an alias key collides with an existing built-in (e.g. {quit:clear} locks out /quit silently). Add a startup warning + test, keep shadowing behavior.

title: stream-json: plugin replacement dropped when final turn left partial buffer
description: In run_print StreamJson final arm, 'else if response_replaced' is skipped when leftover is true (runner died mid-stream + plugin on-response replacement). Stale pre-replacement text is emitted, replacement dropped, contradicting result envelope. Make replacement win.

title: Round 4: offload inline disk/SQLite to spawn_blocking
description: save_session (serialize+fsync) and persist_turn_to_db across done/interjected/context_compacted/overflow/error handlers, /sessions switch/delete/list dir-scan+JSON parse (storage.rs), and the git diff --stat HEAD in the post-done digest (done.rs:490 -> session_digest.rs:225) are awaited inline. Offload the blocking disk/subprocess work to spawn_blocking / fold into spawned passes.
close_reason: git diff --stat subprocess (the one blocking I/O on the turn-end path) moved off the loop. Remaining candidates (save_session, persist_turn_to_db, /sessions scans) are sub-100ms local ops where offloading needs storage-layer refactors with poor risk/benefit — left by design; reopen if profiling shows one is slow in practice.

title: Round 3b: run /wt-merge git merge off-thread
description: DEFER_WT_MERGE handler (mod.rs:2287) runs merge_worktree synchronously (several git invocations, no timeout) then build_agent. Spawn the merge.

title: Round 3a: run shell !cmd off-thread with abortable handle
description: run_shell_command().await (mod.rs:2142/2173, 120s cap) is awaited inline; long commands freeze the UI + Ctrl+C. Spawn with a phase handle so render continues and Ctrl+C aborts.

title: Round 2b: run /btw LLM call off-thread (non-blocking)
description: cmd/btw.rs:16 model.btw_query().await is a direct inline LLM completion (with retries) on the UI loop. Only slash command with a raw inline LLM call. Spawn it; render the result via a select! arm or a spawned task.

title: Non-blocking compaction: wire the two AUTO sites (post-turn auto-compact + overflow recovery)
description:
dirge-dtyn stages 2a/2b made the two USER-INITIATED compaction paths non-blocking (explicit /compress; preemptive pre-prompt compaction) via ui::compaction + the compaction_phase select! arm. Two AUTO-triggered sites still run the summarizer inline (freeze):

1. Post-turn auto-compact: run_handlers/done.rs:361 handle_compress().await (inside handle_done, agent_rx Done arm). Continuation = Nothing. Needs &mut ui.compaction_phase threaded into handle_done + its call site (mod.rs:2790); prepare_compaction + spawn(Nothing); is_running stays true; the 'AUTO-COMPACT FAILED' framing moves to the arm's Failed branch. Low risk.

2. Reactive overflow recovery: run_handlers/context_overflow.rs compress().await. DIVERGENT continuation — only retry on Compacted && !tools_already_ran (tool-side-effect safety), drops the trailing User message from history, NO add_message (prompt already in session), resets last_collapsed. Needs a CompactionThen::RetryAfterOverflow { prompt, tools_already_ran } variant + outcome-aware (Compacted vs NoOp) branching in the compaction_phase arm. Higher risk; correctness-sensitive (must not strand users on overflow). Deferred from the first pass to avoid rushing it untested.

The machinery (ui::compaction::{spawn, CompactionThen, CompactionPhaseHandle}, slash::{prepare_compaction, install_compaction}) is in place; this is wiring the two run_handler sites through it.
notes: Item 2 (reactive overflow recovery) DONE in branch nonblocking-compaction commit 32af163. Only item 1 (post-turn auto-compact in done.rs) remains. NOTE: item 1 is NOT as low-risk as originally written — the auto-compact at done.rs:345 runs before the turn-finalization tail (post-done action 458, loop continuation, reviewer 530, idle drain 597), each of which may spawn a follow-up runner that MUST run against the compacted context. A plain async spawn(Nothing) only works when the turn truly ends with no follow-up; the loop/reviewer/plugin/queued-interjection cases need the tail deferred until after install, OR the eager compaction dropped in favor of the now-async preemptive+reactive paths.
close_reason: Item 2 (reactive overflow) wired in 32af163; item 1 (post-turn auto-compact) resolved by dropping eager compaction in favor of the now-async preemptive+reactive paths. All four compaction triggers are now non-blocking — no inline summarizer call remains in the event loop.

title: Observability: opt-in dump of outgoing provider requests (which prompt, which provider, why)
description:
Local users debugging multi-provider/secondary-call behavior (e.g. a mystery mid-turn side completion that thrashes a single-slot KV cache) have no way to see what prompts dirge sends. dirge only logs cache-prefix HASHES (emit_cache_prefix_event), never payloads, and -v just sets dirge=debug.

Add DIRGE_DUMP_REQUESTS env: =1 logs a summary line per outgoing request (purpose label, provider, tool count/names, reasoning flag, byte sizes); =full also logs the system prompt / one-shot prompt body. Emitted at INFO on target dirge::wire so it lands in dirge.log (-v/RUST_LOG/DIRGE_LOG) or via RUST_LOG=dirge::wire=info. Instrumented at both request-build choke points: provider::summarize::oneshot_with_model (side-LLM: summarizer/critic/approval, carries the 'why' label) and agent_loop::rig_stream_factory (main turn/escalation/subagent/forked review). Off by default, no behavior change.
close_reason: DIRGE_DUMP_REQUESTS scaffold already existed (wire.rs, both choke points wired); completed it by adding provider+model+messages_bytes to the summary/full log lines — the provider field the issue explicitly required was missing.
