Implemented via dirge delegation, reviewed + fmt/clippy/test verified.

title: Scrollback (esp. markdown tables) does not reflow on terminal resize
description:
Chat scrollback is a flat Vec<LineEntry> of already-WRAPPED physical rows (renderer.rs:356). Markdown -> rows happens once at arrival width (markdown_to_styled, markdown.rs:326; render_table at markdown.rs:183 takes max_width but is only called at append time). On resize the handler (mod.rs:1364) only calls request_repaint(), which redraws the SAME stored rows — there is no re-wrap at draw time. Result: nothing in scrollback reflows on resize; tables are the most visible casualty (box borders overflow/mangle at narrower widths, don't expand at wider), while prose just over-runs or stays narrow.

Fixing 'tables reflow' therefore means 'scrollback reflows', which requires re-rendering from retained SOURCE at the new width. Complication: in-flight content (streaming assistant response + tool-call chambers) is not yet in session.messages, so re-rendering from history alone would drop it.

Options:
A. Full reflow: retain per-block logical source (text + color + kind, incl. table source) so the whole buffer can be regenerated at any width. On resize: regen buffer at new width, preserve scroll anchor. Also re-render in-flight buffer. Cleanest result, largest change; must handle streaming + eviction + scroll restore + perf on huge scrollback.
B. History-only reflow: on resize rebuild the buffer from session messages via the existing history-render path at the new width; accept that a resize mid-stream loses the in-progress turn until it finalizes. Much smaller; degraded during active streaming.
C. Table-targeted: tag table row ranges with their source markdown; on resize re-render only those ranges via render_table at the new width (buffer has a truncate+extend splice already at renderer.rs:1280). Narrow blast radius; only tables reflow (matches the report literally) but adds block metadata to a flat buffer.
design:
Full scrollback reflow (user-chosen scope). Make buffer a derived cache over a retained source log.

Stage 1 — source log + rebuild:
- Add blocks: Vec<ChatBlock> to Renderer + ChatSnapshot; buffer becomes derived.
- ChatBlock::{ Plain{text,color}, Markdown{src,base_color,handle}, ToolChamber{tool,banner,output,max_chars,max_lines}, Blank }.
- Route write_line/commit_partial -> Plain; the 5 markdown_to_styled sites (events.rs:238 history, agent_io.rs:88 stream, done.rs:298, interjected.rs:47, btw.rs:21) -> Markdown; render_tool_output (tool_display.rs) -> ToolChamber.
- rebuild(width): clear buffer, replay blocks through existing width-taking renderers (markdown_to_styled, chamber rendering, soft_wrap).

Stage 2 — reflow on resize:
- mod.rs:1364 Resize -> renderer.rebuild(content_width()). Preserve scroll anchor (offset-from-bottom; re-clamp to new max_offset). 

Stage 3 — streaming + edges:
- In-flight response (response_buf/response_start_line owned by event loop) re-renders at new width after mid-stream resize; reset start_line to new buffer_len on rebuild.
- Reconcile replace_from streaming, eviction (MAX_SCROLLBACK=20000, drain 2500, eviction_generation), Ctrl+O expansion absolute-index anchors, selection indices, multi-chat save/load_active.

Choke points are few: all ~90 write_line callers funnel through write_line/push_buffer_line; 5 markdown sites; tool chambers via render_tool_output. Width via content_width() = line_width().min(120).
acceptance_criteria: After a terminal resize, markdown tables in scrollback are re-laid-out to the new width (borders aligned, columns refit). Scroll position is preserved across the reflow. No regression to streaming, eviction, or selection. Tests cover render-at-width-A then reflow-to-width-B.
close_reason: Scrollback now reflows on resize: plain text + markdown (tables) re-wrap to new width via a source-block model + rebuild(). Chambers preserved verbatim (no border fracture); chamber re-boxing tracked as dirge-ghpf.

title: Keybinding rebind across contexts requires explicit unbind (ctrl-r)
description:
User keybinding config is supposed to merge OVER the built-in config: rebinding an existing key to a new command should override the default with no explicit unbind. Today a cross-context rebind silently fails unless you unbind first.

Repro:
  "keybindings": [ { "key": "ctrl-r", "command": "reverse_search" } ]
does NOT take effect; you must prepend { "key": "ctrl-r", "command": "unbind" }.

Root cause — Keymaps::from_config (src/ui/keymap.rs:344-372): there are TWO keymaps, global (KeyAction) and input (InputAction). 'unbind'/'none' removes the chord from BOTH maps. A real rebind inserts into only ONE map: global.map if the command resolves via KeyAction::from_command, else input.map via InputAction::from_command. reverse_search is an InputAction, but the DEFAULT ctrl-r is a global KeyAction. So binding ctrl-r->reverse_search inserts into input.map while the default stays in global.map. The chord is now bound in both contexts; global wins at dispatch, so the user binding never fires. unbind 'works' only because it clears both maps.

So override is 'last wins' only WITHIN the same context, not across global<->input. Any rebind whose new command lives in a different context than the default leaves the default live.

Fix direction: when applying a user (and plugin) binding for a chord, remove that chord from BOTH keymaps first, then insert into the resolved context — so a user rebind is authoritative for the chord regardless of which context old/new commands live in. The dispatch model already treats a chord as single-purpose (see the dirge-fl57 prefix/terminal conflict resolution), so clearing both then inserting one is consistent. Verify plugin layering (defaults < plugins < user, src/ui/mod.rs:209-225) still resolves correctly, and that the same-context override tests (override_rebinds_and_keeps_other_defaults, override_on_an_occupied_chord_replaces_it) plus unbind tests still pass.
acceptance_criteria: Binding an existing default chord to a command in the OTHER context (e.g. ctrl-r -> reverse_search) takes effect without an explicit unbind. New test: a global-default chord rebound to an input command fires the input command and the global default no longer fires (and vice versa). Existing override/unbind tests still pass.
close_reason: from_config now clears the chord from both keymaps before inserting; cross-context rebind works without explicit unbind. Tests for both directions; plugin path covered.

title: Minimal running project context: pinned, eviction-proof project overview
description:
At session start we inject a flat, salience-ranked BAG of individual facts (format_for_system_prompt, memory_db.rs:605). There is no single always-present high-level 'what is this project' orientation — stack, architecture in 3-5 lines, the gestalt the model should read before granular facts. No pinning exists ('No pinned concept yet — every entry is in scope' in memory_curator), so foundational orientation can be demoted to breadcrumb and drop out of the hot block as facts accumulate.

This is Recall's context.md Goal+Summary idea as a durable tier: a curated, pinned project-overview entry that (1) is never salience-evicted, (2) renders FIRST in the system prompt, (3) is REFRESHED (overwritten) by the background review rather than appended to, keeping it minimal. Distinct from the fact bag and from identity-kind (which is who the user is).

Open design questions: new kind 'overview' vs a pinned flag on existing rows; single-entry cap; how the review maintains it (replace, not add).
acceptance_criteria: A pinned/overview project-context entry exists that is exempt from salience eviction, renders first in the system prompt, and is refreshed (not duplicated) by the review. Tests: overview survives a budget-pressure compaction that evicts ordinary hot facts; review updates it in place rather than appending.
close_reason: overview kind: singular, eviction-exempt, rendered first, review-maintained; tests cover singularity, flood survival, render order

title: Session-resume / open-threads digest (cold-start carry-over)
description:
dirge has session replay (--session <id> rehydrates panels) and durable MEMORY.md facts, but nothing produces a compact 'where we stopped + open threads' handoff for the IN-PROGRESS task. working-kind exists but the review prompt says 'prefer not to persist it' and it is first to be evicted, so current-task state has no carry-over between sessions.

Idea from ~/src/recall: a cheap next-step cue regex (still need|todo|blocked|follow-up|trailing '?') + uncommitted git files, used to seed working-kind 'open threads' at session end, surfaced at the next session start. Fills the cold-start-of-the-current-task gap that durable facts don't cover. Pairs with the git/todo signals from the ground-truth layer (sibling issue).
acceptance_criteria: At session end, open threads are extracted (cue regex + uncommitted files + unfinished todos) and persisted as a resume digest; at next session start in the same project the digest is surfaced to the model. Tests cover cue extraction and the round-trip persist/surface.
close_reason: Review-maintained open-thread carry-over via working entries, fed by the deterministic digest; prompt guard test added

title: Deterministic ground-truth capture layer for background review
description:
Memory formation today is entirely LLM-mediated: run_background_review (src/agent/review.rs) forks an agent over build_transcript(session) and asks it to NOTICE facts like 'what build/test commands ran'. This is lossy (LLM omits non-salient facts), rate-limited (15-min claim_review_slot throttle; can return early with zero captures), and costs tokens to rediscover facts the transcript already carries verbatim.

Idea from ~/src/recall: pull deterministic ground-truth straight from the transcript+git WITHOUT an LLM — files touched, commands run (Bash args), git diff --stat, todo deltas, first-goal/last-state. rehydrate.rs already computes modified_files + todo_list; tool calls carry file_path/command args.

Two uses:
(a) Prepend this structured digest to COMBINED_REVIEW_PROMPT so the LLM ranks/classifies KNOWN facts instead of hunting — better recall, fewer tokens.
(b) Use it as a zero-LLM fallback write (working/episodic) when claim_review_slot is throttled or the review errors, so a session's learnings are never fully lost.

Highest-leverage of the set. Puts a deterministic floor under LLM formation, not a replacement.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic session digest (files touched, commands, git diff --stat, todo deltas) is computed without an LLM call; it is fed into the review prompt; and a fallback path persists it when the LLM review is skipped/throttled. Unit tests cover digest extraction from a synthetic transcript and the throttled-fallback path.
close_reason: 1a shipped: deterministic digest module wired into both review entry points. 1b (deterministic fallback persist) declined — carry-over chosen as review-maintained (dirge-hcv8), no deterministic floor wanted.

title: Phase 2: LSP-verify the auto-repair, roll back on error
description: After repair_delimiters auto-closes a trailing truncation, verify the written result with the language server (where one exists). If the repair introduced error-severity diagnostics, roll the file back to its pre-write state and return the diagnostics + relevant code so the model fixes its own un-repaired content. Repair-path only: a clean write is never rolled back (keeps surface-don't-block for WIP). Catches semantically-wrong-but-parseable repairs tree-sitter can't (e.g. nested #[test]).
close_reason: Implemented in #499 — write/edit LSP-verify the auto-repair and roll back on error.

title: Critic/verifier nudge vanishes from chat log after next turn streams
description: A finalization nudge (critic/verifier/todo) re-enters the loop as a user-role message WITHOUT a Done/ToolCall event, so the prior turn's stream anchor (response_start_line) stays live. The AgentEvent::UserMessage handler rendered the <critic> line but didn't reset the anchor, so the next turn's render_agent_stream replace_from(stale anchor) truncated the buffer and overwrote the nudge — it disappears on screen though the model still has it in context. Fix: finalize the in-flight response + reset the anchor before rendering the message (mirrors ToolCall/Done).

title: Prototype: feed verification status into the critic
description: Extend the in-loop LLM critic to be pickier about compile/lint/test. Reuse VerifierGate's existing signal (edited_code/ran_verification/verification_failed) as a read-only status, gate on edited_code as the precondition, and pass it into the critic prompt so it treats unverified/red code edits as concrete INCOMPLETE when verification is runnable and not forbidden.

title: Undo (Ctrl+Z) in the input editor
description: The input editor has no undo. Add a Ctrl+Z undo that reverts the last edit (especially pastes). Snapshot buffer/cursor/pastes before edits, coalesce runs of typing, discrete steps for paste/kill/delete. Reset on submit.

title: Copy wrapped prose as one line
description: When selecting prose in the chat that the renderer soft-wrapped across multiple display rows, copying inserts a newline at every wrap boundary. User has to manually re-join. selected_text() should join soft-wrap continuation rows back into one line (hard newlines/paragraph breaks preserved).

title: /sessions delete unusable when ids share a prefix (compacted-*)
description: Compacted sessions get ids 'compacted-<uuid>' (storage.rs:307). The /sessions list/switch/delete views truncated the id to a fixed 8 chars ('compacte'), identical for every compacted session, so on ambiguity 'be more specific' was impossible — the user couldn't tell them apart or type a distinguishing prefix. Fix: show each id at the shortest length that's unique among the shown set (distinct_id_len), floored at 8.
acceptance_criteria: With multiple compacted-* sessions, /sessions and /sessions delete show distinguishable id handles the user can retype to target one.

title: Keybindings phase 5: docs + close #476/#234
description: Rewrite docs/config.md 'Key bindings': remove the 'input-editor keys not rebindable' note, add the input-command table + their default chords, document sequence syntax ('ctrl-x ctrl-s'), pending-prefix/cancel behavior, and chord_timeout_ms. Add a docs/plugins.md section on creating AND overriding keybindings via plugins (register-shortcut + new bind-key), with the merge/precedence order. Close GitHub #476 and #234 with a summary comment.
acceptance_criteria: docs/config.md and docs/plugins.md reflect the unified system; #476 and #234 closed with comments.

title: Keybindings phase 4: emacs-style chord sequence runtime (#234)
description: Add the pending-prefix state machine to the dispatch loop so multi-key sequences (e.g. 'ctrl-x ctrl-s') fire. On a keypress: extend the pending sequence; complete match -> dispatch + clear; valid prefix -> hold pending and echo it in the footer, swallow the key; no match -> cancel pending and fall through. Esc and Ctrl+G cancel a pending prefix (only while pending). Optional config chord_timeout_ms (default: none, wait like emacs). No sequence bindings ship by default — capability only.
design: Pending state lives in the UI key handler (src/ui/mod.rs). Prefix/terminal conflict resolved phase-2-side: sequence wins, terminal dropped with a startup warning. Footer echo reuses existing status rendering. Applies to both global and input keymaps via the same matcher.
acceptance_criteria: Binding 'ctrl-x ctrl-s' to a command fires only after both keys; footer shows the pending prefix; Esc/Ctrl+G cancels; a non-matching second key cancels and falls through; optional timeout works when set.

title: Keybindings phase 3: Target::{Builtin,Plugin} model + plugin remapping (#476)
description: Make the keymap value a Target enum: Builtin(action) or Plugin(handler_name). Fold today's separate plugin_shortcuts list (harness/register-shortcut) into the unified keymaps so plugin bindings and built-ins resolve through one table. Add a Janet harness/bind-key (keys command) that lets a plugin bind/rebind a built-in command name (or 'none' to unbind), collected after plugin load (like list_shortcuts) and merged at precedence defaults < plugins < user-config. Closes the remapping half of #476 (the adding half already works).
design: Plugin bindings merged through the SAME from_config layering as user config, applied before user config so user always wins. register-shortcut stays for chord->plugin-handler; bind-key is chord->built-in-command. Reserved keys (Ctrl+C/D/Esc) still non-overridable. Document precedence and conflicts.
acceptance_criteria: A plugin can rebind a built-in command to a new chord and unbind one; user config overrides a plugin binding; existing register-shortcut plugins keep working; #476 remapping path covered by tests.

title: Keybindings phase 2: sequence-native unified keymap + config surface
description: Build the keymap data model as sequence-native (Vec<Chord> keys; defaults all length-1) so #234 is a later runtime addition, not a retrofit. Unify config: one keybindings array whose command vocabulary = global (KeyAction) UNION input (InputAction). Keymap::from_config routes each {key, command} to the right context keymap by command-name lookup across both registries; unknown commands still warn. Add prefix/terminal conflict detection (a chord that is both a terminal binding and a prefix of a sequence). Wire the input keymap from config in src/ui/mod.rs alongside the global keymap.
design: Chord parser extended to parse space-separated sequences ('ctrl-x ctrl-s' -> Vec<(KeyCode,KeyModifiers)>); single chord = length-1. Keymap stores bindings keyed on Vec<Chord> plus a prefix set for fast pending-prefix checks. resolve() still works for length-1 (the only defaults). Command-name uniqueness across KeyAction+InputAction is required (validate at startup).
acceptance_criteria: A config.json keybindings entry can rebind an input-editor command (e.g. move cursor_line_start off Ctrl+A); global rebinding unchanged; conflicting/unknown entries warn; sequence parsing is in place though no sequence runtime yet.

title: Keybindings phase 1: input-editor command layer (table-driven, behavior-preserving)
description: Convert src/ui/input.rs's hardcoded text-editing key match into resolve-then-apply: add an InputAction enum + InputAction::ALL default-chord table (mirroring KeyAction::ALL in src/ui/keymap.rs), resolve each KeyEvent to an InputAction via a lookup, then apply the existing logic bodies behind the enum. Single-chord only this phase. Behavior-preserving: defaults reproduce today's keys exactly. Intrinsic keys stay direct (char insertion, Backspace, Enter/submit, @ file-picker, Tab slash-completion). Riskiest phase — touches the editor core, so it lands isolated and green before anything else.
design: Rebindable input actions = named editing commands only: cursor_left/right, cursor_line_start/end (Ctrl+A/E, Home, End), word_left/right (Alt+B/F, Alt+arrows), kill_to_line_end/start (Ctrl+K/U), kill_word_back/forward (Ctrl+W, Alt+D, Alt+Backspace), yank/yank_pop (Ctrl+Y/Alt+Y), history_prev/next (Ctrl+P/N), reverse_search (Ctrl+F), up/down line/history. Preserve subtle behaviors: marker-block aware Left/Right, wrap-aware Up/Down (dirge-5w9v), kill accumulation reset, paste placeholder delete, slash-ghost accept on Right. NOT rebindable: Char insertion, Backspace, Delete, Enter/Shift+Enter, Tab, Ctrl+J, Ctrl+C/D/Esc.
acceptance_criteria: input.rs dispatches via InputAction lookup; full input test suite green; new tests assert each InputAction maps to its default chord and that a remapped table changes behavior.

title: Unified, data-driven keybindings (input + global + plugin remap + chord sequences)
description: Replace the split, partly-hardcoded keybinding handling with one data-driven system: a built-in default keymap that user config (and plugins) merge on top of, covering BOTH the global command keys (already table-driven) and the input-editor keys (currently a hardcoded match in src/ui/input.rs). Adds plugin remapping of built-ins (#476) and emacs-style multi-key chord sequences (#234). Nothing hardcoded except the Ctrl+C/D/Esc panic gesture and intrinsic editing (char insertion, Backspace, Enter-submit, @ picker, Tab completion). Defaults stay as in-code declarative tables (KeyAction::ALL pattern); user config wins over plugins wins over defaults.
design: Two contexts (global/chat + input-editor), one mechanism: action enum + default-chord table + chord parser + config layering. Keymap value becomes Target::{Builtin,Plugin} so plugin shortcuts and built-ins share one resolution table. Keymap is sequence-native (Vec<Chord> keys) so #234 chording is a runtime/UX addition, not a data-model retrofit. Closes #476 and #234; supersedes the 'not rebindable' note in docs/config.md.
acceptance_criteria: Input-editor keys rebindable via config.json; plugins can add AND remap built-in bindings; multi-key sequences (e.g. 'ctrl-x ctrl-s') work with a pending-prefix indicator and Esc/Ctrl+G cancel; docs updated; #476 and #234 closed.

title: Critic: treat permission-denied capabilities as out of scope
description: The critic (src/agent/agent_loop/critic.rs) already respects constraints expressed in the system prompt ('NEVER flag the absence of an action the instructions forbid'), but it's blind to runtime permission denials — a separate constraint channel. It sees the transcript (build_critic_transcript, first/last tool results) but is never told that a denial means the capability is out of scope rather than a failure to fix. Result: it flags 'commit+push missing' as concrete incompleteness and the model complies by routing around the guardrail. Fix: add one rule to CRITIC_PREAMBLE — 'if the transcript shows a tool was permission-denied, treat that capability as out of scope; do not demand it.' Smallest of the consumer fixes and useless on its own if denial still looks like a generic error string, hence depends on the root.
design: Depends on dirge-c7sd so the denial is legibly marked in the transcript (build_critic_transcript can label Denied results distinctly). Then add the preamble rule.
acceptance_criteria: Given a transcript containing a permission-denied commit/push, the critic does not return INCOMPLETE solely for the missing denied action.

title: approval_provider Deny should fall through to human prompt (advisor, not final authority)
description: With an approval_provider configured, try_auto_approve (src/agent/tools/mod.rs:409) short-circuits on Ok(ApprovalDecision::Deny) via ?, so the denial never reaches the human prompt at ~line 559. Only an evaluator *error* (None) falls through. Net effect: the LLM evaluator is the final authority on denials and the user is designed out of the loop — even when they'd have approved. Fix: a Deny from the approval_provider should fall through to the human prompt (treat the LLM as advisor for denials), and/or the denial message should carry the exact '/allow add <tool> <pattern>' string so recovery is one paste. Hard rule-based Effect::Deny stays terminal.
design: Depends on dirge-c7sd for the suggested_allow string. Decision needed: always fall through to human on approval_provider Deny, vs. surface the deny + /allow hint without auto-prompting. Lean: fall through to human prompt, prefilled with the evaluator's reason, since a configured human is present in interactive mode.
acceptance_criteria: An approval_provider Deny in interactive mode results in the user being prompted (or shown a one-paste /allow hint), not a hard ToolError with no recourse.

title: Failure tracker: don't treat permission denials as mechanical failures
description: The recovery-checkpoint escalation (src/agent/agent_loop/failure_tracker.rs) is built for mechanical recovery. A permission/approval denial lands in Outcome::Error with escalation weight 1 (activity.rs), indistinguishable from a wrong-args error, so it feeds the same streak. The emitted checkpoint text ('What root cause — wrong arguments, wrong tool, or wrong approach? Re-read its description and parameter schema... take a DIFFERENT next step') is wrong for a well-formed-but-denied call, and the 'take a DIFFERENT next step' instruction is exactly what drives the model to route around the guardrail (e.g. writing a .sh wrapper). Fix: exclude Denied outcomes from the mechanical-recovery streak, OR emit a distinct checkpoint — 'blocked by permissions; you cannot route around this — ask the user to /allow or stop.'
design: Depends on dirge-c7sd (Outcome::Denied). Once classify() can yield Denied, FailureTracker::record either skips escalation weight for Denied or accumulates it into a separate bucket with its own format_checkpoint branch.
acceptance_criteria: A run of N consecutive permission denials does NOT emit the 're-read the parameter schema / take a different approach' recovery checkpoint; it either stays silent or emits a permission-specific message.

title: Model permission/approval denials as a first-class tool outcome
description: A permission/approval denial is currently flattened into ToolError::Msg(String) (src/agent/tools/mod.rs:155). Downstream subsystems (failure tracker, critic, approval flow) can only re-infer 'this was a policy denial' from a string, so each one mistakes it for a generic execution error. Root fix: give denial a typed identity — e.g. ToolError::Denied { reason, suggested_allow } and/or an Outcome::Denied variant in src/agent/agent_loop/activity.rs — that the failure tracker, critic, and approval path can key off. This is the prerequisite for the three consumer fixes.
design: Two flatten points to fix: (1) ToolError in tools/mod.rs — add Denied variant carrying reason + a suggested '/allow add <tool> <pattern>' string. The denial site is try_auto_approve (tools/mod.rs:409) which today returns ToolError::Msg. (2) Outcome::classify in activity.rs:58 currently maps to Ok|Error|Timeout — add Denied so record_result can route it. Keep hard Effect::Deny (rule-based, terminal) distinct from approval_provider Deny (advisory).
acceptance_criteria: A permission/approval denial is distinguishable from a generic ToolError at the type level; failure tracker and critic can each branch on it.

title: /sessions: explicit verbs instead of overloaded positional
description: parts[1] does double duty as both subcommand sentinel ('delete') and session id, so a session id can never be 'delete'. Move to explicit verbs: /sessions list | /sessions switch <id> | /sessions delete <id>, keeping bare /sessions <id> as a switch shortcut. A bare positional is then only ever a session id.
close_reason: Deleting current session now boots to a fresh session; /sessions uses explicit verbs (list/switch/delete) so a bare positional is only ever a session id

title: Deleting the current session leaves a zombie; boot to a fresh session
description: Deleting the session you're currently in (/sessions delete <current-id>) removes the file but the in-memory session keeps running, pointing at a now-deleted file. Decision: when the deleted session is the active one, swap the live session for a fresh empty one (new id, same model/provider/cwd) and rebuild the agent, mirroring the switch path.
close_reason: Deleting current session now boots to a fresh session; /sessions uses explicit verbs (list/switch/delete) so a bare positional is only ever a session id

title: Goal gate doesn't strip compaction summary from rules (critic does)
description: critic::strip_compaction_summary keeps the critic from treating a resumed session's [CONTEXT COMPACTION — REFERENCE ONLY] summary (its stale ## Active Task) as an outstanding requirement. The sibling goal gate (goal::build_goal_prompt) receives the same merged system_prompt as rules but does NOT strip the summary, so it shares the stale-state bug. It also byte-slices rules (&rules[..N]) which can panic on a non-char boundary.
close_reason: Footer now shows compaction-trigger budget; goal gate strips compaction summary like the critic

title: Footer shows model window, not the compaction budget
description: The status-line footer uses session.context_window (raw model window, e.g. 200k/1M) as the token-usage denominator. That misrepresents how much room is actually left: the loop folds/compacts at effective_fold_threshold * effective_ctx_max, where effective_ctx_max = min(model_window, context_target). Show the compaction-trigger budget instead so the percentage reflects 'how close to a fold'.
close_reason: Footer now shows compaction-trigger budget; goal gate strips compaction summary like the critic

title: Q&A custom-answer paste leaks into main prompt
description: When typing a free-form custom answer in the question modal, pasting routes to input.handle_paste (main compose box) instead of the question's custom-answer buffer. Root cause: dispatch_modal! only matches UserEvent::Key, so Paste falls through to the compose Paste arm.
close_reason: Fixed in #468 — pastes route to the Q&A custom-answer field

title: Config flag to show thinking output by default
description: GH #461: add a config setting (show_reasoning) to make model thinking/reasoning visible by default, like show_tool_details/show_edit_diff. Defaults to false (current behavior).

title: Cost-aware loop guard: consolidate storm + failure tracker behind shared activity log
description: Storm breaker and failure tracker are count-based and cost-blind: a single 120s timeout counts the same as a 5ms error, so neither escalates on expensive failures. Consolidate bookkeeping behind one ToolActivityLog/LoopGuards facade recording {call, outcome, cost_ms}; keep storm's suppress + failure tracker's nudge as two policies reading it. Add cost-awareness: timeouts are a stronger 'stuck' signal — escalate the nudge faster and lower storm's effective threshold for a call that previously timed out.
close_reason: Fixed: setsid detaches the controlling terminal so tty prompts fail fast; non-interactive env defaults added. Loop guard made cost-aware behind a LoopGuards facade (timeout weighting in failure tracker + lowered storm threshold). All tests + clippy -D warnings pass.

title: Expanded thinking block: wrapped rows escape the box (lose the │ bar)
description: In the Ctrl+O expanded thinking panel, render_thinking_block formats each line as '  │ {line}' and lets write_line wrap it. write_line wraps with an empty continuation indent, so continuation rows drop the '  │ ' bar and start at column 0, escaping the bounding box. Fix: pre-wrap each line to content_width-4 and carry the '  │ ' bar onto every wrapped row (same pattern as the blockquote/code-block wrap fixes).

title: Unify Anthropic + OpenAI OAuth onto shared machinery, add /login
description: #455 added OpenAI device-code OAuth as a parallel src/auth/ subsystem that duplicates the Anthropic OAuth machinery in src/provider/ (PKCE, 0600 credential storage, expiry/refresh). Extract shared OAuth primitives so both providers share one base; add a /login TUI slash command mirroring the dirge auth CLI. Follow-up to merging #455.
close_reason: De-dup + dispatch + chatgpt alias merged in PR #457; /login descoped

title: Long lines in fenced code blocks are clipped, not wrapped
description: The chat painter (ChatPane::render in tui/chat.rs) draws one screen row per LineEntry and clips to width. markdown_to_styled wraps prose via word_wrap but emitted code-block lines as one unwrapped LineEntry per source line, so a long line inside a fenced code block was clipped at the window edge (user saw 'only one sentence'). Fix: wrap each code line's colored content to the content width, carrying the gutter onto every wrapped row.

title: Compaction: Active Task can hold an already-completed task, confusing the model (#443)
description: GitHub #443: after compaction the model re-derives/expresses confusion about the original task when it's already done and only follow-up work remains. Root cause: build_summary_prompt's '## Active Task' biases toward the user's original verbatim request, and SUMMARY_PREFIX tells the model to 'resume exactly from there' without warning the original task may be complete. Fix: reframe Active Task to the immediate next action / emergent follow-up and require stating the original done; add a completion guard to SUMMARY_PREFIX; add a supersession probe to the recall harness.

title: Quoted headings/code blocks render without the blockquote bar
description: The #446 fix bars quoted prose via flush_blockquote at TagEnd::Paragraph, but TagEnd::Heading and TagEnd::CodeBlock ignore in_blockquote, so a quote mixing prose with a heading or fenced code reads as disconnected blocks. Also separator-guard misfires after list/heading; inner_w can be 0; flush_blockquote duplicates flush_acc.

title: Memory: hybrid retrieval provider (dense + fusion over BM25)
description: Elastic idea #1. New MemoryProvider impl adding dense recall + fusion over existing FTS5/BM25, behind a config flag, BM25-only stays default. Uses dirge-bov5 trait seam, no tool-layer rewrite. Must beat BM25 baseline on the retrieval eval. Branch feat/memory-hybrid-retrieval.

title: Memory: retrieval-quality eval harness (Recall@K)
description: Elastic idea #5. Complement compaction_recall.rs: generate QA pairs per stored memory, measure Recall@K of search(). CI-gated deterministic mock, pointable at real embedder off-CI. Provides the baseline to prove hybrid retrieval beats BM25. Branch feat/memory-retrieval-eval.

title: Memory: confidence axis + contradiction supersession
description: Elastic idea #4, bundled with supersession per dirge-lerb lesson (a bare confidence column was reverted in v9 for being write-only). Re-add confidence REAL (schema v13) WITH a producer (natural/harsh contradiction classification on replace, harsh lowers confidence + marks old entry superseded_by/superseded_at) and a consumer (confidence factors into search rank + eviction). Superseded entries filtered from snapshot but restorable. Branch feat/memory-confidence-supersession.
notes: PR #437 open: https://github.com/dirge-code/dirge/pull/437 — implemented (confidence read in eviction/search/curation/view + supersession), code-reviewed (high, 3 angles), all 2790 tests green. CI running.

title: Memory: procedural effectiveness ranking (success/failure counts)
description: Elastic agent-memory idea #2. Procedural memories should rank on 'recently effective' not 'recently tried'. Add success_count/failure_count/last_success_at (schema v12), curator LLM infers deltas from transcript, wire effectiveness into search ranking + effective_salience, exempt procedural from disuse decay. Branch feat/memory-procedural-effectiveness.
notes: PR #436 open: https://github.com/dirge-code/dirge/pull/436 — implemented, code-reviewed (high), all tests green. Awaiting merge.

title: Route all edit tools through one syntax_gate choke point; edit_minified was missing auto-repair
description: Code review of dirge-p5fu found edit_minified.rs still used the old reject-only check_syntax/format_errors path — it was never migrated to the delimiter auto-repair, because the repair was wired at ~5 near-identical call sites instead of one choke point (the per-site copies had also drifted: note separator was a leading space in apply_patch vs newline elsewhere). Extracted syntax_gate + append_repair_note into agent::tools and routed write/edit/edit_lines/apply_patch(create+update)/edit_minified through it. Uniform note format; no tool can drift out of the contract now. No behavior change for the four already-wired tools; edit_minified gains the repair.
close_reason: Extracted syntax_gate + append_repair_note choke point; all 5 edit tools (incl the previously-missed edit_minified) route through it. Uniform note format. Full suite green (2750).

title: Auto-repair unbalanced delimiters in code edits (parity with JSON truncation repair)
description:
Inconsistency: a truncated tool-call JSON argument is mechanically repaired (tool_input_repair::repair_truncated_json closes braces/brackets, reports notes), but an unbalanced delimiter in code that the model write/edit/apply_patch is only DETECTED (semantic::syntax_validator::check_syntax) and the edit is REJECTED — forcing a wasteful model round-trip for a mechanical fix.

Make code-delimiter handling consistent with JSON: mechanically close and report, rather than bounce to the model.

Design (safety-aware):
- Refactor the comment/string/char-aware scanner into a structured delimiter_balance returning the unclosed-opener stack (with exact positions) OR a stray/mismatched-closer location. delimiter_summary formats from it; existing messages/tests unchanged. This preserves the semantic, exact-position summary the user wants to keep.
- repair_delimiters: ONLY the pure-unclosed case (no stray/mismatched closer). Append the matching closers (top-of-stack first). Then RE-VALIDATE the repaired content with check_syntax — tree-sitter for grammared langs (the oracle that rejects nonsense closes like fn main( { -> })), the scanner for lisps. Apply only if it now parses clean; else fall back to the current reject-with-summary.
- Only available where we have comment/string/char-aware lex rules (lex_rules_for_path) so we never miscount a delimiter inside a string/comment.
- Report the repair as a note on the SUCCESS result (mirrors JSON repair notes), so it is never silent and the model can correct a wrong guess.
- Wire into write/edit/edit_lines/apply_patch.

TDD: structured scan distinguishes unclosed vs stray; comment/string/char-literal awareness (a ) inside a string/comment is not counted); truncated lisp form + truncated brace get closed and validate; stray/mismatched closer is NOT auto-repaired (still rejected with summary); the re-validation gate rejects a close that does not actually produce valid syntax; success note is surfaced. delimiter_summary tests stay green.
close_reason: Added repair_delimiters + validate_or_repair on the structured delimiter_balance scan. Edit tools (write/edit/edit_lines/apply_patch x2) now mechanically close a purely-unclosed delimiter imbalance and report it, instead of rejecting — parity with JSON truncation repair. Safety: only with comment/string/char-aware lex rules, never a stray closer, and the close is re-validated by check_syntax (tree-sitter oracle) before applying. delimiter_summary preserved; existing tests green.

title: Curator bulk view: annotate entries with kind + use count + id
description:
Follow-up to dirge-26h1. The memory curator LLM pass reads two things: the flagged candidate tables (which carry Target/Age/Uses/Id) and the bulk MEMORY.md/PITFALLS.md dump it actually consolidates from. The bulk dump comes from store.rendered(target), which joins raw entry CONTENT only — no kind, no usage, no id. So for any entry not in a candidate table the LLM has zero usage/kind signal to weigh consolidation, removal, or inline promotion.

rendered() must NOT change — it is also the system-prompt injection path and is kept byte-stable for the frozen snapshot. Add a separate store.rendered_for_curator(target) that prefixes each entry with its kind, use_count, and urn id (so the LLM can target it precisely with replace/remove). Wire review.rs run_background_review to pass the annotated text to render_curator_input, and add a one-line legend so the model knows how to read the annotation. Do NOT turn low use-count into a removal trigger — keep the existing keep-bias; usage is context, not a delete rule.

TDD: rendered_for_curator includes kind + uses + id + content; plain rendered() stays metadata-free (regression); curator input carries the legend.
close_reason: Added rendered_for_curator(target) prefixing each entry with [kind | N uses | id]; review.rs feeds it to the curator instead of the content-only render. render_curator_input gained a legend. Removed the now-unused rendered() (it was curator-only, not the prompt path — that's the frozen snapshot, untouched). Tests lock annotation + delimiter join + legend.

title: Promote durable working memory to procedural/semantic via the curator's LLM pass
description:
Working-kind entries (salience 0.3) are first-evicted by design. A working insight that turns out durable (e.g. the test command is cargo test --bin dirge) currently never graduates — it decays/demotes and the knowledge leaks away, not because working crowds out long-term but because nothing re-kinds it. There is no promotion path.

Fix (decided): extend the memory curator LLM consolidation pass (memory_curator.rs MEMORY_CURATOR_PROMPT + spawn_memory_curator_runner, memory-only allow-list) to identify durable working entries and re-kind them to procedural (default) or semantic. The store already supports this: replace_entry(target, old, new, Some(kind)) re-classifies in place, preserving uid/created_at/usage lineage and bumping salience. Runs between sessions, so HOT stays prefix-cache-frozen within a session.

Work: (a) confirm the memory tool surfaces the kind param on replace so the forked curator runner can actually re-kind (add to the tool schema if missing — must stay within the memory-only allow-list); (b) update MEMORY_CURATOR_PROMPT to instruct: promote working entries that are demonstrably durable (aged + repeatedly consulted) to procedural/semantic, with the bias-toward-keeping caveat; (c) surface promotions in LLM_REPORT.md.

TDD: prompt contains the promotion instruction (string test); replace_entry re-kind path covered (working to procedural updates kind + salience, preserves uid/usage); LLM_REPORT renders a promotion action. Depends on the memory-tool kind affordance being reachable from the curator runner.
close_reason: Curator now surfaces aged working entries as promotion candidates (separate from the stale/removal table) and the LLM pass is instructed to re-kind durable ones to procedural/semantic via replace. CurationEntry carries kind; MechanicalReport.promotion_candidates + render table + REPORT.md count added. replace already preserves uid/usage and bumps salience — locked by promote_working_keeps_usage_lineage. Memory tool needed no schema change.

title: Working-memory HOT reserve: cap long-term in-context footprint so working keeps a toehold
description:
Today the 'memory' target HOT budget (DEFAULT_MEMORY_CHAR_LIMIT=2200) is shared by all kinds; add_entry's compaction loop demotes strictly least-salient. Because salience is kind-derived (working 0.3 < procedural 0.5 < semantic 0.6 < identity 0.75), working is always demoted first — which prevents working from diluting long-term, but as long-term knowledge grows a project full of recently-used invariants STARVES working memory of any HOT space.

Fix (decided): reserve a small slice of the memory HOT budget for working-kind entries (DEFAULT_WORKING_HOT_RESERVE ~400 chars, memory target only; pitfalls reserve 0). Make eviction kind-aware so two invariants hold: (1) anti-dilution — adding/keeping working never demotes a long-term entry; (2) anti-starvation — long-term is capped at char_limit - reserve, so it can never evict working below the reserve. Long-term overflow demotes to breadcrumb (already searchable/expandable on demand). Edge: a single long-term entry between (limit-reserve) and limit still fits by using reserve slack (no infinite demote).

TDD: working entries within the reserve survive a flood of high-salience long-term adds; a working add never demotes a long-term entry; long-term overflow lands in breadcrumb not tombstone; existing snapshot/budget tests stay green (adjust any that assumed working-demoted-first within the reserve). Lock both invariants explicitly. Reframes the 'salience floor' idea into a two-sided guarantee.
close_reason: Implemented conditional working HOT reserve (400 chars, memory target). Eviction victim selection is now kind-aware: long-term demoted while it exceeds char_limit-reserve; working overflow past the reserve demoted otherwise. Long-term still uses full budget when working is absent (not a proactive cap). Two-sided guarantee locked by tests; existing eviction tests green.

title: Snapshot store: move process-global STORE to per-session ownership
description:
The /rewind file-snapshot store (src/agent/tools/snapshots.rs) is a process-global `static STORE: LazyLock<Mutex<Store>>`. dirge runs one interactive/headless session per process today, but subagents spawned by the `task` tool run IN-PROCESS and share this global: their file mutations land in whatever turn bucket is current (the parent's), and a parent /rewind can restore subagent-edited files; concurrent subagents can interleave captures. Also blocks multiple top-level sessions per process and forces the test-only global TEST_GATE.

DECISION GATE FIRST: assess whether this is worth the churn. dirge is single-session-per-process; subagent edits to the same repo arguably SHOULD be rewindable by the parent. Legitimate outcomes: (a) per-session refactor, or (b) decide global is acceptable, harden tests, and document. Decide before refactoring.

If refactoring: mirror ToolCache. ToolCache (src/agent/tools/cache.rs) is an Arc<Mutex<..>> handle constructed once per session in src/agent/builder/loop_tools.rs and passed to each tool via with_cache(); it ALREADY holds a per-session read_files set (Arc<Mutex<HashSet<PathBuf>>>). Options: put the snapshot store INSIDE ToolCache, or add a sibling per-session SnapshotStore handle threaded the same way. capture/capture_bytes move to methods on that handle; begin_turn/restore_from/clear operate on the session's store (UI: src/ui/mod.rs begin_snapshot_turn, src/ui/search_rewind.rs restore_from, src/ui/slash/cmd/clear.rs). Resolve: do subagents share the parent store (rewindable by parent) or get their own? Removing the global also lets tests drop TEST_GATE.

Touch points: snapshots.rs (all pub fns), edit.rs/edit_lines.rs/write.rs/apply_patch.rs (capture call sites), loop_tools.rs (construction/threading), ui/mod.rs + search_rewind.rs + slash/cmd/clear.rs.

TDD: keep the existing snapshots tests green; add a test proving two independent stores don't cross-contaminate (replaces the global TEST_GATE serialization).
notes: DECISION: keep the process-global STORE (outcome b), do not refactor. Rationale: dirge is single-session-per-process, so the global IS the session store — no second top-level session to collide with. Subagents run in-process and never call begin_turn (only the UI does, per user message), so their edits intentionally fold into the parent's turn bucket and are parent-rewindable; a per-subagent store would break that, and a per-session handle would behave identically to the global for a much larger surface. No current correctness bug; only blocks a multi-session-per-process mode that is not on the roadmap. TEST_GATE stays as the accepted cost of the global. Documented the contract in snapshots.rs module doc + TEST_GATE doc; added regression test subagent_edits_fold_into_parent_turn_and_are_parent_rewindable locking the shared-store contract.
close_reason: Decision: keep process-global (outcome b). Documented intentional shared-store contract + added regression test; no refactor — global is correct for single-session-per-process and gives parent-rewindable subagent edits.

title: File-state rewind via content-addressed snapshots
description:
Article gap (howardchen DeepSeek V4 piece): dirge's /rewind (src/ui/search_rewind.rs) only truncates the conversation history; it does NOT roll files back to their pre-turn state. The article's content-addressed snapshot makes multi-hour autonomous loops safe (worst-case rollback = minutes + ~$1). Pairs with --goal autonomous runs.

Scope: before any file mutation (write/edit/apply_patch), capture pre-state content of touched files keyed by turn (content-addressed, SHA-256, dedup). Extend /rewind N to restore those files alongside the existing message truncation. Decide storage location (.dirge/sessions/<id>/objects/ analog) + retention.

TDD plan (round 3):
1. Test: snapshot/restore roundtrip for a single mutated file.
2. Test: /rewind N restores files for all turns >= N and truncates history together.
3. Test: partial-turn safety (mutation applied mid-turn still captured).
4. Test: interaction with git worktrees (don't double-restore).
close_reason: Implemented with tests; committed on branch hash-anchored-editing (article gap-closing rounds 1-4)

title: Harness: cross-turn failure recovery + better error feedback for weaker models
description: Audit of how the harness handles model mistakes found that mechanical repair is strong (tool_input_repair, scavenge, fuzzy edit, truncation closer) but cross-turn failure handling and non-mechanical feedback are thin. Adopt proven techniques (structured reflection arxiv 2509.18847/2509.25238, did-you-mean a la aider, tool-doc re-injection arxiv 2510.17874). Three rounds.

title: Memory guidance: explain frozen snapshot so the model doesn't re-add facts
description: MEMORY_GUIDANCE says memory is 'injected into every turn' but it's a snapshot frozen at session start — a fact the model adds mid-session won't appear in its prompt until next session, risking re-adds. Fix the guidance to explain the snapshot is frozen + writes land next session; make the add tool response signal 'active from next session'; sharpen breadcrumb/tombstone guidance.
close_reason: guidance + add-response clarify frozen snapshot; test pins it

title: DAP permission check must fail closed on poisoned/missing lock
description: janet_bindings.rs:563 DAP_PERM_CHECK.lock().ok().and_then(...) skips the permission check when the lock is poisoned or absent — a security boundary failing open. Deny instead. Opt-in dap feature, plugin-only.
close_reason: decide_eval_permission fails closed on poison; pure + tested for both poison paths

title: Extract shared content-length JSON-RPC framing for LSP+DAP
description: src/lsp/jsonrpc.rs and src/dap/framing.rs have byte-identical encode_frame/decode_frame (16MB cap, Content-Length parsing). Extract one shared module both call; tests move with it.
close_reason: shared jsonrpc_framing module; lsp+dap re-pointed; copies deleted

title: Implement /dap-repl: terse REPL-style aliases over the DAP session
description: docs/dap.md:35-44 document /dap-repl with short aliases (launch, bp, c, p, n, terminate) but only /debug exists. Implement /dap-repl as a real command routing the documented aliases to the existing pub(super) debug subcommand handlers (no duplication), register in dispatch + completion, and rewrite the doc to match (drop the fake persistent dap> prompt and the unsubstantiated 'Janet-powered' framing).
close_reason: /dap-repl implemented as alias layer over shared debug handlers; doc corrected

title: Single FTS5 query sanitizer
description: Three independent implementations: session_search sanitize_fts5_query (6-step), memory_db fts_quote (token quoting), cross_session build_fts_or_query (alphanumeric-only OR-join). Security-sensitive triplication. Consolidate on one sanitizer with thin adapters for OR-join semantics.
close_reason: extras::fts owns sanitize_query/quote_terms/or_query; call sites delegate; tests moved

title: Consolidate 4x forked-runner drain loops in review.rs
description: run_background_review / run_curator_review / run_memory_curator_review / run_cross_session_extraction copy-paste the AgentRunner destructure + AbortRunnerOnDrop + event drain with drifted error handling. Extract one drain_forked_runner helper; slot rollback stays explicit at the background-review call site.
close_reason: drain_forked_runner extracted; four call sites are thin wrappers; fake-runner tests cover order, first-error, channel-close

title: Headless JSON output claims success on abnormal end
description: provider/run.rs hardcodes is_error:false + subtype:success. Error events return early (fine), but channel-close-without-Done (runner panic/abort) emits success JSON with partial response, and max-turns truncation emits success with only a stderr notice. Track terminal state and emit is_error/subtype accordingly.
close_reason: RunEnd classification + headless_result_json; incomplete runs exit non-zero

title: Background shell stuck Running when drain task dies
description: store.finish() only fires from inside the detached drain loop; if that task is aborted or panics, PgKillGuard kills the process but the registry entry stays Running forever — /tasks and bash_output report a dead shell as live. Add a finish-on-drop guard.
close_reason: finish-on-drop backstop in spawn_streaming_shell; abort test covers the path

title: messages_ad FTS delete trigger corrupts index on message delete (latent)
description: v6 made messages_fts hold a redacted+concatenated projection but the surviving AFTER DELETE trigger issues FTS5 'delete' with raw old.content — external-content FTS5 requires exact indexed values, mismatched deletes corrupt the index. No code deletes messages today (latent). Migration v8: drop messages_ad, document that deletes must clean FTS app-side. Also audit trigram delete path and add warn-log for rows dropped by v6 backfill filter_map.
close_reason: v8 drops messages_ad; delete_session_messages is the safe app-side path; v6 backfill drop-count logged

title: Usage-driven memory curation + cadence fixes
description: Round 4 of SQLite memory plan. expand/use events bump use_count/last_used_at; salience reinforcement on use, decay on disuse; eviction = demotion (hot -> breadcrumb -> tombstoned) ordered by salience x recency. Curator consumes usage data; first curator run gated on session count not 7 days; <30-day contradictions eligible under budget pressure.
close_reason: implemented on sqlite-memory-store branch: expand reinforces salience, recency-aware eviction, disuse decay in mechanical pass, usage-aware staleness, session-count first-run gate, budget-pressure scope widening

title: Memory lifecycle: tombstone instead of delete, id-addressed CRUD
description: Round 2 of SQLite memory plan. Evict/remove set status='tombstoned' instead of deleting rows; view and prompt injection exclude tombstoned; memory tool accepts id addressing (substring still works); curator gains archive/restore. Depends on SQLite backend swap.
close_reason: duplicate of dirge-8h22 (Phase 3: supersession/tombstone); that issue covers Round 2, to be implemented on the SQLite backend

title: Memory breadcrumb tier: inline index + expand/search actions
description: Round 3 of SQLite memory plan. Tiered prompt injection: hot tier verbatim, rest as one-line [id] (kind) preview breadcrumbs. memory tool gains expand (by id) and search (FTS over memories_fts) actions. Breadcrumb tier budget raised 10-50x. Index stays inline in system prompt; expansion is one tool call (weak-model-safe per arXiv:2605.15184).

title: Phase 3: Non-destructive supersession + bi-temporal time for memory
description:
Add UMP-compatible non-destructive supersession and bi-temporal time model to memory entries.

## Current State
Memory replace() mutates entries in-place via substring match. No history, no supersession chain, no valid time tracking. Remove() deletes entirely.

## Goal
- Add supersedes/superseded_by fields to track replacement chains
- Add time model with valid_from/valid_to (bi-temporal)  
- Replace operations create successors rather than mutating
- Superseded entries are tombstoned, not deleted
- Remove() tombstones by default (optional hard erase)
- Backward-compatible: existing entries get valid_from=now, valid_to=null

## Files
- src/extras/memory_store.rs — add time/supersession fields, update replace/remove logic
- src/extras/memory_provider.rs — update trait signatures if needed
- src/agent/tools/memory.rs — update tool definition for new semantics
- src/extras/memory_usage.rs — track tombstoned entries
notes: Implement against the SQLite memories table (dirge-18ks), not memory_store.rs markdown files. superseded_by column already planned in the v7 schema.
close_reason: tombstone-on-remove/evict + restore + id addressing implemented on sqlite-memory-store branch; replace deliberately stays update-in-place (lineage preserved on the row), bi-temporal valid_from/valid_to dropped as unneeded — reopen if true supersession history becomes a requirement

title: Phase 2: Injection-resistant rehydration framing for memory
description:
Add UMP-style injection-resistant framing wrapper when memory is injected into the system prompt.

## Current State
Memory entries are injected as raw text blocks wrapped in  XML tags. No explicit untrusted-data framing, no sanitization against fence-breaking.

## Goal
- Wrap injected memory in an explicit untrusted-data frame with structural fencing
- Sanitize entry content to prevent fence-breaking (strip forged frame tags, collapse newlines)
- Add a preamble directive that recalled memory is reference data, not instructions

## Files
- src/extras/memory_store.rs — update format_for_system_prompt to use framed output
- src/agent/builder/preamble.rs — ensure framing survives preamble assembly

title: Phase 1: Record identity + kinds + lifecycle for memory entries
description:
Add UMP-compatible record identity, memory kinds, and lifecycle fields to the memory system.

## Current State
Memory entries are plain strings separated by § delimiters in MEMORY.md / PITFALLS.md. No per-entry IDs, no kind classification, no lifecycle tracking.

## Goal
- Add stable, unique record IDs (URN format) per entry
- Add 5 memory kinds: semantic, episodic, procedural, working, identity
- Add lifecycle fields: confidence (0-1), salience (0-1), status (active/candidate/tombstoned)
- Backward-compatible: existing plain-text entries get auto-assigned IDs and default kind=procedural

## Files
- src/extras/memory_store.rs — add id/kind/lifecycle to MemoryStore entry model, update format_for_system_prompt
- src/extras/memory_provider.rs — add kind param to add(), update trait if needed
- src/agent/tools/memory.rs — add kind enum to tool definition, update Args

title: Unified input mechanism: InputMode state machine, dissolve nested modal loops (#387 follow-up)
description: Follow-up to the rendering refactor. User input is handled in 7 scattered places: the main user_rx arm (compose) + 6 nested blocking loops (permission, question options, question custom-input, dialog confirm, dialog select, plan accept/reject) + rewind picker. The nested blocking loops are the root cause of the questionnaire-freeze class of bug and the input-priority concern. Introduce InputMode enum on UiState + one dispatch_input router; dissolve each nested loop into a mode state driven by the central loop; make select! biased so user events take priority. Branch: refactor/unified-rendering-387.
close_reason: Dissolved all nested modal blocking loops into a unified InputMode state machine on UiState + dispatch_modal! router in the single biased user_rx arm. Converted plan-switch, question (incl. custom-text), dialog confirm/select, and permission; rewind picker was already event-driven. Modal-triggering arms gated on !is_modal() so siblings can't clobber an in-flight reply channel. Full --all-features suite green (2603). Commits on refactor/unified-rendering-387; needs interactive smoke-test of each modal before merge.

title: Unified state-driven rendering: single paint per event + UiState struct (#387)
description: GitHub issue #387. Phase 1: centralize all rendering into a single paint per event — add a needs_paint dirty flag + flush() to Renderer, make mutators set the flag instead of inline tui_redraw, build the StatusLine once per event at the loop bottom, collapse ~85 inline render_viewport/draw_bottom/StatusLine call sites. Phase 2: decouple rendering from state by moving the ~37 event-loop state locals into a UiState data structure that RunCtx + the status line read from. Branch: refactor/unified-rendering-387.
notes: DONE on branch refactor/unified-rendering-387 (5 commits). Phase1: UiState data model + Renderer single-paint mechanism (needs_paint/flush/set_bottom). Phase2: migrated 34 event-loop locals into UiState; render_frame! is the single per-event paint effect (builds StatusLine once, dirty-on-change coalescing preserved); collapsed ~85 inline draw_bottom/render_viewport sites (StatusLine::render now appears once in mod.rs, net -300+ lines). Full --all-features suite green. User verified live behavior. Ready for PR.

title: transform-context/on-compact block a tokio worker (no spawn_blocking); message-end skipped in headless
description: agent_loop/plugin_hooks.rs:451-468,498-535: transform_context_from_plugin_manager, on_before, on_compact call mgr.dispatch() directly in the async block while holding the PluginManager std-Mutex, blocking the OS thread up to INTERACTIVE_EVAL_TIMEOUT (30s) per LLM call — unlike tool hooks which use spawn_blocking + timeout (LOOP-8). Separately, message-end / harness/rewrite-message is dispatched only on the interactive done.rs path (:180), never on the headless provider/run.rs path — a silent no-op under --print/--loop/ACP. Fix: wrap these dispatches in spawn_blocking; fire message-end on the headless path too (or document TUI-only).
close_reason: transform-context/on-before-compact/on-compact now run via spawn_blocking + 10s timeout (no tokio-worker stall, PM mutex not held across a slow hook). message-end now also dispatched on the headless apply_response_hooks path with the rewrite stored. Tests added.

title: Phased /plan forks receive the full session transcript, not just explore findings
description: cmd_plan.rs:147-162 + spawn.rs:353 + review.rs:687: both explore and plan forks are passed transcript=build_transcript(session) (entire session history) embedded as <session_transcript>; the plan fork additionally gets findings. The in-code comment 'the ONLY thing carried over is the findings report — a true context reset between phases' is inaccurate: phases are isolated from each other's intermediate tool chatter but NOT from prior session history. Fix: either restrict the plan fork to findings-only as documented, or correct the comment/docs.
close_reason: Plan fork now spawned with an empty transcript (findings-only via plan_prompt) instead of the full session transcript — matches the documented 'true context reset between phases'. Explore fork keeps the transcript as entry context. Plan suite green.

title: context-depth tracker counts per tool-call, not per turn, contradicting its docs/reminder
description: context_depth.rs docstring, Inner.consecutive, and the emitted reminder all say 'consecutive turns', but record_tool_call is invoked once per tool call (tools.rs:170 sequential, :879 parallel -> :443). Test touches_same_files_increments_consecutive confirms 3 calls => consecutive=3. A single turn with 5 edits to one file bumps consecutive by 5, so the reminder fires far earlier than 'N turns' implies and reports an inflated count. Fix: count per turn, or correct the wording/threshold.
close_reason: Wording corrected across module docstring, Inner field comments, the emitted reminder, features.md, and config.md to say 'consecutive tool calls' (the real per-call unit) instead of 'turns'. Behavior unchanged; test updated.

title: DAP has no session-end teardown; relies on Drop of a static that never runs
description: dap/session.rs:29: DAP_MANAGER is a static Option<Arc<DapSessionManager>>. Debuggee/adapter teardown depends on DapProcessGuard::drop -> kill(-pgid, SIGKILL), but a static is never dropped on normal process exit, and there is no exit hook calling terminate_active/disconnect (contrast LSP's close_all_files at main.rs:1273). Within a session terminate_active runs before each new launch, but on dirge exit a setsid-isolated adapter+debuggee can be orphaned. Fix: add a session-end/atexit hook that terminates the active DAP session.
close_reason: Added dap::session::shutdown_active_session() (terminates the active session via terminate_active) and called it on the interactive exit path beside the LSP close_all_files step, so a DAP adapter+debuggee isn't orphaned when the static manager is never Dropped.

title: /panel auto real threshold is ~152 cols, not the documented 100; PANEL_AUTO_MIN_COLS is dead
description: renderer.rs:1103-1110: side_panel_visible for Auto requires cols>=100 AND content_indent()>=15. content_indent (=:1163) only reaches 15 once cols>=152 (content_width capped at 120). So between 100 and ~151 cols /panel auto (and /panel on) show nothing, and PANEL_AUTO_MIN_COLS=100 (:108) is effectively dead — the indent>=15 gate always binds. README:126 says '>=100 cols'. Fix: reconcile the constant + docs with the real threshold.
close_reason: PANEL_AUTO_MIN_COLS set to 152 (the real cols at which content_indent reaches 15); README/config/features docs corrected from >=100 to >=152. Test pins the constant to the gutter math.

title: --no-color is bypassed by hardcoded diff +/- background ANSI escapes
description: ui/run_handlers/tool_result.rs:310-315 -> box_render.rs:283-290 bake \x1b[48;5;22m (green) / \x1b[48;5;52m (red) directly into diff rows. write_line stores the string verbatim; theme::no_color() (theme.rs:481) only collapses colors routed through theme::*() accessors. Under --no-color, diff lines still paint green/red backgrounds — the one stray-escape bypass of the single-chokepoint design. Fix: source the diff backgrounds from the theme chokepoint.
close_reason: row_with_bg now sources its +/- background escapes via diff_bg_escapes(bg_idx, theme::no_color()) — empty under --no-color. Pure helper tested.

title: apply_summary/compute_compress_window can orphan a tool_use/tool_result pair (latent 400)
description: compression.rs:691-742: the compaction window is message-count based (end = n - protect_tail). assistant tool_calls and each tool_result are separate messages (run.rs:1071,1104,1117). The tail/head boundary can leave an orphaned tool_result (or retain an assistant tool_call whose result was folded), producing an unbalanced history that 400s Anthropic/OpenAI. heal/fix_tool_call_pairing runs ONLY on session load (integration.rs:628), never after a fold. The handle_compress path guards this via align_cut_to_user_boundary (mod.rs:91) — the two compaction impls diverged. LATENT today only because summarize_fn is unwired; becomes live HIGH if that is wired. Fix: snap the window to tool-pair/user boundaries or re-run fix_tool_call_pairing after every fold.
close_reason: compute_compress_window now snaps both cuts to user-message boundaries (head forward, tail backward) so the fold can't orphan a tool_use/tool_result pair. Test: compute_window_snaps_off_tool_pairs.

title: prune_tool_outputs silently no-ops on production block-array tool results
description: compression.rs:419 reads msg content via as_str(); production tool results are block arrays (message.rs:289-298, content = Vec<ContentBlock>). Array content => None => never pruned. So the only compaction that runs in the live loop does nothing to real tool results; headroom is held solely by cap_oversized_tool_results (run.rs:912). The estimator and the cap were widened for blocks (dirge-el3n); this pass was not. Fix: handle block-array content in prune_tool_outputs. (Closely tied to the summarize_fn-dead issue.)
close_reason: prune_tool_outputs now reads text from both scalar-string and block-array content and preserves the array shape on rewrite; the in-loop prune pass (run.rs:378) is no longer a no-op on real tool results. Tests: prune_handles_block_array_content, prune_leaves_small_block_array_untouched.

title: allow_tools does not restrict MCP or plugin tools (reads as a whitelist, only caps built-ins)
description: context/agent_defs.rs:72-83: ToolPolicy::Allow is realized as 'deny every built-in not in the allow-list' over BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES. MCP/plugin tools are not enumerable there, so a profile allow_tools: [read, grep] leaves every MCP/plugin tool fully callable. Users reasonably read allow_tools as a hard whitelist. Fix: extend allow_tools to deny non-listed MCP/plugin tools (e.g. deny mcp_tool umbrella + unlisted plugin tools), or document the limitation prominently.
close_reason: Added plugin_tool to BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES so ToolPolicy::Allow->deny denies the plugin umbrella (mcp_tool was already covered). allow_tools is now a genuine cap over built-ins + MCP + plugin; listing an umbrella re-allows that class. Doc corrected, test added.

title: edit_minified is invisible to the verifier gate and misclassified by the storm breaker
description: verifier.rs:76 hardcodes match tool_name { write|edit|apply_patch }, so an agent editing exclusively via edit_minified (a registered built-in, tools/mod.rs:97) never sets edited_code and the 'verify before done' gate stays silent on unverified code changes. Separately, permission/engine/build.rs:42-62 maps edit/apply_patch/write to Operation::Edit but edit_minified falls through to Operation::Other, so storm (storm.rs:209) does not treat it as mutating (a verify-read after a minified edit can be miscounted). MITIGATED: edit_minified presents as 'edit' to enforce, so plan-mode permission denies still cover it. Fix: derive verifier + storm classification from tool_operation and add edit_minified to the Edit mapping.
close_reason: Added edit_minified->Edit and read_minified->Read to tool_operation (fixes storm default_mutating/default_exempt) and edit_minified to the verifier gate's edit arm. Tests added.

title: /agent off reverts prompt + tools but not the model (model swap leaks)
description: cmd_model.rs:389-408: /agent off clears current_agent/current_prompt/current_prompt_name/current_prompt_deny_tools and rebuilds, but never restores session.model to its pre-profile value. After /agent architect (model->opus) then /agent off, the session stays on opus. Also profile->profile switches where the new profile has no model leave the prior profile's model active. Documented as a deliberate asymmetry but a sharp edge. Fix: snapshot and restore the model on 'off'.
close_reason: Unified composition layer (ContextFiles::recompute_composition): /prompt and /agent are independent layers folded into the effective fields. Denies UNION (no clobber); /agent off restores prompt layer + pre-agent model (model_before_agent). All set-sites routed through layer setters. 6 composition unit tests.

title: summarization_provider and subagent_provider are dead config (advertised as live)
description: Both role keys (config/mod.rs:319-321,449-456) are resolved only for the /agents status display (cmd_misc.rs:72,85) and tests — never consulted at runtime. Compaction always uses session.model (dispatch.rs:115); subagents use the profile/main model (task.rs:440, main.rs:816-832). docs/agents.md:139-140 and the /agents view claim they route work elsewhere. Confirmed by provider + session reviews. Fix: either consume them or stop advertising them.
close_reason: summarization_provider + subagent_provider now consumed at runtime; docs corrected; test added.

title: Route task/subagent + /plan phases to named agent profiles (dirge-ykeu Phase 4)
description: Phases 1-3 (define profiles, /agent switch of the main loop's model+prompt+tools, unified /agents view) are merged. Phase 4: let the task tool spawn a subagent using a named agent profile (its model + system prompt + tool policy) via an optional 'agent' Args field, and let /plan phases bind to profiles. Requires threading the AgentRegistry into TaskTool (built in build_loop_tools) and the plan runtime. Also: cross-provider client switching for /agent (currently same-client model string only). Separate PR — touches the subagent/plan execution path.
notes: task(agent=<name>) subagent routing implemented in PR #386 (branch feat/agent-subagent-routing): per-subagent model + system prompt via a startup-resolved process-global routing table. REMAINING in this issue: (1) route /plan phases to named profiles, (2) cross-provider client switching for /agent (currently same-client model string), (3) apply profile reasoning/temperature on the subagent path.

title: Config-driven, opt-in multi-agent mechanism (unify role-routing + critic + user agents)
description: dirge already routes ROLES to different models via config (provider / review_provider[critic] / escalation_provider / compact_model / subagent). Forge generalizes this: user-defined agents in .forge/agents/*.md (id, system prompt, tools, model, reasoning), lazy-loaded into a registry, each overriding model/provider, layered CWD->global->builtin; agents become /agent-<name> commands. DESIGN GOAL for dirge: a UNIFIED, OPT-IN, config-driven agent mechanism where the existing roles (critic/escalation/summarizer/subagent) are special built-in agents and users can define custom ones (name + prompt + tools + model + reasoning) in config.json or .dirge/agents/. Opt-in: absent config = today's single-agent behavior unchanged. Brings: /agent-<name> commands, per-agent model override. Reuse: adapt Forge's agent_definition.rs schema + agent_registry.rs (forge_repo/forge_services). HIGH value, needs a design spike first. Source: ~/src/forgecode.
design:
UNIFIED AGENT MECHANISM (opt-in, config-driven). Generalize the existing ConfigRole (Default/Review/Escalation/Summarization/Subagent/Critic/Approval — each a config key routing to a providers-alias model via resolve_role) into a named AGENT = { prompt, model, tools, reasoning, temperature }. Built-in roles become reserved built-in agents (today model-only); users can define custom agents and override built-ins (e.g. give the critic a custom prompt). role->model becomes the model slice of agent->{model,prompt,tools}.

CONFIG SOURCE (decided: BOTH, layered): (a) .dirge/agents/<name>.md — YAML frontmatter + prompt body, SAME shape as dirge skills (reuse the skill frontmatter parser), project dir + global ~/.config/dirge/agents/; (b) config.json agents:{ name: { model, prompt, tools:{allow|deny}, reasoning, temperature } } for inline one-offs. PRECEDENCE: project file > global file > config.json > built-in. No agents configured => today's single-agent behavior unchanged (fully additive/opt-in). The existing *_provider keys stay as backward-compat sugar that sets a built-in agent's model.

AGENT FIELDS: name; prompt (inline | @file | named dirge prompt | none->default); model (Option<providers alias>; None->default, reuses resolve_role plumbing); tools (all | {allow:[..]} | {deny:[..]} — reuse prompt deny_tools enforcement at the permission layer); reasoning effort/budget; temperature.

INVOCATION: /agent interactive picker + dynamic /agent-<name> tab-completable commands that switch the active agent (prompt/model/tools) for the session (reuse Forge's sanitize+register from plans/2025-09-13-dynamic-agent-slash-commands-v3.md). Later: /plan phases + the task subagent can target a named agent.

REUSE (port, don't rewrite): Forge crates/forge_repo/src/agent_definition.rs (schema/frontmatter) + crates/forge_services/src/agent_registry.rs (lazy-load + layering + precedence) + plans/2025-09-08-agent-loader-cwd-extension-v1.md (CWD->global->builtin precedence) + plans/2025-09-13-dynamic-agent-slash-commands-v3.md. dirge side: reuse src/skill.rs frontmatter parser, config resolve_role plumbing, prompt deny_tools enforcement.

PHASED DELIVERY: (1) AgentDefinition + loader (both sources) + registry with precedence + guardrail test — LOAD ONLY, no behavior change. (2) /agent picker + /agent-<name> commands switch active agent. (3) fold built-in roles (critic/review/escalation/summarizer/subagent/approval) into the registry; *_provider keys become sugar. (4) optional — route /plan phases + task subagents to named agents.
notes: Phases 1-3 implemented in PR #385 (branch feat/agent-profiles), all CI green: AgentDefinition/AgentRegistry loader (3 layered sources), /agent <name> switching (model+prompt+tools, same-client), /agents unified view incl. built-in role routing. Built-in critic/role routing untouched (opt-in preserved). Phase 4 (route task/subagent + /plan to named profiles; cross-provider /agent client switch) filed as separate follow-up.

title: Add non-interactive 'resume session by id' CLI flag
description: dirge has -p/--print (one-shot) and -r/--resume (INTERACTIVE session browse). A non-interactive 'resume a specific session by id' flag (e.g. --session <id> / --continue) is missing. Prereq for the shell-plugin (:prefix) mode: that plugin keeps all : commands in one conversation by passing a stable id to a headless dirge -p run. Add the flag + wire it to load the named session before the print/loop run. From Forge codebase review (forge --conversation-id).

title: Tool-description quality audit (adopt Forge's standard)
description: Forge's docs/tool-guidelines.md codifies tool-description quality (what / when / when-NOT / param meanings / limitations; <=1024 chars, enforced by tests). dirge's descriptions are strong on some tools (with_contract_hint) but variable elsewhere. Audit every tool's description() and upgrade weak ones to the standard. Docs-only; improves model tool-selection accuracy. Low effort. Source: ~/src/forgecode/docs/tool-guidelines.md. From Forge codebase review.
close_reason: Merged in #381 — descriptions audited + 1024-char guardrail test

title: Internal nudges (todo/verifier/critic) misattributed to <you> in the UI
description: The todo-completion nudge ('You still have N unfinished todos...'), and likely the verifier/critic follow-ups, are injected as LoopMessage::User so the model responds to them — but the UI renders them with the <you> handle, attributing system-generated steering to the user. They should display with a distinct system/critic attribution (or be hidden from the visible transcript), not as user input. Relates to dirge-vcsn (poll_finalization_follow_up) which builds these as User messages.
close_reason: Merged in #375 — nudges attributed to <critic> via finalization_nudge_body

title: /compact (explicit) should force compaction, not be gated by threshold
description: When the loop auto-compacts it correctly checks context is over threshold (should_compress / decide_after_usage). But an explicit user /compact command is currently treated like a suggestion — it appears to be gated by the same threshold check, so /compact below the threshold is a no-op. Explicit /compact must be definitive: force the compaction pass regardless of current context fraction. Separate the 'auto' path (threshold-gated) from the 'user-commanded' path (forced).

title: Unify the mid-loop interjection layer (5+ inject sites)
description: Steering, context_depth, verifier, critic, todo-nudge, and reflexion each have their own trigger + channel + integration point, firing at three different places in run.rs (inner poll ~:69, finalize gates :1327-1377, storm guard :1002) with ad-hoc precedence. Notably verifier silently blocks critic via 'if follow_up.is_empty()' so the critic verdict never surfaces on a turn that also has a red build, and MID_TURN_STEER_WRAPPER is applied at two independent sites. Design + build one interjection abstraction with explicit priority and a single wrap-once point. MED — top consolidation target for a smoother loop.
close_reason: Merged in #366 — poll_finalization_follow_up single precedence authority

title: Single context-budget authority (estimate+thresholds+action)
description: Three modules independently decide 'context is filling': compression.rs (pre-send per-result caps + should_compress), context_manager.rs (post-response fold/exit tiers), context_depth.rs (ambient reminder).
