title: chamber_row uses chars().count() instead of display width
description: Tool output with CJK/emoji misaligns right border. chamber_row_centered already uses UnicodeWidthStr::width.

title: Terminal escape sequences leak to shell on quit
description: On exit, dirge prints raw responses (OSC 11, DSR 63;1R, primary DA ?64;...) to the shell. Suggests a terminal query was sent during init but its response wasn't consumed before leaving raw mode.

title: MCP panel shows (none) on startup
description: Right-hand info panel shows MCP servers as (none) when the app starts, only populates after first key press.

title: Permission alert chamber overlaps previous chamber
description: When the permission prompt renders, it draws on top of the just-written tool chamber instead of below it.

title: Input soft-wrap overflows when content_width is capped
description: On wide terminals (>120 cols), bottom_indent is non-zero but wrap_width is computed as cols-3 instead of cols-3-bottom_indent. Resulting input text overflows the centered content band and the terminal hard-wraps unpredictably, looking like 'wrap is broken'.

title: P4d: harness/set-label, harness/fork, harness/navigate-tree, etc.
description: Plugin-level access to the session tree: bookmark labels (harness/set-label), programmatic branching (harness/fork, harness/new-session, harness/switch-session, harness/navigate-tree). Mirrors pi's ctx.fork / ctx.newSession / ctx.navigateTree.

title: P4c: /tree, /fork, /clone slash commands + UI
description: Interactive picker for /tree navigation. /fork [entry-id|HEAD] branches off (restores prompt into editor). /clone [entry-id] duplicates the path. ASCII tree renderer; label badges in chat view.

title: P4b: switch session storage to node-based (parent links)
description: Replace Session::messages: Vec<SessionMessage> with a node-based SessionTree { entries: HashMap<id, entry>, leaf_id, root_id, parents }. convert_history walks the parent chain from leaf_id. Update compaction, undo, retry to be branch-aware. Backwards compat: legacy linear sessions auto-convert to a degenerate root→...→leaf chain on load.

title: P4a: per-message timestamps + IDs (data model enrichment)
description: Add timestamp: i64 and id: String fields to SessionMessage. Backwards compat via serde defaults (existing sessions load with default IDs and zero timestamps). Update render_session to interleave messages and extra_entries by timestamp, closing the known P2 limitation. Unlocks P4b's node-based addressing.

title: P4: session tree (fork / clone / tree navigation)
description: Switch Session::messages from Vec<Message> to a node-based SessionTree { entries: HashMap<EntryId, Entry>, leaf_id, root_id, parents }. convert_history walks back from leaf_id. New slash commands: /tree (interactive picker), /fork [entry-id], /clone [entry-id]. New harness APIs: harness/set-label, harness/navigate-tree, harness/fork, harness/new-session, harness/switch-session. Versioned session file format with legacy linear-to-tree auto-convert. UI: ASCII tree renderer + label badges. Largest blast radius of the pi parity work — touches Session, persistence, compaction, undo, retry.
close_reason: split into dirge-45n / dirge-wdc / dirge-3va / dirge-16l

title: P1: plugin-registered custom providers
description: (harness/register-provider name spec) lets plugins add LLM providers at startup. Spec carries base-url, api-key-env, api (e.g. :openai-completions), and an explicit models array. After plugin load, main.rs merges plugin specs into the runtime provider registry (same place cfg.custom_providers feeds in). --list-models and /model see plugin-registered models alongside built-ins. Plugins re-register every startup (matches pi's no-persist behavior).

title: P3: granular streaming events (message_start/update/end + turn_start/end)
description: Wire new plugin hook events from the post-P0 turn loop: on-message-start, on-message-update (batched to ~100ms or N tokens), on-message-end (with replace-message slot), on-turn-start, on-turn-end carrying message + tool-results context. on-message-end can return a replacement Janet table that the host re-serializes and uses as the persisted assistant message.

title: P2: custom message renderers via plugin
description: Plugins can register typed message renderers and append typed entries. New harness APIs: harness/append-entry, harness/register-renderer. Session::messages gains extra_entries: Vec<PluginEntry> with custom_type, data (JSON), display flag. UI render_session walks both regular messages and extra entries in timestamp order, calling registered renderer (returns [color text] pairs) per plugin entry. Persists across session save/load. Fallback renderer for unknown custom_type from a no-longer-installed plugin.

title: P0: runner turn-boundary detection (TurnStart/TurnEnd events)
description: Refactor run_stream in src/agent/runner.rs to detect turn boundaries within rig's multi_turn stream and emit AgentEvent::TurnStart / AgentEvent::TurnEnd between iterations. Preparatory for P3 (granular streaming events) and P4 (session tree branch-aware accounting). One Turn = assistant message + tool calls + tool results before the next LLM call.

title: R2: add top-5 missing plugin tests + FFI edge cases
description: From the plugin coverage audit: worker init-failure path, load_file with missing path, store_response round-trip, on-tool-end fires when inner returned Err, concurrent dispatch_tool_hook serialization. Plus FFI edge cases for read_string_arg (keyword/symbol/buffer), read_string_array_arg (empty + tuple-vs-array), wrap_string (empty / multibyte UTF-8). Depends on R1 so the worker init / dialog code is stable.

title: Phase 3b: Janet worker thread + harness/confirm + harness/select
description: Move JanetClient onto a dedicated OS thread so harness/confirm and harness/select can block synchronously from Janet without deadlocking the current_thread tokio UI. Replaces the unsafe impl Send/Sync on PluginManager with a real worker channel. Adds DialogRequest channel the UI loop drains via tokio::select to render confirms and selects. Was originally part of Phase 3 but the refactor is large enough (~2 days) to warrant its own phase.

title: Phase 4: plugin input transform via on-prompt return
description: on-prompt hook returning a string can now replace (not just prepend) the user prompt. Backwards-compat: only replace when a new harness flag (harness/replace-prompt) is set. TDD: replace-prompt fires verbatim; default behavior preserved.

title: Phase 3: UI primitives from Janet (notify/confirm/select)
description: (harness/notify msg level), (harness/confirm title question), (harness/select title opts). Notify drains into a renderer queue. Confirm/select piggyback the existing AskRequest/QuestionResponse channels. Janet's !Send lock must be released around the oneshot await. TDD: notify queue ordering, confirm/select via mock channel.

title: Phase 1: plugin tool hooks (block / mutate-input / replace-result)
description: Wrap each rig Tool in a HookedTool<T> that calls into PluginManager before/after T::call. New harness APIs: harness/block, harness/mutate-input, harness/replace-result write to PluginManager slots that Rust reads after dispatch. TDD: slot read tests, integration test that block returns ToolError, mutate replaces args via JSON roundtrip, replace-result swaps output. Touches src/plugin/mod.rs and src/agent/builder.rs. Closes gap with pi's tool_call/tool_result mutation extension API.

title: Phase 2: plugin-registered slash commands
description: (harness/register-command "mycmd" "my-handler-fn") records {cmd -> fn} in PluginManager. handle_slash falls through to plugin command dispatch when not a built-in. /help lists registered commands. TDD: registration at load, dispatch via slash, /help inclusion.

title: Phase 2B: true mid-execution interjection (turn-boundary inject)
description: Builds on 2A. Wire interject_rx into runner. Convert run_stream/multi_turn into a manual turn loop so between stream iterations we can drain queue and inject as a user message into history. Touches src/agent/runner.rs:138-225 and the rig multi_turn flow. Depends on dirge-qq2 wait, the 2A issue.

title: Phase 3: right-side info panel (cwd, MCP, LSP, todos, modified files)
description: Carve right ~32 cols (auto-hide when terminal narrower than ~100). Sources: cwd from env, MCP from McpClientManager.handles, LSP via new public accessor on LspManager, todos from TODO_LIST mutex, modified files via new shared Arc<Mutex<IndexSet<PathBuf>>> populated by Write/Edit/ApplyPatch tools. New /panel on|off toggle. Default on when wide enough.

title: Phase 2A: queue user input while agent is running
description: Remove 'agent is busy' guard at src/ui/mod.rs:663 and :720 for plain text. Push to VecDeque<String> interjection_queue. Show queue count + dim preview above input. Esc/Ctrl-X drops most recent. Ctrl-C still aborts. Slash commands stay gated to current allow-list. On AgentEvent::Done, drain queue and run as next turn.

title: Phase 1: soft-wrap input box instead of horizontal scroll
description: Replace horizontal scroll logic in src/ui/renderer.rs::draw_bottom (lines ~500-660) with display-column wrap. Drop input_scroll_offset; compute visual rows from logical lines wrapped to visible_width; keep MAX_INPUT_VISIBLE_LINES cap with vertical scroll keeping cursor visible. Move or guard the token counter so it doesn't collide with wrapped text. Add a unit test for cursor (logical -> visual) mapping.

title: Plugin prepare-next-run model swap can't hop providers (apply_next_model uses fixed client)
close_reason: Plugin prepare-next-run swaps now hop providers. New prepare_next_model_client runs before AgentBuildDeps is built (where &mut client is free), resolves via resolve_model_switch, and on a cross-provider match builds+installs the target client in place; apply_next_model then rebuilds the agent from it and updates session.provider only when the client actually moved. Warn+skip on unroutable family or client build error. 4 new tests (client hops deepseek->glm, same-provider rename keeps client, unconfigured family skip, empty/unchanged skip); e2e-verified via a prepare-next-run plugin + dual mock endpoints (turn 1 -> deepseek, turn 2 -> glm).

title: mcp_server: resolve session pointer + project dir via ProjectPaths (not raw current_dir)
description:
Last remaining site from the dirge-vpma.17 audit (item 5). extras/mcp_server.rs:556 serve() sets project_dir = std::env::current_dir()? and passes it to load_or_create_pointer + State. So launching 'dirge mcp' from a repo subdirectory writes <subdir>/.dirge/mcp_current_session.json and resolves the session DB there, while the CLI's own session DB + memory anchor at the git root via ProjectPaths — the same split-brain vpma.17 fixed for plugins/config/prompts/agents.

Fix: route project_dir through crate::extras::dirge_paths::project_root(&current_dir) (git-root walk-up + DIRGE_PROJECT_ROOT). Kept separate from vpma.17 because project_dir also seeds the MCP server's session DB, so it shifts MCP session resolution and wants its own verification (confirm the pointer + attached session land at the repo root when launched from a subdir).

title: cli: --no-session exclusion policy lives in three places
description:
src/main.rs:1602, 1681 (ssot). session_id_for_agent (main.rs:90-96, with tests pinning the --no-session -> None contract) is used only by the --print path (1459); the --loop (1602) and interactive (1681) build_agent calls inline Some(session.id.to_string()) unconditionally, ignoring cli.no_session. Today the divergence is harmless (a never-persisted id gets excluded), but the policy lives in three places — any change to the helper (e.g. folded-id handling) silently misses two of the three agent-build sites.
Fix: route all three through the helper. Audit item 49.

title: cli: 'dirge sandbox setup' without sandbox-microvm silently exits 0
description:
src/main.rs:588-593 (structural). dirge sandbox setup on a build without the sandbox-microvm feature silently returns Ok(()) with no output. User runs setup, gets exit 0 and no message, believes the sandbox is configured; nothing was checked, built, or written.
Fix: print "not built with sandbox-microvm" and exit nonzero. Audit item 48.

title: cli: resolve_mode computed twice at interactive startup — warnings print twice
description:
src/main.rs:227 + 1718 (ssot). Interactive startup computes resolve_mode(&cli, &cfg) twice (inside build_channels, again before run_interactive) and re-set_modes the checker with the identical result; resolve_mode has warning side effects. dirge --yolo --restrictive: "warning: conflicting permission flags..." (and the config-vs-CLI override warning) printed twice on every interactive launch; the duplicated computation is a drift point.
Fix: compute once, pass through. Audit item 47.

title: input: word-delete skips paste-slot tombstoning — large bodies retained unreachable
description:
src/ui/input.rs:1222-1237 (structural). DeleteWordBack/DeleteWordForward use replace_range instead of remove_range, so a paste marker fully inside the deleted range is removed from the buffer without tombstoning its pastes slot (contrast Backspace/Delete at 698-711, whose comment says slots are tombstoned so bodies can be GC'd). Paste a large block, Alt-delete over the placeholder: the (potentially ~1MB) body stays resident in pastes until submit/set_text; unlike kills the text isn't in the kill-ring, so the retained body is unreachable.
Fix: use remove_range. Audit item 46.

title: input: file-picker anchors on rfind('@') — '@' in query breaks splice
description:
src/ui/input.rs:909, 934, 971, 992 (logic). File-picker exit paths anchor on self.buffer.rfind('@'), but the picker's catch-all char arm (920-925) also feeds '@' into the query and buffer, so a later '@' inside the query becomes the anchor. Type @foo@bar then Esc: rfind lands on the second '@', at+1+query.len() overshoots the buffer, and @foo is left behind as stray text (Enter splices the selected path against the wrong anchor).
Fix: record the anchor position when the picker opens instead of re-finding. Audit item 45.

title: input: Yank/YankPop don't reset history_pos — Up discards yanked edit
description:
src/ui/input.rs:1239-1252, 1254-1284 (logic). Yank/YankPop insert into the buffer without resetting history_pos (every other buffer-mutating path does: typed char 1586, insert_str 690, Ctrl+J 1483). Recall a history entry with Up, Ctrl+Y to yank into it, press Up again: LineUp sees history_pos.is_some() (1303) and continues history navigation, silently discarding the yank-edited buffer — the identical flow with a typed character keeps the edit.
Fix: reset history_pos on yank. Audit item 44.

title: input: >1MB paste truncation bypasses placeholder collapse
description:
src/ui/input.rs:635-647 (structural). The >1MB paste truncation path calls insert_str with up to 1MB of raw text, bypassing the placeholder collapse that even a 4-line paste receives (collapse check at 649-650 is only reached below the cap). Paste a 2MB log: input box holds ~1MB of raw multi-line text rendered every frame — the exact buffer/re-render bloat the UI-6 comment says the cap exists to prevent.
Fix: collapse the truncated text into a placeholder too. Audit item 43.

title: highlight: markdown alias maps to nonexistent rules arm
description:
src/ui/highlight.rs:90 (dead-code). normalize_lang maps "md"|"markdown" => "markdown", but rules_for has no "markdown" arm, so the alias is a no-op (resolves to None as the unmapped string would) and the module doc's claimed Markdown coverage (line 9) doesn't exist — markdown fences take the uniform fallback color.
Fix: add markdown rules or drop the alias + doc claim. Audit item 42.

title: highlight: multi-token keywords unreachable (Clojure defn-/if-not, Java non-sealed, Ruby defined?)
description:
src/ui/highlight.rs:363 (dead-code/logic). rules.string_delims.is_empty() && c == '-' in is_ident_cont is always false: every Rules table defines at least one string delimiter, so '-' never continues an identifier (and '?' likewise never matches). The multi-token keyword entries never highlight: Clojure defn-/if-not/if-let/if-some/when-not/when-let/when-some/in-ns (506-551), Java non-sealed (841), Ruby defined? (631). if-not renders as if (keyword) + - (plain) + not (keyword).
Fix: per-language ident-continue chars instead of the string_delims proxy. Audit item 41.

title: renderer: cache_bottom dirty check ignores picker_overlay contents
description:
src/ui/renderer.rs:2751-2758 (ssot drift risk). cache_bottom's dirty detection compares only picker_overlay.is_some(), not contents, so moving the picker selection changes the overlay without marking the frame dirty. Currently masked because the picker key path (ui/mod.rs:2360-2362) calls request_repaint() explicitly — any future caller relying on set_bottom's own change detection (its stated contract) paints a stale picker.
Fix: compare contents (hash or Eq). Audit item 40.

title: renderer: wrap_editor trim_start orphans whitespace from row spans — cursor misplaced
description:
src/ui/renderer.rs:3114-3132 (logic). wrap_editor's word-boundary break does suffix.trim_start(), so the trimmed whitespace bytes belong to no row's [row_start, row_end] span; a cursor byte inside that trimmed run matches no row and keeps a stale/default (0,0) position. Input line with >=2 consecutive spaces straddling a wrap boundary, cursor arrowed onto the second space: cursor paints at the wrong row/col. Related: dirge-culo (fold wrap algorithms) — fixing there may subsume this.
Audit item 39.

title: renderer: copy_to_clipboard stdin write unbounded — UI freeze on broken helper
description:
src/ui/renderer.rs:3225-3255 (logic). The 2-second bounded wait covers only try_wait; the preceding stdin.write_all is unbounded. A clipboard helper that spawns but never reads stdin (broken XWayland, xclip without $DISPLAY) plus a selection larger than the pipe buffer (~64KB) blocks write_all forever, freezing the UI thread — the wedge the timeout was added to prevent.
Fix: bound the write (thread + timeout, or nonblocking pipe). Audit item 38.

title: renderer: unreachable scroll clamp branches
description:
src/ui/renderer.rs:1709-1711 and 2300-2302 (dead-code). The else-if self.scroll_offset > max_offset clamp branches in replace_from and anchor_after_resize_delta are unreachable: they are only reached when scroll_offset == 0 (usize), and 0 > max_offset is never true.
Fix: remove or restructure so the clamp is live. Audit item 37.

title: renderer: stream() vs commit_partial() invariant unenforced (latent corruption)
description:
src/ui/renderer.rs:2240-2270 + 1941-1960 (structural, latent). stream() assumes the open streamed block is source.last() occupying the last open_rows buffer rows, but commit_partial() (called first inside stream) appends a Plain block + rows WITHOUT sealing the stream. If a partial from Renderer::write (no trailing newline) existed while streaming, stream() would truncate the wrong tail rows and overwrite the Plain block, leaving a stale Markdown block whose rows count mismatches the buffer — duplicated/corrupted content on next resize rebuild(). Currently unreachable (the only write() caller, run_handlers/done.rs:271, commits via write_line before any next stream()), but the invariant is enforced on every append path except this one — any new write()-during-stream call corrupts sync.
Fix: seal or assert in commit_partial when streaming. Audit item 36.

title: memory_db: overview in-place refresh bypasses grow-must-compact rule
description:
src/extras/memory_db.rs:887-925 vs 1119-1136 (structural). The hot-budget grow-must-compact rule (dirge-catw) is enforced when an entry grows via replace_entry (make_room_in_hot with cost 0 at 1124-1136) but not on the overview in-place refresh branch of add_entry, which UPDATEs content and commits with no compaction. Hot tier near budget + agent refreshes overview with a much larger body (passes only the whole-budget gate at 868-875): hot tier renders over char_limit_for in every system prompt until some unrelated insert triggers make_room_in_hot.
Fix: run make_room_in_hot on the refresh branch too. Audit item 35.

title: storage: .conflict-*.json siblings never cleaned — parse as Sessions, resurrect deleted ids
description:
src/session/storage.rs:175-176, 287-301, 351-368, 395-406, 426-437, 503-513 (structural). <id>.conflict-<ts>.json siblings are written on save conflicts but never cleaned up, and every directory scan filters only extension == "json", so conflict files parse as full Sessions carrying the ORIGINAL id/origin. Session with a conflict sibling; delete the session (delete_session removes only <id>.json + assets): recents still lists it (parsed from the conflict file); resume via --session <id>: exact load fails, the prefix fallback (main.rs:847-863) matches the conflict file and unwrap_or(m) resurrects the deleted session — additionally with the SwitchSession-class bypasses (no mtime, no migration) — and its first save re-creates <id>.json. Newer conflict copies can also shadow the real file in find_sessions_by_prefix/recent_project_sessions.
Fix: exclude .conflict-* from scans; clean up on delete; consider retention policy. Audit item 34.

title: plugin: hook-error dedup count off by one
description:
src/plugin/worker.rs:494-514 + src/plugin/mod.rs:1185-1198 (logic). The first (already displayed) push sets harness-last-hook-err-count to 1 and both flush sites print the raw count, so N total occurrences render as the original entry plus "(repeated N times)" — overstating by one. The Rust-side comment (mod.rs:1178-1183) documents intended behavior as 50 errors -> "repeated 49 times", which the Janet code contradicts.
Fix: print count-1 (or start at 0). Audit item 33.

title: plugin: harness/notify blob unescaped — multiline messages truncated, level spoofable
description:
src/plugin/worker.rs:448-463 + src/plugin/mod.rs:1207-1218 (logic). harness/notify appends msg into the level	msg
 blob without harness/-escape (every other harness blob escapes; sanitize-hook-err exists precisely because this wire format can't carry raw newlines/tabs), and drain_notifications drops any line lacking a tab. Everything after the first 
 in a notify message is silently lost, and a crafted "x
error	fake" payload injects a forged entry with a spoofed level. (harness/notify "build failed:
see log") shows only "build failed:".
Fix: escape on write, unescape on drain. Audit item 32.

title: dap: DebugPanelData.scopes never populated, .breakpoints never read
description:
src/dap/session.rs:1209-1210 (dead-code). DebugPanelData.scopes is never populated by any producer (debug_snapshot hardcodes Vec::new(); nothing else constructs the struct outside tests) and DebugPanelData.breakpoints is populated but never read — panels.rs derives its BREAKPOINTS box solely from summary.breakpoint_count/function_breakpoint_count (728-732). Both directions are dead data cloned on every UI snapshot.
Fix: drop the fields or wire them. Audit item 31.

title: dap: step() holds active mutex across wait_for_stopped — pause blocked 30s
description:
src/dap/session.rs:882-939 (structural). The release-lock-while-parked invariant (dirge-acgj) is enforced only in continue_; step() holds the active mutex across wait_for_stopped(timeout), so a concurrent pause/evaluate blocks on the mutex for the step's full timeout — the same "pause can't interrupt a free-running program" window the continue_ comment (716-721) declares fixed. step_over onto a blocking line (stdin read, long computation) with no stop event: pause() queued behind the lock for the full 30s default.
Fix: release while parked, as continue_ does. Audit item 30.

title: dap: terminate() propagates error before clearing session state
description:
src/dap/session.rs:1116-1133 (structural). terminate() propagates the RPC error with ? BEFORE setting SessionStatus::Terminated and never clears active on failure — the stuck-dead-session class dirge-ibq5 explicitly fixed in disconnect() (1145-1160) but not here. Adapter crashes mid-session: terminate returns ConnectionClosed, dead session stays active with stale status; every subsequent op errors until the user thinks to disconnect.
Fix: always-clear like disconnect(). Audit item 29.

title: dap: breakpoint_count counts files not breakpoints; remove leaves empty record
description:
src/dap/session.rs:182 with 671-678 (logic). summary().breakpoint_count sums Vec<BreakpointRecord> lengths, i.e. counts records (exactly 1 per touched file), not breakpoints inside them; and set_breakpoints(file, vec![]) (the remove path) inserts an empty record instead of removing the map entry. Set a breakpoint, then remove_breakpoints: adapter has zero breakpoints but format_sessions (debug.rs:687) and the debug panel (panels.rs:728) keep reporting one for the rest of the session.
Fix: count actual breakpoints; remove the entry on empty set. Audit item 28.

title: provider: DeepSeek/GLM base URLs duplicated — dead unwrap_or copies drift
description:
src/provider/client.rs:446-458 (ssot + dead-code). DeepSeek and GLM default base URLs are stated twice: in resolve_provider_base_url (191-198, the authoritative config > env > default resolver) and again as unwrap_or(...) fallbacks at client construction (449, 453-457). The unwrap_or arms are unreachable — the resolver always returns Some for these kinds and is the only base_url source (342). A future endpoint change edits one literal but not the other; if the resolver is later refactored to return None, the stale URL silently becomes live. Note the OpenCode fallback at 463 IS live (resolver returns None for it) — easy to mistake the dead copies for load-bearing.
Fix: drop the dead fallbacks (or panic with a message). Audit item 27.

title: rig_stream_factory: dump_turn reasoning flag passed additional.is_some()
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/rig_stream_factory.rs:262-281 (telemetry). dump_turn's final parameter is reasoning: bool (wire.rs:85-93) but the call passes additional.is_some(), also true when only headers or metadata are present with reasoning off. With DIRGE_DUMP_REQUESTS set and headers/metadata configured but thinking disabled, wire dumps label the turn reasoning-enabled — wrong signal for the debugging sessions the dump exists for. Latent today (headers/metadata unset in production).
Fix: pass the actual reasoning-enabled flag. Audit item 26.

title: rig_stream_factory: merged_request_headers puts Authorization into request body
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/rig_stream_factory.rs:773-789 with 745-751 (structural, latent). merged_request_headers injects Authorization: Bearer <api_key> plus opts.headers under additional_params["headers"], but rig serde-flattens additional_params into the JSON request BODY (rig-core 0.39 openai/completion/mod.rs:1197 #[serde(flatten)]); no rig provider extracts a body-level "headers" field as HTTP headers. If anyone wires the pi-parity LoopConfig.get_api_key/api_key/headers knobs (currently always None/empty in production; only tests set them), the request (a) fails to authenticate and (b) ships the bearer secret inside the body to the endpoint where it can be logged. The mechanism as written cannot work.
Fix: remove or rewire through real HTTP headers. Audit item 25.

title: rig_stream: gap-timeout message reports remaining budget, not stall duration
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/rig_stream.rs:188-200 (diagnostic). The tool-call-gap timeout message reports t.as_secs() where t is the remaining narrowed budget (gap - elapsed, clamped to 1ms at 169), not the actual stall. Consumer-side delay before the wait (or the remaining.is_zero() clamp) yields "stream chunk timed out after 0s while a tool call was mid-assembly", contradicting the 60s figure in the same sentence. Retry classification unaffected ("timed out" substring intact).
Fix: report the configured gap (or measured stall). Audit item 24.

title: rig_stream: stream-end keeps incomplete delta-built tool calls (empty name)
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/rig_stream.rs:475-497 (structural). Stream-end finalization has no validation for entries still in open_tool_calls: a delta-built call that never received its complete ToolCall event is included as-is (name possibly "" from the 411-415 placeholder, arguments truncated JSON), still flips stop_reason to ToolUse, and no ToolCallEnd delta is emitted (removal happens only in the complete-ToolCall arm, 393). Verified against rig 0.39: chat-completions streaming silently drops an assembled call whose name never arrived, so dirge's block survives with an empty name — Done{ToolUse} dispatches tool "" and burns a turn on a spurious error. The mid-assembly invariant is enforced by the gap timeout while streaming but not at stream end.
Fix: validate/drop incomplete calls at finalization (and don't set ToolUse for them). Audit item 23.

title: agent_loop: ExitWithSummary never actually ends the turn
description:
src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:2113-2142 (structural). The PostUsageDecisionKind::ExitWithSummary arm is documented as force a final summary before ending the turn (context_manager.rs:117-119) and logs "forcing summary and ending turn", but nothing ends the turn — no break, has_more_tool_calls untouched — execution falls through to prepareNextTurn/steering. When the summarizer fails or the circuit breaker is open at >80% context, the loop iterates against a still-over-threshold context and the next request can overflow/400 — the case this defense-in-depth tier exists to prevent.
Fix: end the turn (break / clear has_more_tool_calls) after the forced summary attempt. Audit item 22.

title: ui: Ctrl+C busy-interrupt ignores shell_session in pre-mount window
description:
src/ui/mod.rs:1768-1823 (structural). The Ctrl+C busy-interrupt branch handles agent runners and phase handles but never checks ui.shell_session; during the ~120ms pre-mount grace window (shell_box_visible=false, PTY key routing at 1662 not yet active) Ctrl+C during a !/!! run clears is_running, prints interrupted, drops queued messages — while the shell child keeps running. With is_running false, a typed prompt submits immediately, running an agent turn concurrently with the shell session (bypassing the busy gate at 2510-2525); on exit a Visible command's output is still fed to the agent despite the interrupt.
Fix: handle shell_session in the interrupt branch. Audit item 21.

title: ui: plugin shortcut dispatch swallows invoke_command Err
description:
src/ui/mod.rs:2390 (logic, error path drops state). Plugin shortcut dispatch uses if let Ok(Some(msg)) = result with no else, while the equivalent slash-command path surfaces errors ("[plugin] {cmd} failed: {e}", slash/mod.rs:744-749). Janet handler exceptions go through the notification queue in both paths, but infra-level eval errors are lost only here — keypress consumed, nothing rendered or logged; shortcut appears silently dead.
Fix: surface Err like the slash path. Audit item 20.

title: ui: avatar forced Idle immediately after runner spawn (compaction resume, drains)
description:
src/ui/mod.rs:2830, 3867, 3889, 3911 and post-drain 3114, 3935, 4034, 4115 (logic). Avatar set to AvatarState::Idle right after install_into marks a run active (DeferPromptRun; compaction Next::Submit/Retry/Continue), and unconditionally after drain_interjections!() which may have just spawned a runner — the class fixed for the review phase in GH #621 (plan_review.rs uses AvatarState::settled(is_running); avatar.rs:239-247 asserts a busy runner must never settle on idle). Idle face for the whole time-to-first-token while the status line shows busy. Related epic: dirge-u3yw.
Fix: use AvatarState::settled(is_running) at these sites. Audit item 18.

title: Fold wrap algorithms (word_wrap, wrap_editor, Renderer::write chunking) onto soft_wrap
description: g007 (#PR) single-sourced the ANSI-aware visible-char/width primitive (visible_chars/visible_width). The wrap ALGORITHMS still differ: markdown word_wrap (break-on-space + byte-exact ANSI passthrough), renderer wrap_editor (cursor mapping, not ANSI-aware), and Renderer::write's char-chunk wrap. Fold them onto soft_wrap's tokenizer so the same text wraps identically everywhere; wrap_editor keeps its cursor mapping but builds on the shared tokenizer. Higher risk (visual regressions in markdown/editor) - needs careful golden-output testing.

title: Unify session fold lineage into one representation (origin column + reconciliation)
description: m7ja (#PR) made the SQLite fold-link write atomic + retried + logged, but the two lineage models still coexist: JSON effective_origin (flat origin_id, dedup_by_origin/resolve_to_tip) vs SQLite parent_session_id chain (resolve_parent walks it). Full fix: store origin_id on the sessions row (schema migration), derive lineage from origin on both sides, and add a reconciliation pass that repairs already-drifted DBs (rotated rows whose parent link never landed). Needs a migration on live session DBs — do carefully/supervised.
close_reason: Done in #657: SQLite lineage unified onto origin_id (single source of truth). resolve_parent is a single lookup, ensure_session_origin backfills legacy chains, link_fold/set_parent_session write origin atomically. parent_session_id kept as provenance. Cross-store repair of pre-fix broken chains intentionally out of scope (would need JSON I/O per open); going forward drift is structurally prevented.

title: Advisory code-review notices never reach live consumers (need a session-lived side channel)
description: The default Advisory review mode (run.rs:435) emits its SystemNotice via the per-turn loop event channel from a detached task that finishes AFTER the turn's AgentEnd. But the interactive UI drops the per-turn receiver at Done (ui/run_handlers/done.rs:191,330 set agent_rx=None) and the headless consumer breaks at Done (provider/run.rs:270), so neither ever sees the notice — Advisory mode is silently a no-op for real users. dirge-ioym fixed the related leak (detached tasks no longer hold the channel open past AgentEnd — they use WeakSender now) but deliberately did NOT add delivery. Fix: route detached advisory/checkpoint results to a SESSION-lived channel (broadcast or a dedicated background mpsc) that the UI selects on across turns, with an explicit post-AgentEnd contract; headless can drain or ignore it. Touches run.rs finalization + spawn_runner/integration.rs + the ui/mod.rs event select loop. Split from dirge-ioym.

title: Renderer::write streaming soft-wrap counts columns by char, not display width
description: src/ui/renderer.rs Renderer::write (~line 2549-2570): the token-streaming soft-wrap chunks by chars().count() against max_line_width and increments self.col by chars().count(), so a wide (CJK/emoji) glyph counts as one column instead of two — streaming assistant text with wide glyphs wraps too late and can overflow the terminal width. The width-aware wrap_line helper already exists; write was never migrated to it. Split from dirge-p985 (the /btw markdown collapse, fixed via write_styled_lines/write_line which bypasses this path). Affects the shared streaming path so it needs Renderer-level test coverage. Fix: make write's column accounting use UnicodeWidthChar like wrap_line.

title: LSP graceful shutdown/exit handshake before killing the server
description: Follow-up split from dirge-wupp. wupp's orphan-reaping core (shared child_guard: setsid + process-group SIGKILL) now reaps LSP/MCP/DAP subtrees on teardown, so servers no longer orphan grandchildren. Still missing: LSP teardown SIGKILLs the group without first sending the LSP 'shutdown' request + 'exit' notification, so servers don't get to flush/persist state. This needs an explicit async shutdown path on LspManager (Drop is synchronous and can't await), iterating servers to send shutdown+exit with a short timeout before the ProcessGroupGuards drop, plus a call site during app teardown. Deferred from wupp because it's async-teardown plumbing (new surface), separable, and lower-value than orphan-prevention (a group SIGKILL already prevents orphans).
close_reason: Implemented alongside dirge-6klk: LspClient::shutdown_and_exit (shutdown request -> ack -> exit notification, 2s bound) + LspManager::shutdown_all, called on the normal teardown path in main before the guards drop. Signal path still SIGKILLs directly (no time to handshake).

title: Unify wrap + ANSI-aware width measurement (reimplemented four ways)
description: Canonical wrap.rs::soft_wrap + visible_width (32-108); markdown.rs::word_wrap + iter_visible_chars (71-136, breaks only on space); renderer.rs::wrap_editor (3030-3116, not ANSI-aware); Renderer::write char-chunk wrap (2548-2567). Different break rules mean the same text wraps differently by path, and width fixes (the SGR-miscount fix in wrap.rs:28-31) must be repeated per site. Fix: fold word_wrap and write's chunking onto soft_wrap/visible_width; wrap_editor can keep cursor mapping but build on the shared tokenizer.
close_reason: Single-sourced the named duplication — the ANSI-aware visible-char/width primitive (#656). Folding the wrap ALGORITHMS onto soft_wrap (higher visual-regression risk) tracked as a follow-up.

title: compute_modified_rect diverges from RightPanel::render on short terminals
description: src/ui/tui/panels.rs:405-411 vs 519-532: compute_modified_rect returns None when a fixed sub-panel does not fit vertically, while render breaks the loop and still paints MODIFIED into the remaining space. On short terminals the MODIFIED list is painted but the mouse handler sees no rect -- wheel scroll over it is dead and tui_redraw force-resets modified_offset to 0. Fix: have render use compute_modified_rect for its MODIFIED geometry, or make both use identical skip semantics.

title: Consolidate the 100MiB in-place edit size cap duplicated across four tool files
description: src/agent/tools/edit.rs:168, edit_lines.rs:211, edit_minified.rs:25, apply_patch.rs:72 each re-declare the same 100MiB cap plus a copy-pasted meta.len()>CAP stanza with three different error-message shapes; write.rs has no cap. These sit at the text_io seam call sites but were not consolidated -- new divergence risk. Fix: one shared const + pre-read size-guard helper next to decode_for_edit.

title: Computer-use permission gate lives in Janet and only mirrors deny_tools, bypassing engine rules
description: src/plugin/worker.rs:805 (harness/check-computer-action): the computer-use desktop-action gate is implemented in Janet and consults only the deny_tools mirror set by set_deny_tools_for_computer_use, never the engine's configured deny rules or security mode. A user config deny rule meant to block desktop actions is not honored (the gate returns ask/deny purely from the mirror). Fail-safe default is ask so it is not an escalation, but it is a permission decision made outside the PDP that can silently diverge from configured rules. Fix: route computer-use actions through the engine as an Operation.
notes: DEFERRED from the 2026-07-10 P3 cleanup pass (deliberate, not overlooked). Reason: this is a security gate on the EXPERIMENTAL, non-default 'experimental-ui-computer-use' feature, and its current default is already fail-safe (check-computer-action returns 'ask', which prompts the user — the issue itself notes 'not an escalation'). The correct fix (route computer-use through the engine as an Operation) requires a new Janet->Rust PDP bridge that locks the PermCheck, builds an AccessRequest, and authorizes it. Getting that wrong fails OPEN (auto-allows desktop actions) — strictly worse than today. Matches the 'defer to supervised, NOT fire-and-forget' guidance for permission-gate-adjacent work. Do supervised, with a FAIL-CLOSED default (deny/ask on any engine error) and adversarial tests that a config deny rule + each security mode are honored.

title: Delete dead is_high_risk_non_path_tool that duplicates the risk taxonomy and has drifted
description: src/permission/engine/classify.rs:18: is_high_risk_non_path_tool is referenced only by its own definition and a test doc comment; the live taxonomy is accept_eligible (engine/mod.rs:54). It has already drifted -- its tool-name list omits plugin_tool, so if revived it would classify Janet plugin tools as low-risk and Accept-coercible, contradicting accept_eligible/Operation::Plugin. Fix: delete it; keep risk single-sourced in accept_eligible.

title: Bash command-complexity classification implemented three different ways
description: src/semantic/adapters/bash.rs:530 has_complex_constructs (tree-sitter node kinds), src/agent/tools/bash/check.rs:209 (string-contains incl heredoc), src/permission/checker.rs:221 (string-contains excl heredoc/subshell, the /why explainer). The /why explainer reports a heredoc or subshell command as a plain head-matched allow when the semantic runtime treats it as complex, giving the user a wrong trust signal; the two enforcement paths also disagree. Fix: one shared classifier used by enforcement and explanation.

title: Hot/breadcrumb memory char-budget computed with three different formulas
description: src/extras/memory_db.rs:776,938 vs 1500-1501,1774-1775: make_room_in_hot (938) and compact_breadcrumbs (776) charge content.len()+3 per entry; success_response (1500) and hot_usage_pct (1774) charge ENTRY_DELIMITER.len()=4 x (n-1); the real render (534-543) additionally prepends a [kind] tag (~8-13 chars/entry). With >4 entries the reported usage exceeds what eviction enforces and the prompt is larger than either. Fix: one entry_render_cost(entry) helper matching the actual render, used at all four sites.

title: Provider resolution (autodetect + disk probes + stderr notice) re-runs inside every build_agent
description: src/cli.rs:376-405 + provider/build.rs:122: resolve_provider calls auto_detect_provider and auth_detect_provider (reads the OpenAI auth store, stats ~/.claude/.credentials.json) and eprintln!s the auto-detected notice each time; build_agent re-invokes it for the chunk-timeout lookup, so the notice repeats on every agent (re)build including --loop model-swap rebuilds, and in TUI mode stderr writes land mid-alt-screen. A mid-run env change could make a rebuilt agent's provider/chunk-timeout diverge from the client in use. Fix: resolve the provider once in main, store it, pass it into build_agent.

title: Deduplicate summarization-provider resolution (copy-pasted, already diverges on failure)
description: src/provider/build.rs:678-721 vs 734-780: role lookup, default-alias short-circuit, client build, Anthropic-OAuth compaction guard, and fallback-to-main are copy-pasted between build_compaction_model and build_summarize_fn and already diverge on failure -- one bail!s (721), the other silently substitutes a disabled summarize-fn (756,777). A future change to the OAuth guard or role fallback half-applies: UI compaction and in-loop compaction route to different providers. Fix: one resolve_summarization_model(cfg,main)->Result<AnyModel> both adapt.

title: Fold lineage modeled twice (JSON origin_id vs SQLite parent chain) with best-effort writes that drift
description: src/session/storage.rs:308-314 vs extras/session_db.rs:1438-1486 + ui/run_handlers/context_compacted.rs:103-112: JSON files use flat origin_id (dedup_by_origin); the SQLite sessions table uses a parent_session_id chain (resolve_parent walks); the SQLite side is written best-effort (let _ = set_parent_session, let _ = insert_session). If the link write fails during a fold (busy DB/disk pressure) the JSON side still rotates: the session list collapses the chain while search discovery/browse forever show both rotations as separate conversations and resolve_parent returns the child as its own root -- silent permanent drift, no repair path. Fix: persist one lineage representation, or log-and-retry the link write plus a reconciliation pass.
close_reason: Fixed via the issue's 'log-and-retry the link write' option (#655): fold lineage now written atomically + retried + logged. Full model unification (origin column + reconciliation, needs a migration) tracked as a follow-up.

title: Paste cap checks bytes but truncates by chars, keeping up to ~4x the limit
description: src/ui/input.rs:634-643: the paste cap checks cleaned.len()>MAX_PASTE_BYTES (bytes) but truncates with chars().take(MAX_PASTE_BYTES), so a non-ASCII paste labelled 'truncated to 1MB' actually keeps up to ~4MB of UTF-8, defeating the memory/render-bloat guard and mislabeling the notice. Fix: truncate at the last char boundary <= MAX_PASTE_BYTES.

title: visible_lines derives chat height from input_rows, wrong while an overlay is up
description: src/ui/renderer.rs:1720-1723: visible_lines() derives chat height from self.input_rows (editor rows), but with an alert/shell overlay up the painted chat height uses effective_input_rows (961-1007), so every scroll clamp (max_offset 831, scroll_page_up/down, scroll_to_top) is computed against a taller-than-painted window: the user cannot scroll to the oldest content and page strides overshoot. Selection mapping is fine (buffer_line_at_row uses cached_chat_rect). Fix: base visible_lines() on the cached chat rect when present.

title: CHATGPT_ACCOUNT_ID env var is only honored on the CODEX_ACCESS_TOKEN branch
description: src/provider/auth.rs:59-153 + client.rs:230-241: with a legacy ~/.codex/auth.json whose access token lacks an account-id, create_client fails 'Set CHATGPT_ACCOUNT_ID or run codex login'. The user sets CHATGPT_ACCOUNT_ID and retries, but resolve_chatgpt_auth_from uses the account-id parameter only in the env-token branch (78-87); the legacy-file branch (117-153) extracts solely from the file, so the same error repeats. Fix: use the env account-id as a fallback in the file and Dirge-OAuth branches too.

title: delete_session_messages recomputes the FTS delete projection and can corrupt the index
description: src/extras/session_db.rs:1090-1100 vs 1174-1186: delete_session_messages reconstructs the external-content FTS5 delete projection by re-running today's redact_for_fts over the raw row, but FTS5 delete requires the exact text indexed at insert time. The redactor demonstrably evolves (v6 existed because projections changed). A message indexed before a redactor change recomputes a different projection at delete time -> silent messages_fts corruption. No production caller yet but the doc sells this as the only safe delete. Fix: store the indexed projection (or its hash/version) with the row, or switch messages_fts to a standalone table like messages_fts_trigram.

title: DAP disconnect early-returns on a dead adapter, leaving the session stuck active
description: src/dap/session.rs:1131-1148: disconnect propagates the request error with ? before *active=None, so a dead adapter can never be disconnected. Adapter crashes -> user runs disconnect -> the request fails ConnectionClosed -> early return leaves the dead session active; the panel keeps showing it and every op errors until a new launch calls terminate_active. Contrast terminate_active (1217) which ignores the error and clears the slot. Fix: clear active regardless of the disconnect request outcome. (Also: McpClientManager::reconnect at extras/mcp/mod.rs:122 is dead code awaiting raw_connect with no timeout -- fix before wiring.)

title: /rewind restores files with non-atomic std::fs::write
description: src/agent/tools/snapshots.rs:221: restore_from restores with plain std::fs::write while every mutating tool routes through fs_atomic::atomic_write precisely because in-place O_TRUNC writes corrupt on crash. A crash mid-/rewind of a large file leaves it truncated, and deleted-then-restored files get default perms (losing +x that fs_atomic preserves). Fix: use atomic_write_sync.

title: Edit fuzzy-fallback reports diff and line-delta from old_text, not the matched range
description: src/agent/tools/edit.rs:392-401,412-419: when the whitespace-normalized fuzzy matcher fires (block sizes up to +5 lines, 581-582), the matched byte range can differ from args.old_text, but the (+/-N lines) delta and the diff's - lines are computed from args.old_text. A 1-line old_text matching a 3-line block reports the wrong delta and shows removed lines never in the file, drifting the model's mental model. Fix: compute delta/diff from content[start..end] of the matched range.

title: Orphaned image asset PNGs after compaction fold
description: When a compaction fold drops a user message carrying pasted images, the ImageRef leaves the transcript but the <uuid>.png stays on disk under the session assets dir until delete_session. Slow disk leak, not data loss. Consider GC of unreferenced asset files during/after compaction (scan surviving messages' image asset_ids, remove assets/*.png not referenced). Surfaced in PR #571 review (image-paste feature).

title: Salience graduation: consolidate recurring near-duplicate memories, promote on recurrence, dismiss-backlog
description: memory_curator.rs promotes single working entries by age+use_count (dirge-26h1); no cross-session recurrence clustering. Add a curator pass that clusters near-duplicate memory entries (Jaccard/FTS), consolidates a cluster of >=K into one higher-salience entry (reuse supersede), with a content-hash dismiss-backlog so rejected clusters stay dead. Purely additive (curator, not the hot write path).
close_reason: Merged in PR #603: non-destructive recurrence-weighted salience graduation + ledger; also fixed a flaky test-harness dir-collision.

title: Connection warmup: pre-establish TCP+TLS at startup to cut first-token latency
description: dirge has no HTTP connection warmup (only LSP warmup). deepcode-cli fires a throwaway models.list() during first-prompt composition + 180s keepalive so the first real request skips the TLS handshake. Add a bounded fire-and-forget warmup at startup against the active provider base_url. Provider-agnostic (general improvement).
close_reason: Not worth it for dirge's stack. Investigated: the rig client is built once per session (main.rs:1166) and reused, and reqwest already pools connections with a ~90s keepalive — so connections stay warm all session after the first request. deepcode's warmup/keepalive addressed undici's 4s default, which doesn't apply to reqwest. The only residual benefit is skipping the first request's TCP+TLS handshake (~one-time 300ms), and capturing it cleanly needs injecting a hand-built reqwest client via rig's ClientBuilder::http_client — replicating rig's TLS/security config in a sensitive path. Risk not justified by the payoff.

title: glm provider default is glm-4, should be glm-5.2
description: default_model_for(glm) returned glm-4 and the README said the same, but the current GLM model (used in the README config example, CLI and /model tests) is glm-5.2. Flip the default + README. Note: glm-5.2 is not in the context-window table (config/mod.rs), so it uses the 128k fallback; glm-4.6 is 200k — window for glm-5.2 unverified, left conservative.
close_reason: default flipped to glm-5.2 in resolve.rs + README; context-window entry left as follow-up (unverified window)

title: Test-gap backfill: remaining 5 seams (code-review poll, plugin timeout, skill lifecycle, malformed-frame, CRLF/wide-char)
description: Continuation of dirge-79wq (seams 1 fold/persist/resume + 3 resolve_mode precedence landed). Remaining: (2) FollowUpSource::CodeReview poll branch; (4) plugin HOOK_TIMEOUT fail-open + two-plugin-load; (5) skill file<->DB lifecycle; (6) malformed-frame/child-death-while-pending (note: hjee added some RPC drain tests already); (7) CRLF new_text + wide-char wrap (p054 has partial wrap coverage). Pure test additions, no prod change.

title: Resume ignores the session's saved model; initial agent uses CLI/config default
description: Pre-existing (surfaced during ovjk review, NOT introduced by it). On resume, main.rs never reassigns the local 'model' var (resolved from CLI/config at ~735) from the loaded session, so the initial agent build (main.rs:1416) runs the CLI/config default model while the UI rebuilds and runtime completion_model sites use session.model. Resuming a session saved as e.g. 'o3' without --model runs the default for the first agent but shows/rebuilds as o3 -> they diverge. The ovjk change makes session.model and the first agent's model independently derived (so they can disagree in value, not just in the old Codex remap). Fix: on resume with no explicit --model, seed 'model' from session.model. Parent: dirge-8gdv.
close_reason: resolve_startup_model consolidates fresh/resume/--model-override; model_explicit persisted on the session so resume honors the saved model incl. explicit gpt-4o under Codex

title: Resume drops an explicit --model gpt-4o choice under Codex (re-resolves to gpt-5.5)
description: From the ovjk review. main.rs resume shim (src/main.rs ~1189) re-resolves a loaded session.model with explicit=false, because a persisted session stores no explicit-vs-default bit. So a user who ran --model gpt-4o under a Codex login (now honored post-ovjk, saved as session.model='gpt-4o') loses that choice on a later plain resume: the shim maps gpt-4o -> gpt-5.5. Inherent to not persisting the explicit signal; strictly better than pre-ovjk (where gpt-4o under Codex was impossible). Fix option: persist an 'explicit model' bool on the session and skip the shim when set (or drop the shim entirely once pre-ovjk sessions age out — the shim only exists to keep pre-fix default-Codex sessions on gpt-5.5). Parent: dirge-8gdv.
close_reason: resolve_startup_model consolidates fresh/resume/--model-override; model_explicit persisted on the session so resume honors the saved model incl. explicit gpt-4o under Codex

title: restore harness/log: drain plugin log queue into tracing::info!
description: Follow-up from dirge-qawc. harness/log is currently (defn harness/log [msg] nil) — a no-op, so plugin authors have no logging breadcrumb. nrepl/computer_use/workflow plugins still call it. Restore the documented behavior: queue messages on the Janet side and drain into tracing::info! on the Rust worker side (mirror how other harness queues drain), then re-document it in plugins.md as a working debug sink.

title: force-delete of a pinned learned skill leaves a ghost active DB row
description: Follow-up from dirge-0cln. SkillStore::archive() guards on pinned=0, so deleting a pinned learned skill with force=true removes the directory but leaves the row status='active' (archive is a no-op). Same class of ghost-active-row divergence 0cln fixed for the error path. Also: a delete when store==None never touches the DB. Low severity. Fix: archive should clear pinned (or the force-delete path should force-archive) so directory-gone always implies row-not-active.

title: DAP continue_ phase-3 restores receivers without checking the session wasn't replaced
description: From review of PR #567. src/dap/session.rs continue_ phase 3 re-acquires the lock and restores the parked EventReceivers into whichever session is active, without checking it is still the same session. If a launch/attach replaces the session while a continue is parked, phase 3 clobbers the NEW session's live receivers with the old client's channels and stomps its stop_wait_in_flight/status; the new session then never sees another event. Mitigated because dropping the old session drops the event-handler senders, waking the parked continue with Disconnected almost immediately, so it usually re-locks and errors cleanly before the new session installs. Medium-mechanism/low-likelihood. Fix: tag the session (generation/id) and only restore when it still matches.

title: codex_http_client_for TOCTOU: token rotation between two loads freezes an expired bearer
description: From review of PR #564. In codex_http_client_for (src/provider/client.rs ~522), if the stored credential rotates between header resolution (resolve_chatgpt_auth -> load_fresh_openai_oauth) and the second load, the second load refreshes and returns a new access token != bearer, so codex_refresh_applies returns false and the session gets a frozen CodexHttpClient::default() carrying the now-expired bearer -> 401s for the whole session with a fresh token on disk (the dirge-30nl fix silently fails to engage). Low confidence (microsecond window, once per token lifetime; equals pre-fix baseline rather than strictly worse). Fix: re-resolve/refresh the client when the two loads disagree.

title: New-file path resolution can produce a >260-char non-verbatim path on Windows
description: From review of PR #563. In the ancestor-walk branch of src/permission/path.rs (~line 119), the existing ancestor is canonicalized to non-verbatim form and the nonexistent tail is reappended, so the resolved_path for a brand-new file can exceed MAX_PATH without the \\?\ prefix. Downstream std::fs writes then fail with name-too-long/path-not-found on Windows machines without the long-path registry opt-in, where the old verbatim result worked. dunce preserves verbatim only for paths that exist; the reattached tail defeats that. Low confidence / arguably the accepted tradeoff of de-verbatimizing. Fix: re-apply the verbatim prefix when the reassembled path exceeds MAX_PATH.

title: Code-review baseline equality computed on 64KB-capped diff can skip a real review
description: From review of PR #560. run_delta_to_review (src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs ~1975) compares baseline vs current diff, but cap_diff (code_review.rs ~758) truncates both at MAX_DIFF_BYTES (64000). When pre-existing WIP already exceeds the cap, an edit in a file that sorts past the truncation point and leaves total diff length unchanged (e.g. a 1->2 char change) makes the capped strings byte-identical -> run_delta_to_review returns None -> the reviewer that should engage is skipped. Low confidence (needs >64KB WIP + length-preserving edit past the cutoff). Fix: compare an uncapped diff or a tree hash, not the lossy capped string.

title: MCP ProcessGroupGuard::drop SIGKILLs pgid unconditionally on normal completion (pid-reuse race)
description: From review of PR #560. src/extras/mcp_server.rs ProcessGroupGuard::drop (~line 875) is never disarmed: on the success path child.wait() reaps the leader and, if no descendants remain, the process group ceases to exist, so the unconditional kill(-pgid, SIGKILL) at drop targets a pgid the OS may have reassigned. Failure window: delegation completes normally, pid wraps/reuses between reap and drop -> an unrelated process group gets SIGKILLed. Low confidence (microsecond window, needs pid reuse). Fix: disarm the guard once the child is reaped on the success path.

title: keep_recent_tokens not clamped to context window on small-context models
description: compaction_cut_idx (session/compact.rs) walks keep_recent_tokens (default 20k) of tail. On a small-context model (e.g. 8k window) keep_recent exceeds the whole session, so the token walk never trips and the cut lands at messages.len() -> compress_reporting keeps only the summary, dropping the verbatim tail. Affects both /compress and the auto-fold persistence handler. Fix: clamp effective keep_recent to a fraction of the context window. Surfaced while fixing dirge-4kgk.

title: Doc-drift: issues.md missing cancelled status; features.md storm/rewind already tracked separately
description: issue_db normalize_status accepts cancelled/wontfix/dropped and is_terminal_status treats it like done, but docs/issues.md lists only open->in_progress->done+blocked and /issues list filters exclude it -> users can't discover how to list cancelled issues. Fix: add cancelled to the states section and /issues list filter docs. Files: src/extras/issue_db.rs:35, docs/issues.md:34. Round R8.

title: Restrictive mode / coarse redirect scanner / duplicate anthropic system-shaper smells
description: Assorted low smells: (1) coarse (no-semantic-bash) redirect scanner is a second parser to keep in sync with tree-sitter; consider making semantic-bash mandatory for the permission path or add differential fuzz tests (check.rs:210). (2) prepend_claude_code_system is cfg_attr(not(test),allow(dead_code)) duplicating prepend_claude_code_system_value - the 4 tests exercise a fn production never runs; rewrite tests against the live fn and delete the duplicate (anthropic_http.rs:261). (3) stale #[allow(dead_code)] on anthropic_oauth refresh_token which is actually called (auth.rs:223). Round R8.
close_reason: part 2 (dead prepend_claude_code_system duplicate) removed + tests repointed; part 3 (refresh_token stale allow) already absent in current tree; part 1 (redirect scanner: make semantic-bash mandatory / differential fuzz) is a design change left as a future consideration

title: LoopConfig Debug impl and test constructors drifted from the struct
description: Manual Debug omits code_review_fn, compaction_hooks, storm_mutating_tools/exempt_tools, truncation_notes (under-reported debug logs). stream.rs has two verbatim ~45-field LoopConfig literals - every new field means editing N copies. Fix: add LoopConfig::for_tests(convert) constructor and regenerate Debug to include all fields. Files: src/agent/agent_loop/types.rs:494, stream.rs:426,735. Round R8.

title: Two parallel compaction turn-boundary implementations (slash vs loop path)
description: /compress + emergency-prune operate on SessionMessage with align_cut_to_user_boundary (forward snap only); the loop path operates on serde_json::Value with snap_forward/backward_to_user. Same 'never orphan a tool_use/tool_result pair' invariant, duplicated and already drifted (slash snaps forward only). Fix: extract a role-based boundary-snapping helper generic over role accessor, or convert the slash path to the loop's message shape. Files: src/ui/slash/mod.rs:102, src/agent/compression.rs:854. Round R8.

title: is_content_delta duplicated verbatim across retry and billing_fallback
description: The 'committed content' invariant that gates both retry and billing-fallback safety is defined twice (retry.rs:267, billing_fallback.rs:196). A new DeltaPhase updated in only one copy makes the two wrappers disagree on when re-running duplicates output. Fix: move the predicate next to DeltaPhase (DeltaPhase::is_content()) and use from both. Files: src/agent/agent_loop/retry.rs:267, src/provider/billing_fallback.rs:196. Round R8.

title: code_review.rs module-wide #![allow(dead_code)] now hides genuinely dead code
description: The allow was justified for R1 but all rounds landed and the gate is wired. SECURITY_PREAMBLE has zero non-test references (security stance never shipped), and the blanket allow will absorb any future dead item in this 1.6k-line module. Fix: drop the module-level allow; either wire a /code-review security mode or delete SECURITY_PREAMBLE with a roborev pointer. File: src/agent/agent_loop/code_review.rs:21,130. Round R8.

title: Fold token-savings subtracted across incompatible accounting spaces
description: tokens_before/after are estimate_messages_tokens (chars/4 over loop JSON); compress_reporting subtracts that delta from session.total_estimated_tokens (per-message + tool args + 16/call). The totals differ systematically -> context gauge drifts after each fold (saturating_sub masks underflow). Fix: recompute total_estimated_tokens from post-fold session.messages (as recompute_all_estimates does) instead of applying a foreign delta. Files: run.rs:572,776, session/compact.rs:51. Round R2.

title: !-prefix forbidden-command guard bypassable by env/wrapper prefix
description: is_forbidden checks only cmd.split_whitespace().next(), so '! FOO=1 cd /tmp' or '! exec </dev/null' variants with a leading token evade the cd/exit/exec block. Low (user's own interactive ! passthrough, not the LLM path) but trivially defeated. Fix: normalize past leading name=value assignments before the head check, or document as best-effort. File: src/shell.rs:75. Round R1.

title: In-band DIRGE_HOOK_ERR:/DIRGE_TOOL_ERR: prefixes collide with legit return values
description: Hook/command/tool results are strings; a handler returning text starting with DIRGE_HOOK_ERR: is misclassified as an error and dropped/logged. Contrived but cheap to close. Fix: structured sentinel (one-char type tag or random per-eval nonce prefix). File: src/plugin/mod.rs:364,866,1097. Round R4.

title: set_deny_tools_for_computer_use interpolates config into Janet without escaping
description: format!('"{t}"') on each deny-tools entry - a tool name with a quote or backslash (config-controlled) breaks the eval or injects Janet, unlike every other host->Janet path which uses escape_janet_string. Feature-gated (experimental-ui-computer-use), config trusted, hence low. Fix: run each entry through escape_janet_string. File: src/plugin/mod.rs:308. Round R4.

title: register-renderer registry is the odd one out (| separator, no escaping, no dedup)
description: Every other registry is tab-separated, harness/-escaped, deduped via dedup_last_wins; renderers use raw type|fn lines - an entry type containing | mis-parses, duplicate registrations dispatch unpredictably. Fix: migrate to the shared tab-separated + dedup_last_wins format. Files: src/plugin/worker.rs:530, mod.rs:1217. Round R4.

title: truncation_notes.lock().expect() can panic the loop on a poisoned mutex
description: apply_truncation_repair uses .expect('truncation_notes poisoned') while sibling code uses lock_ignore_poison(); a panic in any other holder converts a recoverable situation into a loop-task crash mid-run. Fix: use lock_ignore_poison() for consistency. File: src/agent/agent_loop/run.rs:2138. Round R8.

title: /retry pokes InputEditor internals instead of set_text
description: Sets input.buffer/input.cursor directly, skipping the paste-table/kill-ring/history-draft resets set_text performs. /fork correctly uses set_text; after /retry, stale paste placeholders from the prior draft can linger. Fix: ctx.input.set_text(&msg.content). File: src/ui/slash/cmd/retry.rs:29. Round R8.

title: /model free-form id mislabels session.provider after cross-provider switch
description: When no cross-provider target matches, session.provider is reset to cli.resolve_provider(cfg) even though the live client is still the previously-switched provider. /model vibe-thinker:latest then /model my-custom-tag -> requests go to ollama but session.provider says the CLI default; the next cross_provider_target call reasons from the wrong active provider. Fix: when target_alias is None, leave session.provider unchanged. File: src/ui/slash/cmd/model.rs:133. Round R8.

title: Issue substring search doesn't escape SQL LIKE wildcards
description: format!('%{}%', query.trim()) - '/issues search 100%' or a query with '_' behaves as a wildcard, not substring (% matches everything). Parameterized so no injection, just wrong results for punctuation. Fix: escape %,_ and the escape char and add ESCAPE '\' to the LIKE clauses. File: src/extras/issue_db.rs:440. Round R8.

title: TUI: right panel blank at exactly the documented 152-col threshold
description: PANEL_AUTO_MIN_COLS=152 flips both panels on, but scene has separate draw minima (left>=12, right>=16). At 152 cols the right panel width computes to 15<16 -> left paints, right skipped, leaving a reserved 15-col blank gutter until >=153. Three divergent thresholds for one concept (152, content_indent>=15, 12/16). Fix: derive one threshold (raise to 154 or lower scene right min to 15) from a single constant. Files: src/ui/renderer.rs:147, tui/scene.rs:104,121. Round R8.

title: TUI: --no-color 'single chokepoint' bypassed by ~100 hardcoded Color:: literals
description: features.md:21 says --no-color collapses the whole UI via a single theme chokepoint (themed()), but ~100 raw Color:: literals paint directly (mod.rs 20, chat.rs 15, tool_display.rs 11, panels.rs 9, markdown.rs 9, run_handlers/*) and renderer.rs/scene.rs/frame.rs have no no_color remap -> these emit color under --no-color. Fix: apply theme::themed()/apply_no_color once in the renderer's cell/write_line paint path. File: src/ui/theme.rs:497. Round R8.

title: Refactor: unify duplicated content-guard threat patterns (memory_db vs skills/guard)
description: INVISIBLE_CHARS array and prompt-injection regexes are maintained byte-for-byte in memory_db.rs:266 and skills/guard.rs:18 (guard's own comment says 'same as memory_store.rs'), and have already drifted - guard blocks eval(/.env, memory_db doesn't. Fix: one shared extras::content_guard module with per-store extensions. Files: src/extras/memory_db.rs:266, src/extras/skills/guard.rs:18. Round R8.

title: Refactor: split monolith functions/files (run_interactive, run_loop, main, config, memory_db, worker)
description: Behavior-preserving extraction along existing seams: run_interactive ~4460 lines (extract dispatch_modal state machine, shared abort_run for Ctrl+C/Esc, each select! arm into run_handlers::); run_loop inner body ~750 lines (inject_pending/handle_storm/post_usage_compaction + a turn-state struct); run_compaction_pass_with_focus 11 params -> FoldCtx; main() ~1370 lines (resolve_session/load_plugins/run_sandbox_command/run_print_mode); config/mod.rs (providers/sandbox/model_table submodules); memory_db.rs 4306 lines (move 2200-line test module + legacy-import block out); worker.rs (include_str!(harness.janet) prelude + generic registry codec + single blocking-bridge helper). Round R8.

title: Doc-drift: features.md /rewind command, agent-loop.md compaction pointer, misc stale
description: Batch of doc corrections: (1) features.md:37 documents /rewind as a slash command; none exists (real entry is Esc-Esc). (2) features.md:19 defers compaction architecture to agent-loop.md which has no compaction section. (3) call-site comment run.rs:1881 omits code-review+goal gates. (4) tui.md Ctrl+O described as re-emit, actually expand/collapse toggle. (5) cancelled issue status undocumented in issues.md. (6) stale run.rs:1598 'usage not wired' comment; agent-loop.md:78 'tokio::join_all' is futures::join_all. Round R8.

title: 12-bit line hash lets ~1/4096 drifted lines through edit_lines guard
description: The staleness guard is only the folded FNV-12 hash. A line externally changed to a colliding value at the same line number passes verification and gets clobbered. Consciously documented tradeoff, but a 50-line range has ~1.2% odds of at least one silent pass and the failure mode is exactly the clobber the feature prevents. Fix: also compare line length (or add a range-level checksum) alongside the per-line hash. File: src/agent/tools/line_hash.rs:30, edit_lines.rs:111. Round R3.

title: Wire skill effectiveness inference into the review pass
description: SkillStore.record_outcome(name, success) exists and feeds the effectiveness bonus, but nothing infers skill outcomes yet. Add a review-pass step (like memory's dirge-ygm3) that reads the transcript and records whether an invoked skill led to success/failure, so 'used but failing' skills sink via negative effectiveness — not just 'unused' ones via decay. Also: optional one-time import of legacy .usage.json counters into the skills table (skipped in R5; telemetry is best-effort).

title: R7: docs + site for /learn and skill salience
description: Document the /learn command, DB-resident skills, and salience-based skill curation. Update in-repo docs AND the site in ../dirge-code.github.io (/Users/yogthos/src/dirge-code/dirge-code.github.io). Cover: how to learn a skill from sources/conversation, verification gate, how stale skills decay/archive, pinned file-based skills.
close_reason: docs/skills.md + site index.html updated with /learn + salience curation

title: R6: learn-time verification gate
description: Require ## Verification command per learned skill; run once at creation; pass seeds initial success_count so effectiveness starts grounded. TDD: gate on pass/fail, skip path.
close_reason: Verification gate + parser + grounding-success seed; 13 tool tests + 8 learn tests green

title: OpenAI OAuth: expired/unrefreshable Dirge credential blocks fallback to legacy codex auth file
description: In provider::auth::resolve_chatgpt_auth_from_with_dirge_oauth, when a Dirge OAuth credential exists but is expired and refresh fails (revoked/invalid refresh token), load_fresh_openai_oauth returns Err which propagates and prevents falling back to a valid legacy ~/.codex/auth.json. Previously the legacy file would be used. Consider treating an unrefreshable Dirge credential as absent (fall through) rather than hard-erroring. Narrow case; error is actionable. From PR #532 review.

title: Startup notice when compaction degrades to prune-only (OAuth, no summarization_provider)
description: One-time startup heads-up in the interactive UI when the active route is Anthropic OAuth and no non-OAuth summarization_provider is set, so the user knows context folds will be lossy prune-only. Reuses build_compaction_model as the source of truth.

title: Optimize recent_project_sessions: mtime pre-sort to avoid wholesale session reads on startup
description:
recent_project_sessions (cross-session Up-arrow history, added in #527) reads every session file's full contents via read_to_string + partial ProjMeta parse on every startup to filter by working_dir and rank by updated_at. For users with large session stores this is O(all sessions) file reads at startup.

find_recent_sessions already solves the analogous problem by sorting candidates by filesystem mtime (free from read_dir metadata) and only reading the top N. recent_project_sessions can't pre-filter by working_dir from mtime alone (working_dir lives in file content), but it can read in mtime-newest-first order and stop early once it has max_sessions distinct same-project origins, avoiding reading the whole store in the common case.

Acceptance: recent_project_sessions does not read every file when the newest same-project sessions are found early; behavior (filtering, fold-chain dedup, oldest-first ordering, max_sessions=0 opt-out) unchanged and existing tests still pass.
close_reason: Implemented in #528: mtime-ordered scan with early-stop avoids reading the whole session store

title: Plugin worker: dedup send_dialog/send_lsp/send_sandbox poll loops + reconsider headless dialog fast-fail
description: Code-review follow-ups from #524 (non-blocking): (1) send_dialog, send_lsp, send_sandbox_exec now have three near-identical recv_timeout(DIALOG_POLL) poll loops differing only in the *_should_abort policy + warn message; factor into a shared poll_reply(reply_rx, abort_fn, warn) helper. (2) send_sandbox_exec piggybacks lsp_should_abort/LSP_QUERY_TIMEOUT — give it its own constant. (3) DIALOG_TIMEOUT=600s is generous for interactive humans but a long apparent hang for headless without --auto-confirm; consider a shorter bound or auto-deny dialogs under -p. (4) before_hook/after_hook in plugin_hooks.rs are near-duplicate; optional dispatch_hook_blocking helper. (5) worst-case dispatch_tool_hook is ~(N+1)*HOOK_TIMEOUT with N registered hooks — fine, but a per-dispatch deadline would tighten it.

title: stream-json: simplify misleading empty-id tool_result pairing
description: Positional fallback turn_tool_uses.get(len) is a no-op (ids are all-or-nothing per turn) and wrong under parallel completion-order arrival. Comment claims it mirrors persistence path but doesn't. Simplify to use id directly.

title: Build fails: --no-default-features --features mcp (loop_mode/StripPolicy)
description: cargo build --no-default-features --features mcp fails with: 'no field loop_mode on type Cli' and 'unused imports StripPolicy and self'. Pre-existing (reproduces on merge commit 2e148bb), not in the CI matrix (CI builds mcp additively as --features mcp). Some non-semantic code assumes default features when only mcp is enabled.

title: Chat with a focused subagent via the input field
description:
Idea: when a subagent tab is focused (Ctrl+N/Ctrl+P cycle them — confirmed working, keymap.rs:122-129, dispatch mod.rs:1856-1925), route the input field to that subagent so the user can converse with it, not just watch its stream.

Blocker — current subagent architecture is one-shot/fire-and-forget, so this is NOT a simple input-binding:
- task.rs:493-519 spawns each subagent as model.btw_query_with(prompt) — one-shot, returns Result<String> (dispatch.rs:176), runs to completion then terminal (TaskState::Completed/Failed). Not a persistent multi-turn loop.
- Per-subagent registry holds only an AbortSignal (task.rs:141, register_subagent_abort) — no input channel into a running subagent.
- Submitted input (mod.rs:2072) always routes to the MAIN agent regardless of focused tab.
- chat_idx_to_subagent already maps tab->subagent id; SubagentChatEvent already streams subagent output to the tab; Ctrl+K already kills the focused subagent (mod.rs:1931).

What it would take:
1. Make subagents interactive: replace one-shot btw_query_with with a persistent agent-loop runner that parks between turns awaiting input (reuse the main loop's interjection machinery, run_handlers/interjected.rs).
2. Per-subagent input channel: store an mpsc::Sender<String> alongside the abort signal in the registry.
3. Route input by focus: at mod.rs:2072, when chat_idx_to_subagent.get(active_chat) is Some(id) and the text isn't a slash/main command, send to that subagent's channel instead of the main agent.
4. Lifecycle decision (drives most complexity): chat only while the subagent is RUNNING, vs. revive/resume a finished subagent. Keeping subagents alive holds a model/network handle (the current 10-min SUBAGENT_TIMEOUT exists for this reason).
acceptance_criteria: Focusing a running subagent tab and typing in the input field sends the message to that subagent (not the main agent); its reply streams back to the tab. Main-agent input is unaffected when the main tab is focused. Lifecycle behavior for finished subagents is decided and documented.

title: Re-box tool chambers on resize (reflow follow-up)
description: dirge-qy3y made scrollback reflow on resize: plain text + markdown (tables) re-wrap to the new width. Tool chambers are recorded as non-reflowing Raw blocks (write_line_raw) so their borders stay intact (no fracture) but they do NOT re-box to the new width — a chamber laid out at 120 cols stays 120-wide (clipped) on a narrower terminal. To re-box, record chambers as a structured ToolChamber source block (tool, banner, output, caps) and re-render via render_tool_output at the current width in Renderer::render_block. Complication: render_tool_output has side effects (returns CollapsedToolResult, drives collapse state) — needs a pure layout path. Also: inactive-chat snapshots carry source but their chambers are Raw too.
notes: dirge delegation (branch feat/ghpf-rebox-chambers) produced a clean pure layout_tool_chamber + SourceBlock::ToolChamber that reflows the generic render_tool_output body/bottom. GAP found in review: chamber header (tool_result.rs:124 write_line_raw) + special-case rows (LSP tails, colored diff rows, body-hidden) are still Raw and don't reflow, so header/body mismatch on resize. Full fix requires restructuring the whole chamber-assembly path (header+body+bottom reflow together per chamber, for all chamber variants). Bigger than the issue implied; needs a dedicated refactor, not fire-and-forget.
close_reason: Full chamber-reflow refactor. Every chamber piece is now a reflowing structured source block: ChamberTop (re-fits fit_banner_header), ChamberRow (re-pads chamber_row/_with_bg/_centered), ChamberBottom, plus dirge's ToolChamber for the generic body.
