All 19 write_line_raw chamber sites (tool_call/tool_result/done/events/mod/tool_display) converted to write_chamber_* methods. render_block re-renders each at current width via rebuild(); source stays in lockstep so the done-path header-drop (replace_from) still works. Fixed-width output byte-identical (no regression); integration test proves top+body+bottom reflow in lockstep (the gap the delegated partial missed). fmt/clippy/612 UI tests pass.

title: Redact secrets in stored memory content, not only the FTS projection
description:
redact_for_fts is applied only to the FTS search index (memory_db.rs:892,1082,1991). The memories.content column — which is injected verbatim into the system prompt via format_for_system_prompt and shared across projects under global scope — stores secrets as-is. An agent that writes a memory containing an API key/token leaks it into the prompt and into cross-project/global memory.

Idea from ~/src/recall: redact secret shapes before persisting. Mirror the redact_for_fts pass onto stored content (at minimum for global-scope writes; consider all writes). Threat-scan already runs for injection; this is the secret-leakage complement.
acceptance_criteria: Memory content is run through secret redaction before being stored (or at least before global-scope persistence and before system-prompt injection). A test writes content containing a fake sk-/AKIA/bearer token and asserts it is redacted in the stored row / injected prompt.
close_reason: Redaction now applied to stored content at all three write paths with tests

title: Consider a generic Keymap<A> to dedup Keymap/InputKeymap
description: Keymap and InputKeymap are structurally identical (HashMap<ChordSeq, A> with the same defaults()/resolve()/from_command()/command_list() over an A::ALL table), differing only in the action type. A generic Keymap<A: Action> with an Action trait exposing ALL would write those once. Weigh against the trait-bound complexity; the two are small and stable so this is optional.

title: Unify chord parsers: keymap::parse_chord vs plugin::extension::parse_key_spec
description: Two chord-string parsers exist and have diverged (parse_chord splits on '-' and '+', accepts 'option'/'pagedn'; parse_key_spec only '-', accepts 'bs', rejects 'f01'). bind-key (keybindings path) uses parse_chord; register-shortcut uses parse_key_spec, so a plugin author gets different parsing for the two. parse_chord must stay available without the 'plugin' feature, so the fix is to hoist one shared parser into an always-compiled module and have both call it, not to delete a copy.

title: Optional chord_timeout_ms for emacs-style key sequences
description: Follow-up to dirge-fl57 (#234). The chord-sequence runtime currently waits indefinitely for a pending prefix to complete (emacs-style), cancellable via Esc/Ctrl+G. Add an optional config 'chord_timeout_ms' that auto-cancels a pending prefix after N ms of inactivity. Deferred from phase 4 because it needs event-loop poll-timeout plumbing (track a deadline, wake the loop to clear chord_pending) that's riskier than the core runtime; the core (#234) ships without it.

title: Surface the approval_provider's deny reason in the human prompt
description: Follow-up to dirge-a5ir. When an approval_provider Deny escalates to the human prompt, the user currently sees only the tool + input, not WHY the evaluator flagged it. Surfacing the evaluator's reason would help the user decide. Requires adding an optional reason field to AskRequest (src/permission/ask.rs) and threading it through the 5 construction sites (tools/mod.rs, billing_fallback.rs x2, acp/mod.rs x2) and the UI prompt renderer (src/ui/mod.rs). Deferred from a5ir to keep that change focused on the escalation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: When an approval_provider deny escalates to a human prompt, the prompt shows the evaluator's reason text.

title: New patch release after session-fix PR merges
description: Follow-up: once the session zombie/verbs PR is merged, cut a new patch release (bump version, tag). Also applies to PR #470 (footer compaction budget).
close_reason: v0.9.1 released (#470 footer/critic, #471 sessions); Nix dirge-bin bumped in #473

title: Live-thinking O(n^2) re-render per reasoning delta (follow-up)
description: restream_expanded_thinking truncates and fully re-renders the entire reasoning_buf on every reasoning delta (per-line soft-wrap), so a long expanded burst is quadratic; reasoning_buf is also sanitize_output'd twice. Deferred from the review PR as a perf optimization. Replace with an incremental tail-append.

title: Memory: relevance floor for verbatim pre-recall
description: dirge-0gxb: pre_recall_block surfaces whatever search ranks top-5 with no minimum-score floor, so generic turns ('ok', 'continue') can inject low-signal hints. search_entries doesn't expose BM25 scores at this layer; add a score floor (or a query-triviality skip) to suppress no-real-match turns.

title: Memory: bound the hybrid embedding cache (LRU or clear-on-session-switch)
description: dirge-4hld: HybridMemoryProvider.cache is insert-only, keyed by content hash; vectors for superseded/removed/edited content are never evicted. Bounded in practice by distinct content per session (small), but no hard cap. Add an LRU or clear on on_session_switch.

title: Memory: land the 'recently-effective' decay for procedural (or drop last_success_at)
description: dirge-zygq: last_success_at is written by record_outcome but read by nothing in production (only test asserts). The v12 migration comment promises a future 'recently effective' decay that keys off it. Either land that decay or drop the column so it doesn't become a write-only orphan like the dirge-lerb confidence column.

title: Memory: gate the 'mark' outcome action to the background review runner
description: dirge-zygq follow-up. The 'mark' action (record procedural outcome) is exposed in the shared memory tool schema, so the interactive agent can see it even though only the background review pass is instructed (via prompt) to use it. Risk: main-agent self-grading inflates success_count. Proper fix needs per-context action gating — the review/curator runners filter tools by NAME not action (spawn_filtered_runner_with_cache), so gating requires threading an 'outcomes_enabled' flag from build_loop_tools into a distinct MemoryTool for the review runner. Kept out of scope for the initial procedural-effectiveness PR; mark is already omitted from SYSTEM_PROMPT as a partial mitigation.

title: Memory: verbatim pre-recall (supplemental, snapshot-safe)
description: Elastic idea #3. Auto-recall on the verbatim user message per turn to surface breadcrumb entries the agent wouldn't expand. MUST be supplemental: implemented as an injected tool-result mid-conversation, NOT a mutation of the frozen system-prompt snapshot (dirge-kvfm) and must not churn the prefix cache. Test asserts snapshot bytes unchanged with pre-recall on/off. Branch feat/memory-verbatim-prerecall.

title: Codex/ChatGPT auth needs a default model (gpt-4o fallback fails on the Codex backend)
description: Follow-up from the #428 (codex auth) review. When a user runs the openai provider with auth=chatgpt and no explicit --model/model config, default_model_for("openai") returns "gpt-4o", which the Codex backend (chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex) does not serve — the first request fails with a confusing error. Needs a codex-specific default model (the PR's own test uses "gpt-5.5") OR a clear up-front error telling the user to set --model. Deferred from the quick follow-up branch because picking the exact Codex model id is a maintainer call and threading auth into model resolution (resolve.rs default_model_for / build.rs) is non-trivial. Low priority — works fine once the user sets --model.
close_reason: Resolved by PR #577 (dirge-ovjk): resolve_codex_default maps the non-explicit openai default (gpt-4o) to the Codex default (gpt-5.5) on a codex client; explicit --model is preserved. Stored as session.model at startup.

title: Unify the four FNV-1a implementations into one util module
description:
FNV-1a is hand-rolled in four places with copy-pasted constants:
- src/agent/tools/line_hash.rs:24-25 — 32-bit (FNV_OFFSET 0x811c_9dc5 / FNV_PRIME 0x0100_0193), folded to 12 bits for the 3-char line hash.
- src/agent/tools/snapshots.rs:69 — 64-bit fnv64() for the dedup pool key.
- src/semantic/index.rs:496 — 64-bit, inline, different formatting.
- src/extras/memory_db.rs:1277-1278 — 64-bit, FNV_OFFSET/FNV_PRIME consts.

Low payoff (different consumers, two bit widths) but removes duplicated magic constants and drift risk. Extract a small shared module (e.g. src/util/fnv.rs or src/hash.rs) exposing fnv32(&[u8]) -> u32 and fnv64(&[u8]) -> u64. Rewrite all four call sites on top of it: line_hash keeps its own 12-bit fold over fnv32; snapshots/semantic/memory_db call fnv64. Keep each site's public API and output identical (line_hash MUST produce the same 3-char strings — it is part of the edit_lines contract; semantic/index and memory_db hashes are persisted keys, so their values MUST NOT change).

TDD: add fnv32/fnv64 unit tests with known vectors; assert line_hash output unchanged for a few inputs (regression-lock the existing values); confirm no behavior change at the other three sites (their existing tests should stay green). Independent of the snapshot-store refactor.
close_reason: Extracted src/hash.rs with fnv32/fnv64; rewrote line_hash, snapshots, semantic/index, memory_db on top. Outputs byte-identical (regression-locked line_hash values + canonical FNV vectors).

title: Storm-breaker: synthesize user-facing assistant failure narrative
description:
Article gap (howardchen DeepSeek V4 piece): dirge's stuck-loop recovery (tool-loop circuit breaker + reflect-then-pivot + cross-turn failure-recovery checkpoint) is internal-only. The storm-breaker twist is, on repeated identical failures, to synthesize a coherent assistant message explaining the failure and inject it into history as the model's own message, so the user sees a clean narrative (not a red error) and the model carries the failure story forward.

Scope: on circuit-breaker trip, fold the failure into a synthesized assistant message in the transcript. Reuse existing trip detection. Polish, not new machinery.

TDD plan (round 4):
1. Test: circuit-breaker trip produces a synthesized assistant narrative message of expected shape.
2. Test: narrative names the failing tool + recent errors; doesn't duplicate the internal reflect-then-pivot nudge.
close_reason: Implemented with tests; committed on branch hash-anchored-editing (article gap-closing rounds 1-4)

title: macOS microVM sandbox: silence libkrun probe + spike Apple container backend
description:
On macOS the build.rs libkrun.so/libkrunfw.so probe warns because those are Linux-only. Two parts:
(a) Gate the build.rs libkrun probe to #[cfg(target_os = "linux")] so macOS builds stop warning.
(b) Spike an Apple 'container' (github.com/apple/container) sandbox backend: run a command in an isolated container with the workspace mounted, as the macOS equivalent of the Linux libkrun microVM sandbox. Evaluate before committing to a full backend.

Parked 2026-06 in favor of the memory-cohesion work.

title: Remove cross-session extractor (low yield, re-derives per-session review)
description: The 14-day cross-session theme extractor re-derives durable facts that per-session background review already captures, for the cost of an LLM pass. Remove cross_session_extractor.rs, orchestrator stage 4, run_cross_session_extraction in review.rs, CROSS_SESSION_PROMPT, and update docs/tests. Keep the shared CuratorClock + memory curator.
close_reason: Removed cross-session extractor and all its exclusive deps; orchestrator now three stages

title: Remove dead confidence field from memory store
description: confidence is write-only: set to DEFAULT_CONFIDENCE on insert, echoed in the view JSON, never read by eviction/search/curator. Migration v9 drops the column; remove the struct field, queries, display, and legacy-import handling.
close_reason: confidence column dropped via v9; all code refs removed; migration + survival tested

title: Collapse duplicated provider stream_fn dispatch (spawn.rs + dispatch.rs)
description: Two parallel 8-way matches (spawn.rs:403 over AnyAgentInner, dispatch.rs:209 over AnyModel) both call rig_stream_fn_from_model_with_filter identically. A new provider needs both edited; compiler won't catch a miss. Macro-ify to one source of truth.
close_reason: dispatch_stream_fn macro is the single provider list; both call sites use it

title: Doc + config hardening: sandbox object form, cpus/memory bounds, cwd fallback, RAM numbers
description: config.md:88 documents sandbox as boolean only (object form {mode,image,cpus,memory_mib} undocumented); legacy nested cpus/memory_mib path silently wraps via as u8/as u32 (config/mod.rs:360-365); sandbox/mod.rs:290 falls back to '.' if current_dir fails; README RAM/binary numbers unsubstantiated.
close_reason: sandbox object form documented; cpus/memory bounds-checked with test; cwd fallback warns; RAM wording hedged

title: Shared curator state/gate scaffolding
description: MemoryCuratorState / CrossSessionState / skills CuratorState triplicate load/save + interval gates with already-diverged first-run protocols (seed-and-defer vs session-count gate). Extract shared CuratorClock; align skills + cross-session to the session-count first-run gate. Fold in LOW cleanups: shared preview helper (memory_curator preview vs memory_db preview_line), drop private now_secs wrappers.
close_reason: CuratorClock extracted; all three curators delegate; session-count first-run aligned; preview helper unified in text.rs

title: Phase 4: Feedback + scored recall for memory
description:
Add UMP-compatible feedback mechanism and scored retrieval to the memory system.

## Current State
Memory is retrieved via view() which returns all entries in a target. No scoring, no ranking, no feedback loop. The curator runs a periodic background pass but doesn't track per-entry usage outcomes.

## Goal
- Add feedback mechanism: track whether injected memories were followed/overridden/ignored/contradicted
- Add scored recall: similarity + recency + scope_match + salience signals
- Update memory_usage to capture feedback signals
- Integrate with curator to use feedback for retention decisions

## Files
- src/extras/memory_store.rs — add feedback tracking, scored recall method
- src/extras/memory_provider.rs — add recall() and feedback() to trait
- src/agent/tools/memory.rs — add recall action to tool definition
- src/agent/review.rs — wire post-turn feedback into memory system
notes: Partially covered by dirge-q8wt/dirge-jyks on the sqlite-memory-store branch: expand records use_count/last_used_at and reinforces salience; search ranks by bm25 + salience + recency; curator consumes usage data. Remaining scope: explicit feedback verbs (followed/overridden/contradicted) and similarity/scope_match recall signals.

title: README/docs drift: slash table missing /plan /kill /panel-debug; /mcp & /loop gating asymmetry; isolation/threshold claims
description:
Doc + gating drift from the UI review (slash dispatch is otherwise complete, with a runtime drift-guard + unit test enforcing list/arm agreement):
1) README slash table omits /plan (only in prose at :197), /kill (also Ctrl+K), and /panel's 'debug' subcommand; /agents alias undocumented.
2) /mcp is documented unconditionally but is cfg(feature=mcp)-gated (slash/mod.rs:468,730).
3) /loop dispatch arm (slash/mod.rs:484) is NOT feature-gated while its canonical-list entry IS (cfg loop, :732) — without the 'loop' feature /loop is dispatched but not tab-completable and is_known_slash_command returns false; confirm cmd_misc::cmd_loop compiles feature-off.
4) Doc-claim corrections already filed separately: /panel auto threshold (dirge-8855), automatic compaction (dirge-008x), plan-fork isolation (dirge-hth2), summarization/subagent provider (dirge-nw25).
close_reason: Ungated /loop in canonical list; README table updated (+/plan +/kill +/panel debug, feature-gating notes). Cross-ref claims fixed under 8855/hth2/nw25.

title: Provider low-severity: profile reasoning/temperature ignored by /agent; ModelFamily supports_* computed but unused; install_plugin_providers silent no-op on 2nd call
description:
Bundled LOW findings:
1) cmd_model.rs:382-461 cmd_agent applies prompt/deny_tools/model but never consumes def.reasoning or def.temperature; rebuild reads CLI/config not the profile. docs/agents.md:54-55 list them as honored frontmatter for /agent with no caveat (only the subagent path is disclaimed).
2) model_family.rs:36-39,77-84 compute supports_system_prompt/supports_tools (for the DeepSeek R1 reasoner) but no caller reads them — only is_deepseek_chat() is consulted; the documented R1 handling (relocate preamble, no tools) is not implemented.
3) resolve.rs:337-341 install_plugin_providers discards OnceLock::set result (let _ =); a second install (hot-reload) is silently ignored.
close_reason: Fixed: install_plugin_providers now warns on an ignored re-registration instead of let _ =. Left (documented, feature-scope): /agent doesn't apply profile reasoning/temperature (needs session override plumbing); ModelFamily.supports_* are R1-contract fields consumed only by is_deepseek_chat (R1 enforcement is future work).

title: Plan/loop/lifecycle low-severity: apply_patch skips LSP, worktree leak on canonicalize fail, DAP cancellation unwired, --max 0 unbounded, reviewer bash deniable
description:
Bundled LOW findings:
1) apply_patch.rs has no append_lsp_block — multi-file patches don't surface inline diagnostics (write.rs/edit.rs do).
2) git_worktree/mod.rs:142-144: if 'git worktree add' succeeds but the subsequent canonicalize() fails, the function returns Err leaving a leaked on-disk worktree with no cleanup.
3) dap/session.rs: AbortSignal is ignored in launch/attach/continue/step (_signal 'reserved for future'); a hung op blocks for the full timeout, no early Ctrl+C abort.
4) cmd_misc.rs:636 /loop --max 0 => unbounded (by design, documented); only exit is /loop stop / Ctrl+C / Esc — no wall-clock or cost ceiling.
5) MEDIUM-ish: reviewer fork (spawn.rs:326) carries the parent PermCheck; if the active agent profile denies bash, the reviewer can't run the code and (per its asymmetric bias) tends to emit NEEDS_FIX for everything, silently degrading /plan.
close_reason: create() cleans up leaked worktree on post-add error; merge-test parallel temp-dir collision fixed (atomic counter) + child git cwd pinned (CI flake). apply_patch LSP / DAP cancellation / --max 0 / reviewer-bash documented as deferred.

title: Tools/loop low-severity: read cache note dropped on hit, symlink dup cache key, reflexion non-canonical json, verifier over-broad cmd match
description:
Bundled LOW findings:
1) read.rs:402 stores info without the relational default-note; on a warm cache hit (:233) the 'limit defaulted to 2000' note is missing (cosmetic).
2) read.rs:224 builds cache_key/fs_stamp from raw args.path while the gate uses resolved_path — reading via a symlink path vs real path makes two cache entries (efficiency only, not stale).
3) run.rs:1052 records reflexion via serde_json::to_string (not canonical_json like storm/scavenge), so key-order-different identical calls can appear twice in the abandoned-approaches block (display only).
4) verifier.rs:142-150 is_verification_command over-matches ('build','check','cargo' substring) so 'cd build && ls' or 'git checkout' silences the gate; deliberate-but-leaky tradeoff.
close_reason: Fixed: reflexion now dedups on canonical_json (matches storm/scavenge). Left (cosmetic/efficiency/deliberate, documented): read cache-hit note drop, symlink dup cache key, verifier broad command match.

title: Permission low-severity: grep subtree reads, bg-shell cap TOCTOU, minified-name deny, /allow drift, sandbox cwd
description:
Bundled LOW findings from the permission/tools review:
1) grep.rs:123 checks only the search root then reads every descendant (grep.rs:230) and returns matching content lines; a user 'read' deny on a subtree is bypassable (read maps to Operation::Read = builtin-allow, so narrow). Same walk-without-per-file-check in find_files/list_dir/repo_overview (names/counts only, no content leak). Dotfiles hidden by default mitigates the .env case.
2) bash/mod.rs:128 checks running_count()>=cap then register() (:134) as two lock acquisitions; register only enforces STORE_CAPACITY(32) not MAX_CONCURRENT_SHELLS(8) — transient over-spawn under concurrent dispatch (theoretical; tools dispatch serially).
3) edit_minified.rs:93 / read_minified.rs:111 call enforce with tool string 'edit'/'read', so deny_tools:[edit_minified] is a no-op (no concrete-name probe like MCP tools have). Canonical 'edit'/'read' deny still covers them.
4) cmd_misc.rs:478 /allow add keeps its own KNOWN_PERM_TOOLS list that has drifted from BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES (fail-closed, but contradicts single-source-of-truth).
5) /mode yolo at runtime (cmd_model.rs:139) gives no 'deny rules now inert' warning; the audit-H11 warning is wired only at startup (main.rs:165).
6) sandbox.rs:56 binds std::env::current_dir() not the permission checker's working_dir; can diverge after set_working_dir.
close_reason: Fixed: runtime /mode yolo deny-rule warning; /allow uses BUILTIN_TOOL_NAMES; atomic try_register for the bg-shell cap (+test). Documented/left (narrow or mitigated): grep subtree reads, sandbox cwd, minified-name deny (covered by canonical edit/read).

title: Preserve last reasoning block across context compaction (conditional)
description: Forge extracts the LAST reasoning_details from the folded message range and re-injects it into the first post-fold assistant message (forge_app/src/compact.rs:124-169), preventing reasoning-chain breaks. dirge's compression.rs has zero reasoning handling. CONDITIONAL: dirge currently does not persist reasoning into foldable session history (live-only), so this only matters IF/when dirge persists reasoning across turns (Claude interleaved-thinking cache continuity). ~20-line technique to port when applicable. From Forge codebase review.

title: ZSH ':' prefix shell-plugin mode (port from Forge)
description: Forge's shell-plugin/ intercepts shell lines starting with ':' and turns ':fix the bug' into 'forge -p "..." --conversation-id <id>', keeping all : commands in one conversation with tab-completion — never leave the shell. The zsh scripts are agent-agnostic; adapt them to call dirge instead of forge. New complementary UX (dirge only has TUI + one-shot today). Depends on the --session resume flag. Reuse: port/adapt forge's shell-plugin/*.zsh rather than rewriting. Source: ~/src/forgecode/shell-plugin/.
close_reason: Merged in #383 — lean zsh ':' prefix plugin

title: --no-color only wired at 2 sites; not applied to the ratatui scene
description: Verification during dirge-zh7q found that --no-color (cli.no_color) is applied via resolve_color() at only TWO write_line sites in ui/mod.rs (~2754, ~3624). The main ratatui scene (chat text, panels, status, avatar, frames) receives RAW theme colors — theme::agent()/error()/etc. do not consult no_color, and there is no global desaturation in render_frame. So --no-color is largely non-functional in the TUI. Proper fix needs a design choice: a global no_color flag consulted by the theme accessors (returning Color::Reset), OR threading no_color into the Scene and resolving per-cell — while keeping errors/warnings visible (see theme test 'error_and_warn_stay_loud'). The dead per-struct monochrome fields were removed in dirge-zh7q; this is the actual coverage fix.
close_reason: Merged in #378 — --no-color wired through theme accessors

title: Verify --no-color wiring through ratatui path; remove vestigial monochrome fields
description: After the picker scene-integration (dirge-92em), monochrome/set_monochrome on the pickers and Renderer::monochrome appear write-only — --no-color seems handled at the theme level. Verify whether --no-color is fully wired through the ratatui render path (does it actually desaturate?), then either wire the monochrome flag through where it's needed or remove the dead fields + their setters/callers.

title: Right-side panel boxes not centered in their pane (left ones are)
description: The sub-panel boxes on the right side of the TUI are not center-aligned within the right pane the way the left panel's boxes are. Match the left panel's horizontal centering for the right panel boxes (ui/tui/panels).

title: Unify JSON signature canonicalization for dedup
description: storm.rs dedups tool calls via raw serde_json::to_string (insertion order, no normalization) while run.rs uses canonical_json (sorted keys, normalized numbers) and scavenge.rs does no normalization. A 1 vs 1.0 or reordered-keys duplicate can slip past one but not another. Route all three through one canonical signature fn. LOW.
close_reason: Merged in #368 — shared canonical_json; closes dirge-7bwx review-fix #6

title: Disambiguate the four 'plan/task/work' concepts
description: plan_enter/exit (read-only mode lock), phased /plan workflow (agent/plan/* + ActivePlan), write_todo_list (in-session checklist), and task/task_status (background subagents) overlap in mental model. tools/plan.rs already carries a comment warning it's 'distinct from plan-mode, similar name'. No documented answer to 'what happens if in plan-mode AND run /plan'. Disambiguate naming + document interaction; consider deriving the todo list from ActivePlan on kickoff. MED (naming/docs).
close_reason: Merged in #369 — canonical 4-concept map + module headers; rename rejected (model-facing API)

title: De-magic the 0.75 fold threshold (constant vs inline literal)
description: context_manager.rs:30 defines HISTORY_FOLD_THRESHOLD=0.75; compression.rs:143 should_compress hardcodes 0.75 again. Same number, two modules, no cross-reference — silent drift risk if one is updated. Make should_compress reference the constant. LOW.

title: DECSET-2026 sync-update brackets go to redirected fd 1 (dead anti-flicker)
description: renderer.rs ~696 executes BeginSynchronizedUpdate/EndSynchronizedUpdate on std::io::stdout() (fd 1, dup2'd to the log for the TUI), and gates on IsTerminal(stdout). So the ?2026 begin/end never wrap the actual terminal.draw (which writes to a separate /dev/tty fd) and the per-frame flicker mitigation doesn't run. Fix: write the begin/end through the same /dev/tty writer ratatui uses; base the sync decision on tty availability.
notes: INVESTIGATION (confirmed): renderer.rs ~706-719 runs Begin/EndSynchronizedUpdate on std::io::stdout() gated on IsTerminal(stdout), but terminal.draw writes to the ratatui CrosstermBackend whose writer is /dev/tty (BackendWriter::Tty via open_tty_for_write). Since TerminalGuard dup2's fd 1 to the log, the ?2026 brackets go to the log while the frame goes to /dev/tty — different streams, so the synchronized-update never wraps the draw → dead anti-flicker (and bracket bytes pollute the log). The CORRECT pattern already exists 9 lines below: the OSC title write uses terminal.backend_mut().write_all(&osc) (the /dev/tty backend). FIX: emit BeginSynchronizedUpdate/EndSynchronizedUpdate via terminal.backend_mut() (same /dev/tty stream as the draw), and base the 'sync' gate on tty/backend availability instead of IsTerminal(stdout).
close_reason: Merged in #370 — sync brackets via tty backend + tui_sync_capable gate

title: Picker overlay paints terminal-default bg, not the theme #222
description: src/ui/picker.rs ListPicker::draw / FilePicker::draw write rows via direct crossterm with no background color (and Clear(CurrentLine) resets to terminal default). With the phosphor #222 theme background, opening a picker shows a default-bg band over the charcoal field. Fix: emit SetBackgroundColor(theme::background()) around picker line writes (guard on != Reset), or render the picker as a ratatui overlay inside render_frame so it inherits the bg fill.
notes: INVESTIGATION (corrected diagnosis): the bug is bigger than 'wrong bg color'. picker.rs ListPicker::draw / FilePicker::draw write to std::io::stdout() = fd 1, which TerminalGuard dup2's to the log/dev-null for the whole TUI (terminal.rs redirect_stdout_stderr_to_log). ratatui paints via a SEPARATE /dev/tty fd. So the picker candidate-LIST overlay is written to the redirected fd and never reaches the screen — it is INVISIBLE, not merely mis-colored. The typed @query and the inserted selection ARE visible (they flow through the ratatui input buffer via handle_picker_key), so @-completion is degraded (no visible suggestion list), not dead. git: fd isolation (commit 83e3ea9, 2026-05-23) was added AFTER the pickers and did not refactor them — orphaned. FIX: render the picker candidate list through the ratatui Scene/render_frame (inherits theme #222 bg) — the architecturally-correct fix matching '83e3ea9: every byte on the terminal must come from a ratatui draw call'. A direct /dev/tty write would fight ratatui's diff engine. Issue title/desc should be updated from 'wrong bg' to 'overlay routed to wrong fd / invisible'.
close_reason: Merged in #371 — picker overlays rendered through the ratatui scene (visible, diff-safe, theme bg)

title: Round D: ellipsize + transcript-builder unification + head_tail truncators
description: #6 text::ellipsize/ellipsize_oneline (8 sites, standardize on …). #7 unify build_transcript + build_critic_transcript via render_transcript(items, style) — PRESERVE critic labels/budgets exactly (load-bearing). #8 text::head_tail(s,budget,ratio,marker) for the 3 head+tail truncators — preserve model-facing markers verbatim. M effort/risk.

title: Round C: now_unix_secs() + lock_ignore_poison() ext-trait
description: #4 a time util now_unix_secs()/now_unix_millis() for ~25+ SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)...unwrap_or(0) sites. lock_ignore_poison() ext-trait for ~50 .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) sites. Mechanical, low-risk, many call-site edits.

title: launch_run helper: dedup the 10-site spawn-and-assign block in the UI loop
description: The 'spawn_runner + set agent_rx/abort/interject/cancel + is_running=true' block is copy-pasted across ~10 sites (6 in ui/mod.rs, 4 in run_handlers/done.rs). Most bug-prone duplication (miss one mutable => leaked task). Factor into one helper taking &mut to the run-state slots. Deferred from R2 (dirge-5oxu/F3 done in #348) because it spans the core interactive loop (not just vix code) which has no integration tests — wants its own focused PR. Compiler does NOT catch a missed mutable here, so verify carefully.

title: Single-pass prompt rendering (placeholder-injection hardening)
description: plan_prompt uses sequential String::replace; user request containing literal {{FINDINGS}} would be clobbered by the second replace. Render in one pass.

title: Cross-ref docs: /plan workflow vs plan-mode (plan_enter/plan_exit)
description: Two unrelated 'plan' features collide (plan_tx vs plan_kickoff, active_plan). Add //! to tools/plan.rs clarifying it's plan-MODE not /plan; reciprocal cross-ref in plan_workflow.

title: Group plan modules under agent/plan/ (workflow.rs + runtime.rs + mod.rs map)
description: plan_workflow.rs (policy) + phased_orchestrator.rs (runtime glue) are a coherent pair but share no name prefix, hurting grep-discoverability. Group under agent/plan/ with a mod.rs map.

title: Fix stale doc-comments in vix-port modules
description: minify.rs header says 'not yet integrated' but read_minified/edit_minified call it; plan_workflow.rs allow-note half-true; done.rs //! omits the /plan reviewer loop.

title: run_interactive 12b+: extract permission/question/picker sub-loops (needs UiLoopCtx)
description: Follow-up to dirge-0b0s (12a, merged #309). The remaining run_interactive sub-loops (permission-decision ~90 LOC, question/multi-select picker, modified-files picker) are entangled with ~9 mutable loop-locals (renderer, user_rx, session, is_running, loop_label, context, bg_store, shell_store, interjection_queue, input) plus two inline closures (with_queue, perm_mode) that capture loop state. Safe extraction first needs a shared UiLoopCtx struct (+ a draw_status helper to absorb the repeated StatusLine::render/draw_bottom incantation) threaded by &mut through the loop — a deliberate medium-risk refactor, NOT a drop-in 'safe stage'. Deferred: user scoped this session to safe stages only. When picked up: introduce UiLoopCtx, convert with_queue/perm_mode to methods, then extract the sub-loops as &mut UiLoopCtx consumers. -D warnings + full suite green each step.
close_reason: wontfix: run_interactive's residual complexity is intrinsic event-loop coupling, not accidental co-location. UiLoopCtx would relocate coupling behind indirection (reborrow friction, god-object), not remove it; doesn't touch the ~950-line key handler (the real blob); and lives in the least unit-testable code (live TUI loop) so risk-to-verify is high for small payoff. Revisit only if the permission/question UI sub-loops become a frequent edit/bug hotspot.

title: Test suite assumes Unix semantics — fails broadly on Windows
description: When the windows-default config runs 'cargo test' on windows-latest, ~30 tests fail: they assume Unix path semantics (symlinks, /tmp, '/' separators, absolute-path checks). Examples: permission::checker/path symlink+cwd tests, agent::tools::{apply_patch,glob,output_relay,tests::require_absolute_path}, session::storage::delete_session_removes_file, lsp::init root_uri, tests::checker_tests. The new ci.yml windows job is BUILD-ONLY for now (mirrors release.yml, which only builds) — making the test suite Windows-clean (path normalization in tests/helpers, or cfg(unix)-gate the Unix-only ones) is deferred here. Once green, re-add the Test step to the ci.yml windows job.

title: Expose Timeouts via [timeouts] config + thread resolved values into LSP/MCP/bash/stream
description: Follow-up to dirge-onlr. crate::timeout::Timeouts now centralizes the per-operation timeout defaults and Config::resolve_timeouts() is the accessor seam, but it returns Timeouts::DEFAULT — there is no config override yet and consumers read Timeouts::DEFAULT directly. To make timeouts user-configurable: (1) add a serde [timeouts] block (TimeoutsConfig: stream_chunk_secs, tool_call_gap_secs, mcp_call_secs, mcp_init_secs, lsp_request_secs, lsp_initialize_secs, bash_secs) and merge it in resolve_timeouts(); (2) thread the resolved Timeouts into LspManager::new (currently no Config param; REQUEST_TIMEOUT/INITIALIZE_TIMEOUT are consts), the MCP tool closure + client connect (mcp_call/mcp_init), the bash tool default, and rig_stream's tool_call_gap; (3) consolidate the existing stream_chunk config paths (providers.<n>.stream_chunk_timeout_secs + top-level stream_chunk_timeout_secs) so stream_chunk isn't configured 3 ways. Deferred from dirge-onlr because the manager constructors don't receive Config and threading is cross-cutting.

title: Subagent-chat event channel is unbounded
description: task.rs SubagentChatSender = mpsc::UnboundedSender<SubagentChatEvent>; emit_chat is a sync producer. A runaway subagent could grow the channel if the parent UI stalls. Display-only and LLM-rate-limited, so low severity. Fix: convert to a bounded channel + try_send (lossy on overflow is fine for a display stream; the real result returns via the tool result). Touches ~8 send sites + the test channels at task.rs:884,920.
close_reason: Fixed in PR: bounded subagent-chat channel (1024) + try_send; display-only events so overflow-drop is safe. Tests + full suite green.

title: UI renders on every streamed token (no render coalescing)
description: ui/mod.rs AgentEvent::Token arm calls render_agent_stream on EVERY token. A burst of buffered tokens (agent->UI channel holds up to 256) renders N times instead of once. Fix: in the Token arm, render only when agent_rx.len()==0 (caught up to the last queued event) OR a frame interval (~16ms) elapsed — localized, no event reordering. Must verify the ToolCall/Done arms commit/render the streamed response so a throttled final token still lands. Perf only; bounded by LLM token rate today.

title: MCP stderr forwarder emits many [truncated] chunks for one runaway line
description: spawn_stderr_forwarder caps a long line then clears buf and keeps accumulating the SAME logical line (no drain-to-newline), emitting many ...[truncated] chunks. Fix: a draining flag set at truncation, skip bytes until next newline.
close_reason: Fixed in round 4 provider/MCP batch (TDD): config-alias collision skip, default_model_for_entry/alias provider-type resolution, StderrLineSplitter drain. Full suite green.

title: Memory lock staleness only checked on attempt 0 -> spurious timeout
description: FileLock::acquire only runs stale-lock (PID-liveness) detection at attempt 0; if the holder dies mid-wait, the remaining retries never re-check and it times out, leaving an orphan lock. Fix: re-check staleness periodically across attempts.
close_reason: Fixed in round 3 stability batch (TDD): LSP SpawnSlotGuard, edit keep_disjoint_ranges, memory superset-drift, per-attempt lock staleness. Full suite green.

title: MCP external-path guard only scans top-level args; nested paths bypass
description: extract_arg_paths (mcp/tool.rs:552) only checks top-level scalar string values; nested objects/arrays-of-objects with path args bypass the external-dir guard. Fix: recurse all serde_json::Value nodes.
close_reason: Fixed in round 2 security batch (TDD): invisible-char typo, atomic_write umask split, coarse bash redirect/mutation gating, MCP nested-arg path walk. Tests pass default + no-default-features.

title: Plugin: RPG repository planning graph (follow-up to PlanSearch)
description: Implement an RPG-style repository planning graph plugin for multi-file codebase generation: a structured graph blueprint (capabilities -> file structure -> data flows -> functions) fed in as context to guide coherent multi-file generation. Follow-up to the PlanSearch-lite plugin. Paper: RPG: A Repository Planning Graph for Unified and Scalable Codebase Generation — https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.16198

title: harness/lsp: no timeout on LSP query freezes plugin worker
description: A hung language server blocks the Janet worker thread until shutdown. Add a bounded wait or documented caveat.

title: harness/lsp: run_query duplicates lsp tool dispatch/coords/diagnostics
description: Op match, touch_file, saturating_sub(1), diagnostics canonicalize-fallback mirror src/agent/tools/lsp.rs and have drifted (no goToDefinition/findReferences aliases). Extract shared dispatch helper.

title: harness/lsp: diagnostics path clones entire diagnostics map
description: all_diagnostics() clones HashMap<PathBuf,Vec<Diagnostic>>; harness then clones one entry. Add diagnostics_for(&Path) accessor for O(one file).

title: harness/lsp: read_uint_arg silently coerces negative/NaN/Inf to 0
description: Bad coordinates masquerade as line 1 after saturating_sub. Surface invalid input instead of silently clamping.

title: flaky test: output_relay relay_prepends_header_note races on BASH_INLINE_MAX
description: relay_prepends_header_note and config_override_changes_threshold share the BASH_INLINE_MAX global atomic; run in parallel the threshold mutation intermittently fails the former. Pre-existing; surfaced during dirge-tkyn full-matrix runs. Fix: serial guard or per-test threshold injection.
close_reason: Merged in PR (test-stability-and-path-helpers): dead dev_null parser removed, canonical_or_self helper, output_relay test serialized

title: LOW: misc resolution dups (EXA_API_KEY 3 ways, edit-family classification, preview truncation)
description: (a) EXA_API_KEY read builder.rs:475/915 + config:596 + websearch.rs:41 w/ 2 empty-string policies (trim vs not). (b) edit-family set write|edit|apply_patch grouped differently in tool_input_repair.rs:404, avatar.rs:50 (+todo), classify.rs:18 (+bash/mcp). (c) one-line preview chars().take(N)+ellipsis dup ~8 places vs events.rs:88 truncate_oneline. Fix: shared helpers (exa_api_key(), ToolKind enum, preview()).
notes: EXA_API_KEY single-sourced via config::exa_api_key() (PR #212). Remaining (edit-family classification, preview truncation) deferred — see review notes.
close_reason: EXA_API_KEY single-sourced via config::exa_api_key() (PR #212). Remainder triaged: edit-family classification groupings (avatar +write_todo_list, classify +bash/mcp) legitimately DIFFER per purpose — not true duplication; one-line preview truncation is cosmetic, spread across UI with per-site N values. Not worth a forced helper. Triage-closed.

title: LOW: canonicalize-or-literal reimplemented at 5 sites w/ inconsistent fallbacks
description: best-effort canonicalize w/ literal fallback copy-pasted (modified.rs:41, repo_overview.rs:125, lsp/diagnostic.rs:81/101/141, lsp/server.rs:43/45/89) w/ differing fallbacks (to_path_buf/clone/into); none reuses canonicalize_for_cache (the macOS /tmp symlink handler). Fix: promote canonicalize_or_literal() to a shared crate util.
close_reason: Merged in PR (test-stability-and-path-helpers): dead dev_null parser removed, canonical_or_self helper, output_relay test serialized

title: LOW: dual /dev/null detection (engine classify vs bash tokenizer flag)
description: engine build.rs:70 detects /dev/null by resolved/raw string (single documented place); bash.rs has a parallel parse_bash_segments_with_dev_null _dev_null flag (bash.rs:424) that's discarded. /dev/./null or symlink disagrees. Fix: let classify_path dev_null be sole authority; drop the tokenizer flag.
close_reason: Merged in PR (test-stability-and-path-helpers): dead dev_null parser removed, canonical_or_self helper, output_relay test serialized

title: LOW: per-operation timeouts configured 5 ways; no shared deadline/budget
description: stream(300s,config), TOOL_CALL_GAP(30s), MCP_CALL_BUDGET(120s, the only real elapsed-aware budget), MCP_INIT(10s), LSP REQUEST(30s)/INITIALIZE(45s), bash(120s). Retried ops w/ fresh tokio::timeout take 2x nominal. Fix: a Deadline/budget type (generalize MCP remaining_budget) + one named-timeout config struct. config/mod.rs:408, rig_stream.rs:127, mcp/tool.rs:246, lsp/manager.rs:50.

title: LOW: loop-guard counter bumps per-claim not per-request
description: Engine::commit loops over req.claims and records each; multi-claim requests with duplicate (op,key) over-count, making the doom-loop threshold inconsistent. Dedupe keys per request.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #204 (TDD, 4 new tests); 2104 tests pass at -D warnings

title: INFO: add deny_unknown_fields to permission config structs
description: RuleConfig/PermissionConfig lack serde deny_unknown_fields; post-cutover a typo (operation/pattern/glob) silently degrades to permissive default.
close_reason: Fixed + merged in PR #204 (TDD, 4 new tests); 2104 tests pass at -D warnings

title: Plugin: context-transform hook (transformContext / context)
description: Gap vs pi (types.ts:186,1106). LoopConfig.transform_context exists as a host-only field (types.rs) but has no Janet surface — plugins cannot prune/inject the message array before each LLM call. Add harness/transform-context returning a new transcript, dispatched from stream_assistant_response's transform_context slot. One hook satisfies both pi 'transformContext' and the 'context' event.

title: Plugin: system-prompt injection hook (before_agent_start)
description: Gap vs pi (types.ts:1112). pi extensions can read+append the assembled system prompt before the agent starts. dirge plugins cannot influence the system prompt at all (only register tools/providers). Add an on-agent-start hook exposing the system prompt with an append/override slot (harness/append-system-prompt). Wire from the builder/spawn path where the system prompt is assembled.

title: Plugin: compaction hooks (session_before_compact / session_compact)
description: Gap vs pi (types.ts:1098-1102). dirge plugins have no hook around context compaction — can't cancel a fold or supply a custom summary. Most valuable for memory/curator-style plugins. Add Janet hooks fired from the loop's compaction path (run.rs run_compaction_pass): on-before-compact (cancellable) + on-compact (custom summary). dirge already fires on_pre_compress on the MemoryProvider trait; this would expose the same boundary to Janet plugins.

title: Plugin: mid-run model swap via Fn(Context)->StreamFn factory
description: Second half of the plugin-review HIGH finding. prepareNextTurn thinking_level is now applied (run.rs ~863), but a plugin returning a new MODEL via harness/set-next-model is still dropped with a warning — the StreamFn bakes the CompletionModel at construction (rig_stream_fn_from_model) and isn't part of LoopConfig, so it can't be swapped mid-run. Fix: restructure run_loop to accept a Fn(Context)->StreamFn factory (or a model-keyed registry) so prepareNextTurn can rebuild the stream fn for the next turn. pi parity: agent-loop.ts:229 rebuilds config with new model+reasoning. Until then, plugin model swap is a no-op (warned).
design: DEFER (design review verdict). Run-boundary model swap ALREADY works: harness/set-next-model -> take_pending_next_model rebuilds the agent at Done (done.rs) and headless returns ModelSwap (main.rs). Only the intra-run (between-turns) swap is unwired, with NO in-tree consumer. The warn at run.rs prepare_next_turn handler is honest. BUILD only when a plugin needs intra-run model escalation. Spec'd approach (strategy A, ~afternoon's work): add StreamFnFactory = Arc<dyn Fn(&str)->StreamFn + Send+Sync> type alias in stream.rs; run_loop/run_agent_loop gain trailing param model_factory: Option<&StreamFnFactory> (32 call sites pass None); hold let mut current_stream_fn = stream_fn.clone() and use it at the stream call; in the prepare_next_turn handler, if let Some(factory) + update.model, rebuild current_stream_fn = factory(&model) + update config.model_name. Build the factory in provider/mod.rs spawn_runner capturing client+tool_defs+filter+chunk_timeout+provider, body = client.completion_model(name) + build_stream_fn_with_filter match + retrying_stream_fn wrap (refactor the AnyAgentInner match into a shared free fn). thinking_level swap already works (PR #188). Tests: factory invoked + next turn uses swapped fn; ignored-without-factory negative.

title: UsageStore read-modify-write is not transactional (lost-update TOCTOU)
description: Pre-existing, surfaced during dirge-ba0m review. UsageStore::load() acquires no lock; the file lock in save() only makes each byte-level write atomic, not the load→modify→save cycle. When the skills curator's mechanical pass (apply_automatic_transitions loads one snapshot, issues many set_state+save) runs concurrently with a live skill-tool counter bump (record_use/record_view/record_patch from a new interactive turn), one side's writes can be lost. The dirge-ba0m orchestrator overlap guard reduces orchestrator-vs-orchestrator overlap but does NOT cover curator-vs-live-skill-usage during a fresh turn. Fix: make UsageStore RMW transactional — hold acquire_usage_lock across load+modify+save, or re-load-under-lock before each save. Orthogonal to the three-pass coordination (ba0m); separate change in src/extras/skills/usage.rs.

title: session_db → MEMORY.md insight extraction (audit finding D + E)
description: session_db (transcripts, FTS5-searchable) and MEMORY.md (consolidated knowledge, frozen-snapshot system prompt) never cross-pollinate. The model can re-read its old turns via session_search but can't query 'what did I learn from project X?'. on_pre_compress fires only during compaction, not at every session end. Build an extraction pass that reads recent session transcripts (post-Done, via the unified runner from dirge-ba0m) and proposes additions/updates to MEMORY.md. This implicitly requires an after_turn or post_session learning hook surface (audit finding E) — design that surface as part of this issue rather than separately.
design: Lean FTS-snippet cross-session insight extraction (judge design #2). Skeptic returned DEFER (MEMORY.md is already a cumulative cross-session store; only non-redundant value is aggregating sub-threshold signals recurring across >=2 sessions) — user chose to build the full stage anyway with skeptic's safeguards baked in. NEW module src/extras/cross_session_extractor.rs: CrossSessionState{last_run,first_check,last_scanned_watermark} mirroring MemoryCuratorState. Gating: seed-and-defer first check + 14-day interval (STRICTLY longer than 7-day memory curator) + watermark requiring >=3 new prior sessions since last scan. Mechanical scan: SessionDb.list_sessions_rich excluding current id + fork sources, FTS search_messages over seed-vocab themes (build/test cmd, naming convention, architecture, library quirk, user correction, attempted-and-failed), count DISTINCT session_ids per theme, keep themes spanning >=2 sessions absent from MEMORY.md (token-overlap check), assemble bounded snippet bundle (HARD ~12-16KB budget, no full transcripts, NO new session_db method). LLM half run_cross_session_extraction in review.rs: reuse spawn_memory_curator_runner (memory-only) + AbortRunnerOnDrop + new CROSS_SESSION_PROMPT that feeds verbatim MEMORY.md+PITFALLS.md as ALREADY-KNOWN, enforces >=2-distinct-session bar, forbids single-session facts (background review's job), caps 1-2 adds, paraphrase-dedup discipline (store add() is EXACT-match-only per memory_store.rs:218, won't catch reworded dupes). Orchestrator: 4th/last stage in post_session after memory-curator; thread current session_id into spawn_post_session. Reports under .dirge/memory/.cross_session_reports/{ts}/. REJECTED design #1 (full transcripts + new DB method) and #3 (metadata-only).

title: Unify post-session review + curator runners (audit finding F)
description: Two parallel post-session LLM passes run after Done with no coordination: spawn_background_review (review.rs:114-211, COMBINED_REVIEW_PROMPT, updates skills + memory via tools, rate-limited 15min) and spawn_curator_review (review.rs:226-336, CURATOR_PROMPT/dirge-odv3, consolidates skill clusters, periodic 7d). Both fire-and-forget, both spawn runners, both write to disk, share nothing. A skill the review just created could get archived by the curator before its first use. Unify into a single post-session orchestrator that runs review-then-curator sequentially with shared state, or at minimum coordinates so curator skips skills created in the last review pass.
design: Sequential post-session orchestrator (new src/agent/post_session.rs). Single tokio::spawn runs three stages strictly in order, each .await'd before the next, each bounded by a per-stage timeout: (1) background review (writes skills+memory), (2) skills curator (mechanical apply_automatic_transitions via spawn_blocking + LLM consolidation if candidates), (3) memory curator (run_mechanical_pass via spawn_blocking + LLM consolidation if stale). Ordering is the coordination primitive — eliminates review-creates/curator-archives race + concurrent-LLM-runner storm + .usage.json/MEMORY.md TOCTOU, all via happens-before. review.rs spawn_* split into pub(crate) async fn run_* cores + thin spawn_* wrappers (keeps existing tests green). claim_review_slot stays scoped to the review stage; curators keep 7-day should_run_now gates. A timed-out/errored stage is skipped, next stage still runs. REJECTED: process-global mutex/semaphore (dirge is single-session/process; claim_review_slot + within-task sequencing suffice), CREATED_THIS_RUN set (redundant: record_create saves synchronously + ordering guarantees visibility), LearningPass trait (YAGNI until dirge-6js7 adds a second insight-shaped pass). Extends for dirge-6js7 via one more stage in the sequence.

title: Auto-compaction / Phase 4: proactive 40% fold trigger
description: Reasonix fires a proactive fold at 40% context-ratio inside long multi-iter turns, before the 75/78/80% emergency thresholds. Dirge has no proactive trigger — context manager fires at 75% only. Add a Fold decision when ratio crosses 40% and the turn has accumulated N tool-result tokens.

title: on_memory_write 'content' param ambiguous for remove
description: memory.rs:164 passes old_text as third arg; trait names it 'content'. Plugin providers will interpret content as 'new value being written' and mis-handle remove. Either rename param to 'payload' + document the per-action meaning, OR pass empty string on remove and add a 4th arg for old_text.

title: maybe_fire_session_end silently skips empty sessions
description: Helper added if session.messages.is_empty() { return; } — old inline code in cmd_session fired unconditionally. Providers using on_session_end as a 'flush cached state' signal (independent of transcript) lose the event on empty-but-active sessions.

title: move on_memory_write fire from MemoryToolStore impl to MemoryTool layer
description: Currently MemoryToolStore::add/replace/remove call on_memory_write internally. Means (a) custom providers need to remember to fire it, (b) wrapping providers double-fire. Fix: MemoryTool::call invokes provider.action() then provider.on_memory_write() once. MemoryToolStore impl stops calling it.

title: end-to-end test that build_agent_inner emits the assembled preamble
description: Unit tests verify assemble_base_preamble() content but no test confirms the actual Agent's preamble (as reached by the model) contains those guidance blocks. Add an integration test that drives build_agent_inner with stub DI and inspects the resulting agent's preamble.

title: curator persists per-run REPORT.md with before/after diff
description: Hermes curator writes REPORT.md per pass with consolidations applied. Dirge logs to tracing only — no audit trail. Add report-writing path that records candidate list before/after, tool actions, summary.

title: curator LLM pass should use skill-only runner
description: spawn_curator_review reuses spawn_review_runner which ships memory + skill tools. Curator prompt says skill-only but only the prompt guards. Add a skill-only review runner so the curator can't write memory entries even if it tried.

title: --print --no-session passes Some(session.id) contradicting docstring
description: main.rs:752 passes Some(session.id.to_string()) even when cli.no_session is true. builder.rs:65 docstring says None is only correct for one-shot non-session runs. Fix: gate Some(...) on !cli.no_session OR delete misleading comment.

title: add: MemoryProvider trait for pluggable backends
description: MemoryToolStore is concrete-class only. Hermes has MemoryProvider abstract with on_session_end/on_pre_compress/on_memory_write hooks. Refactor to trait + dyn dispatch + lifecycle hooks.

title: fix: LAST_REVIEW timestamp updated before spawn completes
description: review.rs:103 updates LAST_REVIEW before the background task runs. If spawn fails synchronously, the next session skips review. Mark only after completion.

title: add: LLM consolidation pass to skill curator
description: curator.rs comment says 'Optional review fork (with LLM) for consolidation' but only mechanical aging runs. stale_names is dropped. Either build umbrella-merging pass (cf. hermes curator.py:1369-1555) or remove the comment.

title: MCP: allow per-server external-directory access
description: MCP tool calls are restricted to the working directory like built-in tools. Some MCP servers legitimately need access outside the cwd (e.g. semantic indexers operating on a separate target dir). Add a per-MCP-server config flag (e.g. allow_external_paths: true) that bypasses the cwd check for that server.

title: bash permission: /dev/null redirects should be auto-allowed
description: Commands ending in '> /dev/null' or '2> /dev/null' or '&> /dev/null' should not prompt — there's no observable side effect. Add a whitelist pattern in the bash permission checker.

title: Phase 8 integration: Curator::new() called twice per session end — once for should_run_now() check, once inside the spawned task. Redundant file I/O, state could drift between calls.
close_reason: Already resolved — phase-8 integration cleaned up the double Curator::new() call. Only one Curator::new() exists in the curator check block, used for both should_run_now() and apply_automatic_transitions().

title: Phase 8 integration: memory injection into system prompt (agent/builder.rs) uses synchronous I/O — load_memory() and load_pitfalls() read from disk during agent construction, blocking startup on slow filesystems (NFS). Should use spawn_blocking or async load.

Phase 7 compression: COMPACTION_PROMPT filter-safe preamble may not be sufficient — LLMs can still follow inline commands in reference material despite being told not to. Needs stronger delimiters and explicit prohibitions.
Phase 6 curator: archive_skill removes existing destination before rename. Two concurrent curator processes could race — one removes the other's archived skill just before renaming.
close_reason: Fixed: archive_skill now skips if destination exists instead of removing it. Concurrent curator processes safely no-op.

title: Phase 6 curator: should_run_now() uses last_run==0 as never-run sentinel — if system clock is epoch-0 this would always return false, preventing curator from ever running
close_reason: Fixed: last_run changed from u64 to Option<u64>. None means never run, avoiding epoch-0 collision.

title: Skill name validation in format.rs rejects valid Unicode/special character names that some projects may use
YAML frontmatter parser in skills/format.rs is line-by-line, can't handle nested structures or multi-line descriptions
CI gate: reduce 174 clippy warnings
MODIFIED panel: user-driven scrolling
description: Add PageUp/PageDown (or mouse scroll while hovering the right panel) to walk through older entries in the MODIFIED sub-panel. Currently the panel auto-grows to fill available rows and shows a '+N older' footer when truncated — but the older entries aren't reachable. Needs a modified_offset field plumbed through PanelData, plus key handling in the main loop while focus is on the right panel (or via /modified-scroll command).
acceptance_criteria: User can scroll the MODIFIED list with PageUp/Down or mouse wheel; offset persists across redraws; +N older footer reflects current offset.

title: doc + buffer_pos_at fix: M-R3, H-R2, L-R3, L-R4
description:
Four doc-leaning items + one small code fix from review-2:
- M-R3: PLUGINS.md note that dirge's display=false is suppression-only (no plugin-observable transcript yet)
- H-R2: PLUGINS.md note that prepare-arguments blocks the executor synchronously; keep handlers light
- L-R3: buffer_pos_at uses entry.text.chars().count() which includes escape bytes. Switch to strip_ansi-aware count so column clamping reflects visible chars.
- L-R4: one-line code comment in JanetLoopTool::execute explaining post-handler batch replay semantics for emit-tool-progress

title: cleanup batch: L-R1 dead binding, L-R2 cfg salad, M-R1 dead filter, M-R2 double-lock
description:
Four cosmetic fixes from review-2:
- L-R1: drop unused name_owned capture in JanetLoopTool::execute
- L-R2: simplify the four-#[cfg] block around CustomMessage arm in ui/mod.rs — gate the whole arm under cfg(feature=plugin) since AgentEvent::CustomMessage can't be produced without it
- M-R1: remove dead filter in drain_tool_progress call (filter for matching tcid only — invariant says all entries match, dead-code defensive)
- M-R2: fold resolve_custom_message_render to one PM acquisition instead of two

title: L*: phase-9 polish (key spec strictness, modifier matching, registration validation, unregister symmetry)
description:
Five low-severity items from phase-9 review:

L1: parse_key_spec accepts 'f01' as F(1); s[1..].parse::<u8>() reads leading zeros. Add strict-digits check.

L2: match_shortcut does exact modifier equality. ctrl-x binding doesn't match Ctrl+Shift+X. Documented behavior in PLUGINS.md so plugin authors know to bind shift- explicitly when wanted.

L3: no unregister-* counterparts. Pi has unregisterProvider. Defer unless a plugin author asks; pi's symmetry is also partial.

L4: smoke test depends on plugins/example_*.janet disk state. Convention matches existing test_plugin.janet; document as load-bearing.

L5: harness/register-tool doesn't validate name charset. LLM tool-call mechanism may break on names with spaces or special chars. Add regex match [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ in the Janet helper.

title: H2/M1/M2: handler arg expansion (tool_call_id, display flag, dynamic shortcut refresh)
description:
Three gap-fillers, batched because they share the harness-API expansion theme.

H2: JanetLoopTool::execute discards tool_call_id and on_update. Pi forwards both (toolCallId for LLM correlation, onUpdate for streaming progress). Add tool-call-id as second handler arg; expose (harness/emit-tool-progress text) helper bridged via thread-local to on_update.

M1: Pi CustomMessage has display: boolean (messages.ts:50) — when false the message is in transcript but not rendered. Dirge always renders. Add display to the wrapper (lands with C1's customType change).

M2: plugin_shortcuts snapshotted once at UI startup. Pi rebuilds the shortcut map on demand (interactive-mode.ts:1625, 5324). Re-snapshot when a new registration happens (or on each keystroke — cost is one Janet eval).

title: 9e: docs + integration test + plugin example for phase 9
description: Update docs/plugins.md (or create if missing) covering the new ExtensionApi surface. Add an end-to-end integration test that loads a Janet plugin which registers all 5 things (tool, command, provider, shortcut, message-renderer) and verifies each path. Add an example plugin under examples/ or test fixtures.
close_reason: 9b deferred separately; 9a/9c/9d complete with docs + examples + smoke test

title: 9d: harness/register-message-renderer for custom message types
description: Pi's registerMessageRenderer lets extensions render custom message types. Dirge already has LoopMessage::Custom variant. Allow Janet plugins to declare a renderer by custom-type-name + handler-fn that returns formatted string. Host uses the renderer when displaying messages of that type. Acceptance: a Janet plugin can register a renderer; the UI invokes it for matching messages.

title: 9c: harness/register-shortcut for keybindings
description: Pi's registerShortcut lets extensions register key shortcuts (KeyId + handler). Map to dirge's keybinding system. Plugins register a name + key combo + Janet handler fn. Host calls the handler when the key is pressed. Scope-limited: this is interactive-mode-only; non-interactive runs simply ignore registered shortcuts. Acceptance: a Janet plugin can register a shortcut that triggers a Janet handler when pressed.

title: Summary budget validation before LLM call (M9)
description: ui/slash.rs:87 validates AFTER LLM returns. If summary too large, /compress already ran the LLM call. Pre-prune messages further when the projected summary exceeds budget.

title: Sandbox should fail-fast when bwrap is missing
description: sandbox.rs:10 warns at construction but wrap_command still attempts bwrap. Make Sandbox construction fail when --sandbox enabled AND bwrap missing, OR auto-disable with a louder warning.

title: LSP initial backoff is 10s, too long for transient crashes
description: lsp/manager.rs:109 starts at 10s. Transient crashes (LSP segfault on parse error, e.g.) need faster initial retry. Tune to 1s initial, then exponential up to cap.

title: --api-key flag should support stdin/file input
description: provider.rs:196 warns + proceeds with key on argv. Add support for --api-key-stdin (read from stdin) or --api-key-file <path>. Keep --api-key for compat with warning.

title: SymbolIndex per-file cache has no LRU bound on ensure_file
description: semantic/index.rs ensure_file inserts unconditionally; MAX_INDEX_FILES=2000 only applies to bulk ensure_all. Long sessions in giant monorepos can grow the cache without bound. Cap + LRU-evict.

title: Scrollback buffer unbounded; OOM risk on long sessions
description: ui/renderer.rs:65 buffer: Vec<LineEntry> grows without cap. Long session with verbose tool output (large grep results, repeated test runs) accumulates indefinitely. Add front-truncate when buffer exceeds N entries.

title: Retry sleep not cancellable; full backoff blocks Ctrl+C
description: agent/runner.rs:463 bare tokio::time::sleep(delay).await — no select! against interject_tx. User waits full backoff (seconds for 429 with Retry-After) before any interrupt processes. Outer task.abort kills full agent state — kills graceful state.

title: No concurrent subagent bounding; unlimited spawns
description: task.rs tokio::spawn per task with only 10-min timeout, 32 LRU. No Semaphore, no concurrency cap. An LLM could spawn dozens of background tasks burning API budget in parallel.

title: Ruby private/protected methods exposed as public
description: adapters/ruby.rs:84-117 — every method pushed with is_exported=true. Doesn't track private/protected/public visibility toggles. --exported-only over Ruby files returns the entire private API.

title: .h files always C-adapter even in C++ projects
description: adapters/c.rs claims .h; cpp.rs claims .hpp/.hh/.hxx. C wins by insertion order in find_for_file. C++ headers (with class/namespace/template) parse as C-tree-sitter → classes/namespaces silently missing from list_symbols. Prefix-scan for class/namespace tokens to pick adapter.

title: LSP coverage: 4 servers for 10 semantic languages
description: lsp/server.rs:155 builtin_servers returns rust, typescript, pyright, clojure-lsp only. Semantic adapters: bash, c, cpp, go, java, python, ruby + above. Add gopls, jdtls (or eclipse.jdt.ls), clangd (c/cpp), ruby-lsp/solargraph, bash-language-server.

title: find_callers is regex-based, not tree-sitter structural
description: semantic/index.rs:148 uses regex \b{name}\b. Hits string literals, comments, identifier collisions. find_callees correctly uses tree-sitter find_callees_in_range. Match the approach.
close_reason: Substantial — needs new adapter API mirroring find_callees_in_range across all 10 adapters. Deferred to dedicated round.

title: Semantic tools acquire exclusive write lock even on reads
description: list_symbols, find_definition, find_callers, find_callees, get_symbol_body all call RwLock::write() because ensure_file takes &mut self. After populate, repeat reads should use read() — needs interior mutability on the cache (RefCell or Mutex inside) so ensure_file can be &self.
close_reason: Implemented in this commit. SymbolIndex now uses Mutex<FileCache> for interior mutability; all public methods take &self; 5 semantic tools switched .write() → .read() on the outer RwLock.

title: (H15 followup) Auto-reconnect MCP on tool-call failure
description: Manual reconnect API exists via McpClientManager::reconnect. Auto path requires swappable Peer shared across McpTool clones for the same server. Design + implement.

title: (H17 followup) Auto-continue after auto-compaction
description: Implement compact-and-resume: on ContextLength error mid-stream, fire compaction then re-submit the last prompt. Needs runner/session API plumbing. Opencode compaction.ts:477-558 is the reference.
close_reason: Implemented in this commit via AgentEvent::ContextOverflow + UI auto-compact+respawn handler. opencode-style automatic recovery (compaction.ts:477-558 equivalent).

title: grep/find_files truncation footer reads 'and 0 more'
description: agent/tools/find_files.rs:140-148 + grep.rs:280-288 break the walk at MAX_RESULTS so total - MAX = 0. Footer should reflect 'more results not shown' OR continue counting past MAX without pushing.

title: list_dir has no entry cap
description: agent/tools/list_dir.rs accumulates every entry. Pulling node_modules with include_hidden=true returns 100k+ paths. Add MAX_LIST_DIR_RESULTS mirroring grep/find_files.

title: Git branch lookup at startup has no timeout
description: agent/builder.rs:105-124 — spawn_blocking git rev-parse can hang startup on wedged git (NFS, fsmonitor, broken gitconfig). Wrap in tokio::time::timeout, fall through to no-branch banner.

title: Clipboard child.wait() has no timeout
description: ui/renderer.rs:1414-1426 — pbcopy/wl-copy/xclip child.wait() blocks indefinitely if helper hangs (XWayland breakage, Wayland freeze, no DISPLAY). Bound at 2-3s.

title: Event reader thread races TerminalGuard drain
description: Bg event::read() loop has no shutdown signal; races the drop drain. Benign (bytes consumed either way) but flaky.

title: Phase 4: polish + tests for wrap/queue/panel
description: Resize handling for panel + wrap + queue. Unit tests for wrap cursor mapping, interjection queue ordering, modified-files dedup. Update README/CONFIG with /panel docs.

title: Phase 0: confirm memory tool + /help + /clear work
description: Smoke-test that already-existing features behave as expected: memory tool (view/write/delete), /help lists current slash commands, /clear resets the session view. No code changes unless something is broken.

title: Memory: GC superseded/tombstoned rows (long-term DB growth)
description: dirge-8h22/dirge-fa10: tombstoned and superseded memory rows (and their FTS entries) are never hard-deleted. Consistent design, acceptable short-term, but an unbounded DB-growth vector over a long-lived project. Consider a curator-driven archive/GC for very old tombstones/supersessions.

title: Plugin: document API-shape divergences from pi + align plugin feature default
description: Document-only items from the plugin gap review (not bugs): (1) get-steering/get-followup are PUSH-only (harness/add-steering / add-followup) — there is no pull-slot named like pi's getSteeringMessages/getFollowUpMessages, so a plugin DEFINING those slot names is silently ignored. (2) registerProvider covers base-url/type override only — no custom models, OAuth, or stream handlers vs pi. (3) setModel/setThinkingLevel are between-runs only (model needs the StreamFn-factory bead). (4) 'plugin' is not in Cargo [features] default but build.sh enables it — either add to default or document so a bare 'cargo build' isn't mistaken for the shipped config. Update plugin docs/README accordingly.
close_reason: Documented new hooks + pi divergences in docs/plugins.md; feature-default kept as build.sh-enabled per lower-risk decision (PR #191)

title: Plugin: message + session-lifecycle observer hooks (message_end, session start/resume/fork/reload)
description: Grouped MEDIUM/LOW gaps vs pi. (1) message_end (types.ts:1119): no hook to rewrite a finalized assistant message — only tool results are replaceable via on-tool-end. (2) session lifecycle events (types.ts:1092-1103): plugins only get one-time on-init; no on-session-start/end/resume/fork/reload with reason. Add observer hooks fired from the session rotation/compaction paths (review.rs maybe_fire_session_* already centralizes some of this on the MemoryProvider trait — expose to Janet).
close_reason: message-end hook shipped end-to-end (PR #189). session-lifecycle observer half deferred as low-value (marginal over existing on-init); refile if a consumer needs resume/fork signals.

title: read tool pre-allocates 4096-byte sample for every read
description: read.rs:215-218 — wasteful for tiny files. Use file_size.min(4096) as sample size.

title: recovery::pseudo_random can collide in same nanosecond
description: recovery.rs:146-156 nanos.wrapping_add(salt) — concurrent agents with same attempts salt in the same nanosecond slot get identical jitter. Add a process-local atomic counter to the splitmix input.

title: plugin_tree silently swallows session-save errors before destructive ops
description: plugin_tree.rs:94,110 — let _ = save_session(...) before reset_to_new/SwitchSession. If save fails (disk full, permission) previous session is lost. Surface a Warning effect.

title: question tool silently drops answers on count mismatch
description: question.rs:182-195 zip(answers) drops trailing questions if fewer answers. Pad missing with [no answer] or surface as an error.

title: webfetch perm prompt doesn't surface URLs
description: webfetch.rs:233-247 perm_summary is 'fetch N urls' with no hosts. User reviewing the prompt for 1 URL has no idea which one. Include host(s) when count <= 3.

title: find_recent_sessions parses every session before truncating
description: session/storage.rs:277-296 reads + JSON-parses all *.json then truncate(limit). For 5000+ sessions this is gratuitous I/O. Sort filenames by mtime first, parse top limit.

title: read tool counts every line past offset+limit
description: read.rs:230-265 keeps next_line()-ing past want_end just to populate total_lines. For 1GB log w/ user-requested lines 1-100 still reads all 1GB. Early-break with total_lines='>=N' when limit satisfied + small margin.

title: (R7 doc-only) Add prompt-merge precedence + tool-cache-race comments
description: H14 (prompts.rs load order) + H8 (cache batch-clear race is intentional). Add inline comments documenting the contracts so future readers don't re-flag.

title: Semantic cache key should combine mtime + size (C5+M7)
description: index.rs:36-43 compares only mtime. On 1-sec-granularity FS (HFS+, FAT, NFS), rapid same-second writes after our cache fill yield identical mtime. Add metadata.len() to the cache-validity key.

title: Doom-loop detection bypassed in Accept mode too (M1 partial)
description: checker.rs Accept mode flows through track_doom_loop, but the bypass concern from M1 was specifically about Accept being permissive. Verify: doom-loop should fire even in Accept; if so this is closed already. Otherwise extend.
close_reason: Verified: Accept mode IS subject to doom-loop detection (track_doom_loop fires inside the action!=Deny branch). Only Yolo bypasses, and Yolo's whole semantic is 'all rules off'. Coupled with the R4 H11 warning at startup, this is by-design behavior + visible to the user — not a code bug.

title: Clojure defmethod parent_class is the multifn, not the dispatch value
description: adapters/clojure.rs:155-169 — (defmethod shape :circle …) gets parent_class=Some('shape'), self-referential. Dispatch value :circle is what makes the method unique. Either use dispatch value as parent_class or set None.
